# Thread: MtDNA and Hypoxia Himalayan powerhouses: How Sherpas have evolved superhuman energy efficiency
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtdna-and-hypoxia-himalayan-powerhouses-how-sherpas-have-evolved-superhuman-energy-efficiency.19531/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Coates — 2017-05-24T13:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mtdna-and-hypoxia-himalayan-powerhouses-how-sherpas-have-evolved-superhuman-energy-efficiency.19531/post-214564>

**Himalayan powerhouses: How Sherpas have evolved superhuman energy efficiency**

**Sherpas have evolved to become superhuman mountain climbers, extremely efficient at producing the energy to power their bodies even when oxygen is scarce, suggests new research published today in the _Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences_ (_PNAS_).

The findings could help scientists develop new ways of treating hypoxia -- lack of oxygen -- in patients. A significant proportion of patients in intensive care units (ICUs) experience potentially life-threatening hypoxia, a complication associated with conditions from haemorrhage to sepsis.

When oxygen is scarce, the body is forced to work harder to ensure that the brain and muscles receive enough of this essential nutrient. One of the most commonly observed ways the body has of compensating for a lack of oxygen is to produce more red blood cells, which are responsible for carrying blood around the body to our organs. This makes the blood thicker, however, so it flows more slowly and is more likely to clog up blood vessels.

Mountain climbers are often exposed to low levels of oxygen, particularly at high altitudes. This is why they often have to take time during long ascents to acclimatise to their surroundings, giving the body enough time to adapt itself and prevent altitude sickness. In addition, they may take oxygen supplies to supplement the thin air.

Scientists have known for some time that people have different responses to high altitudes. While most climbers require additional oxygen to scale Mount Everest, whose peak is 8,848m above sea level, a handful of climbers have managed to do so without. Most notably, Sherpas, an ethnic group from the mountain regions of Nepal, are able to live at high altitude with no apparent consequences to their health -- as a result, many act as guides to support expeditions in the Himalayas, and two Sherpas are known to have reached the summit of Everest an incredible 21 times.

Previous studies have suggested differences between Sherpas and people living in non-high altitude areas, known collectively as 'lowlanders', including fewer red blood cells in Sherpas at altitude, but higher levels of nitric oxide, a chemical that opens up blood vessels and keeps blood flowing.

Evidence suggests that the first humans were present on the Tibetan Plateau around 30,000 years ago, with the first permanent settlers appearing between 6,000-9,000 years ago. This raises the possibility that they have evolved to adapt to the extreme environment. This is supported by recent DNA studies, which have found clear genetic differences between Sherpa and Tibetan populations on the one hand and lowlanders on the other. Some of these differences were in their mitochondrial DNA -- the genetic code that programmes mitochondria, the body's 'batteries' that generate our energy.

To understand the biological differences between the Sherpas and lowlanders, a team of researchers led by scientists at the University of Cambridge followed two groups as they made a gradual ascent up to Everest Base Camp at an elevation of 5,300m.

The study was part of Xtreme Everest, a project that aims to improve outcomes for people who become critically ill by understanding how our bodies respond to the extreme altitude on the world's highest mountain. This year marks 10 years since the group's first expedition to Everest.

The lowlanders group comprised 10 investigators selected to operate the Everest Base Camp laboratory, where the mitochondrial studies were carried out by James Horscroft and Aleks Kotwica, two PhD students at the University of Cambridge. They took samples, including blood and muscle biopsies, in London to give a baseline measurement, then again when they first arrived at Base Camp and a third time after two months at Base Camp. These samples were compared with those taken from 15 Sherpas, all of whom were living in relatively low-lying areas, rather than being the 'elite' high altitude climbers. The Sherpas' baseline measurements were taken at Kathmandu, Nepal.

The researchers found that even at baseline, the Sherpas' mitochondria were more efficient at using oxygen to produce ATP, the energy that powers our bodies.

As predicted from genetic differences, they also found lower levels of fat oxidation in the Sherpas. Muscles have two ways to get energy -- from sugars, such as glucose, or from burning fat (fat oxidation). The majority of the time we get our energy from the latter source; however, this is inefficient, so at times of physical stress, such as when exercising, we take our energy from sugars. The low levels of fat oxidation again suggest that the Sherpas are more efficient at generating energy.

The measurements taken at altitude rarely changed from the baseline measurement in the Sherpas, suggesting that they were born with such differences. However, for lowlanders, measurements tended to change after time spent at altitude, suggesting that their bodies were acclimatising and beginning to mimic the Sherpas'.

One of the key differences, however, was in phosphocreatine levels. Phosphocreatine is an energy reserve that acts as a buffer to help muscles contract when no ATP is present. In lowlanders, after two months at high altitude, phosphocreatine levels crash, whereas in Sherpas levels actually increase.

In addition, the team found that while levels of free radicals increase rapidly at high altitude, at least initially, levels in Sherpas are very low. Free radicals are molecules created by a lack of oxygen that can be potentially damaging to cells and tissue.

"Sherpas have spent thousands of years living at high altitudes, so it should be unsurprising that they have adapted to become more efficient at using oxygen and generating energy," says Dr Andrew Murray from the University of Cambridge, the study's senior author. "When those of us from lower-lying countries spend time at high altitude, our bodies adapt to some extent to become more 'Sherpa-like', but we are no match for their efficiency."

The team say the findings could provide valuable insights to explain why some people suffering from hypoxia fare much worse in emergency situations that others.

"Although lack of oxygen might be viewed as an occupational hazard for mountain climbers, for people in intensive care units it can be life threatening," explains Professor Mike Grocott, Chair of Xtreme Everest from the University of Southampton. "One in five people admitted to intensive care in the UK each year die and even those that survive might never regain their previous quality of life.

"By understanding how Sherpas are able to survive with low levels of oxygen, we can get clues to help us identify those at greatest risk in ICUs and inform the development of better treatments to help in their recovery."

The 10th anniversary of the original Caudwell Xtreme Everest expedition will be marked this month by a conference at the Royal Society of Medicine, and an event open to the public on the evening of 23rd May at the Royal Geographical Society entitled A Celebration of Six Decades of Medicine on Everest.

The research was part-funded by the British Heart Foundation.

**Story Source:**

[Materials](http://www.cam.ac.uk/research/news/himalayan-powerhouses-how-sherpas-have-evolved-superhuman-energy-efficiency) provided by [**University of Cambridge**](http://www.cam.ac.uk/). The original story is licensed under a [Creative Commons License](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). _Note: Content may be edited for style and length._

**Journal Reference**:

1.  James A. Horscroft, Aleksandra O. Kotwica, Verena Laner, James A. West, Philip J. Hennis, Denny Z. H. Levett, David J. Howard, Bernadette O. Fernandez, Sarah L. Burgess, Zsuzsanna Ament, Edward T. Gilbert-Kawai, André Vercueil, Blaine D. Landis, Kay Mitchell, Monty G. Mythen, Cristina Branco, Randall S. Johnson, Martin Feelisch, Hugh E. Montgomery, Julian L. Griffin, Michael P. W. Grocott, Erich Gnaiger, Daniel S. Martin, Andrew J. Murray. **Metabolic basis to Sherpa altitude adaptation**. _Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences_, 2017; 201700527 DOI: [10.1073/pnas.1700527114](http://dx.doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1700527114)

**

# Thread: Environmental analysis for optimization of performance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-analysis-for-optimization-of-performance.19533/>

### Mason Baptista — 2017-05-24T23:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-analysis-for-optimization-of-performance.19533/post-214606>

This will be my first entry entailing my daily routine the past couple months. I'll be getting lab results to examine how that went. I'll be moving for the summer then I'll be back here for the next season.
Fort Wayne
Latitude 41 longitude 85 population 250000 Elevation 810

Moving for 3 months too Green Bay
Latitude 44. Longitude 88 Population 100000 Elevation 581

Routine last 4 months
Morning
2 min cold/warm contrast shower
3 min grounding and morning sun exposure
Afternoon
CT 10 min cold shower or ice bath 15-20 both entire body
UVA on while in doors
Little sun in winter but when I was outside I would be in shorts. Also did my wim hof breathing in the sun
Red light IR exposure after the sun set for 2-3 hours
Orange blue light blockers at home in front of the tv or whenever artificial light was on me and clear lens blue light blockers when I went out.

Water
Reverse osmosis water the majority of the time

Diet
90% high protein (seafood,eggs,meat), fat (pasteurized butter in winter, little coconut oil) and greens (Brussels, broccoli and kale majority) 10% gluten free carbs (with travel and road games sometimes would be only option

With the sun out now I'm outside at least 30 mins a day with majority of my skin exposed. Recently had a minor surgery, completely off meds other then Advil for inflammation and am flying to Seattle and back the next week. Know this is both two toxic environments and plan to ground, drink lots of water and be outside every chance I get on the trip. Anyone have some suggestions to mediate the flight?

Will have a new routine up once I get moved into Green Bay

# Thread: HI. My name is Maria... and I'm a BLUE Light-aholic!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-my-name-is-maria-and-im-a-blue-light-aholic.19537/>

### Maria Bacarella Mole' — 2017-05-25T19:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-my-name-is-maria-and-im-a-blue-light-aholic.19537/post-214686>

**Age:** 49 **Sex:** F **Joined JackKruse.com:** \[May 5, 2017\]

**Location:** \[Long Beach NY USA\]

**Dx:** \[No official diagnosis here\]

**Surgeries:** \[N/A\]

**Current Rx:** \[Amour Thyroid, Spironolactone and now some nasal crap for my Eustachian tube/sinuses\]

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** \[Auto-immune protocols for Hashi\]
SIBO self diagnosed, natural protocols: and it worked! | PCOS of the excessive bleeding| Working my way to full on CT... baby steps...

**Health WINS:** \[improving Vit D levels with Dminder \] \[finding Jack has been life altering\]

**Health Goals:** \[Working on improving my BUN/Creatinine Ratio: Can't help but wonder if Spironolactone affects this\]

**My Optimal Journal:** \[Have not yet created one, will do it one day\]

My story, really fast:
Born in Sicily and dragged to NY by my parents when I was a child. I long to move to somewhere with palm trees... sigh. Caribbean has my name all over it and I travel there in winters... it feels like home to me!! I have two children, 19 and 17 and feel blessed to have the family I have, even if they are all crazy
I've been working on computers for 15 years, approximately 100 hours per week in combination with photography and studio lights. Instinctively, I worked on the computer in the dark, not even knowing about blue light.

I've covered my skin COMPLETELY when 0utdoors and applied zinc up the whazoo when in the sun.

I've managed to stay trim, despite my Hashi.
Always had cold hands and feet.
I've been IF-ing for more than 20 years, before it even had a name. It just always felt right to not eat for more than 16-18 hours at a clip.
Sinus infections every year until I moved to the beach.
DHEA in the toilet as per recent labs.
Malnutrition/Anemia according to labs.
Adrenals in toilet.
BUN/creatinine Ratio: 13
ABSOLUTELY exhausted...

Waiting for MTHRFR results

I moved oceanfront 5 years ago when I started surfing and still did most everything wrong except for the fact that I have never worn sunglasses... BUT now I know about Jack and the light and am thrilled I was so "reckless"

Now, for some positive stuff.

Walking in the water/sand on beach every day now sine JK.
Looking to sun in the morning when there are no rain clouds every day now since JK.
Wear my red glasses at night.
Wear my yellow glasses in the day when indoors/computer
HDL: 85
No pre-diabetes/diabetes in sight, low blood glucose levels too
No heavy metal toxicity
CR Protein: 1.4 but will have my HS-CRP checked next time
Ketones found in labs: +1
Heart rate: 58-60
BP: 110/70
Fixing SIBO naturally (And to think, I suffered 3 or more decades, Allison Siebecker is da bomb!)

My primary care doc is wonderful!! He is open to any blood work I want to do. He found the Hashi, not a specialist I went to prior.

The day my life changed: Two weeks ago, when I was laying in bed, instead of at work and was googling Hashi and extreme exhaustion... Jack's blog, "Hashi and Melasma" came up and I've been hooked on him since! Has that nice girl from London ever figured out she HAS to move? LOL

I've been listening to JK every day and have been reading EPI-Paleo RX.

XOXO-
Maria

Sedlmayr is now producing the water and currently ramping up production. They call it "Divinia Water" and i think the price is down to about 5 bucks a bottle now. He has collected some amazing evidence of this water curing chronic kidney disease, prostate cancer, and childhood Nephrotic Syndrome.

### AstralJam8 — 2018-02-23T07:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-ez-water.19541/post-235492>

Has anyone looked into the light absorption spectrum of EZ water? According to this article, bulk water has a peak absorption wavelength of 3.1microns (3100nm, mid-infrared) corresponding to the O-H stretch vibration fundamental with a second peak at ~650nm, and the layer of EZ water has a signature peak at 270nm (UVC.) There are infrared LEDs that can produce the first peak wavelength, but they're insanely expensive. The second peak, ~650nm, is in fact very cheap. It seems like there are two stages to EZ production if you want to go the LED route: First stage is using infrared (3100nm or ~650nm) to resonate the fundamental OH stretch mode in the bulk water and stimulate formation of the EZ layer, second stage is pumping the EZ layer with UV (~270nm.) I would try this in the presence of ice, which goes directly to the EZ phase before mixing with the bulk water, and with some kind of hydrophilic material in the mix (SiO2 / Polished Quartz? Zinc? Nafion?) Also, the study revealed that heat limits the extent of EZ boundaries, so I would also try to maintain the bulk water at 4°C (39.2°F) in accordance with the "anomaly point" of water's greatest density written about by Viktor Schauberger. The thought occurred to me that the bulk water might become excessively positive in charge, so why not dangle a grounded silver-coated mesh at the point furthest from your hydrophilic substrate? One question though is whether it's possible to form free-floating "clusters" of EZ, perhaps dissolved minerals (fulvic acid? activated salt?) could serve as "nucleation sites" ?

Seems EZ enough

Regarding temperature control, are Peltier coolers detrimental due to the DC flow of current through them? I see there are "mini fridges" which operate on 12VDC which would seem ideal for smooth and accurate temperature control.

Here's the article with all the interesting details:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2843558/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2843558/)

### AstralJam8 — 2018-03-04T08:03:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-ez-water.19541/post-236250>

Here's something which might relate to DDW:
[http://www.waterjournal.org/volume-5/whitney](http://www.waterjournal.org/volume-5/whitney)

"The EZ ion with a **Deuterium ion included is at lower energy**, and so forms more easily than the one without Deuterium. This EZ ion will be preferred. So the action of forming EZ water can tend to concentrate the kind of reactant that CF requires."

Also this:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2865192/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2865192/)

"Additionally, heavy water (deuterium oxide) was tested. The size of the EZ was 200 ± 20.3 μm (n = 5), compared to the EZ of H2O, which was 220 ± 17.8 μm (n = 5)."

So it looks like the formation of EZ tends to prefer the heavier Hydrogen isotopes, and because they are slightly weaker in charge, I'm guessing they would comprise the upper stratum nearest the bulk water. If there were some way to "siphon" the deepest layer, or circulate the bulk water so that as much heavy water as possible has the opportunity to fall into the EZ layer, it might be possible to recover DDW on either side of the EZ layer.

# Thread: Why Did Spaceflight Lengthen Telomeres?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-spaceflight-lengthen-telomeres.19551/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-05-27T06:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-spaceflight-lengthen-telomeres.19551/post-214826>

Jack,

Reading your Ubiquination 1 blog post; why did this astronaut's telomeres get longer in space? I guess you could argue it was some kind of pre-cancerous state? Even the article title has nothing positive to say about space travel.

[http://www.nature.com/news/astronaut-twin-study-hints-at-stress-of-space-travel-1.21380](http://www.nature.com/news/astronaut-twin-study-hints-at-stress-of-space-travel-1.21380)

Relevant part is right after the heading "Space Case":

"Studies of the twins' telomeres, the caps on the ends of their chromosomes, showed that during spaceflight Scott's telomeres grew to be longer than his brother's. “That is exactly the opposite of what we thought,” says Susan Bailey, a radiation biologist at Colorado State University in Fort Collins. A second lab has studied the same samples and confirmed this puzzling increase in telomere length, she says.

Once Scott returned to the ground, the length of his telomeres returned to his pre-flight levels relatively quickly. The scientists are working to figure out what this means, and are running a separate study of telomere length in ten unrelated astronauts that, when completed in 2018, may shed more light on how spaceflight affects telomeres.
"

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-28T03:18:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-supps-crossing-the-bbb.19555/post-214893>

The majority of DHA present in the human brain is incorporated during the brain growth spurt which starts at week 26 of gestation and imposes a high demand for the fatty acid until about 2 years of age. DHA is required when neuronal and glial differentiation and migration, and active myelination and synaptogenesis took place in the brain morphogenesis. There is now convincing evidence in the cite below that neural developmental milestones, determine long-term brain functional capacity in adults. It is supposed that when brain milestones has passed it may be too late to intervene with omega-3 long-chain fatty acids in neurological/neuropsychological disorders such as, depression, and bipolar disorder, mood and cognition, schizophrenia, Alzheimer’s disease and Huntington’s disease, among others neurological diseases. It has been demonstrate that as the individual ages, a constant reduction of the DHA content of the brain occurs, and in some neurological diseases, such as Alzheimer’s disease, a more pronounced reduction of the fatty acid occurs to show a serious loss of volume in MRI's in the temporal and frontal lobes. There is also a lot of loss of DHA in AMD and many retinal diseases associated with myopia and retinal tears. Epidemiological evidence now suggest that a decrease in brain DHA levels, which normally occurs during aging, and that is exacerbated by reduced dietary intake of DHA and increased loss from artificial light, may increase the risk of neuro-degenerative diseases and retinal diseases........and points out why a blue lit and microwaved world is a real modern problem getting worse by the day.

CITES:

1.  Das, U. N. (2003). Long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acids in the growth and development of the brain and memory. Nutrition, 19, 62-65.

There is now convincing evidence in the cite below that neural developmental milestones, determine long-term brain functional capacity in adults. It is supposed that when brain milestones has passed it may be too late to intervene with omega-3 long-chain fatty acids in neurological/neuropsychological disorders such as, depression, and bipolar disorder, mood and cognition, schizophrenia, Alzheimer’s disease and Huntington’s disease, among others neurological diseases. It has been demonstrate that as the individual ages, a constant reduction of the DHA content of the brain occurs, and in some neurological diseases, such as Alzheimer’s disease, a more pronounced reduction of the fatty acid occurs to show a serious loss of volume in MRI's in the temporal and frontal lobes. There is also a lot of loss of DHA in AMD and many retinal diseases associated with myopia and retinal tears. Epidemiological evidence now suggest that a decrease in brain DHA levels, which normally occurs during aging, and that is exacerbated by reduced dietary intake of DHA and increased loss from artificial light, may increase the risk of neuro-degenerative diseases and retinal diseases........and points out why a blue lit and microwaved world is a real modern problem getting worse by the day.

# Thread: Floaters and blue field entoptic phenomenon
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floaters-and-blue-field-entoptic-phenomenon.19557/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2017-05-28T13:49:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floaters-and-blue-field-entoptic-phenomenon.19557/post-214914>

Hello.

I noticed a bunch of eye floaters 3,5 months ago. They happend to appear suddenly in both eyes at the same time. Like one day I went out and saw some things rushing through my vision. Now when it gets more bright outside because of summer sunlight I notice more of the floaters and they can actually be very annoying.
Also I can noticed the blue field entoptic phenomenon. I think I didnt had it before and there may be a context.
I can provoke slight flashes of light in absolute darkness when I move my eyes extrem to the sides (again both eyes), but they are barely noticable and must be provoked with movement.

Some stats of myself: male, 27 years old, myopic -6,25 on both sides.

Over the winter I wasnt outside a lot, no grounding, very much screen time (over the day with 2700K f.lux and at night with blueblockers) on laptop. I ate a good amount of carbs.

Do you guys have some ideas?

I read that posteriour vitreous detachment on both eyes same time is extremly seldom plus I am at a relatively young age. But I am somewhat strong myopic.
It is not easy to get a exam from an opthomologist here with no acute problems for that. So I keep asking and researching.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-28T15:50:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floaters-and-blue-field-entoptic-phenomenon.19557/post-214917>

this hints that you might have a lot of issues with Muller cells in the retina and often this is related to the flicker effect of light. 1989 David K. Berler did a study on monkey eyes with fluorescent lights. Studies of Müller cell biology using tissue culture and animal models provide evidence of the remarkable capacity of this cell type for graded responses to environmental insult, the capacity to proliferate, translocate from the retina and alter phenotype and thus, functional characteristics of the eye. There is now evidence that Müller cells function as an effector cell type in traction retinal detachment associated with proliferative vitreoretinopathy (PVR) and proliferative diabetic retinopathy (PDR) which leads to blindness. The first step in this process is that flickering leads to bubbles forming inside the Muller cells and Ithink the scattering of light from the bubbles might be the cause of floaters.

### Butters — 2017-05-28T20:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floaters-and-blue-field-entoptic-phenomenon.19557/post-214947>

So, does that mean, that the flicker effect from my laptop can cause this?
What gave you the hint? The floaters alone or in context with the blue sky phenomenon?
What can I do about that, except ditching of screens?

I just tested my screen with a camera. I use my screen a lot with dimmed lights to the minimum in the winter time, because I thought it would be better for melatonin production and the camera shows massive noticable flicker there.

To all questions?

And when the bubbles scatter light how can it form string floaters and move with eye movement.
I just hope you are right. There seems not to be a lot of information about this.
I quick read some of the study with the monkeys and that was impressive indeed.

I should also say I take an anti-nail fungus (antimycotica) compound to do on the nails for quite a time. Can stuff from there go into my eyes like deposit?

Hi All,

A little about me:

Essentially got the short end of the stick genetically, but mostly with the social environment I grew up in. I was born to two schizophrenic parents, and had a really messed up life until about age 21. Of course this all affected my mental/physical health but I've honestly come out pretty good considering.

Around 2014/15 I was on board with a lot of this stuff, modifying it and doing self experiments. I was in godly shape (some warrior type stuff) and never felt more energetic and happy.

Unfortunately, a combination of stress, really bad life circumstances, etc. happened and I fell off. I really want to get back to how I felt a few years ago and I'm taking steps towards that.

I know how to get back to where I was, but was depressed for a long time and I am now more into an apathy stage at the moment. I'm definitely doing better. Biggest thing right now is getting my sleep in order and have a binge eating problem that I think I'm slowly tackling (these things don't happen overnight)

Anyway, I gotta do this, so here I am. Glad to be back.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-29T05:09:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-on-board.19560/post-214967>

Is Schizophrenia a circadian disease too? Yes, it is. Neuroscientist Warren Meck thought that the basal ganglia’s dopamine loop was an internal clock because of its activation during temporal processing. It turns out it is likely a gear used in the SCN optical clock activated by the sun. Meck was the first to postulate that the rate at which signals traverse this loop offers the brain a time keeping mechanism. No one linked it to the SCN as a distal target of the optical lattice between the retina and basal ganglia.

We know now that increased dopamine increases the rate of the basal ganglia loop cycling. This forges connections between disparate parts of the brain. This suggests that more dopamine we make the faster our internal clock would seem. Our percpetions would be associated with a slower perception of external time. Time dilation 101. This occurs with increases in body temperature and yokes perfectly to UV light's ability to make dopamine in the eye during daytime. Darkness lowers levels of dopamine levels and a slower rate of neural circuit frequency would correspond to a slower internal clock and a perceived acceleration of external time. This is likely why low dopamine states are associated with time contraction and disease states and are linked to circadian de-coupling events. Today the microwaved blue lit world is the major de-coupler of humans. The association in dopamine and melatonin is a non linear relationship because both molecules are created and linked to specific light frequencies and this makes the relationship not so clear-cut for those who do not understand non linear optics well. The under- or over-activity of the dopamine system is linked with impairment in accurate time estimation, sometimes making it seem slower, sometimes faster. There is no obvious or linear correlation between perceived speed of time and the amount of dopamine present as one might suspect but I believe there is a fractal relationship due to a lack of UV and IR light perception in the retina. Schizophrenics have altered sense of time. They also have althered fragmented thoughts because of this inability to time reality properly. This is one reason why nnEMF affects attention and awareness because it ACUTELY can raise and lower dopamine levels seemingly altering reality as it unfolds. That is the world a schizophrenic observes. Focus and attention come easier when one’s CNS is not being pulled down multiple pathways by contravening evoked potentials from man made electromagnetic waves. Paying attention, establishes traction between your internal frequencies and the external world, synchronizing with it, using it to help calibrate your internal pace of timing. Deficits in time perception in Schizophrenic patients is helpful for the quantum clinician in understanding how sunlight builds time management in the brain.
Deficits in time perception have been observed in a range of disorders, and there is typically a trend to misjudge durations in most of them because of the uncoupling of the circadian mechanism:
•Manic patients tend to overestimate duration
•Depressive patients tend to underestimate duration
However, schizophrenic patients show poor estimation without a trend. Results are not systematic (Vogeley and Kupke, 2007)
Schizophrenic patients were asked to answer two kinds of discrimination questions:
•Do two stimuli have the same duration?
•Are they short or long?
They performed worse than controls but without a systematic bias towards over or underestimating. (Elvevag, 2003)
Dopamine transmission plays a massive role in schizophrenia. Goldsmith et al (1997) found an increased number of dopamine receptors in post mortem and PET scan studies of schizophrenics.
Interestingly, increase in dopaminergic transmission increases the speed of one’s ‘internal clock’, whilst inhibition slows it down. (Rammsayer, 1993)
As schizophrenia is characterized by a disruption of communication in the brain, the unsystematic nature of impairment is not surprising. It demonstrates that in healthy individuals, integration across the brain is key for temporal perception. [http://snip.ly/sp1e0#https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28506881?dopt=Abstract](http://snip.ly/sp1e0#https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28506881?dopt=Abstract)

# Thread: Low Leptin & Leptin Resistance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 61
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-05-29T15:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-214978>

It seems most people who are leptin resistant have high serum leptin levels (as seen in obesity, or metabolic syndrome) and one of the major telltales of this is inability to reach satiety, and usually results in being overweight and unable to fat burn. To me, this is a mismatch created between the cells, and the brain: the brain perceives starvation due to inflammation blocking the leptin receptor, while the cells are being stressed due to chronic overfeeding. With all the research I've done, hypogonadism (just one example of a hormone deficiency I focus in on because I suffer from it) is likely caused by leptin resistance, because the leptin receptor needs to be activated in the hypothalamus in order to upregulate the HPGA (Hypothalamtic Pituitary Gonadal Axis). Therefore, leptin resistance should result in low LH/FSH/Testosterone/Estradiol.

Well, I do remember reading JK's info on the Leptin Rx, about how leptin resistance can occur on the other end of the spectrum leading to anorexia rather than obesity. I assume this would be LOW serum leptin levels, but still a dysfunctional leptin receptor. I'm starting to think this is my issue, and the reason I have low LH/FSH leading to my low testosterone. I have no signs of leptin resistance. I am definitely a fat burner. I am lean, and can fast all day, anyday without food. I have 0 cravings for carbs (in fact, I crave fats a lot more, but in general since I've become a mitochondriac and get plenty of sun when it's there, I have much less desire to eat when I live like a tree).

Therefore, I feel healthy, and satisfied by feeding off sunlight, and my own body fat, that I never have a desire to eat a lot of food, and have no cravings. However, I still struggle with low T from low LH/FSH, in which I take HCG for as my hormone replacement therapy which makes me feel great. However, the ONLY time I was ever able to have somewhat normal T levels for my age was when I decided to try a Ray Peat type diet approach. That is, eating breakfast, eating carbs/sugar, eating every few hours, and trying to purposely make myself hungry (by becoming a sugar burner vs a fat burner). This approach seemed retarded to me. I experienced hunger every 2-3 hours, and was grazing. It just seems so counterproductive to living the way humans intended to, that I went back to my old, fat-burning ways, which took some time to adapt (finally a fat burner again). But as I made the switch back, my T levels crashed, and had to go back on HCG.

I was hoping to maybe talk to JK about this next weekend in Vermont should I have that privilege, but wanted to make a thread about it. Achieving normal T levels naturally at my young age of 27 is my ultimate goal. However, if my only way to do this is to become a sugar burner, then I think I would rather take HCG and remain a fat burner, then have naturally high T levels on a Ray Peat diet.

Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-29T16:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-214982>

low leptin and LR = circadian mismatch. Simple.

That is why anorexia and obesity causes are the same but the method to them involves a different type of oscillation problem that ruin the hypothalamus

### kris90 — 2017-05-29T21:54:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-215026>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) actually, I think Jack has given me the answer, and I think in the back of my mind I've known this all along. I'm glad I did my Ray Peat experience, because it's opened my eyes to why some of us perceive we need carbs.

I was able to increase my testosterone from 5.8 nmol/L to 21 nmol/L with following a Ray Peat approach. And if I continued, I probably would've gotten even higher. The reason for this is because living in a microwaved world forces a glucose metabolism, and we cannot fat burn. When I was off my meds (HCG), and trying to continue with IF and/or keto, I was trying to use diet to induce fat burning, but because environment trumps diet, I was left with my body being in ketosis, without being able to fat burn, and essentially starving. That is why my T levels dropped so low. When I started loading up on sugar like Ray Peat advises, I came out of that starvation mode, and my body responded by producing more T.

I realised that HCG is actually the biggest biohack I have ever done for myself. It might in fact be the trapdoor to becoming a fat burner in this microwaved world. It is only when I am on HCG long enough that I feel myself become a true fat burner, whereas without it, I'm forced to be a sugar burner, and without consuming enough sugar, I end up in starvation mode, thus low T.

I think I have 2 choices for myself if I continue to live in this environment:

1) Stay a sugar burner and eat more carbs and calories so my body stays out of hibernation mode, but expect my body to need another 10-20 lbs of fat in order to feel "safe" in this crappy environment, and expect to succumb to modern diseases
2) Use HCG as my trapdoor to access the fat burning pathway that is built in us. HCG is the pregnancy hormone after all, and it is yoked to hibernation to protect against starvation for pregnant women by inducing fat burning

I think I would rather use HCG, have high T, and live as a fat burner, then follow the modern sugar burning ways to thrive. And unfortunately, without the HCG, I will enter starvation mode.

Most that I have seen were attempts to use GnRH modulators.
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GnRH\_modulator](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GnRH_modulator)
GnRH modulators affect the secretion of the gonadotropins, luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), which in turn affects the gonads, influencing their function and hence fertility as well as the production of sex steroids, including that of estradiol and progesterone in women and of testosterone in men.

### kris90 — 2017-05-30T15:14:31+0300

Back in 2014 I used to train high volume calisthenics 90 minutes 3x per week. I would so something like 100 pushups, 100 pullups, 100 handstand pushups, 100 inverted rows, 100 dips, 100 situps and 100 jump squats in a single session. On top of this, I was doing BJJ classes 3-4x per week. We would do drills/30 min warmup, 30 mins technique, and then rolls (spar). The rolling was insane. 5 minute all out submission. Sometimes, I would roll 3 or 4 times in a session.

I did all this training around noon, completely fasted, and would not eat until about 3 hours post training. I was fasting for 20 hours per day, with a short 4 hour feeding window from late afternoon to early evening. I had no issues of hypoglycemia or anything, and no hunger issues.

This is when I was first prescribed HCG by my Urologist. That seems to be the key for me to be a fat burner.

### Sean Waters — 2017-05-30T17:15:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-215109>

Really good thread - I think I've stumbled on a similar issue Kris.

The last few weeks I've been feeling really tired some days, especially if my sleep has been affected or disrupted, I was thinking this was due to:

1.  not being in ketosis after eating too many "accidental" carbs in my foods/ gluconeogenesis
2.  EMF
3.  disrupted sleep
4.  **a combination of the above**

So I started doing the following:

1.  Fasting longer (24 hours to increase ketones)
2.  Avoiding EMF even more
3.  Focusing on not eating post 8pm and blocking light so sleep is better

Results:

1.  Still had no energy. Felt like I had weights strapped to me.
2.  Felt defeated, unmotivated, very down and frustrated.
3.  Words, memories etc. not coming to me as quick

I was in Ketosis between 1.8 and 3.0 mm/mol , showering in cold water daily. But I had these problems. It didn't make any sense.

This was all on Saturday morning.

So I realised my problem and solution is either one of two things:

1.  **The nnEMF** I get at work and in my city could potentially be ramped up, beyond my perception, or just winning the battle against me. And **I need to MOVE to another country to get better** and be optimal.
2.  **I need to start eating more carbohydrates**, as it's summertime, and **Ketosis is mis-matching with my dark brown skin tan** that I have developed over the last few weeks through exposure to UV index of 1 every morning, and UV index of 5 or 6 all day on weekends and at lunchtime during the week.

I've started planning the move.

I've also started eating more carbs

Strangely, I do feel better. Which is weird for me, as when I tested in 2016 I had a Pyruvate Deficiency that blocks glucose metabolism - leading to low energy, cognitive issues, mood etc.

I was discussing this exact thing on a thread in Optimal Fitness: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/)

This means my Pyruvate Kinase Genetic Deficiency - could quite possibly be "turned off" from the Sunlight, epigenetically... (Think I could be quite high on the Gerald Pollack "OUT ON A LIMB" meter with this one) OR I have just become Cold Adapted through CT'ing for over a year now, and developed Metabolic Flexibility.

So I'm not sure where to go from here...

-   Could I have a low testerone problem in Ketosis like you Kris? (_Caveat: I seem to have lots of energy during winter, in Ketosis, with low sun and very cold CT's)_
-   Is the nnEMF causing a perception that I need Carbs?
-   Is it natural for my body to want Carbs, as I've caught so much sun, and my circadian biology is mis-matching in Ketosis with all this sun?
-   If it is the nnEMF causing a false perception that I need carbs , do I stick with the Carbs anyway until Winter time?...
-   If I should eat more carbs, then how much more?

### kris90 — 2017-05-30T17:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-215113>

Interesting man. I feel the same as you. I can stick to ketosis because I have good willpower, but my body seems to enjoy having some carbs around. I find fats much more palatable than carbs, and when I do add carbs in, I need to combine them with fats in order to make them enjoyable (i.e. add coconut oil to my potatoes, or butter to my veggies, etc). It is the same if I want something sweet for dessert. I need to add fats with it to balance the sugar: i.e. milk chocolate, or cheesecake, or a good homemade dessert like a pie or pastry (I'm European, so baked goods with natural ingredients is very palatable and traditional).

For me, 100-150g of carbs would be the max I ever feel I need, and I prefer to eat them in a single setting with dinner. Dinner is always about 4 hours pre-sunset, and now that summer is pretty well here, we dine outside with the sun shining on us while I eat my carbs. After dinner, I am out in the grass barefoot playing with my daughter as the sun slowly sets, and be sure to use that last bit of sun in my eye and skin, and it seems to help me digest my meal.

The problem is, in order for me to see any progress in my T levels, I was having to eat between 250 and 350g of carbs per day, without fasting. I don't enjoy eating breakfast, nor do I enjoy eating every 2-3 hours, and nor do I really enjoy eating high carb. I prefer well balanced, but still a mostly fat diet, so although I got good results from the high carb intake, I did not want to have to be a sugar burner to have high T levels naturally. This is why I think HCG is my trapdoor to fat burning in a modern environment. I think a lot of people are biohacking with HCG without knowing it, hence the HCG diet, and being able to sustain a very low intake of food, while retaining muscle mass, and becoming the ultimate fat burner. I never realised that HCG was my golden ticket to fat burning, I just used it as an LH analog to replace the lacking signal in my pituitary, but it seems to have other important metabolic pathways that I am now looking into.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-30T19:35:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-215125>

**Chemical Transformations Leading to Protein Covalent Modifications occurs how?**
Site-selective chemical modification of a protein requires two key features: an efficient reaction and an interesting molecule to attach. The molecule must relate to the desired application, while the reaction represents the means of attaining the modified protein in high yield and functional form. Performing chemical reactions on a biological macromolecule and maintaining its integrity is not an easy task. The primary reason relates to the distinct requirements for performing chemistry within a living organism as compared with standard organic synthesis. Below, we outline the key requirements for biocompatible chemical transformations that pose challenges to development of successful strategies.

**Water Is the Sole Solvent in cells.......unless you add some methylene blue.**
Cells can tolerate a small quantity of alcohol-based solvent or DMSO, but the inclusion of these solvents induces high stress and significantly modifies normal cellular behavior. This is why I never recommend them.

**A Neutral pH Is Required**
A pH between 6 and 8 should be maintained in order to minimize the effect on the processes of the cell but an alkaline pH is created by sunlight = EZ effect.

**Ambient Temperature (Up to 40°C)**
Cells can only survive within a narrow temperature range, requiring reactions to be performed near ambient temperature. They all work better cooler......why? Semiconductive current all increase in cold temps and not in warm ones. This is the key to understanding why receptors become more receptive to smaller hormone levels. Electric potential in cell memebranes = redox power in mitochondria = solar expossure.

**Kinetics, which Adapted to the Observed Phenomenon (on the Hour Scale)**
From the chemical standpoint, kinetics can deeply differ between room temperature (20°C) and 37°C, but few degrees are irrelevant in most cases unless we are talking about aromatic amino acids.......why? All have benzene rings that absorb all frequencies of UV light.

**Low Reactant Concentrations**
High concentration induces cell stress, leading to abnormal behavior. Still think taking exogenous drugs without understanding the redoc of mtDNA and cell membrane phsyiology is wise. Here is why.......... This means kinetic constants must be orders of magnitude higher than traditional organic chemistry where usual concentrations are in the molar range.

### kris90 — 2017-06-01T16:35:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/post-215262>

Found an interesting abstract about how adiponectin raises SHBG:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24828613](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24828613)

I have been fasting/fat burning for the last few years without significant refeeding. I would assume I have very high adiponectin levels and high activation of AMPK pathway from said fasting, as well as suprasensitive leptin receptor. Why do I say that? Because my appetite is insanely low, but I can raise it if I want to. I can simply skip food altogether, or I can feast, but my brain won't allow me to overeat. I have probably activated an anorexic pathway, although I am not concerned, because I am at a normal weight, with good strength and muscle mass. I like this pathway I am in, and I think HCG compliments it.

I think HCG helps keep me in the fat burning pathway easily, although I know I can maintain it without as well. HCG, cold, fat burning/ketosis/fasting are all yoked IMO, and I am using all of these to live in the fat burning pathway. I can uncouple myself from light, because my body wakes up naturally in the winter in darkness. I can get by on minimal food, and have maximal energy. My brain has never been so sharp, and I exercise infrequently, but when I do, I perform above average. My sleep is very deep, and I don't need a lot of it.

> Furthermore, adiponectin-induced upregulation of SHBG production via HNF-4α overexpression was abrogated by the inhibition of fatty acid oxidation or by the induction of lipogenesis with a 30mM glucose treatment in HepG2 cells.

They stated a way to reverse that effect was to inhibit fatty acid oxidation or induce lipogenesis with glucose in HepG2 cells. So my high SHBG levels is likely due to adiponectin and links to me being a fat burner vs a sugar burner. Sugar burner would have low SHBG.

So this is interesting that not only is this metabolic pathway induced in myself is raising my SHBG, but my androgen/estrogens are also kept low. I would be really curious to measure my binding affinity, because as mentioned, with levels as low as mine were, I should have been suffering hypogonadal effects, but I was not. I maintained strength, libido, sense of wellbeing, and normal spermatogenesis with low LH/FSH/T/E2 and high SHBG and was able to get my wife pregnant.

From Cold Thermogenesis 6:

> It takes the best of CRON and the BEST of HCG and allows us to eat hardly any food at all and not be hungry while our body is burn all our fat to make us this and it increases our body composition because our growth hormone and sex steroid hormones are tightly bound to our receptors firing on all cylinders.

BTW @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) this is by far my favorite blog post. I can't even tell you how many times I read this.

# Thread: Taoist BIGu State - Ketosis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taoist-bigu-state-ketosis.19568/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mobeus — 2017-05-30T23:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taoist-bigu-state-ketosis.19568/post-215142>

BIGu is an ancient Taoist practice that literally translates to "grain avoidance". The kings started feeding grain to the peasants, but the Qigong practitioners knew better, based upon several thousand years of written history.

In 2003 at Penn State there was a conference which included over 150 professionals (many with PhD's) who have not eaten any solid foods for many years. Even whole families with children who almost never eat. They might have an egg or some nuts or a fruit maybe once a month. Their bodies are in the BIGu state, they are living on light, and most practice Qigong.

[https://www.scribd.com/document/40294944/Bigu-Conference-Report-PennState-June-2000](https://www.scribd.com/document/40294944/Bigu-Conference-Report-PennState-June-2000)

I did not see anything in these forums about BIGu, so figured best to post this here for discussion. Thanks to the late/great Professor Rustum Roy for organizing this conference.

### tola — 2017-06-19T20:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/taoist-bigu-state-ketosis.19568/post-216559>

I'd bet most on this forum realize why you would retire to the mountains, live in a cave, drink spring water, get lots of sun, build an energetic relationship with your organs and glands, and reap the benefits of the stars at night. it's kinda cool to see what's going on in this forum as it paralells with taoist alchemy in so many ways.

# Thread: nnEMF Advice
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GFam — 2017-05-31T02:59:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/post-215151>

Hello All,

I am hoping Dr Kruse and/or Scompy can take a look and give me their advice

A little background
I moved into a new house November of last year. I was still somewhat new to Dr Kruse's work but had listened to enough of his podcasts to get the general gist of it all. So from putting together all the information from the various podcasts I took all this into account when we were searching for our new house. From that info here is what we did
\-Moved to small town. Population is 2900
\-Well water, no fluoride
\-Large lot 3/4 acres. Away from neighbors nnEMF. Closest cell tower 3.5 miles away.
\-Great schools. Actually starting to work with them now on removing WIFI, making environment cleaner.
\-During the process hired an EMF engineer to evaluate the property. Everything was great. RF background noise 0.0001 uw/m2. Magnetic fields 0.03 or less throughout the house. Electric fields fixed via shielding and cutoff switches.

So everything was great, or so I thought. So since working with the EMF engineer and continuing to study Dr Kruse's work I have learned quite a bit. Bought several meters (Cornet RF, NFA1000 for magnetic/electric fields, dirty electricity meter, etc...) I usually go through and test the environment once a month and I recently found elevated magnetic fields in the house. These were not present when the EMF engineer was out. Well after further investigation I found out there is a 15kv distribution power line that goes along the street in my front yard. Turns out this 15kv line feeds all the other houses on the block (about 12, each of us have about 3/4 acre lot) So now that summer is upon us and people are using their AC's, etc... the nnEMF from that distribution line is showing up.

The readings are now anywhere from 0.08 - 0.22 mg depending on where you are in the house. I know building biology likes to see 0.2 or less for sleeping areas and I checked Michael Neuert's page and he recommends 0.5 and less. The kids rooms are toward the font of the house and their rooms can hit the 0.22mg reading. Now I can't stop thinking about this problem and my childrens safety. I thought i was doing great by the family and then came to find this.

So here is my questions. Being that these readings are just slightly about the No Concern level for building biology and lower than Michael Neuert recommendations am I being a bit too paranoid. Generally the levels are lower than 0.2 and only occassionally hit that level. Here are my options as I see it
\-Move (this will be hard as this is a fantastic area and really hard to find places)
\-Build new bedrooms on the back side of the house. Expensive
\- Magnetico?? Would getting the kids Magnetico mattresses protect them from the low magnetic fields coming from that power line.

I am very excited to hear some advice from members of this forum.
Thank You.

### caroline — 2017-05-31T03:51:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/post-215152>

Thanks for posting your story. We are looking to move and it really will take so much thought and testing.

You thought you had everything right so you can protect yourself and your family. I am wondering why your EMF engineer didn't notice the potential problem?

Well after further investigation I found out there is a 15kv distribution power line that goes along the street in my front yard. Turns out this 15kv line feeds all the other houses on the block (about 12, each of us have about 3/4 acre lot) So now that summer is upon us and people are using their AC's, etc... the nnEMF from that distribution line is showing up.
>

I know building biology likes to see 0.2 or less for sleeping areas and I checked Michael Neuert's page and he recommends 0.5 and less. The kids rooms are toward the font of the house and their rooms can hit the 0.22mg reading. Now I can't stop thinking about this problem and my childrens safety. >

Generally the levels are lower than 0.2 and only occassionally hit that level. Here are my options as I see it
> \-Move (this will be hard as this is a fantastic area and really hard to find places)
> \-Build new bedrooms on the back side of the house. Expensive
> \- Magnetico?? Would getting the kids Magnetico mattresses protect them from the low magnetic fields coming from that power line.
>
> I am very excited to hear some advice from members of this forum.
> Thank You.

First guess is it may be a cross over to something electrical touching the water pipes. Since you didn't have issue first, it probably isn't this situation in the video below specifically, but could be related to something elsewhere along the circuit into the home. If you find the mG increases closer to the water pipes, that's probably what's going on.

Also, it could be a specific problem with one of your circuit breakers that may have gone bad... test the mG with all of the breakers OFF and see if the problem goes away. If it does, then turn them back on one-by-one to see if the problem re-appears with one of the breakers on. Also, if there is a doubling of your lights+plugs on the same breaker, that can be a culprit producing more mG in some cases based on how many amps are allowed on that breaker.

### GFam — 2017-05-31T18:37:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/post-215179>

HI Scompy, thanks for the advice. However this is not the issue. The water pipes have been tested and I have also tested turning off all the circuit breakers in the house. Same issue. This is what clued me in to the problem. After I turned off all the breakers the levels were still elevated. So I began tracing the signal. This led me to outside the house and down the front yard towards the street. As I crossed over a certain location the levels would drop slightly so I was able to pinpoint the source. At this point I called the utility companies and they confirmed there is a 15kv distribution line that goes along parallel with the street and the houses in that area. I was able to trace this line all the way to the distribution transformer down the street. I have attached a document so you can see more of a visual. My house is outlined in green and the distribution line is red.

So as I said in my first post. Now that it is summer and people are using more electricity the readings are higher reaching a max of about .22mg so far. Now this is typically only during the day with night time being lower .1-.16 mg. Building biology says <.2mg is no concern however its still a man made source, can it really be of no concern? So basically wondering should I look to move, move the kids bedrooms to the back of the house or would a magnetico shield them from these low levels while they sleep?

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-13T02:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/post-216028>

Jump conduction can occur from tires to metal around you. Tires stores charge when they contain metal. And if your body has metal in it even more. So you cannot figure out where your nnEMF risks are coming from? Have you checked your car or truck. Do you have a sick pet who rides with you? You know they will get sicker quicker in this case don't you? Do you mitohack your wheel wells if you drive on a highway frequently? If not you're not a mitochondriac. When hunting for EMF problem areas in modern cars, many focus on the engine and electronics. However, modern tires have become a massive source of induced electric current because of their steel belts. When the belts are in the side wall it is even worse for a driver or passenger. One of my truck driver clients found this out the hard way. It can induce sizable magnetic fields when rotating quickly and the faster they rotate the more induced current they make. As the tire heats the current can act much like an AC current and the power grid and not a DC current. Depending upon car or truck design in many cases, these induced fields can extend to the driver's and passenger's seat. His dog and the truck driver got the same disease diagnosed within weeks of each other. Degaussing can reduce these fields by ~85%. Become a mitochondriac. Your life depends upon this knowledge whether you know it or not. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16404172](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16404172)

### GFam — 2017-06-13T02:42:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/post-216031>

Hi Dr Kruse,

This is very interesting information. I had no idea's about this in regards to cars. Can you comment on my original question in this post as well?

I am hoping Dr Kruse and/or Scompy can take a look and give me their advice

A little background
I moved into a new house November of last year. I was still somewhat new to Dr Kruse's work but had listened to enough of his podcasts to get the general gist of it all. So from putting together all the information from the various podcasts I took all this into account when we were searching for our new house. From that info here is what we did
\-Moved to small town. Population is 2900
\-Well water, no fluoride
\-Large lot 3/4 acres. Away from neighbors nnEMF. Closest cell tower 3.5 miles away.
\-Great schools. Actually starting to work with them now on removing WIFI, making environment cleaner.
\-During the process hired an EMF engineer to evaluate the property. Everything was great. RF background noise 0.0001 uw/m2. Magnetic fields 0.03 or less throughout the house. Electric fields fixed via shielding and cutoff switches.

So everything was great, or so I thought. So since working with the EMF engineer and continuing to study Dr Kruse's work I have learned quite a bit. Bought several meters (Cornet RF, NFA1000 for magnetic/electric fields, dirty electricity meter, etc...) I usually go through and test the environment once a month and I recently found elevated magnetic fields in the house. These were not present when the EMF engineer was out. Well after further investigation I found out there is a 15kv distribution power line that goes along the street in my front yard. Turns out this 15kv line feeds all the other houses on the block (about 12, each of us have about 3/4 acre lot) So now that summer is upon us and people are using their AC's, etc... the nnEMF from that distribution line is showing up.

The readings are now anywhere from 0.08 - 0.22 mg depending on where you are in the house. I know building biology likes to see 0.2 or less for sleeping areas and I checked Michael Neuert's page and he recommends 0.5 and less. The kids rooms are toward the font of the house and their rooms can hit the 0.22mg reading. Now I can't stop thinking about this problem and my childrens safety. I thought i was doing great by the family and then came to find this.

So here is my questions. Being that these readings are just slightly about the No Concern level for building biology and lower than Michael Neuert recommendations am I being a bit too paranoid. Generally the levels are lower than 0.2 and only occassionally hit that level. Here are my options as I see it
\-Move (this will be hard as this is a fantastic area and really hard to find places)
\-Build new bedrooms on the back side of the house. Expensive
\- Magnetico?? Would getting the kids Magnetico mattresses protect them from the low magnetic fields coming from that power line.

I am very excited to hear some advice from members of this forum.

Steel belted tires rotating...sounds similar to the problem I have with fans. I've tried DC fans and still have the same (magnetic) issues as with AC fans. Perhaps the solution for both the tires and the fan motor is similar: encase in metal and ground. Vehicles used to have metal wheel wells that would be easy to ground with a strap dangling to the pavement. Older fan motors were encased in metal as well and could also be grounded.

### GFam — 2017-06-23T01:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-advice.19570/post-216856>

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) Following up on some advice you gave me earlier in this post. So its been 100+ out here for a little over a week. During this time I have been taking continuous magnetic field measurements day and night. Remember this is in the kids bedrooms that are towards the front of the house and there is a PG&E distribution line that runs underground, parallel to the house, towards the front of the yard, to a distribution point down the street which then feeds the other 12 houses on the street. Readings range from 0.05mg to 0.28mg with averages being in the .13-.16mg range.

Yesterday I had my buddy park is big ass Dodge RAM 2500 within 3ft of the bedroom to see if the Iron in the vehicle would absorb some of that field. No go. At the time of the experiment the reading was 0.2mg and with the truck parked in front of the bedroom the reading remained 0.2mg. Tried grounding the engine to a copper rod in the ground as well. No change. Not sure if there isn't enough Iron in the vehicle or if the field is too spread out to make any difference.

So now back to my original question. I know these levels are quite low but "Building Biology" standards call for less than 0.2 for sleeping areas for the "no concern" range. Am I justified in being worried about this slightly elevated reading? Also is the constant fluctuation in levels also a concern?

If anyone is interested I can provide the program and data files from my meter. Can't upload them to this site as the files are too big but if someone wants me to drop them in their dropbox account or something like that let me know. This will show you more details over the monitored time periods.

Thanks to all!!!!

# Thread: Here comes the Sun! <my intro>
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Laur — 2017-05-31T19:44:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/post-215182>

I have learned over the years that when my functioning is compromised (low energy and slow brain for starters) that only the sun and ocean can restore me. Going to SW Florida, Tulum (Mexico), and other parts of the Caribbean have always rescued me: I take what I call "emergency" impromptu trips South-to-the-sun to get my mind and body back on track.

I have Hashimotos, Tinnitus (for the past 4 years), SAD, and TMJ. Twenty pounds overweight that I can't seem to ever lose.
My descent into lesser health coincides with my increase in blue light (laptop, etc) over the past 6 years.
fyi: I live in The lower Hudson Valley area of NY.

Last year I discovered the Jack interviews : ) on youtube, starting first with Dave Asprey's interview....and then many others... Jack was articulating what I have always known to be innately true for myself, I need A LOT of the right light to thrive - and I believe I need it more than most (so now I need to find out what's going on mitochondrially <hope that's a word> )

I am a true Newbie here, and I look forward to "getting started!!"

Thanks Jack - and All...
Laura

### caroline — 2017-06-01T10:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/post-215236>

welcome Laura. Dr. K. has a thread .....the importance of newbies - have a look.

if you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to the monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

Now that you mention it ....we used to go to the Bahamas about three times a year - to regain our sanity! I suppose it is really different now.

### Laur — 2017-06-01T17:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-comes-the-sun-my-intro.19574/post-215266>

I will take a look at the Newbie thread! The sun, warmth, and the negative ions from the ocean change me in a way that noyhing else can...when things are pretty dire healthwise.
I will also take a look at what the Gold membership entails....
I truly appreciate the feedback!

# Thread: CT madness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brett Jacques ND — 2017-05-31T22:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/post-215191>

Here in Huntington Beach I do my CT in the Pacific. Yes we have a lot of Great Whites right now but I continue. Yes I've been stung once or ten times by stingrays still I persist. I've harassed by young sea lions, entertained by dolphins, scared by water birds popping up right next to me but today took the prize for weirdness. I was approaching the shoreline when I noticed lots of birds diving in and out of the water. So maybe some fish were running, I don't know. I enter the water with my usual stingray shuffle get to about waist deep and bam! I'm set upon by a cast of crabs, I kid you not. Nipping and pinching my toes, not giving ground as I dance away. Then they coalesce and scurry away. I started laughing out loud for which I'm certain that if anyone saw me would have questioned by sanity. No blood was drawn and no human was harmed in the telling of this story so therefore I had no additional worry with any juvenile great whites in the area looking for trouble.

### drezy — 2017-05-31T23:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/post-215193>

Mr. Jacques

If you were not injured and you live a more neolithic life than the crabs do, then who gets the last laugh when it comes to electron transfer? You do! You totally stole a whole bunch of electrons from crabs right there. Just try not to start walking sideways.

### Brett Jacques ND — 2017-06-01T03:18:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-madness.19577/post-215214>

My sideways walking has more to do my skewed view of life at large in the OC particularly HB.

# Thread: Weaning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2017-06-01T10:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215237>

What are the best foods for weaning from breastfeeding?

### JanSz — 2017-06-01T14:52:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215246>

The longer child is breast fed the better for the child.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-01T16:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215257>

I'm trying to remember what I weaned my kids with. How old are we talking? I started mushed up foods at six months. I breastfed my boy til he was 3 and my girl til she was 2.

My boy was picky. My girl ate everything and anything! I think we did bits of apple, berries went down well. Some mashed potatoe with stew or mince. Tiny bits of meat.

That's all I can remember

You can still be breastfeeding whilst offering your child food @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

### healthy2015 — 2017-06-02T17:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215310>

He is only 5 months at the moment, I am in no rush to stop breastfeeding just wondered what food others recommended to start the weaning process
Thanks

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-02T17:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215311>

Yoghurt and kefir went down a storm too. And tomatoes...

### Danco3636 — 2017-06-02T17:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215315>

My son now turning 6 months on the 10th.
Since month 5 been giving him some real food along with Breast milk.
He likes avocado and egg yolks with Cream from GF cows mashed together.
He has tried real food.
Banana's and cream
Salmon and cream
Sweet potato and cream
Mushed avocados.
Has even tasted a sardine and oyster.

We have just been getting him use to flavors of whole good foods.

But so far his go to food is the egg yolk, avocado and cream mix.

He's also had fermented cod liver oil with his milk at times.

He was born 1 month early but now is strong and viberent.
No sign of being premature.

He gets daily morning and evening sun. In the water daily. Eithier in the bathtub with dad or pool. Has been under water and gets outdoors for long walks with dad.
We also block most if not all blue light at night.
No cell phones around him.
Sleeps on a magnetico pad.
Wifi and electronics off at night.

He now sleeps through the night and often doesn't eat until a big breakfast after about 11-12 hours.

### Valsun12 — 2017-06-12T16:37:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-215937>

My recent daughter now 7 months gets eggs(loves them), squash and other veggies mashed (I alternate, I noticed she seems to be spitting up when I mix more than two foods together , cant give her anything from jars so far because I tried 2x and she threw up both times. ...but breastfeeding still--> and I notice she eats lots more breast milk during the night, than when she is has pumped bottled milk during the day.(when I am work)

### JuicerMoz — 2017-07-31T12:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-220499>

Weaning is the process of moving your little one on from a diet of just milk to solid food too. The first stage of weaning helps lay the foundations for healthy eating habits, and teaches your baby how to eat from a spoon, swallow and eventually chew.

### shiran — 2017-07-31T13:42:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaning.19581/post-220502>

I gave my kids food only when the teeth came out...
The first foods were:
Bone broth
Avocado
Sweet potato
Thini

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-01T16:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-nutrition-in-to-my-kids.19585/post-215259>

So we've just started eating caviar in the house. My husband has always loved it but I only just started it now (thanks, Jack!)

It turns out you can hide it in kids food pretty easy. My daughter will eat it straight off the spoon (calls it bubbles) but my boy is fussy as hell. So we've been mixing it in to his meals without him realising.

WIN! I feel so good about this hack

# Thread: Nina's Optimal Journal

### Nina Hope — 2017-06-02T02:26:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ninas-optimal-journal.19586/post-215287>

Hey Dr. Kruse,

I'm a new member, just devouring your material in your blogs since February when I stumbled upon your site. From anyone's estimation I would say that I'm a train wreck: Chronic Lyme, Chronic Fatigue, Hashimoto's, insulin resistant, have excessive thirst as one of my consistent symptoms and more! I've been pretty sick for 4 years. Anyway, I'm learning a lot and making changes in my life to get back to a place of health.

Instinctively (or God), I have been eating a ketogenic diet in earnest since October 2016, including sardines (yuck). Since February I've added in boatloads of seafood. Also, I've always LOVED being in the sun but am homozygous VDR. My mother is from Ecuador and hence I have the A2 mitochondrial haplotype. But I live in EMF laden northern NJ, not getting enough sun. I've done 28 phosotidylcholine IVs to clean up my lipids and cell membrane (Patricia Kane protocol through a Lyme literate doctor), which has gotten me functioning again. I was non-functioning for 18 months. As of April 2016, I am on the up-swing.

I've signed up for a consult and would love to be placed on the wait list.

I have just been diagnosed with hypercalcemia (high calcium), but after reading Tensgrity 7, I think that it could be because I started taking 2000IU daily of Vitamin D end of April bc Vitamin D level was low, 35 persuant to my new GYN/Bioidentical hormone replacement doc, who I love.

I could write a book about my health issues and have a million questions. Am I on the right track?

Also, I had an Acumen test done and it shows that I have nickel and bpa on my mitochondria in March 2017.

Still pretty tired,
Nina

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-05T08:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-starfish.19594/post-215409>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=215403)
>
> Time to increase their time on sun,
> at least to what grandma got

I don't try and tan my granddaughters. I'm using tanning to try and strip excess body fat. I wouldn't want to trigger fat stripping in very young children, they've got that subcutaneous fat for a reason, and their thermoregulation system won't have fully developed. Until a child can fully verbalise that they are getting too hot, there's also more risk. Far safer to let them go in and out of the shade as they want (and they head for the light) before the sun gets too hot, and keep them in the shade (picnic lunch or a nature watch) when the sun is strongest. I also believe that fat cells are designed to store light, and that the coolness and relative darkness of the shade of being under the trees at lunchtime prompts the body to move the blood to the surface to collect light, before going out to repeat the process in the afternoon. The rosy glow comes after the sun session. I think its a protective measure to stop overbleaching of the RBCs in a high UV. If my granddaughters go home happy and content with the glow that comes from being outside in the garden for hours with Nana, I'm happy.

### JanSz — 2017-06-15T14:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-as-a-mitochondria-protector-from-non-native-emf.19595/post-216241>

See mold.
Shine light on it.
mold---->>low light
Stay on a bright side.

basements, shower stalls, humid areas
\======================

radon
**Radon** is a [chemical element](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chemical_element) with symbol **Rn** and [atomic number](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atomic_number) 86. It is a [radioactive](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radioactive_decay), colorless, odorless, tasteless

It seeps thru the ground.
It may be present in some basements.
Radon exposure (mostly radon daughters) has been linked to lung cancer in numerous case-control studies performed in the United States, Europe and China.

**Radon Gas Test Kit**
[http://www.homedepot.com/p/PRO-LAB-Radon-Gas-Test-Kit-RA100/100141467](http://www.homedepot.com/p/PRO-LAB-Radon-Gas-Test-Kit-RA100/100141467)

**First Alert SC07 Home Radon Test Kit, RD1**
[https://www.walmart.com/ip/First-Alert-Home-Radon-Test-Kit-Model-RD1/10848835](https://www.walmart.com/ip/First-Alert-Home-Radon-Test-Kit-Model-RD1/10848835)

**Testing and mitigation**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radon#Health\_risks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radon#Health_risks)
