Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

For those who do not understand the above: enthalpy is a thermodynamic quantity equivalent to the total heat content of a system. Some believe It is equal to the internal energy of the system plus the product of pressure and volume of the system. Plasma can really vary its power because it can vary pressure and volume tremendously. This is why a contained plasma inside a tensegrity system (collagen) of the cell is powerful. Much more powerful than we all can fathom. This is also why the idea of Birkeland currents in space are game changers when understanding stars like the sun.

Light, especially UV light, changes the structures of things with mass because of how it interacts with electrons of D shell electrons in transition metals like iron. This is why water in cell has ions in it. Ions have charges and charges make it tunable to the electromagnetic force in which photons are the force carrier. This means light can change all forms of matter in ways we fail to see. It also means different frequencies of light change things in different ways. UV light is best at changing matter in the visible spectrum because it has the most power density. Red light cannot change matter like UV can but it is optimized to move matter best. It increases the way things can move. This is why water is considered the optimal quantum motor surrounding the ATPase to make it 100% efficient.

Recall that the ATPase is a quantum light machine that uses red light and water's viscosity. [http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029)

Now consider this: UV and visible light is capable of changing magnetite by changing the shapes of things it interacts with. MANIPULATING MAGNETIC TEXTURES BY USING SHAPE CHANGES. This research conducted recently sounds like my older blogs on thermodynamics doesn’t it? Recall I told you OSU found in 2014 that acoustic phonons are controlled by magnetic fields. Now I am developing for you why this is the case. People forget water is also a magnetic dipole. It is a small magnet because of the different charges of hydrogen and water at opposite ends of the molecule. This means UV light can really magnetize water easily.

Light can control magnetic properties in ways science is just waking up too. While the ability to easily control the magnetic properties of small electronic systems is highly desirable for future small electronics and data storage, an effective solution has proven to be extremely elusive to scientists especially in biology. Cells magnetically store data in DNA and they use size and shape changes in out mitochondria to control the epigenetic expression or magnetic messages in our nucleic acids. Cells are built to do this by using UV light to control the process. This process is highly dependent upon magnetitie being surrounded by water in cells and this is why all proteins are hydrated in cells. People have also forgotten that proteins act as light antennas whose electrons absorb the light wave and change it into an electromechanical wave that is absorbed in water. That electromechanical wave is built to move........like a soliton or a phonon in a cell and in blood. And I will remind you again that water is magnetic dipole. This is one of my core tenets in my blog of showing people how magnetism condenses matter in us to slow light down so we can use it in ways biology has yet to understand.
A group of researchers from universities in Chile and Brazil are reporting this week in the Journal of Applied Physics, from AIP Publishing, a simple way to gain control of magnetism that starts by controlling the shape of the systems and this is what controls the thermodynamics of LIFE. [https://scienmag.com/manipulating-magnetic-textures/](https://scienmag.com/manipulating-magnetic-textures/)

Very interesting nuggets Dr Kruse,this tells me to increase my water consumption

[https://phys.org/news/2016-06-evidence-human-ability-earth-magnetic.html](https://phys.org/news/2016-06-evidence-human-ability-earth-magnetic.html)

ties in with Jamie Ann's Article
"Over the past century scientists have found that other animals do indeed have magnetic sensors and that they respond to them—birds in flight use the Earth's magnetic field at least in part, as a compass, dogs orient themselves north/south to urinate. The list of examples has grown quite extensive, but one problem still remains—no one has been able to figure out how it happens. Scientists have narrowed down the possibilities Eric Hand writes in two extensive News articles on the subject in the latest issue of the journal _Science_, one is called the Magnetite Model, and is based on the idea that magnetite existing in the bodies of living organisms may be tugged by the Earth's magnetic field, controlling neural circuitry. **The other is called the Cryptochrome Model and is based on the idea that chryptochromes in the retina are turned into radical pair molecules by sunlight and are flipped between states when impacted by Earth's magnetic field.** Kirschvink, Hand, notes, believes the former is the most likely possibility, though his mission has not been to find out how it might work, but to show that it does in humans.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-16T15:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-as-a-mitochondria-protector-from-non-native-emf.19595/post-216338>

Dissolved oxygen (DO) is the amount of oxygen (O2) dissolved in the water. Dissolved oxygen is one of the best indicators of water quality. People need oxygen in the atmosphere to survive and animals that live in the ocean, like fish, need dissolved oxygen in the water to survive.

**_The amount of dissolved oxygen that the water can hold depends on the temperature and salinity of the water_**. This is the key to understanding how CSF chemistry links to the quantum story of life that occurs on our neocortex. Cold water can hold more dissolved oxygen than warm water and fresh water can hold more dissolved oxygen than salt water. So the warmer and saltier the water, the less dissolved oxygen there can be. The maximum amount of dissolved oxygen that the water can hold is called the saturation value.

There is always more dissolve oxygen in cold water. Oxygen is the terminal electron acceptor for mitochondria. IT is also why CSF temps need to drop for melatonon to recyle mitochondria via mitophagy at night in the absence of sunlight.

Oxygen enters the water at the surface of the water where exchange between the atmosphere and the water can take place. Waves and wind help put oxygen into the water. Dissolved oxygen is also put into the water as a byproduct of phytoplankton photosynthesis. The oxygen found in the deeper water comes from mixing with surface water. Photosynthesis can cause the water to have more dissolved oxygen than the saturation amount. When that happens it is called supersaturation.

Dr Karim's **Hemburg**, Switzerland project was a fascinating case study in RF/MW radiation mitigation. It certainly seemed to help symptoms of people exposed to radiation in this little town.

However, this project concluded by the mid-2000s and I have not yet seen a follow up study to examine long-term results. Did people remain symptomatically unaffected up to present day? That would be a revealing insight.

Someone mentioned using **Biokinesiology** methods to measure RF/MW mitigation effectiveness using subtle energy devices and that sounds like a good start in trying to obtain reliable metrics. However, I don't suppose there are any published studies on this. We are still in the wild, wild west period of electrosmog protection.

Bear in mind that mainstream consensus is still that 'subtle energy healing' is at best unproven, at worst pure hokum.

Physical RF shielding materials are indeed imperfect at this juncture, and may even present some dangers. So everyone is basically on their own until we get some hard, evidence-based info are what works safely and effectively and what doesn't.

_Caveat emptor._

### DavidL — 2017-06-22T00:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-alberta-canada.19598/post-216768>

Wait till the quantum photovoltaics come out. They can power under IR light and IR light occurs at night.

My thinking is that in floor radiant heat would be best. The hot dry air coming out of our furnaces creates a lot of positive ions and they are bad for the autonomic nervous system and cause irritability and other things. The Chinook winds are a problem....maybe try a negative ion generator in your house...also try boosting the humidity.

The good part about Alberta (I live in Saskatchewan) is that you have latitude on your side. Plus Calgary is one of the sunniest cities in Canada.
The bad parts about it are that you live on top of geopathic stressors like oil and natural gas fields. Correct me if I am wrong but they lessen the impact of the earths magnetic field. Less magnetic field is bad.
You can get UV light in Lake Louise though in the winter time because of the high altitude. The hack here is go to the ski resort in winter, get down to a bikini top and let the cold air bring the red blood cells to the surface as to trap more of the UV light in the red blood cells.

### DavidL — 2017-06-22T00:27:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-alberta-canada.19598/post-216769>

If you are close to Harvie Heights just outside of Canmore and the Banff East Gate, you should go get some spring water. It's very cold, high in minerals and is naturally structured by the natural geometric formations of the rock. This is some of the best water in Canada IMHO.

### DavidL — 2017-06-22T00:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-alberta-canada.19598/post-216770>

I would be interested to see some stats to with Alberta and botox utilization rates. Many young women I know there seem to get it. The think it's the hard work, I think its the chronic blue light exposure, the dryness, and the altitude and the fact that Calgary is in that high nnEMF valley. The valley is also subject to pollution from cars.

I love Calgary but it can be a challenge to live really healthy there. Oh and visit Meta 4 Foods in Calgary and talk to Eric about his amazing oysters......

### Lori — 2017-06-22T06:15:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-alberta-canada.19598/post-216794>

Thanks David, I am originally from North Dakota, did live in Saskatchewan for 12 years and now am in Red Deer, Alberta. I am a Newbie, so am a bit overwhelmed with all I have already been introduced to on this forum and by watching some of Dr. Jack's videos and reading some of his writings. So far there is a lot of info that is jiving with other things I have learned/noticed over the years, but also a lot of brand new to me ideas...very excited to have found you guys! We use Monashee water...a spring from BC right now...don't know if it is any better or worse than Calgary here, but I do love the fact that there are more trees...I will definitely check out Meta 4 Foods....have my laptop hooked up to a line, use my cell phone on a very limited basis, still have my landline, and am causing trouble with the city over not wanting the "smart meters" that they are in the midst of replacing our analog meters with....that is actually how I first saw a video interview with Dr. Jack....researching the problems with EMR...so far, I have been able to opt out of getting my gas and water meter changed to "smart meters", but they are charging quite hefty and on going fees to opt out....so am trying to raise awareness of the concerns with them, in hopes of changing the way our city is going with this...or at least have them make "opting out" affordable.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oh-my-getting-a-little-dark-over-here.19600/post-215486>

I've been using the Dminder app and I'm getting ridiculously tan and I was hoping for help.

• Walking around in shorts and tee shirt without sunscreen (which is an incredible departure from being covered up anytime I've been outside in the past)
• We've only had "sun" a handful of days in the last three weeks (it's pretty gloomy here)
• I've been out in sun typically mid morning, way before Solar Noon
• I am Sicilian -- the Scandinavian type with blue eyes, light hair and light skin -- and no facial hair )
• According to Dminder, I'm only at 28% for the month and I'm in it for three weeks with lots of rainy days and clouds.

I really want to up my Vit D naturally but am finding this "Something About Mary, Magda tan" a deterrent.

Any insight or advice would be really appreciated.

I was wondering if anyone who bought air tube headset measured the EMF for themselves, both at the earbud end and at the 'speaker end' which is roughly at chest level?

### minimal — 2017-06-07T17:38:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-215569>

Hi everyone,

I decided to combine three things: simple Hello, my gratitude for having chance to associate with You - rather inquisitive People who are not happy with cookie-cutter prescriptions for everyone. I also have quesiton which I didn't know where to put.

I'd like to pose You question on behalf of someone who needs help much more than I. I'm sure this person would like to interact with You personally but unfortunately doesn't speak English. I'm really sorry for doing that as this is my first post on this forum but I think that if He didn't ask me for this favor I would never leave my comfort zone and would never find power to interact with You. Why? Actually for many reasons. The first is that english is not my mothertongue. I'm also very intimidated with Your knowledge and many times I simply don't comprehend what Jack or many of You are on about. This is not the case of my language skills but rather that in comparision with many of You I feel retarded - sometimes I catch the idea after weeks or months of thinking and reading about it.

Anyway, my acquaintance is in his 50's and was diagnosed with grade 4 patellar chondromalacia.
As far as I know, there is not much to do about it. His story is very long but rather typical. In a nutshell he's very active (maybe not chronic cardio but close to it), overweighted, leptin resistant for sure, he's not epi-paleo nor paleo, doesn't do CT's and doesn't mind EMF's so there is a lot to do... But I'm wondering if anyone here have been there and could pass some ad hoc advice to him that could help convince him that his knee is not just separated problem?

I told Jack's and some others story about being optimal and reversing irreversible to many of my friends including him but they don't get it. Many of them still stuck in many common wisdom paradigms so when I mention about leptin, electromagnetism, CT, EMF, mitochondria and many other things they simply smile under their breath. But in face of surgery or other threatening circumstances people start to be more open.

So, is there anything that could help his condition at least put off the surgery? I would be very grateful for any meaningful reply.

Even if this thread will be left with no answer I would like to thank to Jack for bringing his ideas to the World and to others who spread his message.

Best regards,

Arek

PS: English is not my mothertongue so please don't mind my gramar

Wow, that was quick. Thank You Caroline for this as well as for Your suggestions.

Anyway his story is as follows:

1\. He's in his 50s and phisically fit despite his slight overweight. He has no problem with riding 100 km on his bike just like that. Actually he rides his bike as often as he can which is good as I don't think this is chronic cardio. Moreover, this excercise seems to alleviate his aches in achilles and knee. Actually he feels no pain in his knee so far but in his achilles. This issue with knee started with MRI results...So until he got his results he was not aware of thegravity of the situation...

However, when taking into account his blood panel and his injuries: knee and achilles tendon tear it seems pretty obvious that his fitness has split a lot from his health. Many things seem clear for me and can't be credited from his age.

Once, he used to have issues with high cholesterol. He was even prescripted with statins but I asked him to drop them and shift more to high fat paleo which helped but:

2\. He lives at 4th floor which makes him very disconnected from earth's field as from electrons. He doesn't shield himself from EMF when asleep or at all. THe main source of nnEMF radiation in his case would be wifi which is problematic because of neighbours who don't mind it as well as wires in the walls. And of course ubiquitous cell phones...
He reports no issues with falling asleep but he suffers from apnea. I'm not sure if he takes any meds for that.

3\. He does not do CT at all, nor grounding, he doesn't say hello and goodbye to the sun.

4\. He works as a service engineer repearing forkfits. He is absolutelly sure that he hurt his knee because of his work necessities which many times involve crawling, crouching etc. As You can tell, I'm not that convinced...He does not work a lot in front of the computer however he is addicted to TV. 5\. His mom is my girlfriend's grandmother and I can confirm that she's very fit and not that ill if one takes her age - she is 86. She recently suffered from a stroke and doctors said that she won't walk anymore. This was 2 months ago. Today she wanders every day up and down from 4th floor...So she is very strong, fit as a fiddle, despite the enviorement she lives in and hapened not to switch many bad switches by epigenetic mechanism.

### drezy — 2017-06-10T18:50:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-215754>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) Nice list

I'll add a philosophy that I've picked up around here too because I like borrowing people's thinking templates.

When dealing with my family, especially the ones who just don't get it, it's helpful to remember Jack's "Half truth" concept because you often have to dance around it and with it a bit.

Your friend claims that kneeling on hard surfaces regularly caused his issue.
Half truth: It's kinda half true and many people are happy enough to get to a half truth.

The bigger picture is probably more like there is an underlying breakdown going on in his connective tissues throughout his body. Why?
Half truth: We could claim that he's just not young anymore and stop at that half truth. The odd thing about half truths is that two of them do not add up to to a whole truth. It's more like they move you half the distance toward (or sometimes away from) your goal.

The me from a year ago would suggest that he start eating bone broth for most meals because he may very well have a leaky gut that lectins are sneaking through and replacing all lectins with bone broth will patch the gut and stop the internal damage.
Half truth: Since you understandably are unable to completely rearrange this man's life and habits could this be true and help? Sure. But see it still stops with the food half truth (paleo/ketogenic). I was happy as a pig (with 6% more bodyfat but still fitter than my colleagues) with that half truth for the last 11 years. Still it's just a half truth and raises the question: Why? Doc's blog post on leaky gut pretty much required me to try to add a new lobe to my sore brain after reading it.

Becoming good at recognizing half truths in yourself and others can make you better at maybe luring yourself and others down the right path as you absorb more of the whole truth.

Sadly there is no button we can press to make your friend:
get morning sun while barefoot on grass\*
get solar noon UV light nude or in a tan-through\*
not watch TV in the evening\*
jump into a CT tub regularly
asses the labs Jan suggests
alter his diet
avoid as much EMF exposure as possible\*

Doc Kruse did a very long interview where near the end he was pressed to condense priorities here they are/were ( [https://selfhacked.com/2015/04/12/i...use-emfs-biohacking-water-dha-and-blue-light/](https://selfhacked.com/2015/04/12/interview-with-dr-jack-kruse-emfs-biohacking-water-dha-and-blue-light/) ):

Dr Kruse’s order of importance:

Circadian Rhythm (marked above with \*)
DHA
Limiting blue light
Water (spring water)
Sleep
Cold
Psychological stress
Exercise

Whatever you find on this board, written by anybody, regardless of their qualification, education or profession, is not medical advice.
**Use of any of it is at discretion of the user.**

When it works, there may be other reasons, so only user can be happy about it.
When it does not work, all is also on the user.
\----------------------------------------------------------
Thinking of deteriorating knee and patella.
\-------
For healing purpose number of red and IR-A frequencies are often useful.
In particular 660nm and 850nm are popular, but there are other.
In the past (expensive) lasers were used.
With popularity of LED, they can readily be obtained and used.
Flicker effect, power density, power delivered are factors.
Because of flicker effect, DC power is preferred.
\-------
In this category, Quantlet seems as natural tool for the job.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-72#post-214749](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-72#post-214749)
It is designed to fit over wrist.
\--------
From my discussions with @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

May also want to try a combination of two frequently discussed and used lights:
He could make some kind of gizmo that would be basically this 850nm lamp:
[https://www.amazon.com/CMVision-IR130-Outdoor-300-400ft-Illuminator/dp/B004F9LF7E/ref=sr\_1\_fkmr3\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1488125922&sr=8-1-fkmr3&keywords=infrared flood light built](https://www.amazon.com/CMVision-IR130-Outdoor-300-400ft-Illuminator/dp/B004F9LF7E/ref=sr_1_fkmr3_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1488125922&sr=8-1-fkmr3&keywords=infrared+flood+light+built)

surrounded tightly with 10 of this:
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01544PUJ2/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01544PUJ2/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
3Ruby's on long side, 2 on short, 3+3+2+2=10

**He could keep it all daily for hours on his knee.
When he would feel relief, stop using that setup for couple of days then start again.
That is to prevent overdoing.
@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) concern:
"In the past Hamblin has outlined a maximum power vs time dose where treatment actually negates benefits."
**
RubyLux All Red is cold to the touch.
The 850nm are warm, he would have to keep it at 1/2" or 1" distance.

The more hours with that the better.

In Poland there is 220V. Two Ruby Lux in line should work ok.
The 850nm light works on 100-240V.
Take the front cover out.
Put a duct tape over sensor.
Use the five screw on front to hold frame for the 10 RubyRed.

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/): no I don't but I try to follow some routine:
1\. when I wake up (~4:30 - 4:45) I take 10-15min walk barefoot around my garden or nearby meadow, partly naked and stare in the sun.
2\. ~around 11:00 I go to the park and walk barefoot, again staring to the sun (15-20 min or more if possible). I also try to point my wrists to the sun.
3\. before sleep I go again for the barefoot walk. This time I try longer exposition.

I know it's far from optimal (espetially when @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) input is taken into account) but maybe better than nothing...

I like to get up 6AM every day regardless of season, but 4:30AM is just as good.
Important is to keep that time steady.
If you change your waking time by one or two hours at the weekend, that creates effect similar to a jet lag.

Preferably you should be getting up when you wakeup naturally, without alarm.
Try that first on weekends or days when waking up at time different than planned would not cause stress.
\------

What is a "Sleep Cycle"?
.
\-------------
I do this blood tests twice a year:

Spectracell tests.
Usually
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
and
CardioMetabolic Risk

\======================================================================
This test
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-215392](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-6#post-215392)
[http://www.biolab.co.uk](http://www.biolab.co.uk)
[Fatty Acids - Erythrocytes
](http://www.biolab.co.uk/index.php/cmsid__biolab_test/Fatty_Acids_-_Erythrocytes)
Could help you sort out your diet.
What fats to eat, what to avoid.
Lard pigs, beef tally, olive oil, palm oil, maca, coconut oil, MCT, butter, nutmeg, (gałka muszkatołowa), Serenoa or Saw palmetto.......
On this board, Jack highly and frequently stresses importance of DHA.
So there is no question that we have to have it (at optimal level).
In my opinion, supported by numerous tests done by me and others, most people already have sufficient if not excessive levels of Omega3.
Additionally numerous neolithic diseases present with excessive omega3.
So, even very good friend have to know his limitations.
\-----------------------------------------
We are made of cells.
Cells and its components have membranes at their boundaries.
That includes frequently discussed mitochondria.
Membranes are made of phospholipids.
Phospholipids have a head and two fatty acids attached to it.
One is saturated (rigid provides structure),
the other unsaturated (vibrating provides life)
Basic membrane (wall of the house) is highly studded with stuff (pretty pictures), that is often discussed.
It is time to worry also about membranes.
When building is rotten and crumbling, pretty pictures hanging on walls shine less.

Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis
evaluates (functional) levels 35 items
some of it are about phospholipid heads.
add to that
fatty acids analysis
and you cover a lots of important ground.

[https://duckduckgo.com/?q=membranes cells&bext=wfa&atb=v61-2&iax=1&ia=images](https://duckduckgo.com/?q=membranes+cells&bext=wfa&atb=v61-2&iax=1&ia=images)

[https://duckduckgo.com/?q=membranes mitochondria&atb=v61-2&iar=images&iax=1&ia=images](https://duckduckgo.com/?q=membranes+mitochondria&atb=v61-2&iar=images&iax=1&ia=images)

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell\_membrane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_membrane)

If you want to do fatty acids test, you do not have to go to London if you can get that test locally.

Klinika Krakowska is nearby you and is/was one of the best there is in Poland.
Look there for guidance.

As far as diet and fatty acids are concerned,
put your mind set this way:
1--doctor get paid when patient is sick
2--doctor do not get paid when patient is healthy
3--veterinary doctor, caring for animals that are pets gets paid when animal is sick
4--veterinary doctor, caring for animals that are pets do not get paid when animal is healthy
5--veterinary doctor, caring for animals that are raised for food gets paid when animals are healthy and farm is profitable
6--veterinary doctor, caring for animals that are raised for food gets laid off when animal farm is not profitable.

Having this above six observations, find out what animals in profitable farms eat.
Find out how their food is described.
Likely food given to pigs and chicken is more in line with human food than food given to cows.

.............

.

### drezy — 2017-06-16T23:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-216387>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
You and I think very much alike. I love it.

I have a paleo GP ( I visit once every 4 years or so and he complains about the frequency).

He wasn't very impressed with my then 36 year old testosterone numbers. They were Ok but not high.

The ironic part is what he mentioned in passing after reviewing my numbers. He said:
"You know the old 60 and 70yo ranchers who work out in the sun all day have the highest testosterone out of all men I test."
Then he drifted into the next subject. That is what I needed to hear, but I wasn't ready!

He gave me the answer right there and may not have even realized it himself. Both sunlight and lack of EMF are all implicitly bundled up right there in that statement. I had the answer 4 years ago and like a **_dumbass_** I ignored it like ranchers had zero to do with me! I'm still embarrassed to tell the story, but hopefully it'll help someone else.

True story. Reveals my ignorance!

### JanSz — 2017-06-20T17:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-216621>

MCT or coconut oil are of part of consideration.

Eukaryote
A eukaryote is any organism whose cells have a nucleus and other organelles enclosed within membranes.
Humans are eukarytoes, we are build of cells.
There is a lots of variety of membranes in us.
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) discusses often mitochondria, it have double membranes.
Other organelles are also enclosed within membranes.
Membranes are not uniform, they have a stuff inside them.

I think of cells as of building.
Building have a walls, outside walls, inside walls.
Walls have openings, doors, windows, vents, etc.
Walls are membranes.

When you go thru discussions here, you may note that the discussions are about wall openings, and rarely or newer you will see discussion about walls.
When Jack discusses what mitochondria does it is about stuff that is being done by the doors, windows and vents.
Walls are not mentioned in this process.

I assume that walls are important.
When you put expensive art pieces in Louvre, the building of Louvre better be in good shape.

Membranes are made of phospholipids.
Phospholipids are made of two fatty acids and a head.
One fatty acid is saturated, rigid, provide structure.
The other is unsaturated, vibrates, provides life.

I want my membranes to be in best shape I can manage.
To asses status of my fatty acids I do analysis:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
To asses some of the other parts I do:
Spectracell Micronutrient analysis
It evaluates (functionally) 35 items.

I take my fatty acids analysis, use spreadsheet to evaluate them.
Use a system that follows dr Patricia Kane to figure out their status.
Each evaluated fatty acid can place
good
too much
too little

I also know that some fatty acids influence status of other fatty acids.
Most discussed is that Omega3 when in excess will suppress Omega6.

So now, when food's fat content is known one can adjust his diet.

My analysis have limitations, it does (C8-C26) (and not even all of those that are known).
There is one worth mentioning (C4:0) butyric acid, major help in stabilizing gut flora.

Another (important) observation I have made is that there is common claim advertised heavily and that is that everybody is short of Omega3's.
In my opinion that claim is highly overstated.
It might have been correct at the beginning of Industrial Revolution but is rarely true today.
But, fish oil industry perpetuates this myth for its profit.
It is so bad that laboratories exist that always advice to eat more fish oils.
That myth is highly destructive and clashes with another of our realities.
We live in era of high technology, nnEMF abound, add to that that everybody, including those on equator, live indoor, rarely seeing sunlight.
Add another one, food industry that feeds unnatural food.
That must be the reason for a long list of neolithic diseases.
Fatigue, MS, Parkinson, diabetes, ADHD, ALS, many other.
Guess what,
those diseases cause excessive accumulation of Omega3, including (precious DHA) and its brother (EPA).
So,
when doctor tells that kind of person to eat seafood or (more often) fish oil, what will happen?
That kind of person must realize that he must change doctor, not easy.
When you go thru old posts here, numerous people posted results of their fatty acids analysis.
I have found only one girl that was actually low on Omega3.

Our life depends heavily on DHA. Mother Nature arranged that little goes long way, and its half life is long.
So occasional re-supply goes long way.
\===============================================
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
@[minimal](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14206/)
**If you come across information that would help me fill in blanks on my spreadsheet, and suggestions to deal with my concerns, I am all ears.**

%Status
zero% when in mid range
within plus or minus 25% is assumed acceptable range
plus or minus 50% when on high or low laboratory range
higher than 50% is outside laboratory range

My cooling unit is a 15 year old dehumidifier also not technically designed to chill water. I'm a fan of keeping and eye out on cheap used units coolers/dehumidifiers/fridges... Chiller technology isn't that different across products. They are all just thermodynamic heat pumps in the end. Push heat from one spot and dump it to another. The funny thing is I used to chill computer chips to increase performance now I chill me for the same reason. My mitochondrial donor has found a way to crash her's in 60 years. I'm in it to win it with all the concepts here.

BTW my friend who now jumps in a cold tub is thinking about offering my 12 year old $100 on top of dehumidifier cost to duplicate our setup. If he decides to do this I'm thinking about making a youtube of my son in the process of making the whole setup and explaining it.

You don't want me translating Polish.

### minimal — 2017-06-30T15:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-217590>

I just listned to interview with Kevin Cottrell. In particular this one:

[http://livethefuel.com/044-cancer-paleo-sleep-kevin-cottrell/](http://livethefuel.com/044-cancer-paleo-sleep-kevin-cottrell/)

no wonder that this reminded me about this:

[https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-11-paleo-fx-to-practice/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-11-paleo-fx-to-practice/)

I read this article 2 years ago, I think. That is when I first introduced myself to CT.

I'm spitting feathers right now.

Have I been that low with dopamine that I completely lost the ability to learn? This is time to reveal my true colors and make confession about things that are trivial and I'm ashamed of espetially when compared with the content of this forum and JK's blog seem.

I wan't to become optimal that would help me extend my life span that I could visit some beautiful places in the world, meet and entangle with some extraordinary people and share this moments with people I care the most. This actually involves starting new life. But there are so many things that I haven't finnished and I'm not sure if they are even worth my effort. I'll explain this later.

Why did I point that the reason I came to this forum is trivial? I'm not talking about my friend's issue because this is the reason which pushed me to start this thread (and for sure it's not trivial). I mean reading the content...

So the reason is so stupid that I think I it's enough to make the angels weep. Couple years ago I HURT MY KNEE. No, I don't have cancer, don't struggle with MS, Hashimoto...I just hurt my stupid knee. What is even worse it was not bad. No surgery needed, I could walk, although there was no MRI but I was diagnosed with overused injury. The reason? Squats! That is right. Overused injury from heavy squatting 3-5 days in a row for extended period of time. Yes I'm gym rat. That is also part of my confession. And this is the reason why I didn't like to expose this issue because I think it's not right to take time of people like You to help me out to solve my hilarious problem. On the other hand, when I meet with Your response I felt just not right to not reveal my other intention why I'm here...

Before I move to notion I came up with I need to introduce You to my story little bit.

I was into sports from time immemorial: swimming 8 years, 3 years rowing. And this was part of my everyday life ~5 hours a day until end of my highschool. That time I was much knee high to grasshoper and I knew that I will not succed in sports so I dropped it. However, I was already addicted to sport so I had to do something with this abyss. In order to kill to birds with one stone: filling this emptiness and improving my low self-esteem I ended up in the gym. I started to bulk. A lot of bad food! From 65kg I became 100kg Hog (I'm short 173cm – so I looked peculiar). I succeded and failed at the same time. People were afraid to approach me and since I'm rather shy I was happy with that. But I was not happy with my look (I was simply fat like Mark Bell before he started keto...) nor my health. I know that @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) might not agree with me but I think that my o63 was horrible in my case although I didn't test it at all. After years of overusing supplements and eating like porker I impaired my microbiome and we all know where inflamation starts...My gut was never my strength and if I was aware that taking dump 8 times/day is not normal, maybe I would pull myself together. I didn't. I also was not aware of nnEMF and blue light at all.

So, I decided to do something about it. I started with CW calorie restriction. That worked up to a point. Then I stummbled upon my first Paleo book and took some of the most convenient principles for me. Again, it helped little a bit but I still wasn't happy. So I searched more and I came across IF so I introduced IF to my version of paleo (not bad but far from ideal, actually I should call it Low carb). Boom! It worked I dropped nearly 30 kg. I succeeded but my health didn't improved. Since then shifted more to powerlifting syle training, I experimented with different approaches (SS, Texas, 5-3-1, Heppburn, Squat everyday and many more) also with dieting: HighFatLowCarb, HighFat No Carb...with diary, without diary...but everything revolved around diet and training in the pursue of health and strength (I thought that those things go together and didn't understand the difference between fitness and health). That time I wasn't aware of Jack Kruse's existence.

So I worked out in the early mornings, slept 5-6 hours a day because we all know that sleep is for wimps and wooses. I also was not aware of nnEMF and blue light at all and many other things.

And this is when I first hurt my knee. I payed visit to two doctors and physiotherapist. All diagnosed me with overused injury and drained from money. I got better when I dropped squats for some time and started to use...ice cubes on inflamed spot(coincidence?). When the pain was gone I started to implement squats again. I didn't change anything...HFLC/IF/heavy lifting. I was doing the same things all the time expecting different results – yes, that is madness. Then little miracle happened. I moved from flat to cottage and found Jack Kruse's blog. I read his book, started epi-paleo and CT, started to use candles, payed attention to wifi, blue light and circadiam rythms...So I improved much which is difficult if You live with someone who does not give a dime about this woodoo...until the same happenes to herself! Yup – knee, eventhough she does not work out! And to her father as well. But this is another pair of shoes and happened recently.

Anyway since I'm not biohacker I used no labs. I used only my well-being scale and my weights as a measure of my improvement. And You know what? It didn't work. No surprise...

So, after first injury, despite using epi-paleo, CT, bluelight blocking glasses, candles, no wifi at home...I couldn't progress with weights and every other month I got pain in the knee. Nothing that would make me impaired but enough to make me stall with weights. Since I'm stubborn I decided to repeat everything over and over, again expecting different outcome...Stupidity squared!

I even thought that I could blame my technique but I was tested by guys who are oldhands in this field and said that my technique was perfect...I got lost.

Last month is kind of special for me:

I started thread here.

I read Epi-paleo rx again. I read Paul Gunning's book. I read Bulletproof diet.

I started to clean my diet since I found that I have never done anything serious with my gut (BTW my Lipid profile was always fine). I eat once a day – dinner. A lot of veggies. A lot of butter, coconut oil and some meat/fish – Raw (new for me but I feel really good when my meat is raw)/Medium. No roasting! I start my day with 0.5 l water and then 40-50 g fat from butter/coconut oil with some herbs instead of tea or coffe. I drink some coffe/tea with fat during day and a lot of water.

I eat homemade chocolade from organic cocoa/cacao/butter/little honey (before bed time)

I also take some magnesium before sleep and krill.

Once a week I do autophagy day – no protein.

I started morning 15 min walks with no glasses, 30 min walks at ~11:00, 15-30 min walks before sunshine. Weekends all day outside.

I take cold showers every day (again).

I found that when I eat some fruits (even low in fructose) next day morning I get disaster pants! Am I that sensitive to fructose?

Week ago I decided to take some deload and some things become clear to me especialy when I listened to interview with Kevin!

My health and fitness curves for some time were not paired. One went up and the other down. Now they are paired again. They both decline!!!

This is the reason for my knee injury. I should learn from Kevin's example that I should pay more attention to my health first and foremost and that I'm not imperishable...

I hope that one take lesson from what I've just explained... That is my intention. And also this could be my official start on the way to optimal?

### minimal — 2017-07-06T15:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-218073>

My goals (this list will be upgraded with time):

1\. Visit every nook and cranny of the World at least the most beautiful places before I die

2\. Move as close as possible to the area of my mitochondrial DNA origin (at least to its latitude)

3\. Find a way to make myself and my beloved happy all the time or at least most of the time.

4\. Demise due to age or accident, not illness

5\. Do something meaningful at least once in my life

6\. Lift 2.5\* my weight in squat and deadlift/2\* my weight in bench press with no aches (and eventually drop this not very natural injury/recovery vicious cycle) \[Yes. This is stupid, meaningless and potentially harmful\]

7\. Attain as much as possible useful knowledge

8\. Meet at least one of the members of this forum personally

Ad.1 I’m trying to visit at least 1-2 countries /year but I’d like to take French leave at work and make trip around the world trip. For now I’m not planning a lot in this field yet.

Ad.2 I’m planning to send my saliva samples to 23andMe, FTDNA or Ancestry (depending on the price and Your opinion) to find out my Haplotype. Then I’ll chose right latitude.

Ad.3 Clueless yet with this.

Ad. 4 This involves establishing proper habits. Habits that I consider healthy and that I have or I’m pretty close to establish (thank You Paul):

1\. Morning routine:

a. 0.5l H2O straight after wake up

b. 10-15 min barefoot walk in the morning sun

c. Nettle with coconut oil + butter

2\. Work routine:

a. 9-10 5 min sun exposure (UV-A)

b. 10:45-11:15 sun exposure (UV-A/UV-B) + barefoot walking in the adjoining park

c. Orange glasses when I’m inside (a lot of artificial blue light)

d. I try not to sit at all. I put cardbox on on my desk and on the top of it I installed my laptop (I’m going to buy external keyboard to limit contact with my computer).

e. I installed Iris software to cut blue light.

3\. Evening routine:

a. Epi-paleo dinner (kind of IF -> don’t eat in until 4-6PM apart from ~100g of fat from coconut and butter with my beverages)

b. Form of CT (barrel/cold shower)

c. Barefoot walk prior to sunset. When I’m very tired I don’t wait for sunset, I just take a walk and get to sleep.

I’m sleeping in tent since it’s much closer to the Earth’s magnetic field. The tent (pictures attached) is situated at the end of my plot, somewhere I guess EMF contamination is lower. I put grounding sheet in the tent so I’m providing e flux at night.

[https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzaLfb53JmOhOUhjYWNXbHZwUUNKdVhESXYxeC02UldtMnJz](https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzaLfb53JmOhOUhjYWNXbHZwUUNKdVhESXYxeC02UldtMnJz)

[https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzaLfb53JmOhNG9VMHYxT1diOVJCQ0xnSUNiYWNxNEhkRmhv](https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzaLfb53JmOhNG9VMHYxT1diOVJCQ0xnSUNiYWNxNEhkRmhv)

[https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzaLfb53JmOhV0JJdjZxNHJGMEZaMmFfR0VYczVvdlFqUmtz](https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzaLfb53JmOhV0JJdjZxNHJGMEZaMmFfR0VYczVvdlFqUmtz)

I’m about to purchase IR lamp and planning also to buy red LED to use it at work to mitigate blue; UV-B and maybe UV-A lights for the winter.

I’m planning to do as many labs from the epi-paleo rx list list in near future and will repeat this in 6 months to see the difference.

Ad.5 Don’t know yet

Ad.6 This is tied to Ad.4 plus I need to work on my recovery (light therapy and lots of CT). My workout schedule will be very simple and will revolve around the main lifts. But first things first - before I get under the bar I’ll do labs to see what’s going out and depending on the results I decide on starting my strength program or not. For now (2nd week off), I just walk barefooted and in the sun as much as possible and if I’m not shattered I work around my house/garden. I also do 10-15 min cold shower/day.

Ad.7 Read JK’s blog articles/watch podcasts with him (but not only with him)/read threads on this forum and try to pick up as much as possible from You all/read books of other authors that I consider useful and meaningful .

Ad.8 I started this thread…

### JanSz — 2017-07-06T15:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-218075>

When Jack Kruse designed Quantlet, he used
[https://patents.google.com/patent/US20160367833A1/en](https://patents.google.com/patent/US20160367833A1/en)
he used:
**850 and 660 nm** wavelengths,
and a third plurality emitting 630 nm, 532 nm,
and 450 nm wavelengths, positioned in a row substantially parallel to the arm of the user.
The second module includes first and second rows of lights on first and second sides of a cooling unit, each emitting 405 nm wavelengths,

\--------

He did not went over 850nm
The higher you go the more heat, more power use and emission.
Battery operated device to be practical must limit power use.
That
is one pov.
The other is
that you actually want to emphasize on 660 & 850
and having excessive heat would limit the power density of those two.

People have more respect of a doctor who whips out high tech device.
Shaman who will tell you to bask in a camp fire does not get that much respect.

### JanSz — 2017-07-06T17:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-218086>

This can likely be used when planning CT.

[http://www.motherearthnews.com/home...ising-cattle/cattle-in-the-cold-ze0z1701zfis\]](http://www.motherearthnews.com/homesteading-and-livestock/livestock/raising-cattle/cattle-in-the-cold-ze0z1701zfis])
****Caring for Cattle in the Cold**

Body Type**
A three-year South Dakota study found that cows with higher body-condition scores return to heat sooner in the breeding season and are also more likely to become pregnant when bred. **Thin cows (scoring 3 or less) have the poorest chance of getting pregnant**. Several other studies have shown that average body condition (score 5) or score 6 at calving and at the start of breeding season results in the highest pregnancy rates, but many factors can affect the reproductive success of a beef herd. Ideal body condition can vary with breed and cow type, season, and geographic location. **As a general rule, cows in cold climates need more flesh covering than do cows in warm climates.**

~5:00 10 min barefoot walk with sun exposure, tea with butter and Coconut oil

~11:00 30 min of barefoot walking and lying in the park keeping tabs on the sun.

2 x coffee with ccoil and butter, lots of water. And orange glasses all the time. This really makes a huge difference! I ate dinner (potatoes with lots of butter - friday is my autophagy day), took kelp, lugol (20 drops), Mg citrate, gelatin and went to my tent and fall asleep again. I slept well.

Saturday:
Whole routine completed + work around my house/outside whole day. Dinner (don't remember what but probably some meat with lots of butter and greens) + supplements. Very good sleep (tent).

Sunday:
The routine + hanging around all day long.

Question 2: Guys, I've found this:
[https://led-byt.pl/pl/p/Halogen-LED-Podczerwien-IR-9W/349](https://led-byt.pl/pl/p/Halogen-LED-Podczerwien-IR-9W/349)

can I use this one instead of the one You mentioned:

despite it's summer now.

High cortisol, high DHEA is commonly associated with chronic stress ([https://www.drlam.com/blog/adrenal-fatigue-and-low-thyroid-gland-function/5298/](https://www.drlam.com/blog/adrenal-fatigue-and-low-thyroid-gland-function/5298/)) . So if her cortisol is to high maybe she is stressed? By her ferritin one could tell, couldn't he?

It is rarely that I see LH at high range. May be it was a spurious peak, LH is not steady.
Most often LH is about no more than mid range.
So lets say pituitary (working as it should) wants more testosterone, and cranks LH.
That gives 670,10ng/dl
while I would expect twice of that.
So at least testicles are sluggish.

That 670,10ng/dl could be higher but since it is naturally obtained we are happy about
but
FreeT=15,49pg/ml
is about half of what one would expect with such a push.
Indicates that your SHBG is too high and binds too much T.
SHBG most commonly is high due to excessive estrogens.
You should analyze most of your estrogens
E1, E2, total Estrogens at least
If estrogens are too high they will raise SHBG.

.........
High estrogens may be due to high DHEA

If DHEAs is high naturally, I do not know how to get it down.
....

### JanSz — 2017-07-26T14:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-220120>

> [minimal said:](/goto/post?id=220115)

> There are few companies that deal with dna testing internationally. Two (ancestry and 23andme) offer just single tests and third (familytreedna) offers packages.
> What would be the best choice for me? Do I need anything more than mtDNA?

>

I have used 23andme.

At this time, in my mind, may change on a dime:

most value of this type of testing is to figure out where your grandma was located about 10000 years ago (latitude vise), and relocate to that latidude or somewhat closer to equator.
More health problems, get closer toward equator.
(My own experience of a whole year in tropics, not really comfortable Circadian way, days too short, but sun was great). Lobsters tails almost every day, garlic stuff, chased by desserts (almost 100% sugar) and caipirinha and cafezinia, right on the beach.
My current latitude now is about one degree south of where my 10000 y grandma was.
Gliwice, Krakow was little too much north for me.
Between about Rome and Naples is about right.
In Parsippany NJ 07054 now.

The other information, mostly genetic, while trumpeted by others is rather downplayed by @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) .

The 10000 years or thereabout is to account for any evolution of organism to accommodate to a local condition.
Staying at same latitude for 10000 years allows for required changes.
You do not want to go too far past, because eventually we are all from around lake Victoria (almost right at equator).

Closer to equator, diet mostly carbohydrates (lots of them, energy short lasting, need lots of it).

Closer to poles, diet have more fats, seafood shines. Energy from fat lasts longer, less meals.

...

### drezy — 2017-07-27T16:26:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-220210>

I feel your frustration too. I often get the clue that the key for me understanding deeper is to really think about the life of a star and the life of mitochondria & cells.

I need to simplify my thinking. We'll agree that the energy/reaction that produced what later pops out as a free photon happened a long time ago. What holds it in since a photon does not have mass? Dense fields (electrical & magnetic).

Doc wants you to boost your DC charge in those mitochondria. Why? Denser electric fields aka better photon entrapment. Uhh Solar copyright infringement, right?

I also try think about iron in this two sided model too. Iron in a star signals the end of a star because it absorbs the energy a star is producing. Absorbing the sun's energy is the name of our game. Ferritin seems to be like a magnetic anti-catalyst to control for proper timing.

### minimal — 2017-08-01T10:45:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-220598>

That is my second aha moment. Thank You again @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) for Your support. Now this makes sense - ferritin makes her brain to catch up with time.
She does her job outside and expose herself to daylight amap. When it's rainy day, she can't take her computer out and she irradiates herself with IR/red light and UV-A. I wonder how much time has to elapse before her body will trust her and bring ferritin levels down.

I know it's not perfect but for now we can't afford to quit our jobs and move to a better quantum yield place. I know that this sounds like an excuse and probably it is, espetially when taking into account that our jobs are not fullfiling for any of us. She makes enviormental assesment reports which is officials hullabaloo and in most cases is just the way of taking money from investors. She's aware of this and many times she's down in the dumps about it but she was trained in this field and so far it's the best post she has ever occupied. I work in Univerisity Library and in the Faculty of Materials Science and Ceramics...where I run many apparatuses: 2 x-ray diffractometers ,1 fluorescence spectrometer, FTIR and Raman, do some teaching and suppose to do science as well. Those xrd's and xrf are killing me. And not due to x-ray radiation that one could think but due to other EMF's that they emit (huge generators). Faculty is stacked with WiFi's and flurescent bulbs everywhere as well. This science thing makes me sick too. If one wants to deal with science, funds must be gathered for the lab work. Since Poles don't like to find their own way, scientific projects are granted for global all the rage ideas, not mentionning corruption part of the story. Couple years ago I received 3 year-long funding which in my opinion came to nothing. Money was spent, lab work done, small-time results were obtained and this didn't halt anyone from writing not imporant scientific papers...We produce clutter, not meaningful science. I still have pangs of concious and I don't want to be part of this rat race. But my boss puts gently screws on me to write some project. He's very nice guy and I don't want to let him down but I'm not into science anymore. I doubt if I was ever. What is more, I catch myself of lack of curiosity. This is really bad. If there was anything that I'd like to work on it would be something not very fashionable. I'm not enlighted but I'm concious more and this is killing me as well. Often, I just wan't to dig hole in the ground, crawl there and wait to the end. I know that I need to take step, ask for my cards and start new life. But I'm affraid of all the burned boats...Ok. That's it for today - I don't want You to think that I'm "this grumble guy". I just want to release the pressure.

So today's questions are:
1\. If one work's outside but in front of computer, do one need to wear blue blocking glasses? Let's say that one has IRIS software installed which is supposed to block blue light.

2\. If someone works in an office (takes regular breakes for sun exposition) would it be wise to install IR/red/UV-A light anyway close to computer (with IRIS installed as well)?

3\. I'm aware that dr Kruse said that blue light emited from technology sources is much less packed with other wavelengths, thus blue light toxicity. I wary of the software that is supposed to cut the blue component, so for this reason I wear orange glasses anyway when I work on my computer, let alone when exposed to fluorescent bulbs. But what to do when I must work on computer and I want to use UV-A bulb. Unfortunetelly my orange glasses cut UV region as well. Do I need have eye contact with this UV or is it ok to have just skin contact?

4\. Sunrise is not just upon my awakening so during my morning barefoot walks I don't have sun component anymore. Do I make harm to myself if I do barefoot walk and then I irradiate myself with this reptile UV bulb together with red and IR for three minutes to shut my pituary? Or maybe I should switch the order?
When I arrive to the city, before I make entrance to my work I go to the park for the sun capture anyway.

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) once You mentioned famous Polish actor that wore extraordinary all the fashion glasses. Did You mean [Zbigniew Cybulski](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zbigniew_Cybulski) ?

Sorry to hear about your experience with science. Many of the books doc recommends mention this too. My wife spent years as a researcher. She experienced this as well.

Yeah loss of curiosity and/or sex drive are not good signs to me. Do you know that Jan is like 78 and has asked me several questions about software and technology on PM. 1.) A few longtime members do not wear blue blockers and orient themselves so they face the sun if outside on a laptop. If I wear blue blockers too much I sleep too early. It's got it's own diminishing returns for me in that way.

2.)Doc recommended my mom to use UVA and throw IR-A in there if feasible

Thank You @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) for answers. Two first are clear to me now. But I have some issues with the third. Like for instance when I work on my computer or bunk off in the lab but it's time for UV-A sunbath and I don't have possibility to go out. if I turn UVA lamp togther with IR is it ok to wear orange glasses because my skin will absorb UV anyway? On the other hand I should wear hat and scarf or turtleneck sweater to protect myself from blue, right? So no absorption...Or it doesnt matter - If it's AM I don't wear orange glasses, turn UV-A bulb when it's time and don't care about covering myself. When it's PM I start to wear orange glasses paying attention to light outside?

So how did Your Lady got through her issues with science? Is she still a researcher?

When I was little I had two contradicting dispositions. I was curious as every child but I was also meek. It's hard to balace those two features in my opinion. Being submissive leads to stop asking questions but doesen't necessarly lead to being dumb. One still have possibility to train brain by learning things at school. If one follows what is prached by "system" eventually one is brought to his/her knees and start to think that having more is better than being more. For years I was saying that I don't care about posessing things but I'm still pawn going with the stream. I do what it is expected from me: get up, work, sleep, repeat... This leads to some kind of dissonance in my behaviour because I often want to quit things or want to quit (adrenal burnout?). What if I die tommorow? I know nothing, saw nothing, achieved nothing. This is the reason I want to quit my job, despite the fact that it's not that hard and possibly could lead me to better money. But this was never me. I remember how mad was my father at me when I was resisting making money. No, it has nothing to do with being comunist or social leech because I was always working hard and had not much free time but I was always afraid of money - they stressed me out and still do. However I'm aware that money might be a way to reach Your goal. It's bad when they start to take control over person's life. Why do I care about my house/job/enviorment when they do much bad to me? Is there anything wrong with me like hormonal imbalances? Is it kind of sign of a beginning of mental illness?

>
> Adrenal Stress Profile - Saliva Test Kit For 5 Hormone Level Imbalances (DS, Cx4)
>
>

> before I do this (BTW, are all those hormones determined from saliva? What are the rest of those hormones part from DHEA and cortisol?):

That test measures in saliva

DHEAs
and
Four times cortisol level (they also provide a chart as a visual aid.
\-----------------------------
Hormones (specially cortisol) when measured in saliva are considered to be a free (active) hormones.
That is helpful because it gives more **direct status of ones Adrenal Stress Profile**,
because it by passes separate consideration when similar test is made in serum.

When it is made in serum there are other consideration and more measurements may be required.
Such as:
Total Cortisol
variety of protein levels that bind cortisol
some of those proteins bind stronger other bind less strong
then
one have to ferret out remaining amount of cortisol that is not bonded
and considered to be active

that active part of cortisol then may be compared to what is measured in saliva.

...
\================================

With all above consideration
often money or other considerations may have influence on what is actually tested.
For example:
I test above (and more) using blood test.
I am able to get doctor's script and have all done usually twice a year all paid by my insurance.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717)

> We are designed to walk naked in dinky equatorial marshes picking oysters and other creatures.

Wow that's a valid answer to how many questions around here?

If in the home office we use blue blockers uv-a and IR. If working outside in the day it's f.lux as strong as possible no glasses until low sun (d-minder's default 30 degree warning). FWIW d-minder has just defaulted to becoming an alarm system for us as opposed to hyper tracking and data gawking. I feel sorry for all the folks that think big data is going to fix the world...

> So how did Your Lady got through her issues with science? Is she still a researcher?

Switched to outright engineering where the politics are slightly less and the prizes are bigger.

Like power money seems like just a tool that can, in some, amplify some inherent neuroses (that may have been evolved for some use). Out of several people Iknow who had fairly spontaneous acquisition of wealth and power I've seen about 70% crash their relationships, health, or both.

Normally I'd try to come up with something cool to say about dopamine here, but you've been around long enough to know these connections.

### minimal — 2017-08-04T10:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-220885>

Guys, Thank You again (which might sound not sinciere if I thank every post but I really appreciate it).

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) pleqase don't get me wrong. If I'm being dumb I apologise. Some obvious things for You are not that obvious for me. Plus I want to learn as much as possible from You. For this reason I asked about Solar Glow 160W. I read this post again and again and I don't understand why are You using this lamp upon Your awakening. H[ere](http://www.uvguide.co.uk/UVTool-ExoTerraSolarGlo160W.htm) I found:

**Spectrum - UV plus Visible Light**

A mid-day solar spectrum with the sun close to the zenith (Solar altitude 85.4°, location San Bartolomé, Gran Canaria, June 21st 2011 Local time 14:00h) is overlaid for comparison (but note the different irradiance scales).

**Spectrum - UV in more detail**

This spectrum has:

-   typical features of a mercury vapour lamp: extremely discontinuous spectrum ("spikes") very unlike sunlight.
-   no detectable UVC or non-terrestrial UVB (wavelengths below 290nm).
-   very low solar-wavelength (295 - 320nm) UVB output, restricted to small "spikes" at 296, 302 and 313nm. These wavelengths are involved in vitamin D3 synthesis.
-   very low short-wavelength UVA output (320 - 350nm). These wavelengths are involved in the _control_ of vitamin D3 synthesis.
-   high long-wavelength UVA output, but restricted to one "spike" at 365nm - reptile vision includes long-wavelength UVA.
-   Visible light spectrum (wavelengths 400 - 750nm) mainly restricted to only four colours from the mercury arc, but with some additional continuous-spectrum light from the tungsten filament, primarily in the orange and red wavelengths - resulting in only moderate colour rendering (to the human eye).

**Spectral Analysis - photobiologically active UV (wavelengths from 270 - 350nm)**

-   There is some UVB in the wavelengths which enable vitamin D3 synthesis in skin.
-   This lamp emits no hazardous UVC or non-terrestrial UVB (below 290nm).
-   The proportion of UVA from 320 - 335nm, important for natural prevention of excessive vitamin D3 synthesis, is low. This is typical of mercury vapour lamps. It is still unknown as to whether this has any long-term health consequences.

So, I found that it has UV-b component but not continous. It has very low UV-a but it is there anyway. It has no blue but purple. So, again are You using it because it's bright so it will shut pineal gland anyway? And again, wouldn't this 3 minutes of irradiation give signal to Your pituitary to shut other hormons production? (from what You explain in Your post it would) If yes why do You do this so early? One wants to shut pituitary but why that early if this doesnt follow sun's signaling?
\----------------------------------------------------------------------

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

>
>
>
> Wow that's a valid answer to how many questions around here?

/////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
This type discussions always start with disclaimer.
There is nothing that is man made that can replace sunlight.
Nothing, not even close.
///////////////////////////////////////
The three minutes comes from some minimal exposure to original sunlight when there is nothing between eyes and sunlight.
It is very early in the morning,
we are concerned about 460nm-480nm frequency range for the benefit of pineal gland.
460nm-480nm is a visible range, (475nm being specifically called a blue)
(it is not UV range)
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
have thrown a new angle (discussing UVa and UVb in relation to pineal gland)
but
most known, and I guess most powerful is blue range
it is widely used when attempting to correct SAD problems,
SAD - Seasonal affective disorder, also called winter depression, winter blues, summer depression, and seasonal depression

[https://science-edu.larc.nasa.gov/EDDOCS/Wavelengths\_for\_Colors.html](https://science-edu.larc.nasa.gov/EDDOCS/Wavelengths_for_Colors.html)
\===============

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) started discussions on importance of light by observation of Chicago's restaurant crew that was exposed to some kind of black light.
That exposed UVa & UVb as very important to our health.
Then came Circadian Rhythm.

Jack's ways to remedy most health problems is suggestion to move to equator or at least to tropics.
Since not many can do that I always look for some more or less acceptable workarounds.
After few iterations I honed on a (universal) light that is able to sustain (sensitive) lizard in the day time.
Usually terrariums have multitude of different lights and heaters.
ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W during day time and heater at night are able to sustain that lizard and keep it alive, while highly simplified and easy to mange.

There may be a better lights but I do not know about them
or
they may be to expensive or cumbersome in use.
At one time my wife was having rather large reef aquarium.
Lots of light and heat management problems.
My electric bill was as large as five or more my of neighbors fully air conditioned houses including their swimming pool's heaters and cooling.
Much simpler (acceptable) solution is need.
\---------------------
With @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) constantly feeding us with new information,
my
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4)
is constantly updated.

\---------------------
@[minimal](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14206/)
do you have any suggestions to improve that list
(while keeping eye on the cost).

### Outlier — 2017-09-06T07:09:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-222949>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=220157)
>
> Let's have a little science fun spread out over a few posts. On average how old is a photon that hits you when you step out into the sun?

I thought JK said that photons do not experience time. Wouldn't that make them ageless?

### drezy — 2017-09-06T19:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.19604/post-222989>

> [Outlier said:](/goto/post?id=222949)
>
> Wouldn't that make them ageless?

Relatively... <hardy har har>

There are 3 answers I can come up with:

-   8.x minutes old (the transit time) relative to our time
-   0 minutes old relative to the time photon that experiences
-   100k(wish) - to millions of years old relative to our time (depending on how you count tunneling)

Wrapping my head around tunneling:

It helps to listen to Jim:
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?time\_continue=295&v=\_qgSz1UmcBM&t](https://www.youtube.com/watch?time_continue=295&v=_qgSz1UmcBM&t)
\[tunneling at 8:06\]

Even better when photons do tunnel they just about skip (our subjective) time all together:
From: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hartman\_effect](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hartman_effect)

> For large gaps between the prisms the tunnelling time approaches a constant and thus the photons appear to have crossed with a superluminal speed.

I keep imagining the photon in all of these places inner,middle, and outer sun and then harnessed by one cells all at once relative to the actual photon.

Photons with their relative timelessness and ability to tunnel and "cheat" even our subjective time start to sound more and more like a great "base operating system" for all life, especially when you think about the "hundreds of thousands of interactions in each cell per second" covered here semi-regularly.

I've really been thinking about Doc's "Light creates time".

Doc chooses very concise words that have much meaning. It's funny to hear Doc say "make like a sphinx". I know what it means physically. I can't help but think Doc **is** like Sophocles' riddle giving sphinx. Sphinx-Like at least in the fact that if we choose to gawk at the science, something other primates seem to be unburdened with based on my last interaction involving a lower order primate luring visitors close to a cage so they would be better targets for spitting on, we will not be spoon-fed. Doc's totally not like the plague on the land part of Sophocles' sphinx though...

For the record:

1.  I was not spit on by the lower order primate so chock one up for H. Sapiens
2.  3 other people were suckered in closer so -3 for H.Sapiens there
3.  Presumably the lower order primate's "doctors" were as unconcerned about too much sun as the primate itself seemed to be(full junk exposed)
4.  Hearing a chimp laugh after spewing a mouth full of water at zoo goers is just as infectious as hearing a human laugh
5.  There is something special about laughter. Can someone please GDV cam that and tell me?
6.  Based on facial expressions and body language, I suspect my laughing was less forgiven, by targeted zoo goers, than the chimp's

### nonchalant — 2019-06-03T22:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-bath-ct-and-dry-skin.19605/post-271929>

Eating lots of seafood changes the skin for the better. Stronger, denser, smoother, and moister. I haven't had dry elbows/knees in a couple years. Nice change for me. I used to use butter for an emergency fix. Quickly available when traveling, etc.

I have for a while, however, had something that looks like dry skin... a hazy covering on the visible parts of my arms and legs. I'd wipe them with a damp washcloth to remove it and it would smell a bit sulfurous, like fingernail filings. A little disconcerting. It was more difficult to find the root cause. It was nnEMF, of course, but where? Changing my bed quickly eliminated the problem.

edit: No, now that I think about it some more, the hazy skin started to go away when I stopped wearing exposed elastic waistbands. The new bed just finished the problem.

### Martha Ray — 2019-06-04T01:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-bath-ct-and-dry-skin.19605/post-271958>

[**Enjoying posts here on the Jack Kruse Optimal Health Forum!**](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php)
I was fond of lots of skin products in the past but do like the thinking of _not putting on you skin anything you would not eat._ Tallow, Olive Oil and Ghee all sound good. I have those and one retail Tallow cream. I've also been trying Puree of Aloe Vera Lotion with Glycerin by _Forces in Nature (_a _local_ Crozet, VA _product_ sold at Whole Foods) for my forearm radiation damage... my somewhat functional doctor recommended I try to find a body lotion with Neem and Karanja Oils she did not have a source. I did Find a Canadian product from Honey Pot Herbals. Also used _Alaffia_ Unscented Shea Butter for rough hands I get in the winters here in 'Dr. Zach Bush' country where I live... formerly from Houston, TX was a patient of 'preventative doctor' Owen Robbins MD there. I Houston I had lots of humidity and moisture very dry here plus some health crisis and poor lifestyle choices. Been 10+ years now improving Health but have not drilled down on a lot of things. Have to look up every single ingredient in everything. Sensitive to chemicals as well as gluten, nightshades, conventional citric acid....etc.
I was just outside and came and I can confirm that the Tallow does appeal the Great Dane next door was paying attention to my hand.. the one I used as soon as I read this post is _Mother Makings_ _Tallowed Touch_ Sweet Earth Grassfed Beef Tallow Handcrafted in Asheville, NC where our daughters live.
I have more than just dry skin- seriously damaged forearms looking for help with my arms. Paper thing easily cuts and tiny bumps causes large red blotches. I normally wear long sleeves out or hemmed socks when I sun.. not sure if sun helps or hurts but I avoid during the day not on AM sun rise viewing or walks to view sunsets. Oh those Honey Pot Herbals and Mother Makings Tallowed Touch are sold on Etsy if anyone wants to check them out. Hope that's ok to share... I've not posted here 2 times. I am retired and study different 'healths' most of moments-- seriously seeking. Crossed Jack Kruse path but latched on a bit with the passing of time. So glad. I'm a newbie and will be saying that for about 5+ years I suspect.

Hi Martha, I hope you enjoy it here! I wish I could give you some advice about your skin but what I have been using lately is Emu oil from Walkabout Health Products and it feels really really good! ### Martha Ray — 2019-08-19T05:13:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-bath-ct-and-dry-skin.19605/post-276211>

Thank you for your kindness Kathy ... I will look into Emu Oil. One practionier years ago suggested grapeseed oil... I used that and olive oil for a while but did not see much improvement. Whipped Shea butter feels good on my skin but my skin is so very thin on my forarms that I'd rather use food so will try tallow and Ghee next. My forarm skin thinness surfaced after 2.5 months of AZ CTCA cancer treatment. I was atrophied and in a wheel chair when I came home and after the long flight home I developed a blood clot in my left left that did not respond to using **Coumadin/** (**warfarin**) had to have vien surgery and was then gave myself the blood thinner Arixtra shots daily in my stomach for 3 months.... all the drugs they provided me that year had nasty side effects.-- If my genetics were checked before cancer treatment they might have noticed to provide more protective care against blood clots as I am genetically at more risk for them. Genetics would have also helped them know typical doseing of **Coumadin/** (**warfarin**) would not work on me...I did not respond to it and had to have the vien surgery. Hindsight of things I learned years later.

Hoping someone can answer you about the Emu Oil hindering or not with your sun intake...

Here’s hoping that we all learn more ourselves so we can provide our own protection.
