# Thread: What's up light fam!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-07T21:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215577>

Hi,

New to the forum. Looking forward to being part of the change this world needs so badly. The best part is that instead of focusing in on social issues if we deal with biology first a lot of social ills can be resolved. I been reading Dr. K's blog for like a year or more, but in the last 3 months I have decided that despite the breadth of material I will read all of it including all the citations , already its helped me build a more complete picture of what is going on in our mind/body's. Don't be discouraged if it doesn't sink it, it eventually does sink in. This is coming from a math and physics undergrad and MD so be patient and keep at it. It takes a while.

I sucked at medicine don't get me wrong I studied hard and got the 99% percentile on my mcats and had very solid board scores, but the medicine that I was taught was dead/lifeless, and the practice of it was even worse. You are a glorified secretary at this point, I'm terrible at multi-tasking always have been and the physician of today is supposed to mainly be good at multi-tasking.

Not good at asking questions,
Not good at thinking
Not good at pondering interdisciplanary knowledge connections
Not good at talking and deeply connecting with patients interpersonally.

I quit residency partially because the system was environmentally / and emotionally toxic but also because I wasn't good enough. I would never be good at killing , boxing , and packaging my patients out the door in 15 minutes, while making sure everything I did was up to date with the gold standard guidelines, making sure I didn't forget any screenings/vaccinations they had coming up, typing and staring at a screen for 90% of my patient encounter, and then typing and staring at a screen after the encounter.

It was stupid no offense to practicing physicians you are stronger willed than I am so I have a lot of respect, but it really was stupid. They make it so doctors can't think deeply about problems, its probably setup that way on purpose. Thinking slowly doesn't make money.

### caroline — 2017-06-08T01:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215595>

Hi Asad and welcome. That is a heartbreaking story about what our wonderful Doctors are expected to do instead of what they wanted to do - Heal.

What do you do now?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-08T02:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215598>

Welcome. It was good to meet you face to face in Vermont. Love your avatar.

### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-08T06:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215610>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=215595)
>
> Hi Asad and welcome. That is a heartbreaking story about what our wonderful Doctors are expected to do instead of what they wanted to do - Heal.
>
> What do you do now?

Thanks Caroline,

I'm working with three startups based out of SF but I think I'm making a change pretty soon. My body is requiring it if I want to have any semblance of a quality of life lol.
Its would be a heartbreaking story if I wasnt already starting to understand the deeper principles of water I had gotten into Gilbert ling and gerald pollacks work and really taught me the whole edifice of modern research in medicine is in trouble if this is true. I am happy I gained the knowledge I gained and happy to drop the useless components of that knowledge as I reconnect with the art of healing.

### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-08T06:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215611>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=215598)
>
> Welcome. It was good to meet you face to face in Vermont. Love your avatar.

Thanks Dr. K I am glad I made the trek to meet you I knew I had to, thanks for taking the time to answer my questions, jumped in the lake twice haha.

### caroline — 2017-06-08T09:33:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215612>

Have you read Andrew Marino's book? going somewhere: truth about a life in science

### Asad Choudhry — 2017-06-09T03:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-215655>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=215612)
>
> Have you read Andrew Marino's book? going somewhere: truth about a life in science

No I havent yet Caroline , its been ordered along with couple other Dr. K books, i need john ott's stuff and nick lanes stuff as well. The rest I had from before.

### ObstreperousPolarBear — 2018-07-02T05:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-244735>

Always good to see other docs finding this work. Especially interested to see how those of you with math/physics backgrounds handle the material. I feel like I am drinking from the firehose again. Couldn't agree more re: how medicine is flawed and getting more flawed. I struggled with the same issues you mentioned in residency.

I'm curious what your next career move would be... obviously, out of SF is only a partial answer.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-02T16:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-up-light-fam.19606/post-244752>

The more time I spend around academics, I note that when a message offends people in positions of power, one often observes unconventional tactics used to demean the work or person, which evade the thorny issues of engaging in the argument. Academia is devolving. The idea that "science is settled" and that opposing views are met with personal attacks & censorship, rather than arguing ideas on their merit, is the antithesis of science.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-13T01:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-and-emf.19612/post-218627>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=218561)
>
> I'd love to hear Jack talk about the connection between the moon and menstruation. I'm not sure this has been spoken about before? @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)

He did just post on the FB about the moon cycles affecting marine life and a specific protein that has been discovered that can help animals sense moon phase wrt mating.

If I knew how to link to a FB post I would, but here is the article he linked: [http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2007/10/071019-coral-spawning.html](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2007/10/071019-coral-spawning.html). and the text he wrote below. You can scroll his FB page for a moment, and look for a moon. He posts a lot so it's not immediate, but it was only a few days ago

"Tonight is the key night for living things. July 9 is also auspicious for celestial reasons: It’s the longest day of the year with a full moon and this is critical for many animals to reproduce. On Mexican and Caribbean beaches tonight turtles will be laying eggs. Corals in the sea will be cementing their futures with lunar spawning. The full moom will create interesting changes in tides. The periodic rise and fall of water every 12.4 hours plays a vital role in marine environments, giving rise to a vibrant, uniquely adapted array of intertidal organisms. But beyond the tides, many species in water and on land also make use of the moon’s evocative glow tonight.

Most of all, evolution seems fond of using moonlight as mood lighting for sex: Many species of all shapes and sizes use the moon’s ghostly irradiance, and its 29.5-day waxing and waning, to cue various phases of reproduction. A 2007 study of the coral species Acropora millepora shed light on how the corals do it: The animals possess a light-detecting protein called CRY2 that’s exceptionally sensitive to moonlight’s characteristic blue wavelengths. CRY2, along with the protein CRY1, are widely used among animals to help calibrate their internal biochemical clocks. While other cues such as day length and ocean temperature appear to signal the corals to prepare, moonlight acts as the crucial trigger—telling the corals to let loose clouds of millions of eggs and sperm. That cue used to be important for human females before modern changes.[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/…/071019-coral-spawning.…](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fnews.nationalgeographic.com%2Fnews%2F2007%2F10%2F071019-coral-spawning.html&h=ATMMli_ObXILZPrkBCwAswmxaADh3uvKCarXhzU9CV332WInz0SzGWn65amO4mpuTjLoICzZk4N0nT_TMgrJ9nKDHOq4XMkoGssVs_8KOxJx6cHLEVZvEmAHjoUIdS5HvBHew0kflIORXfGgCJdHGftSkDd0YoTEXcLY&enc=AZO1qeuX_WVUKrFM50AaeBRn2xES-0PvVIVy-2QVMSG1xSy_II9AvUKhqZvIST1D7Mk_IW5b7sfjQDCv3bajnI4qI0AsbGw9_qqgiWTGubgqUf6meF9Z6kurfZhstOafb9E4CAKCdLrjH-DetbAe4nmYxGt-m8QxiCH4uMveH1348vUjF_2O73iOz2M3KKxnAR7Q9l2oFH0gN3xna2K8ROew&s=1)"

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-13T09:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menstruation-and-emf.19612/post-218657>

someone commented to me a couple of days ago - someone who i hadn't seen in over a year that I seemed so much happier... that it looked like I had lost weight (not really) - they almost didn't recognize me... (wow really) but in good way

Nothing and I feel great here too Caroline. I changed my environment and I spend a considerable amount of time in Nature and there is no diet or supplement that has helped me like Nature has, being the sun and cold adaption too and of course, all of the grounding that I do here. I finally decided that I have to leave a few pairs if sandals and shoes in my car because I am barefoot more than I am not and I was leaving home without anything on my feet and would have to turn around and go home to put something on them. Barefoot just comes so naturally to me now, just like looking at the morning sun to start my hormone panel for the day. I love the feeling of feeling unconfined by clothing and shoes and walls. I would prefer to live as a wild woman in nature.

I don't have any of these symptoms of menopause and I would like to add leaky to the list. That disappeared the first year on this journey, thankfully.

A few things I can report as well here....

My nail beds are pure white and flat and my nails are hard
My hair just keeps getting thicker and I now have the natural curl that I had as a young girl....it came back.

I just feel very strong, happy and calm, at peace.

I think about how many women could make their transition through menopause like this, just by changing their environment and being true to Nature. They won't find this in any kind of pill, or supplement......

### drezy — 2017-06-23T16:01:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-intermittent-fasting-best-practices.19619/post-216887>

If you have no debilitating issues then go try out a few combinations.

In general I'd rather find something thats 90% optimal and fits my habit set and lifestyle than focus much on a 100% optimal solution that I don't have time for yet.

> I'm finding it challenging to get 5-7 hours of general sunlight exposure though.

I understand that. I try to at least get the morning IR and some solar noon light. I used to use dminder to remind me, but I think it's built into my habits now.

I just tell people I talked to a neurosurgeon and he told me I've got a condition where I need morning and solar noon light otherwise I deteriorate. Give it a try while everyone else self identifies with their disease as their badge you'll fit right in except your condition is the human condition. Maybe you'll even lure a few people outside with you.

EDIT: There are also tan through t-shirts. If you can't increase time you could use these to increase surface area exposed during lunch break walks.

# Thread: For Jack; Raw Food - TCM Yin/Yang - Importance of Vegetables?

### Technosmith — 2017-06-11T13:34:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-215803>

Hi folks,

Not sure if Jack responds directly to these posts so I will direct this question to the masses but hope that potentially he will step in and comment alongside other well-informed members of the forum.

Do we know what Jack's current perspective is on raw food? It would seem that raw food is much more electron dense, better for the microbiome, and natural but Traditional Chinese Medicine suggests that raw food is too 'Yin' and can unbalance the body. Some alternative practitioners feel thsi is a key aspect of dietary recommendations.

Jack, do you have a scientific explanation to 'debunk' this concept or is it potentially a valid recommendation in relation to healing?

Finally, does he still feel that vegetables are not that important in the diet?

Fully appreciate that the environment is the key over-riding factor but would be good to get his current take on these questions?

Thanks,

Phil

### Inger — 2017-06-12T12:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-215896>

I love my raw wild oysters..... they are my medicine!
I eat most part of my food raw. I love it. Sometime i do want a steak cooked rare, or some lightly cooked seafood (non fatty stuff), but honestly I have to say, I have my best energy and mood when I eat raw animal foods!
I think the viruses and bacteria in the food are benefiting me...
Also, it tastes better
And get less nnEMF  (except if you cook with gas, but I do not own a gas stove)
and making a meal get way faster = saving time

But if I feel like doing a fish soup, or a cooked mussel dish I do!

I do not eat much veggies at all. I do love my fresh picked nettle smoothie or wild edibles tho

### Inger — 2017-06-12T17:31:56+0300

cold water is best.

up north you need more.. when you have lots of nnEMF you need more....

and what to cook also differs... Seafood has most benefits eaten raw

### drezy — 2017-06-12T18:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-215970>

Jack really tries to lay out the facts so that we can make our conclusions. Once in a while I'm sure it gets tiring and he belts it out in no uncertain terms.

> If your redox is good......you can eat shit on a shingle.

From: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...estion-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/#post-214013](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/#post-214013)

Maybe demystify your perception of Traditional Chinese Medicine a bit. Think them as just early biohackers.

Why do you ever have to biohack? It's because you've become uncoupled.

I trust but verify Jack's comments. In the past if I ate one piece of bread I would get stomach aches, the craps while even gaining weight, and really bad skin breakouts for days.

I'm all "What the what?!? Crazy doc up in here telling me 'shit on a shingle'? I'll prove him wrong!"

4 light, water, & magnetism months later I find the biggest and most shitty GMO burger with all kinds of toppings with the least Ying and yang you've ever seen with GMO wheat coated onion rings stacked in there too. I threw in Large onion rings on the side too. To make it the shittiest of "shingles" I had a 16oz cheap beer afterward. That beer with this meal would be a crippler and guarantee me I'd be working from the crapper pretty much all day the next day.

Results:
My nicely coupled and more diverse micro biome laughed it right off. Like a costume party they even got to pretend for one night they were Redneck microbiota. HooooWeee! It was lolz for them! I also lost 2 lbs when normally would have gained 3-4 assumedly in inflammation.

What changed? My environment/coupling. (In the same period I cut workouts and any supplementation to really try to break doc's theory)

Not long ago I would say yes to both, but not today.
Brain fog may be due to many reasons.

DHA, have a very long half life, little goes long way.
Test what you have then make decision.
Too much of a good stuff in one place depresses another good stuff in another place.
Optimal is the word.
From those that did the testing and posted here there was one girl that had, have low DHA.

DHEA, the desired limits that I have seen and posted should have added caveat.
Monitor your estrogens and then decide, if not often at least from time to time.

hahaha you actually are the funniest motherfucker on this forum...

Agree with everything you just said though... People put food in their mouth, have a reaction, and blame the food. Makes logical sense. But why is the reaction occurring, is it the food or are your mitochondria struggling because of something else, like nnEMF?

This last week, after I watched the Wild Alaska BBC one show with the bears, and hearing they eat 40kg of fish and 40kg of fruits and grass and all kinds of sugary shit a day, I've decided I want to be a bear cause they are cool as fuck.

I've been eating rice, bread, cakes, Biscuits, anything I can shove down my gob that tastes good - and plenty of meat and seafood.

I've waited for a bad reaction, to the sugar, but nothing... It has been really really liberating, and it has finally unlocked me (FULLY - I was already half way there) from the chains of the Food Guru bullshit. I have kept the seafood in take up to at least once a day, but apart from that - Ketosis or Carb diet doesn't seem to matter for my energy anymore... In fact, I feel better with the carbs right now, probably because it's summer.

For someone with diagnosed with Crohn's, being told you can't eat "bread, sugar, gluten etc. etc.", it's absolutely amazing to get to this point and truly not give a fuck about eating McDonalds or Sugar anymore, and not being the guy in the office who has to say no to a biscuit to stay in Ketosis...

Bravo Dr Kruse, Bra- fucking-vo.

### kris90 — 2017-07-28T14:26:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-220291>

Definitely finding the same. When you can increase your quantum yield, food doesn't matter. When you've got the DC electric current running strong throughout your body, you can do whatever you want, and cool shit happens.

I treat myself a lot in the summer. I don't crave foods per se, probably because my B-endorphin is through the roof with all the sun I've been getting, but I definitely have been pigging out lately. As long as I get lots of sun, ground, CT and some seafood, those electrons just move so damn fast, and it's impossible for me to put on fat. I've dropped to 9% bodyfat (may even being approaching 8% now) from 10-11% around spring, and I've been having a lot of fun with my eating and food intake. I'm not even really hungry, but summer is all about pigouts with family and friends.

When you can use sunlight to convert food electrons into useable energy, you don't have a requirement to store fat. It's like Jack says, calories only matter when you are leptin resistant, because you lose your ability to handle food electrons.

### drezy — 2017-07-28T16:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-jack-raw-food-tcm-yin-yang-importance-of-vegetables.19622/post-220300>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=220290)
>
> hahaha you actually are the funniest motherfucker on this forum...

Wow. That is a high honor for me. The ability to make someone in another place and at another time laugh is invaluable to me. Especially given the immensity of the firehose we are all in the process of drinking from here. It can be daunting.

I think laughter/humor may be a way to actually accelerate change in a mind like a catalyst. Kings kept a jester around often to serve as a cathartic prop for subjects that otherwise might revolt, but also be informed himself in a less threatening way. Also I think you mentioned you're married. If your spouse does something annoying you can either have a big fight about it or simply poke a little fun of it. The latter technique can lead to peaceful change.

I even think women with their thinner myelin have groomed men not just to be provider/protectors but also comedians since during laughing gals let their guard down. It's like the first intoxicant. I've gone at least a day without being raunchy around here so think about that thing hanging between our legs. We've all been to that party where either out of drunken volition or prank, some guy's shorts get pulled down. It slays the whole room with laughter. I suggest that even the most comedically challenged guy has himself a mini comedian kit right there. There's gotta be a a quantum tie in there somewhere...

Hey, I'm really glad to hear that you have come such a long way in your health and see the results of your higher redox and decoupled from beliefs that would have kept you ill. For the record I've since kept it epi-paleo, but like you, love knowing at social events I don't really have to pay attention to whatever is being served.

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-28T16:46:39+0300

For the record I've since kept it epi-paleo, but like you, love knowing at social events I don't really have to pay attention to whatever is being served.

Honestly a lot of your posts actually make me laugh out loud... which is really hard to do on the subject of disease and being optimal. Science isn't associated with humour, naturally. But like you I think it is so important... The more optimal I am, the more I laugh... that's one thing I've noticed was a big change from getting better.

I'm not married but nearly at the 1 year mark with my partner who I adore, mainly because we spend all of our time together laughing - and when we don't, one of us isn't feeling so well - and so we try and get each other better to get back to that place again.

Laughter in relationships is absolutely pivotal, which based on the above, means being optimal is absolutely pivotal - and the only way it can truly last. I think Dopamine is the quantum tie, whenever I get out of an Ice Bath I am literally giggling like a 10 year old and making jokes with my girlfriend, full of energy and flirting like you do in the "honey moon stage" of a relationship. Which is why I always CT and get AM sun, because it directly affects my relationship. 1 year later, we are still in the honey moon period, purely because of Dopamine.

Yeah I think epi paleo is more important the more uncoupled you are, and I do bring home the DHA everyday... but once you are in the high Redox zone... like Phelp's, who apparently ate 10 pizzas a day during training for the Olympics, or Wim Hof who eats spaghetti and drinks beer after his ice baths, I think it matters not what fuel comes in... if you are a Ferrari, you are a Ferrari.

>
> What we found was that when we were doing CT regularly, any food binges resulted in more bodyheat - Leptin sensitivity at work burning off excess fuel.

Exactly. The excess energy is released as heat when our cell has reached its capacity for energy requirements. It's the exact opposite of conventional wisdom that excess energy is stored as fat.

Turns out, body fat is a "backup" system humans use to re-capture lost energy that could not be used inside the cell, so fat gain occurs as energy flow slows, and if you keep electron tunneling speeds high, you can eat very little food and maintain an energy balance (tightly coupled), or you can uncouple and release excess energy as heat. That's why I stopped counting calories, carbs, and focus on doing things that keep me tightly coupled: 1) sunlight, 2) CT, 3) Fasting/high fat diet and 4) the right kind of exercise. You do that, you eat whatever you want, when you want, and life becomes more fun.

# Thread: allergy to cinnamaldehyde and other spices

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### Sunny1966 — 2017-06-12T13:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergy-to-cinnamaldehyde-and-other-spices.19630/post-215903>

Last week I have been diagnosed with a cinnamaldehyde allergy and a fragrance mix 1 allergy by a skin prick test. In my puberty I had already been told to avoid spicy meals, chocolate, pork and a lot more foods, but as years went by I forgot about it more or less and my alergies got much better, until I was affected by hormonal birth control based on synthetic progesterone in an IUD. The last few years I noticed that many spices give me a hormonal reaction and I still had to be careful with spices. Synthetic progesterone in birth control is derived from testosterone and testosterone is derived from cinnamaldehyde. What is the relationship with light, water and magnetism to spices and what can I do to improve my allergy to spices?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-12T14:46:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergy-to-cinnamaldehyde-and-other-spices.19630/post-215909>

You have a redox problem........sunlight build redox fastest because it rebuilds the sea inside of a cell by allowing mitochondria to make water.

The last few years I noticed that many spices give me a hormonal reaction and I still had to be careful with spices. Synthetic progesterone in birth control is derived from testosterone and testosterone is derived from cinnamaldehyde. What is the relationship with light, water and magnetism to spices and what can I do to improve my allergy to spices?

At your present location take 2-3 months of intensive skin sun conditioning.
Take two months vacations real near equator.
Go there with a bag of cinnamaldehyde.
On second month of vacation eat cinnamaldehyde.
See how that affect you.
If you get no reaction, you know where you want to spend the rest of your life.

\---------------------------------

cinnamaldehyde
Cinnamaldehyde is an organic compound with the formula C6H5CH=CHCHO. Occurring naturally as predominately the trans isomer, it gives cinnamon its flavor and odor.

Thanks Jack, sun feels indeed healing to me. I will do my best to get as much sun as possible. Two months before the skin prick test I was not allowed to get sun exposure otherwise the results would not be accurate, so it makes a lot of sense to heal with sunshine. I did not have all these weird allergies for many years, until I got this hormonal IUD. I have read somewhere (in the chapter on Melasma I guess) that you wrote we cannot take any supplements or herbs in order to heal. I guess for me it will include spices on my food as well.

Thanks for the advice Jan! Unfortunately there is no possibility for me to spend two months near the equator, I will spend as much time outside as possible and go to the beach for a swim as much as possible. I will spend 3 weeks in the south of Italy in July . I am not sure I am willing to take the risk for a bag of cinnamaldehyde. It is about everywhere and difficult to avoid. I started to eat organic food last year only to find out now that cinnamaldehyde is allowed to spray as a fungicide on most of the organic vegetables and fruits. It also is in most personal care products. So I am sure I will get a dose of Cinnamaldehyde anyway every day and therefore it might be the cause of my slow healing process.

Twice that is ok too.

twice daily 16 minute exposure to Red Sperti
while almost touching the lamp.

In September 2016 I was measured at the endocrinologist and again I was 1.76 cm. I have always been 1.74 and was measured during prenancies etc. So this was a real surprise that I had actually grown taller at my age. In December 2015 and September 2016 my hormones were a complete mess. I secrete very high progesterone in my 24 hour urine test, very high cortisol, too high DHEA, too high protein in my urine, SHBG and IGF1 too high. I developed a severe frozen shoulder in December 2015 and it cleared after I stopped eating pork and tomatoes in November 2016 and started to apply topical magnesium. I feel much better now with my hormones, but havent tested anything, only I started to get a rash around my ankles in February, so I got the skin prick test and I think it is a useful piece of my healing puzzle. # Thread: coconut habanero/chipotle cream sauce for fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Coldsnow — 2017-06-13T02:09:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216023>

This is bomb for a fish tacos - I don't specify amounts because intuition I think is crucial
in the kitchen and creates the real magic. (oh I hope this fits into the Epi paleo RX; I think habanero's do)

\-coconut cream (native forest is best)
\- habanero diced thin - go easy and take seeds out
\- chipotle - de seeded
\- ginger (lots)
\- lime juice and skin
\- turmeric - diced
\- sea salt
\- bone broth powder

put in little bullet blender and mix till creamy - great for little dip with chips or
on pescado. Great mayo alternative

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-13T02:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216029>

Sounds quite good. I am going to build it Friday

### caroline — 2017-06-13T03:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216035>

sounds great - thanks for posting Coldsnow.

I have found that I can use my garlic press with fresh numeric ......

bone broth powder = gelatine?

### Coldsnow — 2017-06-13T22:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-habanero-chipotle-cream-sauce-for-fish.19631/post-216098>

### freeheartbk — 2017-06-13T19:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-pregnenolone.19636/post-216084>

Hi all! I have chronic lyme and chronic active EBV, my hormones are totally trashed. Saliva test about a year ago showed very low cortisol throughout the day (as well as low estrogen and even lower progesterone) so I started taking hydrocortisone, which has helped a bit with energy.

I recently had other hormones checked as well and the results were terrible: DHEA-s at 16.5, Pregnenolone is 19 and Testosterone is less than 3. I just ordered Pure Encapsulations DHEA and Pregnenolone and am very excited to start taking them, but am a bit concerned about combining with the hydrocortisone. How would you suggested I titrate the dosages and possibly wean off the HC while going up on the DHEA and Pregnenolone?

### Bonnie — 2018-06-16T06:15:29+0300

I’m healthy 54 yr old. I’m doing Julva (DHEA) and PPr (pregnealone) cream as per Dr Anna Cabecca. I sleep great with it. I do wonder if I am violating the NO SUPPLEMENTS rule. Does anyone have an opinion about that?

### Sue-UK — 2018-06-16T11:10:58+0300

### CatherineRaye — 2018-09-15T02:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-what-says-you-toxic-necessary-evil.19637/post-251008>

We are 100% non-vax, and perfectly happy and comfortable with it. Before I was pregnant with our oldest, a vaccine developer's relative warned me off, so I started researching. He told her never to let anyone she cared about get Gardasil, so she said she didn't even know why she was telling me this because I was too old to get the shot, but repeated his warning to me. That set me off researching.

Our oldest caught whooping cough at about 7 months old. It was scary, but basically we just kept her comfortable and I nursed her non-stop, which I have always nursed them on demand until they choose to wean, anyhow. She was a lot better after about a week, and the residual cough gone within another two weeks. She had also had H1N1 about 4 months before that, and she has never caught another flu since.

We use nosodes, and are perfectly comfortable with that, and have had no problems. People in North America and the UK tend to think they are strange, but in France and Switzerland, they are considered totally normal. You get them from the corner pharmacist, who is trained in pharmaceuticals, herbs, essential oils, and homeopathy. Especially in Switzerland, because pharmacists are so highly trained (and holistically), when one is sick, they usually go to the pharmacist, and only go to a medical doctor if the pharmacist isn't able to assist them first.

My big thing is protecting them from people who are vaxxed and shedding while they are really small.

We have the occasional little bumps in the road, but overall, they are far healthier than most other children. We also do not have wifi or cell phones or a microwave or a (not-so) "smart" meter. We feed them as healthy as possible, but allow them junk once in a while. With the exception of the first year or so of our oldest life, we have only lived in places without fluoride in the water. We homeschool, so they have unlimited outside time to play. All of these are factors, but I feel that vaccines are a big factor in damaging children's developing immune system. Even ignoring all the toxins in the shots, it isn't natural to expose someone to an illness by injection. That is my big ol' vaccine rant.

Regardless, I think the most important thing is for mothers and fathers to exercise their rights to make their own choices for their families.

### freeheartbk — 2017-06-13T22:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trashed-hormones.19638/post-216099>

Hi all! I have chronic lyme and chronic active EBV, my hormones are totally trashed. Saliva test about a year ago showed very low cortisol throughout the day (as well as low estrogen and even lower progesterone) so I started taking hydrocortisone, which has helped a bit with energy.

I recently had other hormones checked as well and the results were terrible: DHEA-s at 16.5, Pregnenolone is 19 and Testosterone is less than 3. I just ordered Pure Encapsulations DHEA and Pregnenolone and am very excited to start taking them, but am a bit concerned about combining with the hydrocortisone. How would you suggested I titrate the dosages and possibly wean off the HC while going up on the DHEA and Pregnenolone?

### kris90 — 2017-06-16T20:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216361>

So the CT is important because it lowers leptin by releasing fatty acids from adipose tissue. Basically, forces fat burning, because electrons from fat will release more heat/light. CT can help get DHA into your cell membranes so you can assimilate light better in your eye. The more light you can assimilate, the less mass you require to make energy (think E=mc2). But you also have to eat seafood in order to provide the DHA. Don't use fish oil pills, get it directly from food.

>

Yes, CT can definitely reduce your carb cravings as you transition into a fat burner. Being a fat burner destroys hunger, because whether you eat or not, your body is constantly tapping into an energy source: fat. Either from your diet (when you eat) or from your adipose tissue. That's how you lose fat and destroy hunger simultaneously by feeding your body itself lol.

As for morning, 4:30 is quite early. Where do you live? Is the sun out when you wake up? If not, I'd recommend not eating until the sun is up, even if you wake up before sunrise.

### kris90 — 2017-06-16T21:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216369>

Ok, so yes, that is a good sign that you aren't hungry when you wake up since the sun is not up. I wouldn't stress over eating within 30 mins of rising, I would moreso just eat as soon as the sun is out, and make sure you get morning sun shining into your eyes prior to eating.

Also, personally, in winter, because I'm up long before the sun, I do CT first thing in the morning. And actually, anyday I know it will be rainy or cloudy and I won't actually see the sunrise, I hop in the shower and do CT. That's what I did today, because it was rainy and dark out this morning.

That's good, keep it up. Thanks, yes we are expecting a boy! Very excited about having a son. I have a daughter now and it's great.

### drezy — 2017-06-16T22:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216380>

@[kris90](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19157/)
Congratulations! Having a little man in the house is going to be fun.

You are one of many people who confirm my low regard for "reference levels" and how they are established in the first place.

@[Billybats](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20191/) kris is certainly your wingman here and doing a great job ushering you into the group.

> but could be water weight but not going to analyze it.

Yeah, don't over analyze at this point. "Use the force" and go by how you feel and your energy levels.

The carb craving is part habit and often larger part environment. As you quell the habit and become more aware of your environment and it's effects it will become easier.

The effects on men and women can differ a little in speediness due to many factors. Women sometimes have to be more patient than us testosterone fueled oxen. Stick with it though, I think it'll payoff. My own wife is almost a decade older than me . She has been doing CT for 3 sessions at ~59F 3 times a week. She complained that she has nothing near my weight loss, but does admit she has:

-   wayyyyyy better sleep
-   more mental clarity
-   higher libido (Thanks Jack!)
-   All the while under pressure of one of the biggest professional projects she's ever taken on. She said "I should be falling apart by now."

Whatever the case, you've got a really great setup with kris keeping an eye on you and some of the changes your mentioned seemed to come easily enough. My money is on you @[Billybats](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20191/) .

Hi everyone,

### kris90 — 2017-06-19T14:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-216534>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=216520)
>
> It is not really about food.

This.

I think Jack uses the BAB as a strategy to manage hunger and re-yolk the peripheral clocks (gut) with the central clock (brain). Your main focus should be on AM sunlight, and just try to get something into you that is high in protein, and some fats. Don't overthink it. Your instincts are what is most important, and AM sunlight = release dopamine so you can increase signal-to-noise ratio and think clearer. Follow your instincts.

As for potatoes, I don't see a problem, but I would cook them in lots of coconut oil or butter. I always add CO/butter to all my carbs. Carbs are much less energy efficient than fats, so when I eat them, I just "fatten" them up with saturated fats. It's like upgrading to a higher-grade fuel.

### kris90 — 2017-08-03T20:51:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thank-you-dr-kruse-and-kris90.19639/post-220854>

Good observations. It's interesting how subtle changes make a world of difference. That's how E=mc^2 works, because the effects of light are exponential on energy, and energy is tied to sense of wellbeing, low cravings, and weight regulation.

One thing I find that helps on those cloudy days is to still get outside and ground when you can, and up your seafood intake and maybe a bit of CT. It really makes up the deficit. It'll cut your carb cravings, and you'll likely continue to lose weight despite not feeling like you yielded much sun. Remember, when we don't have the power of the sun, we can release the light within our cells to make an EZ, and bring the respiratory proteins together to improve electron tunneling. We have the sun's power stored in our cells, just have to release it when the sun is absent. CT is a great way to do this, and load up on seafood to get more semiconducting power to grab what little sun there still is.

# Thread: lemon/dill/cauliflower sauce
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Coldsnow — 2017-06-14T05:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/post-216126>

this one borders on a soup but can easily go into the sauce world. Its my girlfriends
invention; Marisol, so she gets the credit - the sauce muse is speaking

[http://www.marisolkim.com/](http://www.marisolkim.com/)

\-----

a cup of steamed cauliflower
lots of lemon juice, fresh with some zest peal - like a lot of lemon
sea salt
lots of dill
cilantro
diced ginger simmered in a pan of butter with tumeric and black pepper
till things are soft; really soft and you can smell.

When the flavors come together in this one it's like a music; like a stringed section on the Clifford
Brown L.P. - i really love to go overboard with the herbs though

\*i like the dance with the dill on the fish but you can sub for rosemary or really any herb
as the cauliflower is a great vehicle for various flavors

\* you can easily take out the dill and add curry paste and serve on chicken; jus sayin

We really need some simpler ways of doing stuff on the road. Last night we tried something we had heard on the radio.

Place salmon fillets in a bowl and fill with boiling water, cover with saran wrap and leave for ten minutes. It was beautiful. next time I will put some aromatics in the bowl with the salmon. This time I put some coriander stocks in with the salmon.

Then I made a slurry of coriander and basil and coconut aminos and MCT oil and also a tiny bit of honey.

Somehow I have to learn how to live without my magic bullet on the road!

### james coldsnow — 2017-07-30T09:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lemon-dill-cauliflower-sauce.19640/post-220414>

I got some bone broth recipes coming down the pipe also a new array of vegan dishes with andouille sausage and
bison and salmon cuts - gotta keep the vegans on their toes; hell maybe even eat a couple of their toes and let
them know planet earth wasn't always a goji berry wonderland of whole foods outlets everywhere. I got accosted at
our local health food store today for eating bacon by one of those older hippies with an afro of grey hair. Only a vegan
would come up to you and say something as your loading your plate; I mean the nerve. Maybe Jack's new sauce book
could have a chapter on 'how to deal with vegans' maybe even a schematic of the neuro chemicals that are misfiring and
the lack of DHA. We can't blame em; it's probably dry as bone upstairs - why else would anyone turn in their Mongolian leather
wallet for a Velcro wallet; I know people who have done that. Sorry Caroline, that turned into a minor rant; but seriously

# Thread: CSF leaks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JohnnyAZ — 2017-06-14T06:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216131>

I just completed EMF 2 and I got lost at the CSF leak area. It mentioned you can get a leak from sinus surgery (had it twice) but I did not understand how it connected to EMF. I searched the forum for more info on CSF leak and found some info but am still a little lost. Can anyone point me in the right direction?

Also listened to both EMF webinars and the Mito webinar twice.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-14T18:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216166>

Leaking CSF means less water around the brain and water is a natural Faraday cage......less water means less protection from nnEMF

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-14T18:52:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216168>

Most Faraday cages are made of mu metal but biology uses water to do it. This is why CSF has ions in it. Water with ions in it becomes a conductive plasma to electric forces/fields. Faraday cages, also known as Faraday shields, are enclosures of varying sizes and shapes that are made up of highly conducting materials. When an electric field is applied to the exterior of the cage, electrons in the conducting material are displaced so that one side of the cage becomes negatively charged, whereas the opposite side of the cage becomes positively charged. This induced electric polarization cancels out the external electric field almost entirely inside the conductors and, to a large extent, also in the nonconducting parts inside the enclosure, protecting its interior from electromagnetic waves.

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-06-14T18:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-216170>

Got it! Thanks. I am all caught up...for now.

### Jack Thompson — 2019-12-14T05:57:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/csf-leaks.19642/post-283550>

> [JohnnyAZ said:](/goto/post?id=216170)
>
> Got it! Thanks. I am all caught up...for now.

I had bad CSF leak in 2017. Had multiple blood patches and two major spinal surgeries at Duke before I got fixed. I then experienced rebound high pressure which has dissipated but I still have some symptoms like horrible tinnitus which is 100% from the leak, not Nn EMF. Is there anything I can do to help?

### Pogo — 2017-06-15T05:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-focus-on-grammar-and-spelling-over-content.19645/post-216226>

Within the narrow confines of social media, that is fair enough. Particularly so, on Twitter with the minimal message length.

Even on Facebook, the dearth of formatting lends itself to shorter, snappier content. But entirely forgoing use of the <Enter> key is just plain lazy. Exposing the lack of readability or legibility of content, is not "focusing on grammar and spelling".

In blog postings, articles, books etc, there are no excuses for egregious grammatical errors. Language exists for the purpose of communication. Failing to use it properly leads to miscommunication, inconsistency and ambiguity.

# Thread: Are you addicted to the internet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-addicted-to-the-internet.19648/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2017-06-15T09:19:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-addicted-to-the-internet.19648/post-216233>

[What Happens to Your Body When You Use the Internet](http://everlast.mercola.com/r/?id=h79c33496,12c5aae9,131cddff&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118).

This is a great blog by Dr. Mercola. For sure it is all stuff that Dr. K. has been telling us for years now. I was thinking that this format would be great for teenagers to "get it" but it sure struck home with me. I have been trying to spend way less time on my laptop - but I am afraid - my name is Caroline ....and I am an internet junky! It is really hard to limit my time - I always feel like I am missing something! I have been programmed it seems and I hate it!

see what you think......

**By Dr. Mercola**

The internet has made connecting with new business partners, discovering health information and finding long lost friends, easier and cheaper. However, while digital connections have distinct advantages, digital dependence does not. You’ve likely seen — or have been part of — a family outing where one by one, everyone pulls out their phone to check notifications, text messages or email.

It happens in restaurants, on busy streets and commuter trains. The desire to be rewarded by your phone may have even been so great that you endangered your life by attempting to read a text or send one while driving. Toddlers get their own devices to keep them busy and 10-year-old children are carrying their own phones.[1](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn1) Where once children talked on the phone, set up face-to-face time and engaged with real people in real situations, they now spend hours keeping a “streak” alive.

Dependence on digital communication presents several physical and emotional health challenges. Mitochondrial damage, exposure to electromagnetic radiation and failing social skills are just the tip of an iceberg that may have deeper roots than anticipated. Recent research has now identified immediate physical symptoms that occur when your digital devices are just out of reach.

**Digital Dependence Affecting More Than Teens**
Cellphone ownership has reached 95 percent in America, up from 68 percent measured in 2015. Of those owners, 77 percent use a smartphone.[2](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn2) The people who own the smartphones are distributed equally across gender, age and ethnicity, with the lowest number of people owning smartphones being over the age of 65.

Dependence or [addiction to a digital device](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/02/02/are-you-addicted-to-smartphone.aspx) hooked to the internet affected 6 percent of the world population in 2014.[3](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn3) This number may not appear to be significant on the surface, but consider that 6 percent of the world population was over 420 million people.[4](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn4)Comparatively speaking, according to the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime, 3.5 to 7 percent of the world population between 15 and 64 years had used an illicit drug in the past year.[5](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn5)

The percentage of those addicted to the internet may actually be higher as only 39 percent of the world in 2014 had access to the internet,[6](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn6) driving the real percentage of those addicted to 15 percent. Symptoms of addiction are similar to other types of addiction, but are more socially acceptable. The authors of the study found an internet addiction (IA) is:[7](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn7)

_“… \[G\]enerally regarded as a disorder of concern because the neural abnormalities (e.g., atrophies in dorsolateral prefrontal cortex) and cognitive dysfunctions (e.g., impaired working memory) associated with IA mimic those related to substance and behavioral addiction. Moreover, IA is often comorbid with mental disorders, such as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and depression.”_

Reach Out Recovery identifies conditions that may trigger internet addiction or compulsions, including anxiety, [depression](http://articles.mercola.com/depression/what-is-depression.aspx), other addictions, social isolation and stress.[8](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn8) Internet activity may stimulate your brain’s reward system, much like [drugs and alcohol](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/07/28/opioid-painkiller-addiction-conspiracy.aspx), providing a constant source of information and entertainment. While each person’s internet use is different, the results may be the same. Long-term effects may include:

Irritation when someone interrupts your interaction online

Difficulty completing tasks

Increasing isolation

Experiencing euphoria while online

Inability to stop despite the consequences

Increasing stress

**Physical Effects of Internet Withdrawal**
The physical and mental effects of addiction, coupled with the physical effects of withdrawal, may increase your risks for long-term health conditions. In a recent study involving 144 people between the ages of 18 and 33, researchers discovered both heart rate and blood pressure are affected in those who report spending extended periods of time online.[9](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn9)

Past research has associated cold turkey withdrawal of the internet from heavy users will produce anxiety type symptoms, similar to those experienced by people addicted to drugs or alcohol.[10](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn10) The current study also linked physiological changes, including an average of a 3 to 4 percent [increase in blood pressure](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/11/30/how-to-lower-blood-pressure.aspx) and heart rate of the participants.[11](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn11) Some participants experienced up to an 8 percent increase.

This was the first controlled demonstration of physiological changes triggered by internet use.[12](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn12) The increases noted during the study were not enough to be immediately life-threatening; however, these types of changes are associated with anxiety and a reduction in the function of the immune system.

The changes in anxiety levels may also be a physiological trigger for users to re-engage with their digital devices in order to reduce the physical response and anxiety level. Dr. Lisa Osborne, co-author of the study from Swansea University, commented:[13](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn13)

_“A problem with experiencing physiological changes like increased heart rate is that they can be misinterpreted as something more physically threatening, especially by those with high levels of anxiety, which can lead to more anxiety, and more need to reduce it.”_

In other words, especially in people who may experience anxiety more frequently, the physical symptoms of internet withdrawal may increase their anxiety and lead to behaviors to reduce it — namely, going back to using the internet.

**Putting Down Your Phone May Raise Your Anxiety Level**
Forty percent of the participants in this study admitted they had some level of an internet-related problem and acknowledged they spent too much time online. Participants reportedly spent an average of five hours each day on the internet and 20 percent spent over six hours a day. By far the most common reasons for engaging online were social media and shopping.

Previous studies from this same group of researchers have demonstrated study participants would experience short-term increases in anxiety levels when their digital devices were removed.[14](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn14) When those devices were removed for longer periods of time, they reported increases in loneliness and depression, with some researchers finding changes to the actual structures in the brain.

Research psychologist Larry Rosen, Ph.D., and his colleagues at California State University looked at the effect technology has had on our anxiety levels. They have found the typical person checks their phone every 15 minutes, whether or not they heard a notification from the device. In his words you may be thinking:[15](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn15)

_“Gee, I haven’t \[checked\] in \[on\] Facebook in a while. I haven’t checked on this Twitter feed for a while. I wonder if somebody commented on my Instagram post.”_

These thoughts generate increased secretion of cortisol, which begins to increase your anxiety levels. Eventually, you notice the rising anxiety and seek a way to reduce the experience. Checking in to your social network on your phone may be one of the ways you’ve found to reduce your anxiety.

The authors of the study from Swansea University speculate that internet use is driven by more than short-term excitement or the joy of using [technology](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2015/04/01/wearable-technology-devices.aspx). Instead, it may produce negative physiological and psychological changes, such as anxiety that may drive you back to the device that is causing the problem in the first place.[16](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn16)

Multiple studies from around the world have demonstrated overuse of the internet and digital devices leads to physical and psychological symptoms of addiction[17](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn17) and family dysfunction.[18](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn18) Poor health, unhappiness and depression were found in men and women who report overuse of the internet, but depressed girls demonstrate a higher rate of internet addiction than boys.[19](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn19) Overall, those with an addiction to the internet have lower impulsivity control.[20](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn20)

**Google Would Like You to Keep On Using**
It should come as no surprise that companies that make money when more people spend more time and money on the internet are consciously trying to manipulate your behavior. Former Google product manager Tristan Harris revealed how digital giants are engineering smartphone apps and social media feedback to get you checking and double-checking online.[21](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn21)

However, while internet use is more socially acceptable, digital companies aren’t the only businesses using neurological and psychological strategies to increase their profit margins.[22](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn22) Behavior patterns are often etched into neural pathways,[23](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn23) and when those behaviors are also linked to hormone secretion and physiological responses, they become even more powerful.

In fact, Harris describes the reward process of using a smartphone as “playing the slot machine.”[24](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn24) And, Google has discovered a way to embed that [reward system](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2014/02/06/hugging.aspx) as you use the apps on your phone. This process is so important to digital corporations that Apple turned down a new smartphone app for their store that would help people to reduce their use of the internet and their smartphones.

The goal of any corporation is to increase your use of their product and the potential you will spend more money with them. In the case of smartphone devices, these companies are contributing to programing your actions, and how you think and feel. This is how companies satisfy their advertisers, who are paying for the privilege of your eyes on their ads.

**Have You Been ‘Brain Hacked?’**
Some programmers call this process “brain hacking,” as they incorporate more information from neuropsychology into the development of digital interfaces that increase your interaction with the program. For instance, getting likes on Facebook and Instagram, the “streaks” on Snapchat or cute emojis on text messaging, are all designed to increase your engagement and desire to return.

The continual scroll on [Facebook](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/01/05/facebook-negative-health-effects.aspx) keeps you engaged on the page longer, with a greater chance you’ll click an advertisement on the page. Keeping a “streak” alive on Snapchat keeps you coming back to the app, especially when you have multiple streaks going with multiple people.

Harris describes it as a race to the bottom of the brainstem where fear and anxiety live, two of the most powerful motivators known to advertisers. Both advertisers and computer software developers are using these techniques to write code that will engage your attention.[25](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2017/06/14/digital-dependence.aspx?utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=art2&utm_campaign=20170614Z3&et_cid=DM147609&et_rid=2042836118#_edn25)

**More Physical Effects From Your Digital** ​

# Thread: Epiphany about hormones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epiphany-about-hormones.19649/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-06-15T16:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epiphany-about-hormones.19649/post-216267>

My most recent area of interest is hormone levels; that is because I myself have had low hormone levels, which some (including myself) may have considered suboptimal, however I did not have symptoms, and have functioned well despite the levels.

I think I have just come to an epiphany, so I just wrote this thought down:

"Food controls hormone levels. Light controls hormone function".

Am I correct? And if so, what is more important, your hormone levels, or your hormone function? That proves light trumps food. Think about leptin: it increases when we eat food. That is because food provides electrons (and photons from the sun that created it via photosynthesis). So technically, one would think we want to increase our leptin levels, because leptin shuts off hunger in the brain, and increases the metabolic rate via signalling the thyroid. But we all know the key is to be leptin sensitive. It is about being able to activate the leptin receptor in the brain, and that is why light is the key (the leptin molecule is full of photon traps).

I absolutely know I am very leptin sensitive based on how well I thrive and function and how intact my circadian rhythm is, and that food doesn't matter for me: i.e. I can eat 1,500 calories and feel satisfied, or I can stuff my face at a family gathering and eat 4,000 calories, and activate uncoupling so I release the excess as heat, and do not store fat. I attribute this metabolic flexibility under the control of an optimally functioning leptin receptor to my cold adaptation, fasting and abiding by the natural light cycle.

Now the hormone in question it testosterone. Again, it also has photon traps, and so if I have a suprasensitive leptin receptor because I yield a lot of energy from my environment, and less from food (thus keeping leptin levels low assuming food controls hormone levels), then having low testosterone levels, or any low hormone levels should not matter. When you maximize function of something, less is more.

Perhaps E=mc2 can fit into this, where E = energy/function, M = hormone, and c2 = light.

If we can increase c2 (light), then we can increase energy (function) without increasing mass (hormone). But on the contrary, when decrease c2 (light), then we must increase mass (hormone) in order to increase energy (function). This is likely why the food gurus have it all wrong.

Thoughts?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Dysautonomia- i messed something up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dysautonomia-i-messed-something-up.19657/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2017-06-17T08:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dysautonomia-i-messed-something-up.19657/post-216411>

I'm not even certain if this is because of dysautonomia but i think it is.
I do everything pretty much right- sun early morning and evening, no emf, if i need to use my phone at night i have a red filter on it, grounding several hours/day and only drink spring water from a spring that i fill from. The other day, after a coffee enema, i was craving salt so i added a little to my water.
Within minutes, my dissociation and light sensitivity thinned out a little for the first time in 4 years, which was amazing! But a host of crappy effects came as well; i got immediatly fluish, headache, diarrhea, kidney pain, peeing non stop and i became emotionally volitale. I'm talking, blubbering like a teenage girl at a Hanson concert.
My guess was the coffee enema dehydrated me past a certain point and now my dysautanomia is all out of whack? I've been messing with balancing salt and potassium the past couple days but it's really tough to get the ratio right. Too much salt, or even too much pure water and im back to emotional basket case, peeing and fluishness within seconds. Also, i get incredibly hungry only _after_ eating now.
Im going to start taking electrolytes again that my doctor used to give me but i don't think that's any panacea. What did i do? And is there any way i can get back to the thinner dissociation without all the side effects? That would be a blessing, at this point.

### JanSz — 2017-06-17T14:20:27+0300

# Thread: Hey, can adrenaline situations increse dopamine?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-can-adrenaline-situations-increse-dopamine.19658/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erik — 2017-06-17T14:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-can-adrenaline-situations-increse-dopamine.19658/post-216417>

Ive always been afraid to fight, since i was a kid... The other day, i went to the night club with my brother, who got really drunk and some guys knew him and hated him because of some girl issue, so they hit him in the face really hard and i took him to the bathroom to help him clean and everything, i was so scared,,,, but then, when we were about to leave the place, those 3 guys came again and hit him and my brother fell to the ground like a brick wall, so something woke up inside of me,... i just jumped on the hitting guy and smashed his face with all of my fury... but i may have hit him 5 or 6 times and his siblings kicked my face and my stomach and i fell to the floor also, but the security guards took us all out of the club... In the rest of the night and in the other day, even i was hurt, i felt incredibly well, strong and proud of myself... thats not all, i had no hangover at all, and my immflamation was even down (which i measure with my rosacea)... and that effect lasted maybe 4 days lol.... could it be an immense raise in dopamine which had this effect???

### Erik — 2017-06-17T14:43:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-can-adrenaline-situations-increse-dopamine.19658/post-216418>

Maybe fighting increases an acentral pahtways in our blood that has healing properties or something like that?? (like hunting or going to war or protecting our loved ones, like our mother would protect us from anyting...)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-17T18:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-can-adrenaline-situations-increse-dopamine.19658/post-216439>

**UV and IR light** and cold are a medical solution to a medical problem.

Cold water immersion at 14°C increased metabolic rate by 350%, norepinephrine by 530% & dopamine by 250%. Wow. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/)

# Thread: Leo's journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leo cosen — 2017-06-17T16:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/post-216430>

Hi everyone!

I am so pleased to have joined this forum. I started out with bulletproof diet 6 months ago and soon left it aside getting into Jack Kruse's amazing wealth of experience and understanding.

I am Swiss French born by lake Geneva , I am 27, cadet of a family of 4 children, my grandmother died in her mid'80s and my mother is in her late 50s. Both never had health issues but did and do have thin hair and a lot of skin tags and wrinkles.

I moved to London when I was 19 and have been here for over 8 years now.

**Brief background:**
I was very anxious as a teenager and played computer games (semi-pro) from age 11 till 18.
Drank 2L of ice tea a day
Ate 2x packs of biscuits a day
Smoke 5-10 rollies a day

**Life in the present tense, after having read some of Jack kruse's literature over the past few months, looks like this:**

Energy levels are great along with creativity and motivation to do things.. but also be still and quiet!

Very athletic and in shape (185cm / 67 kgs / 9% body fat (doing 15mn HIIT in park barefoot 3x a week- no more gym).

I work as a yoga, gong sound bath facilitator, meditation teacher and hold retreats,etc so lucky to be able to spent lots of time without artificial light. If I do it's on my iPhone and I make sure I'm outside.

\-I go outside everyday to catch morning sun for up to 2 hours
\-I'm doing cold baths for 10-20mns every night before bed (sleep about 7.5hrs)
\-got a reptile red infrared lamp (250W) which I sometimes use at night but I'm not sure whether it's the right one to use. I also have an LED laser that usually comes with security cameras.
\-I eat full keto during winter and have started adding baked sweet potatoes every other day at night accompanied by grass fed organic raw butter or single cream
\-I eat lots of sardines and mackerel and anchovies and rock oysters
\-2L of still spring water in glass bottles (Highland)
\-I take all the mito supplements advised including shilajit (just started)
\-I eat two Meals a day now (early am usually 7 or 8) and then round 5pm
\-I have earthing sandals / walk barefoot a lot and get about 4-5 hours outside daily. This sadly is made more difficult when it rains in London and it rains a hell of a lot. So summer is usually a relief for me.

\-I also have blue light blocking glasses and a filter on my screen in addition to flux (does it really generate a flicker?)

**The things that I'd like to work on are:
**
Thin coating on my tongue which isn't always there now that ive changed my way of living.
\-Get rid of floaters which I have in both eyes
\-increase my ability to absorb nutrients and water.
\-Regrow my hair in places (I seem to have come "scalp to scalp" with male pattern baldness)

Many thanks for reading and for any tips.

P.S: I'm thinking of moving back to Switzerland to live in the mountains. I also really like the south of Spain where I teach a lot of yoga retreats.

Blessings and mito hugs to all
Leo

### Mito1 — 2017-06-19T21:34:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leos-journey.19659/post-216562>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=216549)
>
> I have floaters in my eyes (also myopic). What are you doing to target these specifically?

Retinal dopaminergic amacrine (DA) cells synthesize both dopamine and GABA.

If a retinal dopamine neuron can not clear GABA all the time under light, you will see an eye floater all the time when you expose your eyes to light.

The eye floater size depends on how many DA cells get involved.

.

### Micka — 2017-06-18T08:13:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/micks-labs.19661/post-216470>

Hi there! Longtime kruse listener, first time poster.

I was wanting to get some insight on some recent labs that I have run recently. Did a basic blood panel and DUTCH test, which i’ll attach for reference but below are the main points. If anyone have any recommendations or suggestions further to the advice provided by the supporting report, i'd be very grateful.

27 year old Male
Ocean Grove, VIC, Australia (38.25\*S, 144.51\*E)
187cm, ~95kg, 22% BF (Dexa)

Blood Panel

HBA1c - 5.1% - 32 mmol/mol (Ref <7.1%, <54mmol/mol)

Reverse T3 - 202 pmol/L (Ref range 170-450)

OH-Vit D - 102 nmol/L (Ref >50)

HSCRP - <0.10 mg/L (Ref 0-10.00)

FreeT4 - 15.8 pmol/L (Ref 10.0-19.0)

TSH 1.71 mIU/L (Ref 0.50-4.00)

Free T3 - 5.6 pmol/L (Ref 3.5-6.5)

Fasting Blood Glucose 5.2 mmol/L (Ref 4.0-6.0)

Total Cholesterol- **6.5 mmol/L** (Ref <4.0)

Triglyceride- 0.9 mol/L (Ref <2.0)

HDL-C 1.5 mmol/L (Ref > 1.0)

LDL-C **4.6 mmol/L** (Ref <2.0)

Non HDL-C **5.0 mmol/L** (<2.5)

Chol/HDL- 4.3

DUTCH test (main points - see attached report)

Progesterone markers are high

DHEAs and metabolites are high

Higher preference for 5a reductase

**E1, E2 and E3 are all high**

Methylation activity is low

Preference for cortisone metabolites

My main concerns are the estrogen pathways.
Second concern is the LDL/nonHDL panels. Should I be concerned about these considering there’s no correlating increase in total TG’s and HSCRP?

For some background, my home and work environments are reasonable. I’m eating seasonally, within a time restricted feeding window of 6-8 hours, during daylight hours. No wifi at home, blue blocking, good sleep, plenty of spring/filtered water, barefoot beach walks and cold-adapting swim sessions.

Any insights would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!
