Sometimes we don’t choose our path. It chooses us. Kristian Olaf Bernhard Birkeland was a Norwegian scientist. He is best remembered as the person whose theories of atmospheric electric currents elucidated the nature of the aurora borealis. He understood the sun better than most scientists, in my opinion. His genius was buried in his work on ammonia and linked to electric arcs. In order to fund his research on the aurorae, he invented the electromagnetic cannon and the of fixing nitrogen from the air. Modern science, especially cosmology and astrophysics have no idea how brilliant either of these pursuits were. The fortunate accidents for many scientists and clinicians that came after him. Some of them have realized that buried in his work come some amazing perspective of how stars and mitochondria do things in similar fashion. Birkeland was nominated for the Nobel Prize seven times. He never won it once. That is another scientific tragedy. Today, science still has no idea how brilliant this man was. He was driven to the grave by his own work. He avoided nature and became unable to sleep and used barbituates to try to continue on. He was unsuccessful, like many with sleep disorders. The ultimate irony, is his work contained the answer to his sleeplessness. He never found that Rx. People who benefited from his work have and have decided to carry to fruition. Birkeland proposed in 1908 in his book The Norwegian Aurora Polaris Expedition 1902–1903 that polar electric currents, today referred to as auroral electrojets, were connected to a system of currents that flowed along geomagnetic field lines into and away from the polar region. Such field-aligned currents are known today as Birkeland currents in his honor. Those currents, I believe hold the entire key to the universe and how biology of living things connects to it. His hidden brilliance is hidden in his work on the Birkeland-Eyde process. This process was buried in science because it was not economically exploitable. Fritz Haber perfected the process, and brought in the era of nitrogen based fertilizer. His method of creation turned out to be quite economical and profitable. His work had many unintended consequences and ultimate have led us to corporations like Monsanto and glyphosate. Birkeland used an electrical arc that was easily formed between two coaxial electrodes, and through the use of a strong magnetic field (pinch). The electric plasma, when created in this way, was spread out into a thin disc of plasma. This plasma disc is seen in star creation as well. The plasma temperature in the Birkeland disc was in excess of 3000°C. Air, from the Earth's atmosphere, was blown through this arc, causing some of the nitrogen to react with oxygen forming nitric oxide. By carefully controlling the energy of the electric arc and the velocity of the air stream, yields of up to 4% nitric oxide were obtained. What people still fail to realize is this is the process that is critical to the nitrogen cycle on this planet for everything that uses chlorophyll as a pigment to collect light and make a DC electric current from water. The electric arc is so strong that is can break the triple bond of Nitrogen. The irony is that a leaf uses a 3D cage around a magnesium ion to create an electric arc from water using sunlight. This process is critical to the creation of the entire food web on Earth. The process is extremely energy intensive and this is why it was not economical, profitable, and buried by scientists and businessman, but little do they know that this science is critical to understand life for a mitochondriac. The colony of mitochondrion in our tissues harness and reverse the process that occurs in a leaf to make water from a mitochondria and bury massive electric arc charge within it. This makes the water made by a mitochondria and electric plasma. This water is not similar to your tap water at all. This water is one of the greatest secrets of life. Birkeland used a nearby hydroelectric power station for the electricity as this process demanded about 15 MWh/Ton of nitric acid. Living things use the power of the sun which is significantly greater and cheap. In fact, it is free. The rate of energy capture by plant and animal photosynthesis is immense by hemoglobin and chlorophyll. Living things get 100 trillion watts (1 trillion watts = 1 terra watt (TW)). This power is ten times the current power consumption of the human race. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to that power station in chronic fashion. Perhaps these facts are why Big Pharma would like to bury the sun in our modern world??? They have to protect the franchise from the real science of nature because its energy is free when one knows how to use it. The same reaction, Birkeland found is carried out by lightning, providing a natural source for converting atmospheric nitrogen to soluble nitrates. Nobody seems to connect the fibers that weave nature's quilt. Some of us however, observe better than we see reality in textbooks. Genius always strives to answer questions the ordinary forgot to pose. Being in medicine today and believing that "knowing cellular biochemistry and not being able to connecting it to anything else in nature, like sunlight, is akin to buying a guitar that Prince once owned and then expecting that you could play Purple Rain as he did in life............That is the absurdity of modern science today. Nobody observes science from this perspective today who teaches clinicians. Why would any patient expect their doctor to "know" this perspective? Instead the paradigm creates the meme than any alternative perspective on things is quackery. Your perspective on people comes from the cage you ARE currently held captive in. Wise folks understand there no facts, only "present interpretations" of the data collected and it is subject to how it was obtained. This is why one person's version of "crazy" is another person's reality. This makes intuition and insanity relative. It depends on "who" has "who" locked in 'what cage'. So why am I taking this moment to teach you a bit about Birkeland? Once you learn about his work you'll begin to be able to clearly question what is at the fundamental core of life. Light, water, and magnetism. Why is the direction and flow of time central to human experience, but why isn’t it central to physics? The answer is buried in Birkeland's currents. It also gives the answer to modern patients' ills. Unconventional does not mean the Rx does not work. Unconventional means the Rx has not made it into conventional textbooks.............yet. **Pantothenic acid**, also called **vitamin B5** (a [B vitamin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B_vitamin)), is a [water-soluble](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solubility) [vitamin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vitamin). Pantothenic acid is an [essential nutrient](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Essential_nutrient). Animals require pantothenic acid in order to synthesize [coenzyme-A](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coenzyme-A) (CoA), as well as to synthesize and metabolize [proteins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein), [carbohydrates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbohydrate), and [fats](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fat). The [anion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anion) is called **pantothenate**.
Pantothenic acid is the [amide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amide) between [pantoic acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantoic_acid) and [β-alanine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beta-Alanine).
\-------------------
@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
@[Debntx](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19762/)

2\. A craniosacral therapist once made the claim of an immune system reset after 3 days of continuous grounding.
3\. 72 hour fast
4\. Being around children all day, like a teacher or daycare worker, but in a natural setting. When I am coaching flag football I get a boost just being around the kids. They are not concerned w/ the stressful world we lived in... they are concerned only w/ the group that is there at that moment. They really live for the moment. Consequently I cease to worry about external issues Their energy and mindset is very stress relieving.
5\. Days w/o wearing glasses. In my estimation there are 2 main reasons I feel better when doing this. #1 Obviously more UV/IR into eyes and periorbital tissue. #2 My glasses are titanium... no Bueno for obvious reasons. I've ordered a new pair on line that are solely plastic (so I was told).
6\. Freediving

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-13T01:08:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/give-me-your-top-50-bio-hacks-youd-like-to-see-for-a-project-i-am-doing.19668/post-225705>

A neurologist finds peace and happiness in the feeling of constant acceleration in NATURE under the sun adjacent to the ocean to stop being an asshole. I know a few neurologists/neurosurgeons who need a pair of skates.............or need to buy a farm and do what they want on that farm........Would you take on this hack on if you could? Can you afford not do it? People with big lifestyles don't realize just how exxpensive poor health or a poor atitude is until they realize they are that person. Being sick is the most expensive thing I know of in life truthfully. Would you stop your life abruptly and reinvent yourself to build your dopamine bank account up by exercising the receptor cells in many of your senses to rebuild your neocortex to become a new person????? Sounds crazy, doesn't it? Can humans, using light, balance, touch, hearing, and taste, incorporate motion to expand consciousness by improving mitochondrial functioning? When you learn about how mitochondria work in different systems the "hacks" you can come up with are amazing. As your mitochondria make more water and CO2 they create a pure fuel source of protons that can "seed" your neocortical mitochondria to fuel conscious expansion because mitochondrial heteroplasmy lowers. Nature is simple if we just get out of our own way and get back to the basics. You have to see the sunlight and exercise the senses to reconnect with your natural instincts................This will be a large discussion for my member event coming this December and January because I've taken on this hack on now.............How I will be doing it is very different, but I expect this one to be epic ride of learning and discovery.

"Having spent decades as an affluent but ‘typical, institutionalized, educated Western man’, the neurologist John Kitchin MD radically reassessed his life after finding that he was losing his eyesight and had grown unsatisfied in his work. Emerging from a bout of hopelessness with the realisation that all he wanted to do was ‘the basic things and skate’, he gave up his practice. Now a minor celebrity on San Diego’s Pacific Beach boardwalk, Kitchin – or Slomo, as he’s become known – practices an idiosyncratic, seemingly slow-motion style of inline skating that doubles as meditation. A charming and light-hearted vision of what can happen when you actually do what you want to, Slomo (2013) won dozens of awards upon its release, including Best Short Documentary at the SXSW Film Festival." [https://aeon.co/videos/a-neurologis...iness-in-the-feeling-of-constant-acceleration](https://aeon.co/videos/a-neurologist-finds-peace-and-happiness-in-the-feeling-of-constant-acceleration)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-songs.19669/post-223160>

Fur die german members ein bischen deutsch:
@[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) @[Queen of Elves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18728/) @[Theka](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17862/) @[Butters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9350/) @[Verena1028](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19511/)

1...2...3...4...5...6...7...8...9 und aus

Alle warten auf das Licht
fürchtet euch, fürchtet euch nicht
die Sonne scheint mir aus den Augen
sie wird heut nacht nicht untergehen

... und die Welt zählt laut bis zehn

eins... hier kommt die Sonne
zwei... hier kommt die Sonne
drei... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
vier... hier kommt die Sonne

Die Sonne scheint mir aus den händen
kann verbrennen kann euch blenden
wenn sie aus den Fäusten bricht
legt sich heiss auf das Gesicht

Sie wird heut Nacht nicht untergehen
und die Welt zählt laut bis zehn

eins... hier kommt die Sonne
zwei... hier kommt die Sonne
drei... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
vier... hier kommt die Sonne

fünf... hier kommt die Sonne
sechs... hier kommt die Sonne
sieben... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
acht... hier kommt die Sonne

Die Sonne scheint mir aus den Händen
kann verbrennen, kann dich blenden
Wenn sie aus den Fäusten bricht
legt sich heiss auf dein Gesicht
legt sich schmerzend auf die Brust
das Gleichgewicht wird zum Verlusst

läßt dich hard zu Boden gehen
und die Welt zählt laut bis zehn

eins... hier kommt die Sonne
zwei... hier kommt die Sonne
drei... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
vier... und wird nie vom Himmel fallen

fünf... hier kommt die Sonne
sechs... hier kommt die Sonne
sieben... sie ist der hellste Stern von allen
acht... hier kommt die Sonne

1...2...3...4...5...6...7...8...9 and out

All waiting for the light
have fear, fear not
the sun shines out of my eyes
she will not go down tonight

... and the world counts loud to ten

one... here comes the sun
two... here comes the sun
three... she's the brightest star of all
four... here comes the sun

the sun shines out of my hands
can burn, can blind you
if she breaks out of my fists
lie hot on your face

she would not go down to night
and the world counts loud to ten

one... here comes the sun
two... here comes the sun
three... she's the brightest star of all
four... here comes the sun

five... here comes the sun
six... here comes the sun
seven... she is the brightest star of all
eight... here comes the sun

the sun shines out of my hands
can burn, can blind you
when she breaks out of my fists
lie hot on your face
lays painful on your chest
the balance come to loss

lets you go down hard
and the world counts loud to ten

one... here comes the sun
two... here comes the sun
three... she's the brightest star of all
four... and will never fall from heaven

five... here comes the sun
six... here comes the sun
seven... she is the brightest star of all
eight... here comes the sun

This song was originally going to be used as heavyweight champion Vitali Klitschko’s entrance music, but was never used. The demo name of the song was Klitschko. Even without the use as Klitschko’s entrance music, the song still references boxing through the verses and the counting to ten, as a referee does for a potential KO. @[Victor Mifsud](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19152/)

This is the same JC I drive by with animals who have a massive cell tower 5 feet away from them. Ended up purchasing from my local farmers market and haven't had any problems eating them raw since.

# Thread: Frankie's optimal journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Francesca Sathan — 2017-06-21T15:19:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216707>

I've been building up the courage to start this journal and to finally write down my health history. I guess I've assumed an ostrich like "head in the sand" position in relation to my health over the last few years as I've been too busy juggling work and young children. However, a trip to my GP in December has opened up a Pandora's box of problems which has given me the wake up call I needed. So, deep breath......here goes.

I'm a 47 year old woman of Italian descent, living in the UK. I have two children 6 & 9 years old. Here is a summary of my medical history in order of diagnosis:

10 yrs - fall resulting in recurrent, intermittent back pain
22 yrs - Anemia
24 yrs - PCOS -
took Dianette
25 yrs - 2 herniated discs - L4/L5/SI
29 yrs - Discetomy (both discs)
32 yrs - High Blood Pressure - taken off Dianette
36 yrs - 2 miscarriages
38 yrs - Birth of my son. Gestational diabetes controlled with food. Induced and my son was born with cord wrap and jaundice. I had a post partum hemorrhage. Awful birth - amazing son!
40 yrs - Birth of my daughter. Gestational diabetes controlled with metformin. Induced but much easier birth. Equally amazing daughter.
40 - 47 yrs - Always tired. Overweight. Constant headaches. Constipation. Anemia.
47 years (Pandora's box) - Diverculitis, Gallstones, High Blood Pressure (taking Ramapril), loss of hearing in right ear. Discovered Dr. Jack Kruse.

So this is the year when I decided to turn my health around. I look at my mum who needed to take a small suitcase of medication on holiday with her on Sunday and I know I need to sort my health out now. I owe it to my children to make sure I'm fit and healthy till I'm an old lady.

The first steps in my journey:
1) Acutely aware that I was spending all day in the office or in the car, I have changed my working conditions so that I work from home two days a week. I work sitting in the garden taking in as much sunlight as possible. I've stopped wearing my sunglasses although need prescription glasses to work on my laptop.

2) I take my dog and sometimes a child out for a walk at 6am and walk barefoot in the park. I gaze at the sun and meditate for 5 minutes. Today I cycled to school with the children and we're spending as much time outdoors and barefoot as possible.

3) I'm switching off the wi-fi at night and when we're not using it. I'm wearing blue-blockers once the sun goes down to watch the TV.

4) I'm going to a yoga class once a week and doing yoga at home when I can.

Next, I plan to look into structured water. Although I have been eating paleo on and off for the last two years, I need to revisit what I'm eating. I haven't eaten meat since 25 yrs old so it's quite restricting and expensive to always eat seafood. I really need to move to the seaside!

Well that's me. It's been therapeutic writing this and hope to report progress in my next installment.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-21T16:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216719>

welcome. tell us more about Mom and grandmother

### Francesca Sathan — 2017-06-21T16:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216721>

Thank you Jack for replying. My mum is 75. I suppose she had a similar history to me when she was younger. Anemic, 3 miscarriages and then 3 children. She worked the night shift at local hospital for over 20 years as well as looking after us three. She was depressed and on medication for it in her 40s and then went onto have a hysterectomy after a prolapse and then bladder surgery. She now has osteoporosis and has broken her leg/hip twice, degenerative spine with small fractures. I think she takes calcium? Also high blood pressure and a heart murmur. She's been overweight since my brother (child no 3) was born. My maternal grandmother also had osteoporosis and had kyphosis when she was older. She died after a stroke in her 70s but I don't know much more. My dad's family are much healthier with my paternal grandmother living to the ripe old age of 96. But I have a sneaking suspicion that it's my maternal family which will have a bearing on my own health?

### JanSz — 2017-06-22T02:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frankies-optimal-journey.19671/post-216787>

We are always inheriting our haplotype (and mitochondria) form mother.
Over time, over number of generations mitochondria adjusts to location it finds itself in.
At this time it is mostly about latitude.
So if your mother and number of your grand-mothers stayed in approximately same latitude, that is also your optimal latitude.

I did my analysis here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-73#post-216580](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-73#post-216580)

it turns out that over last 10000 years my grandmothers traveled mostly E-W, and very little NS.
And my current location is one degree south of where my my grandmother was 10000 years ago.

# Thread: Incarcerated 50 year old with high anxiety
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### the lovely brenda — 2017-06-21T19:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/post-216738>

My 53 year old brother is incarcerated for life without parole and has been there for 4 years. He had an adverse reaction to adderall while drunk with his girlfriend of 8 years. They were both alcoholics and had a fight. She was screaming at him and he has a psychosis episode due to the mixture of adderall and alcohol and stabbed her to death as a way to shut her up. She was the director of the battered women's shelter for the local county and he was an electrical engineer. Because of her position and this heinous crime of passion, women from all over who never met him called for him to never see the outside world again.

He is locked in a 6x9 foot cell and gets a bit of sunlight every few weekends first thing in the AM or in the evening after 6PM. This only occurs a few months out of the year because we live in Wisconsin. This has been going on for the past 3 years. The first year he went 9 months without seeing the light of day.

He is college educated and has always been an amazing family man. He is having issues with anxiety and is surrounded by extremely loud thugs in a maximum security prison who have little social skills to function in society. He has stopped eating more than once per day when the guards bring him a meal. He has lost his drive to leave his bed and he has requested that our family stop seeing him. When he knows he has a visitation coming up he has panic attacks and can not handle it.

I am wondering if this is a Vitamin D and lack of sunlight issue? I will be contacting the prison to request some labs to be taken on him and I would like some recommendations. He has been begging for anti anxiety medications but I worry about the side effects. It is difficult to see a psychiatrist in his maximum security prison and he has been waiting around two weeks for an appointment. He attempted suicide during the second year of incarceration and ended up in a coma.

I have sent him breathing exercise hand outs but he wants a magical pill or to be left alone to sleep. He has a long time to sit and think about this. His girlfriend was the love of his life and he misses her along with our family and his freedom.

Any recommendations will be helpful.

Thanks!

You have a deep and caring heart. I worry about you and would be game when you drop in tidbits concerning your brother.

Mind and body are equally important.

### Huck — 2017-06-23T16:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/post-216893>

>
> Dopamine depletion = poor quality of mind control fuckery. The more they keep them in the cells the worse they get. Put them in tent city Oklahoma and you will get a more docile prisoner.

It puts a new spin on Sheriff Joe Arpaio's tent city.

> Prior to the election of Sheriff [Joe Arpaio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Arpaio) in 1993, the prisoner population in [Maricopa County](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maricopa_County) Jail, [Arizona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arizona), the 4th largest jail system in the world,[\[39\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tent_city#cite_note-39) exceeded the maximum number of inmates allowed in its facilities. Prisoners were routinely released from custody prior to completing their sentence due to the overcrowding. In a study conducted in 1993 it was estimated that construction of a new facility would cost approximately $70,000,000.\[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed)_\] Sheriff Arpaio, concerned about the cost of a new facility and reasoning that military tents were good enough for the men and women of the U.S. armed forces who fought in the [Gulf War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gulf_War), ordered that a tent jail be constructed using inmate labor.[\[40\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tent_city#cite_note-40) It consisted of [Korean War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korean_War)\-era tents donated by the [United States armed forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_armed_forces), and a 50 ft (15.4 m) [observation tower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Observation_tower) with a vacancy sign mounted on the front. The final cost of the project was approximately $100,000 and it is capable of housing over 2,400 inmates. [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tent_city#Maricopa_County_Jail_modification)

>
> Good news there: plenty of sun
> Bad news there: 120 degree heat and I didn't see an oyster bar in any of the pictures of that prison.

I was sleeping on this kind of bunk beds, 4 years in high school then 5.5 in college dormitory.

..

### Brent Patrick — 2017-06-25T11:41:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incarcerated-50-year-old-with-high-anxiety.19672/post-217048>

Yes I work as a correctional Officer in a Maximum Security Correctional Facility , your best bet for your Brother is to have his security classification graded to an acceptable level where he is given more options to where he can be placed within the Gaol and hopefully be transferred to a prison farm.This all comes down to mental health and behaviour of Inmate.
Prison Farm is a positive solution given by the members above as your Brother will have access to Sunlight ,magnetism (bare earth) and copious amounts of Water. The seafood may be an issue as Dr kruse mentioned above and is right on the money "**When you lose control of your environment based upon your choices you are subject to their choices and those outcomes."JK
**
You mention Brenda
"I am wondering if this is a Vitamin D and lack of sunlight issue?"
"He has a long time to sit and think about this. His girlfriend was the love of his life and he misses her along with our family and his freedom".

This is a problem ,but ultimately the issue for your Brother is it could be that he hasn't come to terms with his situation and the loss of his loved one and wanting to commit suicide is testimony to this, I see this very often in inmates I deal with. Taking ownership and accepting of the situation he is in , is how he is going to crawl out of the dark hole he currently in. Medication is going to turn him into a Zombie, if he has a unsympathetic Psych he will be drugged up to his eyeballs so he will not be problematic for staff within the correctional Centre. This is a really bad situation to be in as Dr kruse mentioned "**make humans docile and more controllable' JK
**
Best solution is for him to make appointments to see support staff such as Welfare ,Chaplain (if Religious) and Psychologist to express his concerns especially regarding Yard Time (Sun).Inmates who survive in Gaol are those who are proactive and Vocal until they are takin notice of my staff. You must remember a Inmate is just one of thousands in a Gaol .Your Brother needs to make noise but in a smart way.
The Sun is a major problem I have personally seen dozens of inmates over the years take to their arm with a razor blade due to lack of Sun and confined to a Small cell it is a terrible cycle to get into and this is what you are seeing with your brother.

In Australia we have Official visitors come to Correctional Centres to listen to Issues Inmates and Staff may have ,this is a Government body that works for the Attorney General's Dept . An Official Visitor will then report back to this Dept with anything they they deem to be irregular. Attorney General's Dept is separate too Corrective Services so it is in nature their to show Transparency and to report that the running of Gaols and Inmates are being looked after in a humane environment.

So Brenda you need to start doing research as too who the government Body is in your state that overseers Correctional Facilities, dont give up it wont be easy and the key is for you too follow the advice on this Forum and Dr Kruse's work as you being full of inner fire is what is going to help bring your brother out of his dark hole .Once he see's you and others caring about him and the effort you are making he will hopefully start to make an effort himself.
I have seen this happen many times an inmate with no hope turn full circle and deal with the hand given to him.
Yes Drezy Inmates can take on work ,,,and the smart ones always take outside work gardening maintenance etc

You said Brenda

"It is difficult to see a psychiatrist in his maximum security prison and he has been waiting around two weeks for an appointment." unfortunately the wheels turn slow in a Correctional Facility,, Your Brother cannot give up

JanSZ "**He must be aware of things that influence him and have to assign priority."** Very true as Gaol influences come in good and bad packages your Brother has to work out which is good which is not!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp-and-the-cp.19673/post-216755>

ok so I am reading EMF 4 and listening to the PPP webinar and I have a question. If I am understanding this correctly carbs can disrupt the body's utilization of the PPP and we should be aiming to become fat burners and activate the PPP. However I don't know how the creatine phosphagen system (CP) interacts with the PPP. What I mean is this. If I am a person just now adopting epi-paleo and dealing with some health issues, should I capitalize on the CP while also nourishing the PPP or does the CP shut off the PPP the same way carbs do? If I should be utilizing CP what do I take to counteract the problems with depleted methyl groups? Or should I just ignore the CP and focus on the PPP?

Side notes: I am a person who works out regularly and used to supplement creatine. I have minor fibromyalgia type symptoms in my past medical history.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) I think I just had a "boom" moment due to this article and the PPP webinar (along with EMF 1-4). So I started going to the gym due to FM/CFS symptoms and my FM symptoms went away as I continued to nourish my muscles. However during this time (looking back in hindsight now) my brain fog symptoms started to appear as the muscle pain went away. In the PPP webinar you explain that in muscular builds the electrons move towards the collagen in the muscles due to the higher levels of collagen and this is at the expense of possibly the brain or heart. So basically I have always had an electron deficiency but before I was muscular the deficiency was felt in my 150lb frame while now that I am 200lbs of muscle, the deficiency is felt in my cognitive capacity. # Thread: The emerging role of Nrf2 in mitochondrial function
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emerging-role-of-nrf2-in-mitochondrial-function.19678/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-06-22T13:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emerging-role-of-nrf2-in-mitochondrial-function.19678/post-216814>

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0891584915002129](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0891584915002129)

Abstract

The transcription factor NF-E2 p45-related factor 2 (Nrf2; gene name _NFE2L2_) allows adaptation and survival under conditions of stress by regulating the gene expression of diverse networks of cytoprotective proteins, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and detoxification enzymes as well as proteins that assist in the repair or removal of damaged macromolecules. Nrf2 has a crucial role in the maintenance of cellular redox homeostasis by regulating the biosynthesis, utilization, and regeneration of glutathione, thioredoxin, and NADPH and by controlling the production of reactive oxygen species by mitochondria and NADPH oxidase. Under homeostatic conditions, Nrf2 affects the mitochondrial membrane potential, fatty acid oxidation, availability of substrates (NADH and FADH2/succinate) for respiration, and ATP synthesis. Under conditions of stress or growth factor stimulation, activation of Nrf2 counteracts the increased reactive oxygen species production in mitochondria via transcriptional upregulation of uncoupling protein 3 and influences mitochondrial biogenesis by maintaining the levels of nuclear respiratory factor 1 and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ coactivator 1α, as well as by promoting purine nucleotide biosynthesis. Pharmacological Nrf2 activators, such as the naturally occurring isothiocyanate sulforaphane, inhibit oxidant-mediated opening of the mitochondrial permeability transition pore and mitochondrial swelling. Curiously, a synthetic 1,4-diphenyl-1,2,3-triazole compound, originally designed as an Nrf2 activator, was found to promote mitophagy, thereby contributing to the overall mitochondrial homeostasis. Thus, Nrf2 is a prominent player in supporting the structural and functional integrity of the mitochondria, and this role is particularly crucial under conditions of stress.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-22T15:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-emerging-role-of-nrf2-in-mitochondrial-function.19678/post-216824>

[https://duckduckgo.com/?q=Jack+kruse+"NRF2"&t=ffcm&ia=web](https://duckduckgo.com/?q=Jack+kruse+%22NRF2%22&t=ffcm&ia=web)

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-06-26T00:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-than-40-of-children-aged-5-15-own-a-smartphone.19679/post-217087>

Well. drezy, I wouldn't debate the darkfield studies/live blood analysis. I used to get them every six months for a few years and these are what revealed my problems and helped save my life. As someone who took her diet very seriously and her many supplements and training in gyms, I really thought that I was doing everything within my power to achieve optimal health. After all, this is what we have been fed from all of the health gurus, right? Well, so much for their half truths and advice. I was a mess inside and falling apart! You know, the blue straggler star that Jack talks about? Perhaps the worse part, like so many others, I was in denial and my Dr. was pissed at me. Not too long after my test revealed how my blood platelets were all clumped together, I discovered Jack and he read a blog post of mine from a Zero Carb Zen website and he knew I was in denial, pretending, or why even sugar coat it, full of shit! You can't fool Jack! Jack knows his shit! He flat out said that anyone who trains in a gym, under artificial light, is an idiot! That was my wakeup call. I knew that all of the good food and supplements were of absolutely no use to me when my environment sucked. I knew what I had to do and I haven't looked back in over two years now, NO pretending either when I say that I feel amazing at almost 57 years young.

For the past three months now, I've been working on my own project here and it's going to be my contribution to humanity or perhaps a wake up call as to what goes on in the Fitness Industry and why my Sisters and Brothers are falling like flies, as a result of the half truths that they buy into. I'm working on a project and I'm doing it my way, the EpiPaleo lifestyle that I have thrived on. Enough for now. Here's a picture of me when I was 54 and training for my first NPC Figure Competition, looking like a rock star on the outside and falling apart on the inside. Looks can be so deceiving, can't they though? Here I am as a blue straggler star.........

# Thread: ROS in the Hypothalamus
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ros-in-the-hypothalamus.19680/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Coates — 2017-06-23T14:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ros-in-the-hypothalamus.19680/post-216883>

[https://health-innovations.org/2017...late-amount-of-insulin-the-pancreas-produces/](https://health-innovations.org/2017/06/16/study-finds-small-colony-of-neurons-modulate-amount-of-insulin-the-pancreas-produces/)

Results show that POMC neurons are involved in the control of the intake and the amount of insulin secreted by the beta cells of the pancreas. Data findings show that this communication between the hypothalamus and the pancreas depends on the activity of the protein Mitofusin 1. The group explain the alterations are due to a disproportionate, though transitory, increase in the production of radical oxygen species in the hypothalamus; when the levels of radical oxygen species in the hypothalamus are restored in the laboratory, the pancreas starts to secrete the correct levels of insulin again.

From loh in ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/understanding-black-light.16985/page-2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/understanding-black-light.16985/page-2) )

Doc's response: "it will help Loh"

Rough search on Amazon.uk for this specification:
[https://www.amazon.co.uk/s/ref=nb\_s...=365nm+blacklight&rh=i:aps,k:365nm+blacklight](https://www.amazon.co.uk/s/ref=nb_sb_noss?url=search-alias%3Daps&field-keywords=365nm+blacklight&rh=i%3Aaps%2Ck%3A365nm+blacklight)

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-25T09:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-217047>

> [Paul Keenan said:](/goto/post?id=217008)
>
> I went to B & Q today, they had nothing. I know there are U.S sites like this [https://lowbluelights.com/product-category/lighting-products/220-240v-lamps/](https://lowbluelights.com/product-category/lighting-products/220-240v-lamps/) but I will get hit with customs etc.
>
> Any UK links?
>
> cheers

Hi Paul

I'm hacking low wattage incandescent bulbs and stage light filters. I got a free Lee filters swatch book from Stage Electrics in the UK, and went through the swatch to find the closest match to the -6 deg elevation, comparing the spectral patterns on the lee filters website to part A of this picture. Looking through the swatch tester helps me pick a suitable one for my own n=1. (There's a Rosco swatch as well, but at the time it was easier to get Lee filters than Rosco, but that might be different now).
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-25_7-5-52-png.5150/)

The minus 6 degrees has a distinct drop in the yellow-green, a drop in the 460 to 480, but still has 430 to 465. Reality 4: _Melanopsin works with light 430 -464 at dusk_. With low quantum yield already, I think my getting the sunset/dusk circadian cycle frequencies matched better than just blocking blue is a must. I now wouldn't use any high yellow or UVB anywhere around sunset. Using single or limited frequency bands without a circadian backdrop is missing a trick.
These are my after sunset blue blockers. They are so relaxing I wouldn't risk driving in them. A 1.22m x 53 cm piece cost under a tenner delivered, from ebay. I'm going to be using the rest of the sheet to make a low ambient light source for rooms used in the evening, and I have a darker shade that I'm in the process of turning into a bedroom light, so I can choose when I go from dusk to deep dusk to darkness in the evening as the days get shorter.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?attachments/upload_2017-6-15_11-30-16-png.5046/)

**
2\. General Lamp Shade (GLS - this just means General incandescent light, at one point this was THE ONLY type of "standard" light and is hence called a GLS. Come in all different colours and shapes for decoration like pearls - the more basic the better for general lighting and health with these)
[**https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/gls-240v-25w-e27-pearl/**](https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/gls-240v-25w-e27-pearl/)

### WalterNL — 2017-08-08T17:45:44+0300

@[shiran](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20240/) The general idea is to reproduce natural light. As you see below, later in the day it should get more orange/red, the color temperature goes below 2000K.
So it's probably the best choice to get a soft light that produces a warm color <2000K or get candles. Of course this would still depend on personal context.

### drezy — 2017-08-08T22:20:35+0300

Oh Jan... You and your elastic brain. You are truly a testament to the power of bacon and I'm beginning to suspect that you're younger than you profess.

In fact, I can hear a higher calling that makes it incumbent upon me to not make any jokes about naked Jan sandwiched in between two of these units.... Not one. No mention of Dry aged beef sandwich with toasted buns, Polish sausage wrap, or Kentucky hot brown sandwich. No sir, we are going high class and science-like here and now.

I realize that it's not the same screen but the technology is similar in this review of a different samsung screen:
[http://www.rtings.com/tv/reviews/samsung/qled-q7f-q7](http://www.rtings.com/tv/reviews/samsung/qled-q7f-q7)

The main thing I kinda don't like is the forced 120Hz flicker that I assume will be there if the image changes or not. Also I haven't got a meter near one so I'm flying blind there.

The underlying quantum dot technology, aside from the fact that it flickers/updates the image 120 times per second, is interesting if you could just stop that flicker. For the application that you suggest images changes would only need to be 5 minutes apart or so (again forced to 120/sec by an internal control board right now).

Also at some point I need to look at the conversion of these screens 400 cd/m2 to the equivalent w/m2.

Beeswax candles are the way to go

Beeswax is made by nature’s worker bees. No artificial scents or colours are required and it’s free from petroleum products and chemicals. Beeswax candles last up to three times longer than paraffin wax candles and twice as long as soy candles of the same size. Beeswax candles do not drip and are smokeless.

### Phosphene — 2018-08-24T16:28:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-249280>

[@Christina Gagnon](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21726/), I had a similar situation when staying with friends outside Portland Maine last fall. Despite being so close to water it was surprisingly hard to catch a good sunrise. On the days we made the effort to drive 10-20 minutes to a good spot I seemed to notice a difference in mood/energy for the day. There is nothing like that delight at seeing the first red—a little gasp escapes my lips even now.

We made a game out of sunrise-chasing, and I annoyed my friends a bit by vetoing a few spots near power lines that were otherwise perfect.

As far as the light science goes, I’m still not sure, and would love to know as well. It certainly varies per location.

### WalterNL — 2018-08-24T18:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-249293>

> [Christina Gagnon said:](/goto/post?id=249268)
>
> [@WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) From my house I can't actually see the sunrise when it first emerges and looks reddish - I only see it when it has yurned fairly bright yellow (still it's fairly early 7AM). Do you think I am still getting the good red light?

Difficult to say without measurements. The UVA/IR transition appears to be most crucial of the sunrise / sunset. It differs per location and season. In summer the UV rays can show up more quickly because the angle of the sun is such that the rays have to travel a shorter distance, making the transition earlier.

Either way, getting sun at 7AM is better than getting no sun, you're still early and before UVB shows up.

### John Saf — 2018-08-25T14:52:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-249357>

Becase of the flicker effekt i have not been using any lights (day or night) in my house since april.
My wifes migranes have cured. The only artificial light is some sun through the windows when they not are open. Sun through windows is lacking in uv an IR, but it does not flicker. But we try to spend all day outside. Dr.A.Wunch says spectrum is only half for Health when searching for optimal. Because of this i have sold my crappy joovv. So trying to copy the sun connected to the grid will never be good? Im now testing different types of red leds and 3w orange filter halogen connected to a seperate DC of the grid powersource . human is not supposed to have any light after the sun is down? Only little moonlight i guess. And no light in nature flicker . So finding a artificial light for night will never be optimal.

It have to be under 15 lux i guess:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/29504270/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/29504270/)

Does not say anything a how brigth, But red can also inhibit melatonin:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/11763987/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/11763987/)

[@sean](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/8952/) Watres Do you use all these buls simiutaneously to try to get a complete spectrum indoors?\[

QUOTE="Sean Waters, post: 217112, member: 19464"\]**UV Light**

1\. Exoterra Solar Glo 160 W (JanSz posted above) ^^

2\. Osram ULTRA VITALUX 300 W
[https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/osramvitalux-radium-sanolux-300watt-lamp-230v/](https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/osramvitalux-radium-sanolux-300watt-lamp-230v/)

**Black Light
**
1\. CFL Blacklight
[https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/blacklight-13w-uv-368nm-e27-cfl-flyzap-lamp/](https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/blacklight-13w-uv-368nm-e27-cfl-flyzap-lamp/)

2\. LED 7W E27
[http://www.ebay.co.uk/itm/CroLED-7W-E27-A19-365nm-LEDs-Ultraviolet-UV-Bulb-Bulbs-Blacklight-AC100-240V-New/263016564563?\_trksid=p2385738.c100677.m4598&\_trkparms=aid%3D222007%26algo%3DSIC.MBE%26ao%3D1%26asc%3D20160908110712%26meid%3D4283fcdacf0341b083522c2e32d0d244%26pid%3D100677%26rk%3D2%26rkt%3D5%26sd%3D122215257410](http://www.ebay.co.uk/itm/CroLED-7W-E27-A19-365nm-LEDs-Ultraviolet-UV-Bulb-Bulbs-Blacklight-AC100-240V-New/263016564563?_trksid=p2385738.c100677.m4598&_trkparms=aid%3D222007%26algo%3DSIC.MBE%26ao%3D1%26asc%3D20160908110712%26meid%3D4283fcdacf0341b083522c2e32d0d244%26pid%3D100677%26rk%3D2%26rkt%3D5%26sd%3D122215257410)

**Infra-red**

1\. Philips R95
[https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/philips-r95-infrared-100w-es-red-lamp/](https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/philips-r95-infrared-100w-es-red-lamp/)

2\. Philips Infraphil
[https://www.amazon.co.uk/Philips-In...=1498485392&sr=8-1&keywords=philips+infraphil](https://www.amazon.co.uk/Philips-InfraPhil-HP3616-Infrared-Effective-x/dp/B00MWPKPII/ref=sr_1_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1498485392&sr=8-1&keywords=philips+infraphil)

**Incandescent**

1\. Rough service (just means a bit less easy to blow/ brake - made for factories etc.)
**[https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/gls-rough-service-240v-100w-e27-clear/](https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/gls-rough-service-240v-100w-e27-clear/)
**
2\. General Lamp Shade (GLS - this just means General incandescent light, at one point this was THE ONLY type of "standard" light and is hence called a GLS. Come in all different colours and shapes for decoration like pearls - the more basic the better for general lighting and health with these)
[**https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/gls-240v-25w-e27-pearl/**](https://internationallamps.co.uk/product/gls-240v-25w-e27-pearl/)

I have a full document that I'm working on to do with Artificial "healthy" Lighting. I will upload and send if anyone wants it.

Cheers\[/QUOTE\]

### Sue-UK — 2019-01-09T18:10:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-259855>

> [Charyam said:](/goto/post?id=259847)
>
> Hello Sue, can you give the spec and the source for the Ebay "1.22m x 53 cm piece " ?
>
> " A 1.22m x 53 cm piece cost under a tenner delivered, from ebay. I'm going to be using the rest of the sheet to make a low ambient light source for rooms used in the evening, and I have a darker shade that I'm in the process of turning into a bedroom light, so I can choose when I go from dusk to deep dusk to darkness in the evening as the days get shorter."
>
> Thanks
> Charlie

Hi Charlie...

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=217047)
>
> I'm hacking low wattage incandescent bulbs and stage light filters. I got a free Lee filters swatch book from Stage Electrics in the UK, and went through the swatch to find the closest match to the -6 deg elevation, comparing the spectral patterns on the lee filters website to part A of this picture. Looking through the swatch tester helps me pick a suitable one for my own n=1. (There's a Rosco swatch as well, but at the time it was easier to get Lee filters than Rosco, but that might be different now).
> [View attachment 5150](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/5150/)
>
> The minus 6 degrees has a distinct drop in the yellow-green, a drop in the 460 to 480, but still has 430 to 465. Reality 4: _Melanopsin works with light 430 -464 at dusk_. With low quantum yield already, I think my getting the sunset/dusk circadian cycle frequencies matched better than just blocking blue is a must. I now wouldn't use any high yellow or UVB anywhere around sunset. Using single or limited frequency bands without a circadian backdrop is missing a trick.
> These are my after sunset blue blockers. They are so relaxing I wouldn't risk driving in them. A 1.22m x 53 cm piece cost under a tenner delivered, from ebay. I'm going to be using the rest of the sheet to make a low ambient light source for rooms used in the evening, and I have a darker shade that I'm in the process of turning into a bedroom light, so I can choose when I go from dusk to deep dusk to darkness in the evening as the days get shorter.

Not wishing to be unhelpful, but if someone was to read what I had written and got the swatch, and compared the different filters to the post sunset frequencies and decide to give it a whirl, it was one thing. But someone doing it - particularly the glasses - based on my spec was and is something else. For example, the Ra Optics glasses weren't developed based on the frequencies of the filters I used. Reporting a hack giving the spec out might have led to others trying it without realising that the most important thing to them was something I didn't know I didn't know.  I didn't and can't give the spec, because it wasn't and isn't part of the teaching. I've learned more about flicker etc since that old post. I've moved onto 10-12 hours of total darkness in the winter as a better choice for sleep. I've learned more about non linear relationships.

So sorry - I know now that I didn't know enough for myself, so my sharing the spec wouldn't be a good idea.

### Charyam — 2019-01-09T19:10:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-259863>

Thanks for the reply Sue, I've sourced a film, Amber 81, from Window Film Company to block blue light up to 500nm from which I've made two screens for my work PC's. It works OK but on some monitors (we hot desk) there is distortion.
I've also cobbled it to on to my mobile phone.

I understand your reasoning about the specification, I don't need that of course as it is applicable to you.
I would like to find an alternative film from another manufacturer to avoid the distortion.
Can you give the ebay company as I'd like to find an alternative film.

Charlie

### Sue-UK — 2019-01-09T19:37:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-259865>

> [Charyam said:](/goto/post?id=259863)
>
> Thanks for the reply Sue, I've sourced a film, Amber 81, from Window Film Company to block blue light up to 500nm from which I've made two screens for my work PC's. It works OK but on some monitors (we hot desk) there is distortion.
> I've also cobbled it to on to my mobile phone.
>
> I understand your reasoning about the specification, I don't need that of course as it is applicable to you.
> I would like to find an alternative film from another manufacturer to avoid the distortion.
> Can you give the ebay company as I'd like to find an alternative film.
>
> Charlie

If you are UK based a free swatch is available from [https://www.stage-electrics.co.uk/shop/sales/lighting/colour-filter-and-gel/colour-swatches](https://www.stage-electrics.co.uk/shop/sales/lighting/colour-filter-and-gel/colour-swatches). The filters are available from them, but unless you want a big piece, the ebay sellers who cut it into smaller pieces is probably the cheaper option. I've bought from different sellers at different times, because they didn't all stock the full range, or were possibly out of stock when I wanted it. Once I'd picked which one I wanted to try, I put the description including the colour code number into ebay uk search, it brought up the sellers who had that particular one in stock.

Caroline, I have bought the kit to use the wine bottle idea, but I haven't settled on an oi lto use. What kind do you find good? I tried olive oil as I had some at home, but that didn't work. Does "Lamp Oil" from on line sellers give off nasty fumes?

### Charyam — 2019-05-30T18:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-271620>

Olive and Veg oils are 'heavy' and won't travel up the wick in a wine bottle (hence the flat oil lamps of days of old)
Olive burns more cleanly, veg oils smell of cooking to me.
Proper lamp oil for wine bottles is around £5 a litre. I've tried a bulk 'odourless' oil from a local supplier in Nottingham but it's not as good as the shop bought at £5 (I got it from a small independent shop)

I'm going to be using the rest of the sheet to make a low ambient light source for rooms used in the evening, and I have a darker shade that I'm in the process of turning into a bedroom light, so I can choose when I go from dusk to deep dusk to darkness in the evening as the days get shorter.
> [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?attachments/upload_2017-6-15_11-30-16-png.5046/)

I love those glasses could you please tell me what they are the brand please thanks

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2023-05-12T06:14:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-purchase-bulbs-to-use-at-night-uk-sites.19683/post-321254>

If anyone is interested in oil lamps Ive been involved in antique ones for 10 plus years.

the best thing to use is lamp oil ( which is paraffin that has been refined) it doesnt smoke and it gives a decent burn. paraffin wax is what they make candles out of. They dont use tallow candles anymore ( I remember a story where monks ate the tallow candles because they were really hungry) Oil lamps at night are exceptionally good for eyes. Unfortunately, unless you get a centre draught 50 to 100 candlepower lamp you cant actually read by them unless youre right next to them.

Im currently using bulbs by block blue light dot co dot uk They seem to be doing the business and they are very low flicker,. You can get red or yellow for daytime reading. Theyre not cheap sadly but thats life.

### Technosmith — 2017-06-25T16:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-we-need-to-consume-potassium.19684/post-217064>

Nice one Jack. Thanks.

Am I to assume you agree with the irrelevance but not the science behind the overall conclusion - he is linking potassium to salt/sodium & hydration (see below);

_For our cells to be able to hydrate they have to use osmosis. Osmosis is movement of liquid from one place into another through a semipermeable membrane. This membrane is a cellular membrane, so water from the circulatory system has to enter into the cell through the cellular membrane. There are no special valves or portals that will enable the water and particles to enter or exit the cellular membrane.

The extracellular fluid (in the circulatory system) is loaded with sodium but it has minuscule amounts of potassium as well.

The intracellular fluid (within the cell) is loaded with potassium but it has minuscule amounts of sodium as well.

Atom of sodium has 11 electrons in its orbit so it has a size that can easily pass through the membrane. Sodium can enter and exit the cell at any time so it cannot accumulate there.

Potassium is slightly larger having 19 electrons in its orbit. Because of its size it cannot pass through the membrane without a help of pressure so it becomes trapped within the cell. More potassium that accumulates within the cell will increase the viscosity of the intracellular liquid. Increased viscosity starts to absorb the liquid of lesser viscosity and the water with its elements starts to be sucked into the cell. This water movement will persist as long as the viscosities equilibrate themselves or to say it plainly, until the liquids achieve the same viscosity or even out.

This means, the more potassium there is within the cell, the higher water pressure there will be.

The question is, if potassium cannot pass the membrane without pressure and it becomes trapped within the cell, how did it get there in the first place?

Scientists are telling us that we have to eat vegetables that have high potassium content so how does this potassium enter our blood since it has to pass cellular membranes on the way? Later it has to pass cellular membrane to enter the cellular structure so that it can accumulate within the cell and how can this happen when the cell is already pressurized?

Something is wrong in Paradise, wouldn’t you say?

Popular science is giving us all kinds of bogus solutions ignoring the truth and the truth is that cells will produce desired amount of potassium so their osmotic pump can work effectively. This is done through a process called cold fusion.

Wait a minute, we have been told that cold fusion is very difficult to achieve. Another lie we have been swallowing.

Cells have an organelle that increases the voltage and serves like capacitor. This organelle is called the prismatic body. So if the cellular brain which is within the cellular membrane called the receptor does not find toxic elements within the extracellular liquid, it gives the order for potassium production. The prismatic body zaps sodium with high voltage, excites it and bonds it with an atom of oxygen. 11 electrons of sodium bond with 8 electrons of oxygen and 19 electron potassium is created. As more and more potassium is being produced, the intracellular pressure is rising and under that pressure toxic elements including heavy metals will be expelled from the cell. As particles and water are leaving the cell under the pressure, the cell will lose potassium as well. This is how potassium ends up in the blood circulation.

Now you know that supplementing potassium through the food is impossible so all medical recommendations regarding potassium supplementation are ridiculous.

Now you understand why is salt so necessary for our hydration.

No salt, no potassium._

I had to turn mine off and replace it with a 2G phone. Even then, it's on Airplane mode from after work @ 5pm until when I get to work @ 8am. On weekends I turn it off Airplane mode to contact friends but then off again as soon as I'm with them, or leave it on in a different room.

I notice so many symptoms if its near me when it's on... Initially its:
\- Headaches
\- Sneezing
\- Blocked nose

\- Pains in the hands

\- heart palpitations
\- a need to run the toilet
\- bubbling/ gas from my gut
\- sharp stomach pains
\- depression/ anxiety/ paranoia
\- serious fatigue and aching, similar to fibromyalgia

That's just my 2G phone. I also get the above ^ after using iPads, Laptops etc. (albeit a lot quicker/ more intensely). > I notice so many symptoms if its near me when it's on... Initially its:
> \- Headaches
> \- Sneezing
> \- Blocked nose
> \- Eczema on my hands flares up and itches me badly.
> \- Pains in the hands
> \- Irritation/ aggravation (often ends up being in an arsey and snappy mood with my other half which is shit.)
>
> After a while I get more of the above... and some different ones:
> \- heart palpitations
> \- a need to run the toilet
> \- bubbling/ gas from my gut
> \- sharp stomach pains
> \- depression/ anxiety/ paranoia
> \- serious fatigue and aching, similar to fibromyalgia
>
> That's just my 2G phone. I also get the above ^ after using iPads, Laptops etc. (albeit a lot quicker/ more intensely). Sometimes it happens just walking past the EMF hell of McDonald's on the way to work. Thing is, I have an autoimmune disease called Crohn's disease, or I like to say **HAD,** as I have it controlled (on no medication - 18 months and counting). Jack believes autoimmune diseases are effectively an "_**ALLERGY TO EMF**_". So, you may not have the same severity of symptoms as me. But, I believe, honestly, that all mitochondrial diseases are due to non-native EMF of some sort. Whether it's Artificial Light (nnEMF) or higher frequency of EMF such as WiFi or iPhones.
>
> In the wild, where there is none of this nnEMF, they don't see the same mitochondrial diseases as we do...
>
> Look at this website on Grizzly Bear diseases, for example:
> [http://animals.mom.me/diseases-grizzly-bears-1944.html](http://animals.mom.me/diseases-grizzly-bears-1944.html)
>
> "Some bears suffer from Arthritis during old age"... Hmm, really? Or is that just in your Blue Lit Captivity Cells?
>
> Otherwise - it's largely just infections, but it notes at the top... that these are RARE.
>
> That means, on the whole Grizzly Bears are 850805860589653495 times more healthy than us. I don't think I need to say WHY to anyone reading this.

Yeah, I'm "checking" it throughout the day. It's on from about 6am until 7pm. Sometimes if I'm carrying it in my bag I'll put it on aeroplane mode.

How do you even get a 2g phone? I got this phone from my husband. It's his old workphone but I've no idea what model it is... It's about two years old, maybe more. I'd be happy to dumb my phone down a bit for sure. I've just left social media and planning on getting rid of email notifications and just checking twice a day.

I never put my phone directly to my head but it is near my head when I record voice messages on whatsapp. I also feel it in my hand, a horrible tingle - not pleasant.

I don't have any autoimmune but you've got me thinking and my sinuses have been a bit blocked for years now. I don't feel like I breathe properly through my nose. Could be EMF.

My meter comes tomorrow. God bless Amazon Prime. I'm excited but equally shitting my pants (sorry, sore point for Chrons) about finding horrible things in my home. I'm pretty sure my oven (one of those induction hobs) is going to be off the chart. Off to buy a barbeque. An indoor one #Scotland

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-13T11:02:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-this-and-a-dumb-question.19686/post-218662>

> [TANN said:](/goto/post?id=218612)
>
> I know I'm asking a question that I should find out on here for myself but how did you hack your environment?

My environment is probably the worst in the UK... It's London.

I hack it right now by doing the following, in order of importance:
1\. Avoid EMF as much as humanly possible:

-   Sit far away from laptop, use keyboard and mouse.
-   Got rid of iphone, got a 2G phone and keep on airplane mode before and after work
-   Keep your room tech free, better if your house is but if you share a house its difficult
-   Cover your cookers and washing machines etc. panels and LCD displays with Tin Foil (thanks Emma)
-   Generally stand far away from electrics and tech. Use a breaker at night where possible

2\. bathing myself in 10kg of ice and cold water in a bath every night (creates a faraday cage, protective of EMF)
3\. Sun + Grounding - in my garden with the morning sunlight for at least 15 minutes. Generally get as much sun as possible.
4\. Sleep - strictly no food or light in my eyes (unless it is infrared) after 8pm.
5\. Eyes - blocking out blue light with Cyxus blue blockers at work, and UVEX safety blue blockers at night.
6\. Skin - cover up indoors under artificial lighting
7\. Food - eating a Seafood only, ketogenic diet. Always eating BIG in the morning with sunrise. Skip lunch if you can and eat at 4-7pm for dinner. Seafoods Expensive, but so worth it. It increases your bodys electric current. Mussels, Shrimp and Clams cooked in tomato spicy broths for the win.

Wow, I didn't realise. Fuck living in London @[Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/)

Like Sean, I do a lot of those things minus CT, I'm still being a dick about the cold baths. I mean, working my way up to them.

Here are some things I do: -

1.No WIFI in house. Now actively avoiding going to places with lots of WIFI (like city centre or venues with loads of people).
2.Smart phone on airplane mode during the day.
3.CT face dunks every day.
4.Morning AM light + grounding - as soon as I'm awake I sit outside for 20 mins to get the light. It ain't sun #Scotland
5.As much natural daylight as possible
6.As much grounding as possible - I have leather soled shoes
7.Eating as much fish as humanely possible
8.All electric trip switches get put off at night. With the exception of the kitchen
9.We don't use any artificial light in the house - it's Summer, this is going to be fun in Winter. Think we'll get candles
10.Blue blockers for all screen use no matter what time of day
11.Blue blockers for any shop or supermarket that has LED or fluorescent lights (that's fucking all of them btw) with a baseball cap and fully clothed <-----literally care factor: zero with what people think when I'm fully in my gear

### drezy — 2017-07-14T17:02:26+0300

> I don't think there's an easy fix like that but clearly I don't know anything about the Wave rider. Just based on my instinct.

Stick with that instinct Emma. Any product that tells you it's "cancelling" the emf is not believable to me. I layed it out in a overly-geeky way here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hello-from-the-florida-space-coast.19474/#post-214080](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/hello-from-the-florida-space-coast.19474/#post-214080)

I think I can make a better analogy though. Say you challenge Hussein Bolt to a race where all the spectators are your mitochondria and they are right at the finish line.

-   Mr Bolt is very fast (aka the speed of any rf/emf through air).
-   You're allowed to start the race partway into the track instead having to start at the official start line, but you can't start running until the second Mr. Bolt is shoulder to shoulder with you(detection of a rf signal coming your way).
-   You will have to be carrying one of your babies though (aka the speed of signals in any consumer/prosumer circuitry)
-   Lastly to qualify as finished at the end you'll have to write Mr. Bolts name backward on a piece of paper somewhere along the way (like a processor would have to do to invert a signal after detection)

The above product doesn't make that particular claim.

In that scenario it would be more like you came up with the idea of shining bright lights of varying colors and intensities and maybe a confetti cannon or two into the eyes of your spectators for the whole race.

Could it help? I'm not behind it for a similar reason that I wouldn't buy a PEMF device, way more study needed and enough people willing to experiment on themselves.

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-21T10:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-this-and-a-dumb-question.19686/post-219584>

I owe my company £5000 for paying for my tuition, I have to stay for another 2 years by law for it to be wiped to zero. I don't want to break that, as I did it last year and had to pay £11,500 back to my previous company in one hit. Won't be doing that again... My only option would be to say to them "I'm not moving to another company, and I'm quitting due to ill health"... and ask them to wipe it off early as a gesture of good will. Otherwise, I will be leaving the country with Debts... may get a CCJ.. and may not be allowed into another country due to fraud. If I leave now, they will likely want the money back, but give it a year or so and things might be a little easier to swing.

The other option is to relocate back to my Mom's in Birmingham. It's in the countryside, much better environment. However, I'd still need to work in an office 5 days a week and drive an EMF hell hole vehicle everyday. I may also have to take a pay cut, due to getting extra £ in London. Although, it's definitely an option, as I'd be rent free so it would probably work out the same. The other issue is that my missus would find it difficult to get work down here, and there is no space at her mom's for her to move back to, so she would have to live at my mom's which is not a problem but not ideal either.

I'm considering buying some EMF shielding for my bedroom to improve my sleep, but I'm not sure what's the best way to do it. I also have had success with some Lodestone and Hematite iron stones around my bed. Anyone know if these really work?

If I can make it until next year, then I can have enough money to get out for good and forever.

# Thread: Hi from Austin, TX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-austin-tx.19688/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thiago Gatto Tannus — 2017-06-26T03:05:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-austin-tx.19688/post-217093>

I'm 22 years old and originally from Salvador, Brazil, but I've been going to undergrad school in Austin since 2014. I first learned about Jack when researching more about ketosis (I had implemented it in late October 2016 along with IF and seen good results in weight loss and energy levels) and I watched a podcast he did with Tristan from Primal Edge Health on why people are doing ketosis wrong. I was obviously blown away by what I heard given that I was had been a strict Paleo preacher for a year and at the moment was viciously blaming carbs for mine and other people's problems. So you can have an idea how lost I was, when I watched this I was in vacation in Salvador (12th latitude south) in December (summer in Brazil) as I do every year for school winter break and I was eating a near-zero carb paleo keto diet.

As soon as I went back to ATX for spring school semester I bought Jack's book and started watching several of Jack's podcasts. As a finance major I fundamentally understand very little of the science but I'm able to follow the reasoning and extract the practical conclusions and applications (no need to speak mandarin to eat chinese food right?). I started following the Leptin Rx (even though I weighed around 160 lbs and considered myself in decent physical shape) to start brand new and try to reset my circadian cycles after 21 years of intense blue light exposure (for example, I used to go to sleep watching movies on my laptop...) and sunlight restriction due to conventional "wisdom" of cancer threat. I felt the changes really quickly after I started paying attention to light exposure, cycles, water, environment and implementing CT (who'd say the kid from Brazil was the one wearing shorts, shirt and flip flops around campus during winter...). My sleep cycle got structured and I've been able to fall asleep much more easily, I have less cravings (I used to eat 5x/day...) and feel better in general. The one thing I'd particularly like to fix, if possible, is my need to go to the bathroom 2x/night (which I have no idea when/why it started... possibly the effect of life-long circadian mismatches?) and understand to what extent this affects my sleep cycle, which has definitely improved since I dream a lot now. I also basically stopped using my phone and laptop unless necessary, started spending a lot of time outside and in nature in the parks around Austin (btw I can definitely feel the impact of nnemf and not being grounded when I go back to my 6th floor apt complex where only college students live). I already exercised a lot outside since I've been following Ido Portal since 2015 but I'd also like to understand more how to exercise for longevity while maintaining movement complexity, which is my goal: to live longer and better.

This is but a fraction of my experience and changes and sorry for the rambling but there is so much I want to share, ask and learn I could go on for a long time. I've been visiting this forum since the beginning of this semester and I decided I needed to learn more about how I can be better and help others as well. I've shown Jack's podcasts and book to some of my close friends who have started to understand it and have taken measures that have affected their lives and I feel really good about having such a positive impact. I've been also trying to explain many of these things to my friends, and especially family, in Brazil; my father suffered from depression and still hasn't fully recovered and my grandmother has been slowly developing Alzheimer's while my family let's her watch TV all day and rarely get in the sun. This has been an incredibly hard task since people are so brain washed by the lies they've heard all their lives that changing seems painful (low dopamine maybe!?) and quantum biology literature in Brazil is non-existent. Anyways I can't thank you enough, Jack, for showing me the light (pun intended) and doing what you do, and also thanks to the members of this community for sharing their knowledge and spreading the word!

and my grandmother has been slowly developing Alzheimer's while my family let's her watch TV all day and rarely get in the sun.

everybody
you, your father and your grandmother
be extremely diligent about sunlight
make sure that the sunlight reaches maximum skin area for maximum time
to find out if each of you is diligent enough
do not use vit D supplementation
measure:

post test results here, attach test results as PDF file to a post

\------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The one thing I'd particularly like to fix, if possible, is my need to go to the bathroom 2x/night
\----

I am 78yo man.
Until recently I had to go 2-3x/night to bathroom.
I sleep 8hr, now I go to bathroom at 7th or 7.5th hr, so it is work in progress.
But it already made tremendous difference in how I feel.

There is ADH hormone, (Anti Diuretic Hormone) called vasopressin.
Increasing that hormone causes body to capture more water (and pee less).
For sick people who have low level of that hormone there is medication.

One of them is called
Desmopressin nasal spray 0.01%
Person who needs that hormone takes 1 or 2 sprays to the nose.
I am not sick in that area, you are not sick.
We just go to bathroom at night to often.
Using that medication, even one spray would be disruptive (I tried that already)
I assume that my vasopressin is minimally low.

Recently, early this year, FDA approved medication for that particular use.
For use by people who go to often to bathroom at night.
So the medication is approved, there is a company that will be making that medicine sometime in the future.
There is even name of that medicine and its description. Name is Noctivia.
Really it will be the same Desmopressin, just diluted very much.

I got a hold of that medicine (my doctor obliged, have given me script for it), the rest I bought over internet.
Long story short,
it is working.
If interested read attached file.

////

If the AHA could kick the person in the ass responsible for most of their trouble, they wouldn't sit for a year. #keeppostingshitscience

The most classic horror tale of this latter type told in the Old Testament of the AHA is about saturated fat; their meme's and narratives over 60 years has become human research Astro-Turf buried in the literature to torture modern patients and physicians in a kind of spiritual Superbowl between God and Satan. Choose your scientific religion wisely folks. What you believe might just bite you in the ass and kill ya. The AHA playbook: In the book Grassroots for Hire: Public Affairs Consultants in American Democracy, Edward Walker defines "astroturfing" as public participation that is perceived as heavily incentivized, as fraudulent (claims are attributed to those who did not make such statements), or as an elite campaign masquerading as a mass movement. # Thread: How does cold boost dopamine?

**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-06-26T17:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-cold-boost-dopamine.19692/post-217117>

In this study 60 minutes of immersion at 55 degree water boosted dopamine by 250% (Something like this).

What is the mechanism for this? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

We know cold causes mitochondria to release IR, structuring cell water and increasing tunelling in the mitochondria.

**Does increased electron flow in mitochondria increase dopamine? If so, how?**

The Effect of Heat, Cold, and Exercise on Plasma Dopamine-β-Hydroxylase Activity in Man

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/articles/4777247/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/articles/4777247/)
