What I'm curious about are the mechanisms. It is obvious that cold does have this effect, but my question is **how**? Any ideas?

### kris90 — 2017-06-26T22:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-cold-boost-dopamine.19692/post-217132>

I think it's pretty similar to exercise. The cold activates muscle tissue via thermogenesis (shivering, and non-shivering which involves converting WAT to BAT).

We know exercise, fasting and cold all raise adrenaline and noradrenaline to trigger lipolysis, and dopamine is the precursor to both of those neurohormones. So it somehow upregulates the dopamine -> noradrenaline -> adrenaline pathway.

I made a post on facebook the other day:

> Any athletes or bodybuilders: what is your take on overtraining? I think most would logically agree that it is a possible outcome of training excessively. It is clear there are unfavorable endocrine changes that can be measured in hormone panels and inflammatory cytokine levels. Most would agree that too frequent muscle contracting is a chronic stress that can inhibit recovery and therefore adaptation to the repeated stimuli, however, I think most have forgotten one of the primary purposes of muscle tissue besides contractile power: thermogenesis.
>
> Can we build muscle by simply enduring a cold environment? The science (and rather, evolution) says yes. It turns out, both shivering, and non-shivering thermogenesis require massive amounts of energy generated by muscle tissue, and the adaptation from shivering to non-shivering thermogenesis is the conversion of WAT (white adipose tissue - aka body fat) to BAT (brown adipose tissue) which in itself is highly thermogenic. This adaptation is triggered by a mechanism where muscle and fat tissue communicate with each other in response to a cold environment. It appears as though this exact same mechanism occurs in exercise (to a larger degree with high intensity work). Therefore, it appears as though the full-body muscle contractions involved in shivering thermogenesis is equally as stimulating as resistance training, and the anabolic as well as fat burning and insulin sensitizing effects of the hormone irisin are among the same. But there is one important difference: overtraining. I think we all agree chronic stress is detrimental to health and performance. But what if you could chronically stimulate muscle tissue to reap the anabolic/fat burning benefits without overtraining? Welcome to the cold-adapted pathway built into each and everyone of us.
>
> Humans have evolved to fully adapt to cold environments via the browning of WAT which is activated by muscle. If this were not that case, we would have never been able to migrate away from the equator to northern and southern hemispheres. When you choose to train as a warm-adapted athlete fueling your body with carbohydrates, you can never enter the cold-adapted pathway that gives you immunity to overtraining. What is the difference between chronic exercise as a warm-adapted human versus a cold-adapted human? Excess energy is dissipated as HEAT in the cold through mitochondrial uncoupling of oxidative phosphorylation, but in a warm environment when one is tightly coupled, the excess energy creates more free radicals which increases oxidative stress leading to inflammation. Inflammation is not an issue in the cold. Ask any athlete who uses cold as part of their training and recovery. Ask Michael Phelps who eats a whopping 12,000 calories, probably triple of what an obese person consumes.
>
> Another beauty of the cold when you understand how it effects magnetism, is that that it increases the electrical tension within our cell membranes, improving hormone receptor binding affinity. Why does that matter, you ask? Because when systems work more efficiently in your body, less is more. This is how I cured my illness of 3+ years in a matter of days, because LOW hormone levels do not matter in this pathway. What matters is you are functioning optimally on less, making you more efficient.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24506871](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F24506871&h=ATO6RvglqaNlOUtGUteyKRbpGXxYdoa4TXZSqxz_MSiCxOuQgnGsM1iRs6mytjbedN1GGkz8KtQP-ApSJK9vTjFZmI9jMzAmrZ9N3hQzOJOMVKo5jRS29BnhWTojwykRndbAZ1ugc1If4l-ko9JCAgUfFAOzYEbb0w&enc=AZO6oIHcLeLUG6vHNZrh2ayoGWBZCFdnTYvP8OxgJP49c4SSI4PnaPjJd2ol1erq49hHcBT9ImTNVPQDBv70spr3o9QmWuuI5f9jQ3T3OdnFUeCpJ5jrkZnrBxGMOmBdD1tOAsSuTIV-57HYko3MfoJT9hZDpYMk9KJ5CwRmpvNJKg3rlAbsl396TxWDoK-aREI&s=1)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daytime-indoor-glasses-that-do-not-affect-alertness-yet-support-circadian-rhythm.19693/post-217124>

I have the attention of an optic lab tech and I would like him to design glasses that can be worn to reduce blue exposure in an environment akin to an OR, yet not affect alertness. Would I be correct in saying that you would need two sets of glasses one to reduce the "midday"blue glare to the early ramp up morning light spectrum after 10 am, wear these until solar noon and at X hour change to a second pair that reduces the artificial light spectrum to a "ramp down" spectrum and after hours Blueblockers as normal. A key consideration would probably be to support and respect heart circadian rhythm as much as possible. What frequencies would be the desired ones ? Would a set up of this kind be better tan nothing at all or are there pitfalls? Is this the basic idea behind your glasses Dr K or am I way off base? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Would you mind explaining your glasses set up a bit more in detail ? **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-ez-water-sea-salt.19695/post-217128>

Hi folks,

I know that we are saying the primary cause of cellular dehydration today is our unnaturally blue-light swamped environment.

However, is it definitely the case that sea salt water is inferior at hydrating than plain spring water?
It would seem logical that drinking sea salt water would help with hydration as a saline solution is used in medical establishments as a front-line treatment for dehydration. Also, consider the work and findings of Rene Quinton.

I believe the work/research of Gerald Pollack counter this argument, so what do we all currently feel is optimal and any experiences to back this up?

Thanks,

Phil

### JanSz — 2017-06-26T22:24:22+0300

I would prefer to define dehydration in a way that one could test it, figure out status and have logical prevention choices.

The way it is now, sick person, all swollen, on water pills, can be called dehydrated.

\-----
Now, the way I am hearing,
dehydrated person have enlarged mitochondria, that is so large that some of the EZ water it makes turns to bulk water within mitochondria.

Or may be, dehydration is
insufficient EZ content, and too much bulk water content in the cell.
...........
\===========================================

So there are two concepts of hydration.
Old one, usually extracellular.
New, water content and its phase state inside cell and inside mitochondria.

\------------------------
Note that phase change comes at the cost of latent energy.

\------------------------

Gerald Pollack talks about EZ water.
It looks like there is EZ water (near surface) then some kind transition zone and bulk water.

Mitochondria, specially the volume within inner membrane is very tight space.
I think I recall that there EZ may be just couple or few atoms thick.

Even at few atoms thick one can still think of stuff being uniform.
But not.
_Prof. Martin Chaplin presents a new theory on the structure of water in the cell that switches between **low-density and high-density clusters

The rotation of the proteins will cause changes in the water structuring outside this closest hydration shell. At the breaking surface, hydrogen bonds are ruptured, creating a zone of higher density water. Protein rotation thus creates a surrounding high-density water zone with many broken hydrogen bonds.
**_

> Is he off the mark?

[https://darkovelcek.wordpress.com/2015/09/22/how-to-hydrate/](https://darkovelcek.wordpress.com/2015/09/22/how-to-hydrate/)

**HOW TO HYDRATE?**
I am being bombarded by question “how to hydrate” since a lot of my readers are health conscious and drink plenty of water daily yet they are still experiencing the symptoms of dehydration. So **what to do? How much water to drink? Can it be to much of water?…**

In our body we have two different systems that needs to be hydrated. One of them is the circulatory system (the blood and lymph) and the other is the cellular system (the cells that create our body). Those two systems are not just separated by cellular membrane but they also have different electrolytes that are involved in hydration. The blood is loaded with sodium (Na) and the cells are loaded with potassium (K). I do not want to get deep into it but if you are interested read my book.

Salinity of the blood is calibrated and has to be maintained. This is important to know because one cannot hydrate if either water or salt are missing. Immediately here we are in controversy with allopatic medicine that blames salt for lot of health problems. Contrary, lack of salt is the reason for those problems. No salt, no hydration it is as simple as that. Why is it so? read my book for this article would be way to long if I go into explaining the details.

So as you start drinking the water with sea salt (not refined pure sea salt) your circulation will be filling up. If you had been dry for a long time your blood vessels are dry and they have lost elasticity so filling up the blood pressure will go up. Do not worry this will be temporary symptom because as there is sufficient water in circulation the cleansing organs will start their job and toxins will be leaving your body through the skin, mucous tissue and kidneys. Here you will see the signs of detox and in most cases they will resemble the flue. **It is enough to ad half teaspoon of sea salt per one liter of water**. **Drink one liter of this solution first thing in the morning and do not eat for 45 minutes or an hour so water can do its cleansing job. During the rest of the day you have to drink another two liters or more. There is no limit as long as you have sea salt in the water.**

**If you drink water without sea salt you actually dehydrate because you do not have enough salt to bond the water so your body sweats it or urinates it out loosing more salt and this way dehydrates you further.** I have seen plenty cases where exactly this have happened.

As your blood cleanses, it becomes cleaner so your cells will start to use it for their own hydration. Cells do not want to hydrate if the blood is toxic and dense, dry blood is always toxic. This is the main reason for most chronic diseases.

Cellular hydration is the second phase of hydration. As cells hydrate they themselves start to eliminate their toxins and they end up in the blood polluting it once more. This is the cause when people initially feel better and then really bad. Usually they blame it on the protocol, the salt, the “adrenals” and all kind of stupidity because they are scared taking salt since it is contradictory to the common knowledge. Most severe detox symptoms will experience people that used pharmaceutical drugs but I have seen bad things in people that were using medicinal plants for long periods of time as well. Usually within two weeks of these symptoms they fade away. Now cellular healing takes place and if cyst or tumor have to be eliminated, macrophages will start the destruction and this will also be followed with localized pain.

Bare in mind that cells will not hydrate if toxins are present in the blood that cells consider dangerous. Some of them we like to indulge on like **caffeine, nicotine, alcohol and garlic**. Any medicament or medicinal remedies including medicinal spices and plants interfere with cellular hydration. We can see this in their anti inflammatory action. Inflammation is forced hydration and anything that stops this natural healing process will prevent cells from hydrating.

**There is no other liquid that can be used for hydration, only pure water and sea salt.** Water should be structured so do it with your thoughts.

**IS IT NECESSARY TO SUPPLEMENT POTASSIUM?**
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I wrote about potassium in my book – The Manual For The Human Body, and I have mentioned it in several of my articles but several people have asked me to write about potassium because its role in the body is not clear to them. This does not surprise me at all since our health experts are confused themselves.

Let us see what the “experts” think about potassium:

**Medical News Today**

“Potassium’s primary functions in the body include regulating fluid balance and controlling the electrical activity of the heart and other muscles.

Potassium is an electrolyte that counteracts the effects of sodium, helping to maintain a healthy blood pressure. It is also important for [maintaining acid-base balance](http://www.todaysdietitian.com/newarchives/121112p50.shtml).”

**University of Maryland Medical Center**:

“Potassium is a very important mineral for the proper function of all cells, tissues, and organs in the human body. It is also an electrolyte, a substance that conducts electricity in the body, along with sodium, chloride, calcium, and magnesium. Potassium is crucial to heart function and plays a key role in skeletal and smooth muscle contraction, making it important for normal digestive and muscular function. Many foods contain potassium, including all meats, some types of fish (such as salmon, cod, and flounder), and many fruits, vegetables, and legumes. Dairy products are also good sources of potassium.

Having too much potassium in the blood is called hyperkalemia; having too little is known as hypokalemia. Keeping the right potassium balance in the body depends on the amount of sodium and magnesium in the blood. Too much sodium, common in Western diets that use a lot of salt, may increase the need for potassium. Diarrhea, vomiting, excessive sweating, malnutrition, malabsorption syndromes, such as Crohn disease, can also cause potassium deficiency. Use of a kind of heart medicine called loop diuretics can also cause you to be short on potassium.”

We do not have to go further because wherever we look we are going to hear the same “scientific” truths about potassium in regard to our health.

**Since we are told that the levels of sodium and potassium have to be ba**lanced, the alternative medicine jumps on the bandwagon to score on the supplementation market.

Here is what Dr. Mercola has to say about potassium:

[10](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/04/25/potassium-benefits.aspx#_edn10) This is especially problematic because potassium is a nutrient that needs to be kept in proper balance with sodium in your blood.

If you consume too much sodium, which is common if you eat a lot of processed foods, you’ll have an increased need for potassium.

Deficiency may be related to taking diuretic medication, however, anyone who eats a poor diet — an excess of processed foods and not enough fresh, whole foods — is potentially at risk of inadequate potassium levels.

It should be noted that your body generally does a remarkable job of regulating levels on its own, without any outside help from a supplement, but also, too much potassium in the system is a rarity.”

Although Dr. Mercola recommends that we get the necessary potassium through the food, many people will reach for the supplement to make sure that they have enough of that stuff in their body.

There are many sites promoting potassium supplements and many people are loading on them and on the vegetables that contain potassium.

To take a way the confusion I will show you what potassium is really about.

For our cells to be able to hydrate they have to use osmosis. Osmosis is movement of liquid from one place into another through a semipermeable membrane. This membrane is a cellular membrane, so water from the circulatory system has to enter into the cell through the cellular membrane. There are no special valves or portals that will enable the water and particles to enter or exit the cellular membrane.

The extracellular fluid (in the circulatory system) is loaded with sodium but it has minuscule amounts of potassium as well.

The intracellular fluid (within the cell) is loaded with potassium but it has minuscule amounts of sodium as well.

Atom of sodium has 11 electrons in its orbit so it has a size that can easily pass through the membrane. Sodium can enter and exit the cell at any time so it cannot accumulate there.

Potassium is slightly larger having 19 electrons in its orbit. Because of its size it cannot pass through the membrane without a help of pressure so it becomes trapped within the cell. More potassium that accumulates within the cell will increase the viscosity of the intracellular liquid. Increased viscosity starts to absorb the liquid of lesser viscosity and the water with its elements starts to be sucked into the cell. This water movement will persist as long as the viscosities equilibrate themselves or to say it plainly, until the liquids achieve the same viscosity or even out.

This means, the more potassium there is within the cell, the higher water pressure there will be.

**The question is, if potassium cannot pass the membrane without pressure and it becomes trapped within the cell, how did it get there in the first place?**

Scientists are telling us that we have to eat vegetables that have high potassium content so how does this potassium enter our blood since it has to pass cellular membranes on the way? Later it has to pass cellular membrane to enter the cellular structure so that it can accumulate within the cell and how can this happen when the cell is already pressurized?

Something is wrong in Paradise, wouldn’t you say?

Popular science is giving us all kinds of bogus solutions ignoring the truth and the truth is that cells will produce desired amount of potassium so their osmotic pump can work effectively. This is done through a process called cold fusion.

Wait a minute, we have been told that cold fusion is very difficult to achieve. Another lie we have been swallowing.

Cells have an organelle that increases the voltage and serves like capacitor. This organelle is called the prismatic body. So if the cellular brain which is within the cellular membrane called the receptor does not find toxic elements within the extracellular liquid, it gives the order for potassium production. The prismatic body zaps sodium with high voltage, excites it and bonds it with an atom of oxygen. 11 electrons of sodium bond with 8 electrons of oxygen and 19 electron potassium is created. As more and more potassium is being produced, the intracellular pressure is rising and under that pressure toxic elements including heavy metals will be expelled from the cell. As particles and water are leaving the cell under the pressure, the cell will lose potassium as well. This is how potassium ends up in the blood circulation.

Now you know that supplementing potassium through the food is impossible so all medical recommendations regarding potassium supplementation are ridiculous.

Now you understand why is salt so necessary for our hydration.

No salt, no potassium.

The time required for potassium buildup so that the osmosis goes into action is 10-15 minutes and it takes about the same time for the liquids to even their viscosity after the potassium production was stopped which will stop the movement of water into the cell.

This is the reason why when first time exposed to a toxin, cells will absorb it until they realize its toxicity and then they will still soak it in for another 10-15 minutes.

On the other hand if the cell is familiar with the toxin, as soon as receptor senses it, it stops potassium production so only several minutes toxin will be able to penetrate into the cell. Just a trace of known toxin will shut down the whole cellular structure of the body.

This explains why the first dose of the chemotherapy has such destructive effect and the following treatment do not result with any effect or just a mild effect at best.

For many years now I am telling you; “forget what you know and absorb like a child the new information you are exposed to”

With love and light

With nnEMF exposure it increases the intracellular dehydration effect and this would push the Vitamin D3 level lower and LDL cholesterol higher while also lowering hormone production from LDL cholesterol as well. This process requires T3 and Vitamin A. Vitamin A levels are destroyed by blue light toxicty via the retina. When photoreceptors are stimulated, the light-sensitive pigment in the retina, called 11-cis retinal which is Vitamin A compound, oxidizes to a light-insensitive form of Vitamin A called all-trans. This changes the photo signaling in the central retinal pathways to the pituitary and leptin receptor. Nobel Prize-winning work conducted 50 years ago demonstrated that all mammals use an enzyme pathway called the visual cycle to convert all-trans back to light-sensitive 11-cis retinal (Vit A). When scientists learned that multi-step enzymatic reactions like this often work slowly, it presented a mystery to them. How could the classic pathway replenish the 11-cis retinal fast enough in bright light (UV and blue in day light), which oxidizes the light-sensitive pigment much more quickly than dim light conditions?
UCLA researchers found that when stimulated by blue light, a phospholipid residing in rod and cone membranes, called N-ret-PE, undergoes conversion to 11-cis retinal without enzymes. It is activated by blue light. It is quantized to sunlight's amount of blue light during daylight and not man made light during dusk and night time. This is why Vitamin A deficits are linked to circadian disruption of blue light at night in diurnal mammals with photopic retinas. When you are blue light toxic you become very dehydrated as well. The RPE thickens as a result and BP and HR goes up. This stimulates the hypothalamus thirst center and the salt osmolarity center. Salt helps the brain off set the ICF and ECF dehydration from poor mitochondrial function. This means in a blue lit microwaved world you need a higher salt intake then you would think. As a result, you become less thirsty when you eat salt because your body is retaining water and as a result begins to be able to burn fat via beta oxidation because the water forms the MINOS layer around the respiratory proteins to bring them closer together. This change in geometry improves the inner mitochondrial oscillation closer to the key 100Hz frequency. Salt intake can leads to catabolic muscle mass loss, increased AMP and ADP levels in skeletal muscle which is a stimulus to AMPk pathways, and this can decreases cardiovascular energy expenditure and reduce cardiac mitochondrial reserve. It makes you more tightly coupled and helps affect your redox. ### Penny — 2017-07-14T19:30:45+0300

Shallow medical background is actually rather kind of you I'm a software engineer - and as far as respiratory surfaces go, NAC apparently totally rocks for curing COPD :
**The Overlooked Compound That Saves Lives**
May 2010
By Julius Goepp, MD

**Lung Disease Defense**
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), which includes chronic bronchitis and chronic emphysema, is a rapidly growing problem with global impact.35 COPD is the result of years of oxidative damage to delicate lung tissue, with resultant chronic inflammatory changes.36 The disease is worsened by air pollution and cigarette smoking, but is by no means limited to people with those exposures. Over time, victims’ damaged airways may become colonized with dangerous bacteria, leading to chronic infection and still more inflammation in a vicious cycle. Current treatment consists mainly of anti-inflammatory steroids and lung-opening medications used in asthma, with the addition of antibiotics when infection threatens.

With its ability to reduce oxidative stress and simultaneously quash chronic inflammatory changes, NAC is emerging as a game-changing therapy in COPD. A randomized pilot study of adults with acute exacerbation of chronic bronchitis and positive bacterial culture in the sputum demonstrated that 600 mg of NAC twice daily led to a near doubling of the rate of bacterial eradication compared with standard therapy, while reducing the number and duration of acute exacerbations and improving quality of life.35 NAC treatment of patients with moderate-to-severe COPD improved their physical performance on lung function tests, especially after exercise.37

Patients with advanced COPD frequently require low-dose oxygen therapy because of their lung damage. In many cases, however, oxidative stress induced by the disease has already rendered them glutathione deficient, so they have diminished protection against ongoing oxidation.38 NAC administration at doses of 1,200-1,800 mg/day along with low-dose oxygen powerfully counteracts this oxidative stress. At doses of 1,800 mg per day, it has been shown to completely prevent further protein oxidation.38 A dose of 600 mg twice daily over a 2-month period rapidly reduced exhaled hydrogen peroxide, a measure of oxidative burden in COPD sufferers.39

In one study utilizing a dose of just 600 mg per day for 10 weeks, NAC disrupted the molecular relationship between oxidative stress and inflammation, protecting lung tissue.36 When NAC is added to inhaled corticosteroids, still further reductions in inflammatory parameters are found.40

Emphysema can be the unfortunate endpoint of advanced COPD, with lung tissue breaking down and losing much of its ability to exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide. Animal studies show that NAC attenuates COPD-related lung damage and emphysema by supporting expression of important protective genes in the cells lining the lung.41

Another devastating chronic lung condition called idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) also involves increased oxidative burden and a deficiency of glutathione in lung tissue and fluids.42 This progressive disease has a poor prognosis, even when treated with standard corticosteroids and powerful prescription anti-inflammatory drugs.43,44 The median survival is only about 3 years regardless of therapy.45

Oral NAC supplements now offer a ray of hope for IPF sufferers. NAC significantly increases lung glutathione levels in both animal and human studies of IPF.42,46 Given as an aerosol treatment, NAC may delay disease progression, and at doses of 600 mg three times daily preserves lung vital capacity and gas exchange better than standard therapy alone.43,44

In summary, evidence suggests that NAC may offer benefits at doses of 600 mg 2-3 times daily for people who have, or are at risk for, chronic lung conditions such as COPD and IPF (idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis).

### Penny — 2017-07-14T19:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-ez-water-sea-salt.19695/post-218832>

It also is great for exercise induced stress:
**Reduce Exercise-Induced Oxidative Stress**
Health-conscious people know that regular moderate exercise is vital to maintaining the integrity of the human body. Of course, everything has its price, and the rapid increase in metabolic activity during exercise produces some unwanted side effects.20 These include an increase in oxidative stress that can overwhelm the body’s antioxidant defense mechanisms and lead to tissue damage and abnormal activity of certain immune system cells.47,48 Exercise also increases plasma levels of inflammatory cytokines such as TNF-alpha and various interleukins.49 The solution, of course, is not to reduce your exercise regimen, but rather to look for ways to optimize the way your body handles those metabolic challenges.

NAC, with its powerful antioxidant and gene-regulating powers, is an excellent means of maintaining good exercise performance and limiting the damage caused by oxidative stress in the process. Supplementation with NAC (2,000 mg daily for 3 days, followed by 800 mg prior to exercise) in strenuously exercising adults lowered key interleukin levels to undetectable amounts and abolished the exercise-induced TNF-alpha response.49 And in patients with severe COPD, NAC supplementation improved exercise endurance time by 25% compared with placebo, while significantly reducing levels of oxidative molecules released by stimulated immune cells.50 NAC supplementation also dramatically curtailed production of oxidized proteins in this group of highly oxidant-stressed chronically ill patients.

In vigorously exercising men, 1,800 mg per day of NAC prevented the expected decline in intracellular antioxidant levels and increased activity of the enzyme responsible for recycling and restoring glutathione to normal levels, protecting cells from oxidative stress.51 And in mice, NAC supplementation significantly protected brain tissue against exercise-induced oxidative changes.52 NAC also preserves normal levels of vital lymphocytes, which can decline after vigorous exercise.48,53-55

Regular supplementation with NAC at up to 1,800-2,000 mg per day may be an effective means of optimizing exercise performance while minimizing the effects of exercise-induced metabolic stress.

### Penny — 2017-07-14T19:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-ez-water-sea-salt.19695/post-218833>

**Bring Glucose Levels Under Control**
Oxidative stress and inflammation are closely linked to insulin resistance and rising blood glucose levels. These effects are not limited to those with diabetes, but in fact are found even in obese, non-diabetic people and those with metabolic syndrome.56 There are multiple steps in the cascade of events leading from oxidation to damaged insulin receptors and insulin resistance, so it makes sense to seek a supplement that can target many of those steps independently.57,58 NAC is emerging as one such multi-targeted supplement.56

Over time, chronic high blood sugar initiates a downward spiral by helping generate advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that then impair normal responses to insulin, perpetuating elevated sugar levels. NAC reverses those effects in laboratory models.22 Increasing blood sugar levels in laboratory animals triggers a pro-inflammatory response in fat tissue—also effectively reduced by NAC.21 In an experiment that recreates a common human dietary trend, rats were given a diet high in the sweetener fructose, which produced increased blood pressure, plasma insulin levels, and triglyceride levels. Yet all of these dangerous physiological alterations were inhibited by NAC.59

Human studies of NAC to improve insulin sensitivity have recently appeared, especially in a group of people typically very difficult to treat. Profound insulin resistance is seen in women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), along with a variety of other metabolic disturbances. One study showed that NAC at 1,200 mg per day along with 1,600 mg of the amino acid arginine promoted a trend toward normal ovulatory cycles and substantially improved insulin sensitivity.60 A short-term study showed that 1,800 mg of NAC daily helped improve insulin sensitivity in women with PCOS.61

Virtually all Americans consume too many calories and are at risk for at least some degree of insulin resistance. Daily supplementation with NAC at 1,200 to 1,800 mg per day may help to reduce the impact and slow the damage wrought by AGEs.

### Penny — 2017-07-14T19:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-ez-water-sea-salt.19695/post-218834>

**Cancer Prevention**
The strong and growing links between oxidative stress, inflammation, and cancer make NAC a natural go-to compound for cancer chemoprevention. True to form, NAC has multiple anti-cancer activities acting at multiple targets to provide layers of cancer protection against a large variety of cancer types. NAC induces programmed cell death (apoptosis) in multiple types of human cancer cells.62 In human gastric cancer cells, NAC not only induces apoptosis, but also stops DNA synthesis, preventing cancer the cells from replicating.63 In melanoma cells, NAC inhibits NF-kB, preventing expression of signaling molecules needed by the cancer for growth.64 NAC inactivates and promotes destruction of c-Src, a chemical control molecule that is overproduced in many human cancers, providing a completely unique means of slowing or stopping tumor development.65 Finally, NAC protects DNA from breakage induced by ionizing radiation, but does not prevent cell destruction by radiation.66 That’s a vital finding because it means that NAC might allow radiation therapy to effectively kill cancer cells while minimizing the risk of so-called secondary cancers that could otherwise arise as side effects of the radiation.

Animal studies strengthen the case for NAC still further. NAC protects mice from cigarette smoke-induced lung cancers and other lung changes, a finding with enormous implications not only for current smokers but for ex-smokers and people exposed to second-hand smoke.67 NAC protects rats from chemically-induced liver cancers immediately following tumor initiation.68 This early interference with cancer development bodes well for NAC as a chemopreventive agent in the many human toxin-related cancers.

Human studies are similarly encouraging, even in the most challenging patient groups such as smokers. A randomized, double-blind chemoprevention trial of NAC 600 mg twice daily for 6 months vs. placebo in otherwise healthy smokers showed a significant reduction in formation of damaged or oxidized DNA segments, telltale early markers of cancer development in lung fluid.69 The same study also demonstrated reductions in abnormal, pre-cancerous cell changes in the mouths of supplemented smokers. These effects support the scientists’ conclusion that NAC can reduce tobacco smoke carcinogenicity in humans.

Colon cancer is another malignancy with strong links to oxidative stress and inflammation. Preliminary studies in humans show a 40% reduction in colorectal polyps in patients given 600 mg per day of NAC, compared with controls.70 In a group of people with a previous history of pre-cancerous colonic polyps, 800 mg per day of NAC for 12 weeks significantly reduced the proliferative index, indicating a decreased risk of colon cancer.71

Supplementing with 600-1,200 mg per day of NAC appears to be an entirely appropriate means of adding to your general cancer-prevention strategy.

### Penny — 2017-07-14T19:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-ez-water-sea-salt.19695/post-218835>

**Gastritis, Ulcers, Cancer, and Helicobacter pylori**
_Helicobacter pylori_ is a bacterium that colonizes various regions of the stomach and upper part of the small intestine. _H. pylori_ infection produces major oxidative stress on tissues already vulnerable to extremes of pH and other chemical challenges, and the resulting inflammation produces pain and promotes development of gastric and esophageal cancers.19 NAC is an obvious candidate for fighting _H. pylori_ infections, both because of its powerful ability to interfere with the oxidant-inflammation connection, and also because of its potential to break down some of the gastric mucous layer beneath which the organism hides.72

NAC fights _H. pylori_ in at least two ways. It markedly inhibits growth of H. pylori both in culture dishes and in live mice, helping to reduce the total load of organisms present.72 But NAC also powerfully regulates gene expression in stomach lining cells, reducing hydrogen peroxide production induced by H. pylori, and decreasing activation of NF-kB and subsequent release of inflammatory cytokines.19,73 In human trials NAC improves eradication rates of _H. pylori_ produced by standard treatment with antacids and antibiotics, when given at doses of **1,200 mg** per day.74,75

People who have gastritis or gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) may be infected with _H. pylori_ and may benefit from supplementation with **1,200 mg** per day of NAC, especially during co-treatment with drugs to eradicate the organism.

**Summary**
N-acetyl cysteine is a broad-spectrum compound traditionally under-utilized in conventional medicine. A burst of new clinical research reveals that NAC exerts dual effects, functioning both as a powerful antioxidant that replenishes cellular antioxidant systems (glutathione in particular) and also as a potent modulator of gene expression, regulating inflammation at multiple, fundamental levels. It has been shown to be an effective intervention against influenza, chronic lung diseases, cancers, insulin resistance, and gastritis caused by _H. pylori._ NAC’s further value is shown in its ability to mitigate otherwise inevitable metabolic and immunological disturbances caused by exercise.

Do we understand the variables ?
I think all of us here we want to optimise ourselves and the most important to help others to help themselves without having to go to the guys with the white robes

# Thread: Golden Ratio and Szygvy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-ratio-and-szygvy.19697/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4

### bionaut — 2017-06-27T01:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-ratio-and-szygvy.19697/post-217153>

So since we are starting to talk about harmonics, there is an effect that I am curious about and it has to do with lunar cycles.

I look at the financial markets as a bulk collection of thought of speculators moving like a flock of birds. Sharp turns can be found in points of time. There is a technique which takes a natural rhythm of the lunar orbit in days and applies the square of the fibonacci sequence to come up with two seperate additive sequences. This then is applied to highs and lows, of moves and clusters of dates can be mapped in the future to dictate a potential turning point in time.

So in a nutshell the golden ratio can project potential future turning points in markets using lunar timing.

So here is where it gets interesting, below is an image of how the golden spiral maps in time through the lunar cycle. Typically, the focal points of the spirals created in time can be found in harmony with the saros cycle, and lunar alignment with the spring and fall equinoxes.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The most important one is the spring equinox + the new or full moon at that time. This can be a predictor of market crashes in the fall equinoxes two years into the future, when the sun and moon are in syzgvy (Conjunction).

This corresponds to the perigean spring tide. So in the image below you have the new moon configuration and the full moon configuration.

In the new moon configuration, the moon is closest to the earth and deflecting solar wind.

What is happening  in relation to forces acting on the point of the planet furthest from the sun and moon and on people and animals as we pass through this window? How are the effects of gravity, magnetism , dielectricity etc, altered at this point in time?

Secondly, in either configuration does anyone know of biological effects that would affect
decision making?

There seems to be a sort of bulk groupthink globally based off of this cycle that can reverberate years into the future or past off of this point. Financial markets are not the only ones reviewed in the book The Spiral Calendar, some geopolitical changes as well as hostage crises have followed this timing. There is a few examples I can post if anyone's interested, pretty neat to say the least.

# Thread: Hi From Portland, Oregon (moving soon)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-portland-oregon-moving-soon.19699/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MissyMoo50 — 2017-06-27T13:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-portland-oregon-moving-soon.19699/post-217175>

My main issues are anxiety, depression, and I fall to pieces over the slightest stress. But thanks in large part to Dr. Kruse's wisdom I am moving to Phoenix, Arizona to get more sunlight.

I'm 49 years old and dad flew down to Phoenix with me this week to help me find a house.

I have a chronic problem with staying up all night messing around on my laptop or phone and sleeping during daylight hours, which I think from what I've reading means i should try something called a leptin reset?

I'm not sure what EZ water is - is that bottled spring water?

I have always eaten a lot of junk food too so I want to learn how to eat better.

### JanSz — 2017-06-28T14:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-portland-oregon-moving-soon.19699/post-217327>

> [MissyMoo50 said:](/goto/post?id=217175)
>
> My main issues are anxiety, depression, and I fall to pieces over the slightest stress. But thanks in large part to Dr. Kruse's wisdom I am moving to Phoenix, Arizona to get more sunlight.
>
> I'm 49 years old and dad flew down to Phoenix with me this week to help me find a house.
>
> I have a chronic problem with staying up all night messing around on my laptop or phone and sleeping during daylight hours, which I think from what I've reading means i should try something called a leptin reset?
>
> I'm not sure what EZ water is - is that bottled spring water?
>
> I have always eaten a lot of junk food too so I want to learn how to eat better.

Hi MissyMoo50
Welcome.
Human race started some place around Lake Victoria in Africa.
Eventually we migrated around the world.
We are creatures of light, sunlight.
Sunlight provides (or should provide) directly 2/3 of energy we need for life.
The remaining 1/3 of energy is also from sun but indirectly, in form of food.

Sunlight availability varies with latitude.
**The fact that we use clothes** is a major factor when we are created to be dependent on sunlight being able to reach us.

Migrations that started at Lake Victoris took long time, many generations.
Our bodies, our mitochondrias had a time to adapt to new places.
New haplogroups developed over that time.
Thise days extreme migrations take place within just one life.
No time to adapt.

I assume that if one's haplogroup stays at about same latitude for 10000-15000 years that is enough time to adapt to that latitude.

From my personal experience, extreme migration is extremely stressfull.
I have spend one complete year in tropics. I was ok with the sunlight and heat and humidity, what got to me was very small variations in the length of the day. All days and night are real close to 12/12hr.
That was causing stress.
\---------------------------------------

Portland Oregon 45.5202° N
Phoenix Arizona 33.4486° N

It would be good idea to know ones haplogroup and figure out its latitude.
Below is my as example.

\========================================
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lauras-new-beginning.19663/#post-216547](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/lauras-new-beginning.19663/#post-216547)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=216524)
>
> welcome.......do you know your haplotype your mothers haplotype or grandmother's? if not........tell me more about where they come from and where they lived and for how long. We need to know more about that. Then on to you. Where where you born and where have you been and where are you now.

**Answer to this question may possibly give a clue to person's ideal latitude where one would look for residence.**
My maternal haplotype is H5.
23andme report have this line:
Your maternal line stems from haplogroup H5, a branch of haplogroup H that traces back to a woman who lived nearly 10,000 years ago, soon after the end of the Ice Age. The haplogroup appears to have sprung up somewhere near the **Caucasus Mountains or in forests near the Black Sea**,

Wiki gives me:
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caucasus\_Mountains](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caucasus_Mountains)
The **Caucasus Mountains\[a\] are a mountain system in Eurasia between the Black Sea** and the Caspian Sea in the Caucasus region.
Latitude 42°30′N
So, over 10000 years my ancestral women traveled **from:
42°30′N 45°00′E**
and My Mom have given me my life at:
48°37'0" N 26°10'0" E
My first 31 years I have spend at about latitude
50.28333° N, 18.66667° E
**and my next 47 years I have spend at:
40.8579° N, 74.426° W**
of which the first 25 years I have spend with my Mom in the same house.
And then she departed on the trip to see our **INTELIGENCE.**

So my current location is closer to Equator by one degree,
when comparing to the origins of H5 haplogroup.
**That is likely as ideal as one may wish for (if I am not mistaken).**
My mitochondria for 10000 years stays at about same latitude,
hopefully enough time to get used to that latitude.

Saga continues.

### JanSz — 2017-06-28T15:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-portland-oregon-moving-soon.19699/post-217332>

> [MissyMoo50 said:](/goto/post?id=217175)
>
> My main issues are anxiety, depression, and I fall to pieces over the slightest stress. But thanks in large part to Dr. Kruse's wisdom I am moving to Phoenix, Arizona to get more sunlight.

>

Leptin reset
leptin
leptin resistance

Please do not do blood test for leptin, (can be done but it is useless in addressing this condition).
Leptin resistance is a state that needs a change.

\============================================
EZ (easy water)
it is a state of water
solid, liquid, gas, 4th state

note that change of state requires addition or subtraction of energy

EZ water is able to exist only in very tiny places.
Some of our cells are too big.
Our EZ water is made by our mitochondria (it does not come from the water we drink)

When you drink water do not worry about its EZ content.
Spring water is very good for drinking.
\--------
Dehydration.

Number of different concepts. Often it is hard to figure which one is being discussed.

Thirsty person is dehydrated, should drink water.

Person can be dehydrated while drinking enormous amounts of water if his hormones are not functioning properly.

Person can be dehydrated while all swollen up (swollen=full of water), eating water pills to get rid of some water.

**Person can be dehydrated when his mitochondria is enlarged and not able to make sufficient amounts of EZ water.**

The last situation is mostly discussed by @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) (I think).

/////////

# Thread: Green Lights for Healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-lights-for-healing.19701/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dean6789 — 2017-06-27T21:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-lights-for-healing.19701/post-217287>

I talked to a woman who had a Healing Retreat for 40 years. She said that sometimes people would be carried into the retreat, and she would use green lights on them (a 250w "indoor/outdoor" light, as she called it) for 15 to 20 minutes at a time, four times a day, about 10 to 15 feet away from them -- and four days later they would walk out. She said that she had lots of "miraculous healings" over the decades using those lights.

She knows nothing about wavelength, just has certain ideas about certain colors. Says green is so healing because it's "like the color of grass and trees". So I found a manufacturer with some green incandescent 150w bulbs (haven't found 250w bulbs out there, but was told by this woman that 2 - 150w bulbs would work just as well) and they told me that they were "approximately 526 nm". I'm just learning about this stuff so I don't know much, but it seems to me that more like 380nm or 830nm - 850nm would be more ideal.

What do you all think about these green lights for healing?

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-28T09:58:01+0300

She said that sometimes people would be carried into the retreat, and she would use green lights on them (a 250w "indoor/outdoor" light, as she called it) for 15 to 20 minutes at a time, four times a day, about 10 to 15 feet away from them -- and four days later they would walk out. She said that she had lots of "miraculous healings" over the decades using those lights.
>

So I found a manufacturer with some green incandescent 150w bulbs (haven't found 250w bulbs out there, but was told by this woman that 2 - 150w bulbs would work just as well) and they told me that they were "approximately 526 nm". I'm just learning about this stuff so I don't know much, but it seems to me that more like 380nm or 830nm - 850nm would be more ideal.
>
> What do you all think about these green lights for healing?

I don't think its just about the colour we "see" with camera vision, the mitochondria and cells detect frequencies. I've got stage lighting filter gels, the Dinshah filters, and a Bioptron with standard and colour therapy filters. The important thing seems to be seeing what frequencies they subtract, add, dilute or intensify, not relying on the colour I see from camera vision cone stimulation. When I can't get natural sunlight, I use them with natural light through the window, incandescent, or halogen lights, which includes the IR frequencies. I'd be wary of using a restrictive range of one colour, particularly in an LED. Alright in an emergency, but used chronically or as a lifestyle choice .... ? That could become a real problem.

### Dean6789 — 2017-07-11T21:14:37+0300

RubyLux got back to me. All they were comfortable saying was this: "Â Green light therapy is most often used for pigmentation issues, like age spots or hyperpigmentation. It can be used alone or in conjunction with red light therapy."

Based on what I was told by the woman that I mentioned above, I thought that the idea was worth exploring. Especially since I'm just learning all this now. Using green lights for healing sessions just isn't making sense to me. I'll just stick with UV and IR supplementation for those time that I need to be inside. And use the Therabulb Nir-A a few times throughout the day.

# Thread: Science fat question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Billybats — 2017-06-27T22:11:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217291>

Hello to the geeks,

First I had no idea were to post this question. Sorry if it is weird or stupid one.

When one loses/ burns fat how does it leave the body. Is energy the only way you loss the fat. Does it become a liquid to convert to energy. Also does sweating mean anything with this process.

Thank you.

### JCDC — 2017-06-27T23:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217295>

Primarily CO2 gas. You literally breathe it out. About 80% CO2 and 20% water I believe

### Billybats — 2017-06-27T23:26:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217296>

> [JCDC said:](/goto/post?id=217295)
>
> Primarily CO2 gas. You literally breathe it out. About 80% CO2 and 20% water I believe

Wow. That I didn't expect. .really interesting. What do you mean water, that the fat becomes water, then comes out in the form of pee and sweat.

### kris90 — 2017-06-27T23:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217298>

Yes exactly. Humans reverse the photosynthesis process (that is using light to make CHO and O2 out of CO2 and H2O) via cellular respiration (taking the CHO and O2 and converting it back to CO2 and H2O).

Fat is essentially CHO, so when it's burned via cellular respiration, we are releasing CO2, H2O and heat. So we breathe it out, pee it out and sweat it out.

### Billybats — 2017-06-27T23:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217299>

Thank you guys. Awesome to know tje mechanisms. You would think then I would be thinner. .lol since I wake up though the night to pee and during the day. Also wake up thirsty and sweat alot. I know since getting more sun and eating at the correct time (daylight) I have experienced some change, and like you Kris and others have said it isn't all about food. I guess I am a mini geek deep down. I was the one asking why is the sky blue.lol no need to answer that one..Lolhahaaaaa

Thank you again.

### Optimalbound — 2017-06-27T23:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217301>

This Is Where Body Fat Ends Up When You Lose Weight

[http://www.sciencealert.com/this-is-where-body-fat-ends-up-when-you-lose-weight](http://www.sciencealert.com/this-is-where-body-fat-ends-up-when-you-lose-weight)

### Billybats — 2017-06-28T00:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/science-fat-question.19702/post-217302>

Choices, about light, and nothing else defines healthcare outcomes. What controls wet and dry season in the tropics where this country is?? Sunlight and orbital tilt do. Is this quantum information then sent to the food on Earth wirelessly by the photosynthetic web? Yes, it is. Does this guy or research project realize this? No. They have no idea about the quantum story built into life and food.

# Thread: Hi from Sydney.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sydney.19704/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mitchell97 — 2017-06-28T14:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sydney.19704/post-217323>

Hey everyone. My name's Mitchell and I'm a 19 year old from Sydney. I've been a lurker and a reader of Jack's for about 2 years now, so I'm definitely not new but I just wanted to join the forum and say hi . I came across Jack's blogs when I first started to have psoriasis issues on my face (after starting "paleo" around 6-12 months prior or so) and had no idea why and what to do. I came across some sites about Vitamin D supplementation for it and it didn't do shit, but then I came across Jack's blog while searching on Google, "Vitamin D: The Sunshine of Your Life?" which made sense to me to get it from the sun and not some man made pill. Of course I got some noon sun which was still winter-spring around that time and IIRC it went away within a week or so and completely forgot that I even had it. Around that time also I found some podcasts between Jack and Joe Cohen, and also Primal Edge Health's one which I was subscribed to prior to coming across Jack. Every podcast I've listened to with Jack automatically makes sense to me and resonates with me, and I'm not skeptical about it as other people are. I was also a heavy computer user and gamer prior to getting into optimising my health, but I never had any health issues to begin with; I just wanted to stay as far away as possible from illness because I know that is ramping up fast now in our world and I just want to be healthy.

I also had a job for close to a year this past year and late last year that I got rid of intuitively because it was ruining me. I was 50-100 metres away from a cell tower. No bueno. F\*ck that. It was from 7AM - 330PM and was about an hour there and back home, so I guess you could say it was pretty shit. Right now I don't have any desire or direction of what to do with my life, just that I want to be happy and get a lot of sun . Also going to Mexico at the end of the year to meet everyone. Can't wait. Thanks for reading if you did

### Technosmith — 2017-06-28T14:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sydney.19704/post-217329>

Hey Mitchel,

Howdee!

### caroline — 2017-06-29T04:08:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-sydney.19704/post-217401>

welcome Mitchell. I am down the coast near Wollongong.

### Mitchell97 — 2017-06-29T10:46:38+0300

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-29T11:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-to-the-sun.19706/post-217428>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=217419)
>
> See, this is where I'm confused. My haplotype is Northern European but Jack said Scotland and UK is not good enough due to no sun. So moving to southern Spain goes against my haplotype...

Weather patterns vary considerably over the UK, so I don't accept that I have to move just because I live in the UK. I look at it as my haploptype in the context of the exact position in the UK that I live. That context has to include population density, cell tower coverage etc, as well as weather patterns. I think that some northern European haplotypes can do well in the UK if they spend enough time in the sun when its available, and have the ability to store light in SQ fat for when light levels drop and/or its cold. The ancients managed to innovate ways to survive the climate, the challenge now is to innovate ways to survive the technological climate we've created, that stops us assimilating the light we do get. I've considered moving to Spain but it would mean dragging a reluctant DH, my relationships with my children and grandchildren would be mainly on facetime, and getting bucket loads of sunshine will be useless if my cells are bombarded by the EMF's and wifi and can't use or store the light. I predict I could get worse not better, and its too big a risk to get wrong. So putting population density as a top priority, living or working in a town in Spain, Portugal or Greece (or anywhere for that matter) would be out, before I even started looking at cell towers etc. ( @[seanb4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1910/), Gibraltar would be a definite no for me). Where I live now in the UK I have less than 100 people within a 1 mile radius, I don't pick up my neighbours wifi, and the garden is thriving - there's enough light if I stay out in it, and a couple of times during the winter its a 4 hour flight to Fuerteventura, something the ancients didn't do.  I love visiting there, but I wouldn't be happy living there for months on end. The location would become a stress, despite the sunshine. I now don't give a sh\*t about getting a tan, I want light stored in my SQ fat, and not leak it faster than I can store it, so that I've got something to tap into when the UK weather isn't so good. (I've hacked it in the last couple of months, its doable for a good spell followed by 5 days of crap weather, grounding as much as possible is critical in a low sunshine area). A couple of winter sun top ups ... and I'm thinking about a winter light recycling hack ....But I can't recycle what I haven't stored. And that means living in an area with low population density, less wifi, nnemf and artificial light pollution ... so back round I go .. to here for now at least ...

My take.
My mother and all of my grandmothers for the last 10000 years,
lived at approximately same latitude.
I am at one degree south of those latitudes.
I am basically healthy person.
I lived for a one full year in tropics, felt great physically but had a problem with short days, 365 short days.
I live at one place for the last 47 years,
no hurricanes, no tornadoes, decent humidity, no snakes, no tarantulas, no alligators, little snow.
While in Poland, 1940-1971, while on farm, air was clean.
When moved to big industrial city, air was dirty.
Eventually I (and some of my friends) developed sinus problems, resulting in surgeries.
Lucky for me, I escaped that surgery.
When I moved to USA, NJ, clean air here resolved my sinus problems.

One of the solutions is making sure to spent plenty of time outdoor.
I see people posting here, they located in tropical areas or right at equator, spending time indoor, suffering from light deficiency.

Problems people facing is inconsistent exposure to sunlight.
Most people have a skin that is not sun hardened.
During infrequent vacations, exposing unaccustomed skin to sunlight burns skin, that is asking for cancer.
**It takes one whole month of incremental exposure to harden skin.
It is very important to keep skin sun hardened (or else).**

£23000=$29752.8 month
£276000=$357033.6 year
good or not bad
\--------------------------------------
£2.3K = £2,300 a month = £27,600 per year. Currently.

\--------------------------------------------------------
365/7\*40= 2085.7 working hours in year
35703.36/2085.7= 17.118 dollars/hr
barely above hotly discussed desired minimum wages
people on government assistance make more

Hello

In the Leptin Rx is says this for BAB for carbs ( the one highlighted in blue):

**STEP THREE: EAT WITHIN 30 MINUTES OF RISING**

-   Make sure breakfast is little to no carbs (less that 50 grams)
-   **LOTS** of protein (50-75 grams)
-   **Overweight:** limit carbs to 25 grams
-   **Fit:** less than 100 grams of carbs

In the Leptin Rx FAQ, it says this, I will highlight in blue for both.

**How strict is the 50 grams of carbohydrates per day limit?**
It is strict for the overweight and not strict for the underweight. How should they be spread out over the day? The most carbs should be eaten when cortisol is the highest, in the morning. Dinner should be the meal with the least amount of carbs. Eat the most food in the morning, with decreasing amounts as the day goes on. As you progress, you will notice yourself naturally falling into this pattern.

### dusty doris — 2017-06-30T21:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/contradicting-info-for-carbs.19707/post-217637>

I have been wondering about UVA as well. I typically go outside naked at sunrise, which is currently about 5:20AM. I spend about an hour outside (till about 6:30). I wasn't previously aware of the UVA importance, just the concept of "morning light". So, I was curious if there was any UVA during that timeline and if I need to extend it or start it later in order to get the most benefits.

So, today I ran an experiment. I used the UV meter that Scompy mentioned in that article. It says it detects both UVA and UVB. It doesn't differentiate between the two, just measures those spectrums.

[https://www.amazon.com/Educational-...UTF8&qid=1485276935&sr=8-2&keywords=UVB+meter](https://www.amazon.com/Educational-Innovations-Atmospheric-Light-Meter/dp/B008MB1ZU8/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1485276935&sr=8-2&keywords=UVB+meter)

I also heard that you can approximate the UVA by taking going about halfway between sunrise and the start time of UBV according to the dminder app.

Here are my notes from this morning.

Location: Phoenix, AZ
Sunrise: 5:21AM
Dminder Vitamin D: 7:58AM
Estimated UVA Time: ~6:40AM

Using the meter, I got no UV readings from 5:15AM until 6:30AM. At 6:35 I started getting readings. I wrote down the degrees elevation that dminder gave me as well. The UV index on my meter hit 1 a few minutes before dminder did, but it was pretty close. I'm not sure what exactly the UV Index means on the meter, I'm assuming its just count of total UV, rather than specifically UVB as dminder said that I wouldn't have Vitamin D potential until well after the UVI was above 0.

Readings:
6:30AM - 0
6:35AM - 4 mW/m2, 0 UV index, 13 degrees sun
6:40AM - 8 mW, 0 UV index, 14 degrees
6:45AM - 14m, 1 UV index, 15 degrees
6:50AM - 18m, 1UVI, 16 deg
6:55AM - 25m, 1UVI, 16 deg
7:00AM - 30m, 1UVI 17 deg
7:30AM - 55m, 2UVI, 25 deg
8:00AM - 55m, 2UVI, 32 deg

So, according to the meter, it looks like I started getting some UVA at about 6:35AM, which is 5 minutes before the estimated UVA (based on the half way point between sunrise and UVB). So, in my location, right now, it appears that is a decent way to estimate UVA.

Another interesting note, is that I was told UVA penetrates windows, but not UVB. However, using that meter today, I was showing 0 UV through the window this morning. Even pointing it directly at the sun. Perhaps its just not sensitive enough or the windows can only block UVA up to a certain amount, then more leaks in. But, when I opened the window I was able to read some UV inside the house although not as much as being outside.

Based on this, I am now going to try to extend my session until about 7AM. Or at the least, I will always open the windows and door in my kitchen (which faces the sun) to allow some UVA to enter the home while I am cooking the family eggs and bacon.

Image nnEMF as the noise interferring with a signal, e.g. FM radio signal. In electrical engineering, we take noise for granted, as a factor that is there and affects the quality of the signal we want to transfer.
So what we do, is to find a way to amplify the signal and ground the circuit properly so as the noise's effect to be insignificant. That's what you should do as well.

Leaving in the city is very hard to avoid nnEMF, but instead of going through expensive solutions, you can simply make sure you are properly hydrated and get adequate salt (or simply drink - high quality sea - salt water) to increase body's conductivity - i.e. allow current flow more easily - and do not forget to be grounded as much time as possible during the day.
I know this is not a protection from nnEMF reaching your body, but this is definitely a way to "flush" nnEMF-produced current in your body, to the ground easily.
... and last - but not least - don't forget to get you daily dose of sun, as this is you amplifier.
**
The stronger the signal from the preferable source (sun and earth), the less the effect of the noise (nnEMF) in the system (your body).**

# Thread: 6 day water fast with loads of sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/6-day-water-fast-with-loads-of-sun.19709/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2017-06-29T03:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/6-day-water-fast-with-loads-of-sun.19709/post-217398>

Hi There,

Today is the last day of my 6 day water fast. Over this time i have only drunk water, got loads of sun, taken psyllium and activated charcoal 3 x a day. My routine has been normal. Wake before sunrise, go to beach with dogs....walk for an hour n a half, get ready and off to work at 9, work til lunch, come home and nude 15min front and back in midday sun (UV 9-12), go back to work finish at 5.30 , go to beach for sunset, then bed by 9.30pm. In addition to this I have had daily coloncs , i know Jack is not a fan of these but due to my regular travels i find its good to clean out every once in a while, especially with GMOs and alike lurkng in our food chain.

The point i have found very interesting is that with all the sun i have been allowing myself i really have had hardly any blood sugar ie insulin response. I would usually crash pretty hard about the end of day 2 into day 3 but this time it has been smooth sailing all the way through.

I have not been physically exerting myself but have been working and socialising as per normal (sans food).

The last 3.3.5 days i have been very keto and yday and today i have began to feel this in my muscles.
Today i will break my fast and get back on the food but the reason for my writing this is with all of the good water i was drinking and the sporadic periods of sunshine I really have had not blood sugar issues at all and , comparitivey to past experiences i was really amazed. This time it was comparatively very easy.

Just thought to add my brief experience

Cheers

Simon

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-30T10:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-my-cornet-meter-this-happened.19713/post-217557>

Opened it up last night and walked round the house. RF in the living room was off the charts even though we'd switched off wifi.

Discovered the 'alarm' system in the corner of the room was the perpetrator - we live in a rented home. I'd seen it flashing away in the corner when I moved but never really thought about it!! I guess that is Jack's comment about humans being shit observers

So my husband disconnected the system by pulling out the wire and wham, back to next to nothing.

The house seems good but the upstairs bedroom has a higher reading especially when the window is open - there is a cell phone mast in that direction about quarter mile - would that be it?

FREAKED THE FUCK OUT when I put the meter against my iphone. Seriously. Switched that bastard off and it hasn't been touched since. Now figuring out how to model my life around no phone or buy a model that accepts only 2G.

The induction hob as I feared was off the chart, even before I switched it on just holding it at the LED clock which we stand in front of ALLL the time.

Depressing.

The wifi was quite scary and now I'm working out how to extract TV from my kids as our TV runs on the internet - if this is well known forgive me, I stopped watching TV back in the terrestrial channel days.

So here's my question - I'm not sure what the 'normal' readings should be for using this meter. I still haven't figured out what the third setting is for? EFIELD?

Does the EMF Webinar cover all this @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

Or is there somewhere I can get decent information about what readings mean what using the Cornet? @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

### Scompy — 2017-06-30T18:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-my-cornet-meter-this-happened.19713/post-217621>

Heya Emma,
The quarter mile away phone tower could bring in higher readings depending on convergences and geography. But knowing the direction of the tower (if this is the only one at the moment), it can be easier to mitigate. If you are lower than the masts, like more in a valley, you may be getting more geographical mitigation also. I literally had convergence of a phone tower when I was working in Seattle where they had a small-cell on a rooftop and the building I was in was only about 300 feet away--looking out the other side of the building realizing I was parallel to the full blast of about 5 mW/m2 on average. I had to do radical things to minimize exposures... ultimately it meant moving. I know x2 more people that have come down with cancer since I've left the building. RF from a phone is about x10 this amount averaging around 50 mW/m2 within an inch from the body, but varies widely based on conditions like proximity to the tower (farther away, higher the number you'll see). In coffee shops, I see numbers like 5 mW/m2 if I'm in line and 2 feet away from the person in front of me entranced by the blue-light smurf.

### Sean Waters — 2017-07-04T13:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-my-cornet-meter-this-happened.19713/post-217924>

I sent them all my new number on WhatsApp, and then turned it off and waited.

All my close family text me the same day. Most of my best friends got in touch within a day or 2. A few others after a month or so. Some haven't bothered, but the ones that matter have come through.

It's strange, the ones I considered closest and most loyal came through the quickest... with an almost pro-rata of time/care for the others... more time = less care.

It's a good way to suss out who really wants to be in your life and who doesn't. Those that matter will send you letters on an Owl if they love you enough,

# Thread: Why is Jack such a vile arrogant SOB?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/>

### Paul Keenan — 2017-06-30T16:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/post-217596>

_'Change comes fastest to those people who are capable of unlearning and relearning. People who do not listen to feedback, will not be able to pivot based on changing circumstances, because they refuse to acknowledge reality. This is tied to a low dopamine state. Moreover, because they think they already have all the answers, they are not open to receiving feedback or contemplating advice designed to help them'.

'I look for and seek people who are honest about their limitations, and who are willing to put in the hard work needed to find out the answers in their environment to what they don't know, rather than someone who is happy remaining confidently ignorant'.

'Until you ALTER your thinking, you will always recycle your experiences...............I don't waste my energy or knowledge on people who “cry” for help but don't really want to hear the truth. Your intelligence is a powerful force that will change people's way of thinking, but only when they're ready. Paul is not. Most out there in the world are conditioned “pharmaceutical or food guru sheep” that have been led for years that masking the disease is the way forward not disease prevention or the promise of a disease reversal. I am not emotional about the truth's nature has revealed'_

How dare you. I have only just read this, I am furious. Ignorant? ABOUT WHAT? I know we need the sun. I know all about mitochondria and ATP. I have personally spoken, and exchanged letters with brilliant biologist John McLaren-Howard who developed the Mitochondrial function tests in the UK, which Dr Myhill and other use for CFS. I have constantly sought answers. Not moving due to debt means one does not want to learn? They want to remain ignorant and sick? What hogwash. Pure bullshit from a megalomaniac.

My thinking has got me from being completely bed ridden to walking 2200 meters this year with no NHS help at all. You have no clue you arrogant idiot. I have worked tireless to be where I am now. I could not feed myself at one point. No doctor has helped me, I have done everything.

Not everyone can implement everything Jack recommends. Jack presumes somebody is ignorant or deluded if they can not up and move to a sunnier location. As if everyone in the UK wants to live there. Or everyone comes to this forum not knowing anything about the sun.

My recommendation is to drop the arrogance and stop presuming people other than yourself do not know anything. To reply like this to somebody such as myself who has been through hell is absolutely vile, very arrogant, dogmatic and lack empathy.

Yes ultimately a change of environment is ideal, but many have to cope the best they can within their environment. Bear this is mind.

### kris90 — 2017-06-30T17:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/post-217603>

Jack is not telling you what HE thinks you should do, he is simply dropping a TRUTH bomb on you from an biological evolutionary perspective.

You can shoot the messenger, or you can accept the hard truth, and open your eyes to reality. Money is a fictional, materialistic, meaningless item that is in no way tied to our evolutionary biology or ability to thrive. Compare yourself to someone living in an off-grid ancient tribe at the equator. You have something in common: you are both broke. What is the difference? The tribe member has their health, happiness and freedom, but you do not.

Money allows a higher power to lower your dopamine, so it can control you. The fact that you think you need money to thrive in this world just proves that "THE SYSTEM" has got you wrapped around its fat, greedy finger.

The sooner you are ready to face the reality, the sooner you will be able to unlock the gate to optimal. We are all trying to help you uncover the "blindfold". There is nothing else we can offer you. You'll be ready when you're ready.

### Huck — 2017-06-30T19:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-jack-such-a-vile-arrogant-sob.19715/post-217624>

Paul, I shared that thought a couple of times, but recently I think I finally understood what Jack meant.

I need to move to a sunnier location and I have several excuses for not already doing so. In other words, I'm not at the point where I put moving as my number 1 priority. No matter how valid my reasons are for putting off moving, if I value my health as highly as I should, then I would do what is necessary to make the move happen.

When dopamine is low, it is hard to overcome obstacles. Really a catch 22. I need to move to feel better and I need to feel better to move.
