# Thread: Your smartphone versus your friends...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-03T18:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217855>

I'm in the process of hacking my smartphone. Ideally I don't want to own one at all.

I've just come off all social media (facebook and instagram) which has meant a lot less 'checking' my phone.

I'm getting used to not satisfying that immediate urge to look something up - you know, random things that come into your head and you just automatically google them?

There's something quite satisfying about waiting to look them up later when I get on the computer at home. but yeah, I guess it's all about convenience.

My biggest obstacle is Whatsapp. I use it to stay in touch with my friends through voice messages (I hate texting as it takes forever). I feel that switching to a normal phone would be fine but I wonder what would happen to my connection with friends.

My question is, if you have rid yourself of your iphone and social media how did you replace that connection with your friends?

Did you notice less contact?

Gone are the days of the landline just phoning people up, right?

It's sad but I genuinely fear I'd have a lot less contact through getting rid of this one app.

At the moment my hack is having the phone on flight mode at all times with the exception of once in the morning and once at night. I take it out of flight mode, allow the messages to download, put it in flight mode, record my replies and repeat to send.

Faffy, I kmow!

So yeah, just curious what others have found...

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-03T20:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217867>

Emma, I hardly use my phone and I still maintain my connections with family and friends. My home phones are landlines and everyone knows that they can call and I will pick up and if I don't pick up, leave a message and I will get back with you. Really, the only thing I will use my phone for now is an occasional picture and for emergencies.

I have two lady friends who I now meet with for naked tanning sessions in the woods and we catch up that way, out in nature and it's good for us.

### caroline — 2017-07-04T10:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217921>

I have always hated my iPhone .....hated being a beck and call girl. It is mostly always on airplane.

I keep in touch with a couple of friends on FB messenger - or whatever it is called. Just very short messages when needed.

I have had less contact the last few years anyway .....most people don't get my obsession with all things "Kruse"

I am finding that "real" connections come in different ways these days. Face to Face contact with strangers at the ocean or shopping mall or grocery store or parking lot have been a goldmine of very interesting and exceptional people. We treasure these chance meetings.

When you are open - people are attracted to you........We have made some wonderful friends from chance meetings. My Partner sold his business to someone who casually sat down beside us at the ocean.....

### Inger — 2017-07-04T16:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217939>

I never had a smartphone because I knew it makes one addicted, huh!
I have an old samsung phone that I use very little, it does not even have camera, no touchscreen, and no internet.... even this phone is almost too much for me.. I would love to have no phone at all, just land line. I use my landline when talking to friends, or internet at my computer, corded. I do not like talking on the phone or internet much at all tho... I love to talk face to face to people... I never feel lonely, I rather have the feeling I need to run away to find some silent time with me, which I love.....

In the evenings I switch off my landline and cellphone early, no one can reach me.. and that is for me the most valuable time. I love to completely disconnect! That is when everything that happens is so ... real

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-04T18:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-217946>

> [Mystic Rose60 said:](/goto/post?id=217867)
>
> I have two lady friends who I now meet with for naked tanning sessions in the woods and we catch up that way, out in nature and it's good for us.

Wow! How'd that come about? I'd love to find people on the same wavelength that I could do naked sun time with!!

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=217921)
>
> When you are open - people are attracted to you........We have made some wonderful friends from chance meetings. My Partner sold his business to someone who casually sat down beside us at the ocean.....

That's amazing! You're so right, I've often met fantastic strangers! I'm going to go out with the intention of meeting amazing people, no matter if I see them again or not.  Thank you!

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=217939)
>
> I never had a smartphone because I knew it makes one addicted, huh!
> In the evenings I switch off my landline and cellphone early, no one can reach me.. and that is for me the most valuable time. I love to completely disconnect! That is when everything that happens is so ... real

Yes, you've hit the nail on the head. Where is the fear to disconnect coming from? It's classic FOMO, isn't it?

Thank you, ladies. I appreciate your input

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-08T13:38:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-218236>

Emma, you're going to find that you are not going to be the one to seek them out, they will seek you out for the truth. That's what these two ladies did.

### Lahelada — 2017-07-08T15:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-218242>

I have a smartphone and I use it twice a day. I use it judiciously and my friends know it. My clients know it. They know not to send me cat photos or photos of their dinner. If it is real ,I want to know. If you have a problem I will spend time on the phone with you but if it is just a venting session I will cut you off. Limiting your smartphone use actually lets you sort your friends. There are the comfort friends who find solace in knowing that they are ok just because you are the same. They will probably fall away as now all over sudden you are not the same. Some will grow with you, new ones will be attracted to you. Friends falling away is not a bad thing. They were just hangers on . Have a read of this written by Neil BB, a great thinker . [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/on-the-thermodynamics-of-comfort.10786/)
I also heard it from an old man yesterday consoling the twentysomething about the dreary weather we have had for the past three days: "Chile,how would you appreciate a nice day if you did not know bad ones ? Life would be boring." There are a lot of "bored to death " people....

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-08T15:57:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-218243>

Smart phones make you dumb and subtract your longevity by subtracting your sleep. Once you realize it how smart where they?

[http://www.bbc.com/capital/story/20...nd-sleeping-less?ocid=ww.social.link.linkedin](http://www.bbc.com/capital/story/20170707-the-worrying-effects-of-working-more-and-sleeping-less?ocid=ww.social.link.linkedin)

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-09T21:00:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-218301>

One really has to be aware of what's going on around them too when they go out because the tech idiots are not paying attention . My brother got hit on his bike last summer by a woman on her phone. I've been rearended in the grocery store on several occasions. I think these phones are actually a form of assisted suicide.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-10T09:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-218339>

Absolutely. I think they should invent cars that make phones not work when you're in them

### Michalis — 2017-07-21T13:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-smartphone-versus-your-friends.19728/post-219600>

I am in IT and never owned a smartphone... People laugh a lot about this especially coworkers.. Someone called me a peasant, others asked me if i wear a tinfoil hat too. I mainly do it because i get stuck with technology so in my down time i would rather not look at the phone, the other reason is that i feel i would be "sampled and spied " 24/7 by all these apps... People walk around like zombies these days and they don't talk to each other.. It definitely means less contact because with a smartphone you are always on but the quality of the communication is not good as it is somehow forced.
If someone really wants to talk you they will probably call to arrange a meeting or drop by..

The other is time when we have opportunity to make vit D. That time is about two hrs before and two hrs after solar noon. That is the time when we can make vitamin D.
That can easily be checked by checking vit D levels.

# Thread: About me & need help to get pregnant!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-me-need-help-to-get-pregnant.19730/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jen N — 2017-07-04T01:14:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-me-need-help-to-get-pregnant.19730/post-217880>

Hello! I stumbled upon a Dr Kruse post in early September 2016 and have been reading and listening to podcasts ever since. I'm currently a student studying to become a therapeutic nutrition consultant and I'm thankful I found Dr Jack's site, as it will drastically change how I practice when I graduate. I stole Rubicon’s journal format, as I found it super easy to read…thanks Rubicon!

**Goals**
Keep Lyme disease and co-infections in check, after lots of work I’ve been infection free since Oct. 2014.
Get pregnant, ASAP. Many doctors have told me to give up, I firmly believe I can get pregnant but just need the right “hacks” to make it happen.
Live a long and healthy life

**Background**
Born in Metamora, MI, Weight at birth: 6 lbs Length: 19 inches
Current height: 5’4" Weight: 125 lbs
Married 18 years, no kids

**Parents**
My father is 68 and had a mild stroke in March 2017. He takes at least 7 prescription medications. He was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes a few years ago. He began smoking as a teen and smoked till about 3 years ago. He eat’s the typically SAD diet. He has also drank heavily since his late 30’s and I would say he’s a functional alcoholic, that behavior began about a year after his older brother died from a massive heart attack at age 42, his father died the same way, at the same age. I’m suspicious they both were compound homozygous for MTHFR. He worked in a factory setting from his early 20’s till about 40, during that time we lived on a lake, he was in decent shape, spent a lot to time in the sun during summer, and grounded often since he rarely wore shoes outdoors. He lives in Florida now, is never outside and spends most of his days on the computer or watching TV. Sadly, he won’t listen to a thing I say so I don’t even try to give him advice about improving his health and I just try to enjoy the time I have while he’s on this earth.

My mother is currently 66, she got pregnant with me at age 24, after trying for a year, I was her first child. She also began smoking as a teen and quit in her early 50’s. She smoked during the entire time she was pregnant with me. She has suffered with anxiety, on and off, especially during my parent’s horrendous divorce in her early 40’s. She rarely drinks. She ate the typical SAD diet up until about a year ago when she was diagnosed with Hashimoto’s. I encouraged her to see an ND and change her diet to help manage that condition rather than take Synthorid that her MD was recommending. She was a stay a home mom until I was about 8 and after that worked part time in an office setting. She is a self admitted sun worshipper, never wore sun screen until recently, and would mow our acre of lawn all summer in her barefeet and bikini! She was in great shape then and is still in what I consider good shape compared to most women in her age group. She does yoga regularly and walks. She spends a decent amount of time on her computer playing game and does watch TV. She thankfully will listen to most of my recommendations and says she is feeling much better taking Natur-throid and eating healthier. She suspects she’s had thyroid issues most of her life but it wasn’t until recently her doctor checked her thyroid.

**Body Composition**
I've been slim my whole life, I always needed slim size pants. I was a fairly healthy child. I had all the immunizations recommended back in the early 80’s. I do recall having strep quite often and always took antibiotics.

As an adult, I was quite thin, in my early 20’s I maintained 105 lbs. During the many years I didn’t know I had Lyme and co-infections, my weight would sometime get as high as 140 lbs. Since getting rid of the infections, I’ve been able to maintain 120-125 lbs without issue.

**Diet**
I was not breastfed, the doctors encouraged my mom to give me formula. I ate the SAD diet my entire childhood and I grew up breathing second hand smoke from both my parents. Cereal for breakfast, sandwiches for lunch, sugary and salty snacks, usually chicken or pork for dinner with a salad of iceberg lettuce or canned green beans. Occasionally, we’d have TV dinners.

Teen years was mostly of the same.

My mid 20’s to mid’s 30’s I decided milk, fat, and most meat was bad. My main source of protein was chicken breast, occasional sushi, and lots of processed “soy meat” and I drank a lot of soy and almond milk during that time. I still ate cereal for breakfast and pasta was on the dinner menu often.

In 2010, after being unsuccessful at conceiving for 5 years using every type of assisted reproductive technology, acupuncture, and Traditional Chinese Medicine, a friend recommended I read Nourishing Traditions. It changed my whole view of food and I immediately started eating the way the book recommended. Since 2010, I’ve followed either a Paleo or WAPF type diet. For several years after Fukushima, I was nervous about eating seafood so I didn’t eat much until about 2 years ago.

**Alcohol**
I've rarely drank, I have never tolerated alcohol well, usually I would get a headache after one drink. I have maybe 2-3 drinks a year. I dislike the taste of beer and most wine gives me a headache.

**Water**
I grew up drinking well water that was not filtered or fluoridated and I have no reason to believe it was contaminated in any way. My water source since 2012 has been from a well on our property that has continually tested clean.

**Environment**
From birth until 17, I lived in a rural part of Michigan on an acre of land. As a child, I played outdoors all summer, usually in my bathing and barefoot. I spent a lot time swimming and playing in the woods. My mom fertilized the hell out of our lawn because she liked it to be perfect. The water in our lake was not treated with chemicals. We also spent a decent amount of time outside during the winter or any other time the weather was nice. Our family was not an indoor family unless the weather was really bad. I LOVED laying in the sun all summer. I was tan every summer and the only part of my body that ever burned was my nose and cheeks, it was often sunburnt and peeling.

From age 17 till 20, I lived in a suburb in the metro Detroit area.

From age 21 till present, I have live in Santa Cruz County, California. I would call my current home rural suburban - the parcels are 1/2 to 1 acres and the home are surrounded by all types of trees including oak, pine, and redwood.

**nnEMF**
Between 1997 and 2013 I spent a lot of time sitting in front of a computer at work. I attended community college in the evenings from 1997 until 2000. I began using the computer in the evenings leisurely between 2005 and 2013. I stopped working in 2013 and now only need to use the computer for school, which is maybe 15 hours per week.

I have never kept a computer or router in my bedroom. I got my first cell phone in 2001 and didn’t use a smart phone until 2008.

I became aware of the danger of blue light about 2 years ago and would try not to use my phone or computer in the evening, this sometimes proved difficult with my former job. I still have my smart phone but it does not get used much. I was always wary of talking on the cell phone and have preferred to use a land line for phone calls when possible.

I have opted out of smart meter’s since they rolled them out in California. We did use wifi in our home between 2005-2016 and began to turn it off at night in 2016. We had our entire home wired for internet about 8 months ago and ditched wifi completely.

Our home has solar but the router for it is located in our garage which is detached from our home and on the opposite end of all bedrooms. According to Antenna Search we have about 7 large cell towers within 4 miles, the closest is about 1/2 mile away. There are roughly 171 small antennas within 4 mile sand the closet is also 1/2 mile away.

Our neighbors operate a HAM radio station and have a large antenna on their home. Their smart meters are also located closer to our home than their home and these two things make me a bit nervous. I have an EMF consultant coming to our home in a couple months. We sleep on a Magnetico and cut the breaker to our room at night.

**Exercise**
As I child, I hated the running the had to do in gym class. I distinctly remember having a severe pain in my side when they made us run a mile. I have never been a runner. From my mid 20’s to early 30’s, I would take weight training and HIIT classes at the gym, usually 3 days a week, in the evenings. Once the fertility issues began, I switched to yoga and I’ve felt that was better suited for me. From 2012-2015, I took midday yoga class, 4-5 days a week. Since I’ve stopped working outside the home, I spend a lot of time outdoors working on our yard and our homestead. I feel like I get great exercise this way. I have two dogs and I walk them daily, those walks vary from 1.5 - 4 miles. I now have a space to do yoga outdoors and I’ll begin my practice again.

**Light**
I feel the light situation in my current location is better than it was in Michigan. I’m still learning about light. I see the sunrise everyday. The winters here can be a bit dreary for a few weeks but much better than the several months of darkness in Michigan. I have never needed prescription glasses. I worked for an optometrist form 1994-2000 and he stressed the importance of sunglasses to “protect” my eyes, so I wore sunglasses most of my adult life. I ditched them last fall after discovering Dr. Kruse and had no issues adapting.

**Circadian Cycle/Sleep**
I have always been an early riser and seem always wake up with the sun, no matter how late I go to bed. In my 20's, I had minor issues “shutting” my brain off during those time when I tried to sleep. I have never had any major sleep issues. I am usually in bed between 9:30-10:30 pm and get at least 8 hours of sleep a night. We sleep with black out curtains to make sure no night light pollution gets in and these curtains still allow sunlight to filter in the morning.

**Libido**
I though my libido was “normal” but after stopping birth control at age 29 and getting rid of Lyme disease and co-infections, things really improved! I feel like it’s a normal level for my age but I wouldn’t mind it getting better.

**Stress/Anxiety/Emotion**
I believe I had panic attacks a few times as a child but didn’t realize what they were until recently. I was determined to not take medication for any anxiety that I might encounter. I do feel like I was quite edgy and anxious person most of my life but never to a point where I felt like I needed medication. I also had some moderate PMS and moodiness with menstrual cycle. I noticed a huge reduction all of these issues once I began treatment for Lyme disease. My former job in the mortgage industry was extremely stressful and I believe had a negative affect on my health. I have sought counseling with my husband to help us process our fertility challenges. I have also done EFT to help release stuck emotions around not yet becoming a mom.

**Supplements/Medications**
I have taken a plethora of supplements, antibiotics, and herbals since my Lyme diagnosis in 2012. I currently take very little compared to what I took during treatment. Currently I take:

Prenatal vitamins
Extra methyl-folate (3 grams)
CoQ10 (200mg)
PPQ (20mg)
DIM & I3C (400mg)
Milk thistle/dandelion blend (400mg)
Rosita cod liver oil (2 tsp/day)
Magnesium via baths and topically.

I sometimes take desiccated liver (when I don’t eat it regularly) or Oystermax (again when I’m not eating it regularly).

I take Chinese herbs recommended by my acupuncturist, these vary depending on the current health goal. I’ve been taking different blends for fertility over the past 8 years, with a 3 year break during my treatment for Lyme, when I was not allowed to try to conceive. I feel these have been of extreme help to my overall health and fertility. I also began taking bio-identical progesterone post ovulation, as my the levels in my luteal phase were low. I currently take 450 mg post ovulation.

**Dental**
I had braces from age 11-16. I amazingly have never had a cavity. I did have my wisdom teeth removed at age 18 even though I was not having any pain, the dentist said they should come out. One issue I noticed in my mid 20’s was gum recession near certain teeth. It seemed to begin at the same time I started having my first Lyme symptoms and my gums would often feel “achy.” It has not worsened but has not gotten better either. I would love to regrown the gums in the area that are affected, which are my four bottom teeth, two on the lower jaw near the back and the same two on the upper jaw. I’ve brought this issue to many dentist’s attention and most have told me it’s either from my teeth shifting back after having braces (I never wore my retainers), brushing too hard, or grinding my teeth. I used to grind my teeth in my early 20’s and wore a night guard for several years. I no longer grind my teeth and haven’t in at least 10 years.

**Genetic Testing**
My 23andMe results indicated:
**Maternal haplogroup:** U5, subgroup of R
**Region:** Europe, Near East, North Africa
**Example Populations:** Basques, Saami (Lapps) of northern Scandinavia
**Highlight:** Though primarily a European haplogroup, U5 was recently found in mitochondrial DNA extracted from the remains of a 6th-century AD Chinese chieftain.

**My Current Symptoms/Additional Health Info**
Thankfully, I currently have no particular symptoms I feel need to address other than not being able to conceive.

I won’t bore you with myriad of symptoms I experienced from my early 20’s until 36 when I was finally diagnosed with Lyme, Babesia, and Bartonella. I will share a bit more about my fertility history.

I took birth control pill from age 16 (my mom’s idea) until my early 20’s, when they began to give me serious nausea. I switched to the patch around age 25 and stayed on that till they took it off the market. I stopped birth control on my 30th birthday. Tried to conceive naturally between age 30-32, my husband is 4 years younger.

Between, 2007-2010 we did 6 rounds of IUI and 5 rounds of IVF, when I didn’t get pregnant after transferring three, day 5, genetically tested blastocyst embryos to my uterus, I realized the fertility doctors were clueless. Prior to the that cycle, I had transferred 2-3 embryos during the 4 previous cycle, none resulted in a pregnancy. Both fertility clinic's I've been to had no explanation and told me to get an egg donor. Instead I focused 100% on acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine. I wish I would've done this first.

Between 2010-2012 my Lyme symptoms were their at their worst. The steroids and hormones I’d taken for IVF made me much sicker.

Finally in 2012 a Doctor of Chinese Medicine suggested I find a functional MD to test me for mold, heavy metals, and Lyme. Once diagnosed with Lyme, I worked with a Lyme literate ND for 2 1/2 years, I did short courses of antibiotics and anti-malaria drugs combined with herbal treatments. In Sept. 2014, I learned about stem cell therapy for Lyme.

My biological clock was ticking hard because I was turning 40 in 2015. I traveled to a clinic Germany in Oct. 2014 and had thymus and stem cell therapy along with supportive therapies, like chelation, dioxychlor, and ozone to help get rid of Lyme and co-infections. They were hopeful the thymus and stem cells would improve my fertility.

I have felt consistently well and stable the past 3 years, this is a BIG deal for me. I did make a mistake in 2016 and took Nature-throid at the recommendation of a new ND to help “optimize” my thyroid to hopefully help me conceive. The dose was too much and within 5 months I had some mild hyper thyroid symptoms and slowly weaned myself off with the help of my acupuncturist.

I’ve also had an entire work up with the Alan Beer Center for Reproductive Immunology and with Dr. Kwak Kim at Rosalind Franklin University, I mainly wanted to see Dr Kwak-Kim because of the extensive ultrasound she does to assess if there is adequate blood flow to the implantation zone of the uterus. I was not interested in following her heavily medicated IVF protocol. My first ultrasound in March 2016 showed inadequate blood flow to the implantation zone and a small uterine fibroid. My follow up 6 months later showed adequate blood flow and the fibroid was gone, this was after 6 months of Maya Abdominal Massage, castor oil packs, and acupuncture focusing on blood flow to the uterus.

Recently, my new OB/GYN inquired if I had ever been checked for endometriosis, I have zero typical symptoms except fertility issues. She stated that a high percentage of women with unexplained infertility have asymptomatic endometriosis. I didn’t want to undergo the procedure to check for endometriosis and instead have been working with my acupuncturist to treat me like I have endometriosis using acupuncture and herbs. The results have been good, my cycles have lengthened and I definitely have been clearing something in my reproductive area.

My husband has been thoroughly checked. Back in 2007, he had borderline low sperm count and slightly poor morphology. He had a varicocele repair early in our fertility journey and it didn’t help much. What has helped is regular acupuncture and Chinese herbs, his count and morphology have been at normal levels for several years. We even had him checked out with the Turek Clinic, where they look at the sperm for DNA fragmentation, he was totally fine. Once again that doctor was stumped as to why we had not conceived. We are considering doing the new epigenetic test Episona just to make sure we don’t have a missed male factor issue and I have a call with Dr Turek to discuss if he thinks it would provide helpful information.

I was extremely hopeful getting rid of Lyme would be the last piece of the puzzle keeping from conceiving but so far it seems I still have some work to do.

I have recently embarked on a few cycles of mini IVF to save some eggs before I get any older. We have one frozen embryo in storage that we are considering transferring to a gestational carrier since I am not confident enough to transfer our only embryo to my uterus based on my 5 previous implantation failures of several embryos.

Since learning of Dr. Kruse, I’ve been nude sunbathing regularly, making sure to see the sunrise and sunset, grounding daily, eliminating all blue light, and reducing nnEMF as much as possible.

I would love more than anything to conceive naturally and prove all the fertility doctors wrong!

**Testing Notes**
I have had TONS of testing done over the years. I made Dropbox with my last 18 months of testing:
[https://www.dropbox.com/sh/9zfh2f5lbsi84q9/AAB71o7FEwMXC50Lks-lVzvEa?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/sh/9zfh2f5lbsi84q9/AAB71o7FEwMXC50Lks-lVzvEa?dl=0)

**Future Testing**
Boston Heart and SpectraCell Follow Up

**Optimal Education**
I've read EpiPaleo RX, Overpowered, and The Body Electric
I've read lots of blogs and forum posts, mostly those related to hormones

Like Rubicon said “I still feel like the more I'm reading, the more I realize I still don't know. More to come.”

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-04T01:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-me-need-help-to-get-pregnant.19730/post-217882>

"Our neighbors operate a HAM radio station and have a large antenna on their home." = fertility issue.

Move up the appt with the nnEMF engineer fast.

Dropping fertility rates in millenials = the blue light hazard and nnEMF from many sources (HAM) and most of the researchers have no idea how how the two are connected. Maybe they should speak to the Japanese opthomologists. Aversion to marriage and intimacy in modern life is not unique to Japan. Nor is growing preoccupation with digital technology. In Japan this syndrome is called the celibacy syndrome. It is well known there and many have linked it to the use of technology. In Asia this syndrome is associated with myopia. This is why the eye docs there are aware of the situtation. Why is myopia and low sex steroid hormones linked to missing AM UVA-IRA light?

Chronic man made blue light exposure leads to pseudohypoxia and low NAD+ at cytochrome 1 in mitochondria. This increases heteroplasmy rates and it lowers the voltage in mitochondria. What does pseudohypoxia (low NAD+) mean to our hormone panel? The cost of a high aerobic capacity is a low fertility rate; I mentioned that in Ubiquitination 6 blog post. This is why the pregnenolone steal syndrome exists in modern humans everytime I look and why males suffer low testosterone and females have an upside down PG/E2 ratio’s. They are all dehydrated too. This dehydration stems not from how littel water we drink but from how little water can be made by a mitochondrial when the voltage drop occurs in a mitochondria. This is also known as a low redox potential. If you are trained to observe it on MRI's you can see its effects within tissues of the body on MRI’s scans. It is not what the primary care doctors or the anti-aging docs think is behind this .[https://www.washingtonpost.com/news...reaking-out/?tid=ss\_tw&utm\_term=.091d037b906d](https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/to-your-health/wp/2017/06/30/the-u-s-fertility-rate-just-hit-a-historic-low-why-some-demographers-are-freaking-out/?tid=ss_tw&utm_term=.091d037b906d)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-04T01:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/about-me-need-help-to-get-pregnant.19730/post-217883>

If you google this topic with my name in it many things pop up.

Some one asked a good question today at the blog and I want to share it here: From the Hormone 101 blog post key point:
Dr. Mitch Moore asksJack so I re=read the Hormone 101 blog post. I read it to say high stress causes HIGH CORTISOL which, due to pregnenolone steal, causes decreased T3, sex hormones, sarcopenia and osteopenia.
But you said my cortisol was LOW.......and you wanted it higher
So low cortisol could be a symptom also of pregnenelone steal – right? And increasing T3 and vit D is the key to making more pregnenelone and other hormones as well – right? Why is low Vitamin D levels an epidemic?
Answer: @[Mitch](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1473/) when the effect is CHRONIC the decision in the cell is always on…..meaning all the pregnenolone shunts to cortisol to help you survive. This leaves little substrate behind to make the sex steroid hormones. It is usually associated with leptin that is not excited by sunlight below the skin in the subcutaneous layer of fat (IRA-UVA). When we measure cortisol in the plasma it is low…..that is a sign the PVN nucelus is working over time and this is sign your oxidizing your cells. This is driving the sympathetic part of the ANS. The result is all the hormones going the other way in the hormone synthesis chain are very low……..that is the reduction path. Re Read BG 11. Low cortisol= low melatonin = epithelial cancers = Leptin Resistance for some reason. Oxidation = LR. Low cortisol is not a good thing especially in the morning when IR-A light and UVA light are present. ### shiran — 2017-07-05T14:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-217995>

Hello everyone
My name is Shiran
I'm sooooo excited to be here , finally

I'm 32 years old
Hight-1.69 m
Weight- 58 kg
Married to Oded
mom to my two little treasures yair ( 8 years old) Ella (6 years old).

I'm excited to share my life story:

Born in Israel ( 31 latitude) , was very sick until the age of 3 , from asthma to Pneumonia
Mom smoked in pregnancy not breastfeed
Weight in birth 2.5kg
I'm the 4 child my mom was 38 worked in a kindergarten, befor worked in a hospital .
She got divorced at the age of 25 then meet my dad (his wife dyed from cancer)
When I was 3.5 we Relocated to South Africa,Johannesburg ( latitude -26)

For 4.5 years stable state
Then at the age of 8 my father (47) got murdered - childhood trauma

That was the first point my life changed ...

Went back home to Israel ...

At the age of 18 everything started to flout up...
Hashimoto not treated and eventually Hypothyroidism
A role In the army that included night shift and stress

A very early marriage (23)
Ulcerative colitis ( no steroids- , rafasal , mental stress) lasted for a period of 4 years on and of .

My fist experience as a mother was the second time my life changed..
my son yair was born with a genetic disease called GSD glycogen storage disease type 3a
Me and oded my husband are carriers we didn't know ..
It was a roller coaster ....
until the moment he died in my arms on the way to the hospital .
Blood sugar 18
Seizures
Coma

And no clue what is going on

He was a miracle and still is
His name in hebrew means light up
That's his journey here and he definitely lights my life and I'm grateful for that.

\----—--------------------------------------------------
when my health started to fall apart ...
At the age of 22 I started learning naturopathic medicine.
That helped me a lot
I helped my body cure itself totally from colitis, mentally and physically.
and for the last 6 years I'm totally clean from it
Like it was never here .

My colitis was treated firstly with Rafasal
Enemas of 1 gr and Rafasal pills 3-4 gr a day
For a few months total .
When it got in a little remmition
I adjusted my diet no gluten or milk and took supplements : l-glutamine, alovera , probiotics , magnesium And more .

Here we can not check RT3 trying to find it private

My current labs :
Free T3 - 4.6
TSH- 2.37 ( did take the euythirox befor the test )
Free T4- 20.6 (high)

Can my doctor give me T3 ?
If so how much to take?

I addressed mental issues with Teta healing Guided Imagery and much more.
For the last 6 years , I do not take any medical drugs for colitis .
As for supplements I take only probiotics .

**Family history**

My mom ( is 71) medical history:
Breast cancer age 60
Hypothyroidism age 47
High blood pressure age 60

Grand mom died in the age of 64
From Bone Marrow cancer

Dad was born in Morocco parents too
Mom born in Israel
Grandmother born in Israel -parents from Romania and Poland
Don't know my haplotype

**My lifestyle in the past year**

No wifi at all in my home.
No smart meters

Meser nnemf with cornet There is nothing significantft in the house.
We live out of town , 5 min walk from the beach.
North Israel next to the border
There is Emf here all Israel is boomed with it.

I Work from home so I get to be a lot outside.
Get grounded at least 1 hour a day
Get sun exposure every day :
Morning- one hour after sunrise grounded watch the sun with Swimsuit for 20 min
Noon - at the beach sun bathing with Swimsuit using dminder full body grounding as wall as inside the sea
Sun set- usually watch grounded for 20 min
Tan easily - now my skin is a bit dry

No sunglasses or Eyeglasses

RO filtered water

No blue light bulbs in the house
All devices have blue light filters
My blue block sunglasses are on the way.

Sleep in a very dark room
No tv

A **thing about my sleep**
For 6 years I haven't slept !
Since my son was born we woke up in the middle of the night to feed him
Now we have help so
Since I'm sleeping a full night sleep I'm feeling great
I dream every night and remember my dreams
Go to sleep 9-10
Wake up -6
I sleep for 9 hours +-
But still it's not perfect

The hypothyroidism is my main issue,
As well as pain in my muscles especially my back .

**Hormones**
Period age 12

Birth control pills from age 17-21

Last 2 years Intrauterine ring .
Good libido , especially right after period
Feel my ovulation
Brest painful befor period
Moderate bleeding without strong pain
Starts brown blood for 2 days
Then 2 days of bleeding
Then 1-2 days of A little bleeding

Both pregnancies are normal without fertilization
normal births without epidural
2.5 kg both
Brest feeding until 10 mounts both

After my second pregnancy 6 years ago I had a bad sinus infection that came and went eventually after changing my environment and putting my face in cold water its gone

**Food**
Protein and fat usually in the morning
Seafood at lest once a week
Butter,beef , fish , olive oil , coconut oil , MCT oil, lots of vegetables, eggs
a little local grains like wheat and some rice in the evenings
Try my best to eat locally by season

\---------------------------------------------------

So long story short...
my journey connected me with Dr jack kruse...
That is the moment my curiosity lightened up
I'm here to learn more
I have a lot of questions
And this is the beginning of a good story

\==============
euthyrox=T4
Active thyroid hormone is T3.
Many people cannot properly convert T4---T3
Selenium, Lugols, Natural Thyroid Hormone from pigs with some additional T3 may give better results.
Check your
FreeT3
RT3
to asses your situation.
Take your thyroid hormones always in the morning, chew before swallow.
On the day of blood draw for testing, draw blood first, then take your daily thyroid hormone dose.
FreeT3 should be in upper 1/8 of laboratory range or even somewhat over.
RT3 should be in the middle.

Hashimoto
TSH =1.2
You may get rid or greatly suppress your thyroid antibodies if TSH is nil.

If you wish, look up the references I posted.

To treat Hashimoto and thyroid will be hard to do without knowing RT3

thyroid
use Natural Thyroid Hormone from pigs (NTH) (some rabays allows eating pig in this form, it is medicine)
use enough of it until your
FreeT3 is at high point of laboratory range or even slightly over
Do that for 2-3 months or longer to stabilize
Then
you must know RT3, it should be in the middle of laboratory range.
If it is higher
Replace some of the NTH with Cytomel(T3) or other name, as long as it is T3.
1grain NTH=(38-T4)+(9-T3)
T3=4(T4)

Again;
after you figure all that out and stabilize, look at your TSH
If TSH is more than some very low value,
to eliminate or reduce thyroid antibodies
you will need to add even more thyroid hormones.

Eat thyroid hormone pills always in the morning.
Chew all of those pills.
If you go for testing, do that in the morning, give blood first then take your normal thyroid dose.

### JanSz — 2017-07-10T19:30:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-218377>

To convert NTH to T3 use this table:
3 grains is usually sufficient dose.
Some need more, go by blood test results
but
give blood for testing when you are on steady dose, every day the **same dose and time at least week** or better 2 weeks,
and blood is give 24hr after last thyroid hormone were taken.

Biggest Cytomel pills are 25mcg
that is about 1.25 grain.
When starting replacement use 25mcg-(T3) instead of 1grain.

T4 is slow acting (about 7 days)
T3 is fast acting (hours)
Sometime you may need to split T3 into 2 doses daily.
That complicates testing, do it only if you must.

Spend lots of time on the sun, naked 100% if you can.

### shiran — 2017-08-03T21:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-220859>

Want to share my thoughts
Since my son yair was born in 2009 , and we got the diagnosis of his genetic disorder GSD my sleep was really not sleep. I was a zombie for years...
until 2016 when we started getting help at night .. a caregiver
So I started living a normal life after almost 7 years without a good night sleep.

A strange thing happened to my body when I graduately stared sleeping..
I started to get mid back pain in the bra line every night it would wake me up
It's a very deep pain going through my muscles between the ribs spreading out to the sides but has a very deep core to it as if it is emotional
I felt my body didn't know that it is to sleep 7-8 hours straight without getting up from bed.
It got better at times And it even disappeared..

But the issue with pain in the muscles and skeleton is always in the background for me
This week I went to a massage since i have mid back pain big time ...
it literally opened a pandora box I am in huge pain since...

It made me think a lot about pain in a mitochondriacs point of view...
What aspect is the most affecting the muscles and nerves,
Why is my back in pain ?
Is every pain in every tissue similar?
Thinking about movement and moving the body in earth ..
movement and sound even a Dance all connect to a vibration a wave a frequency and eventually to light
Do what am I missing?

### Linz — 2017-08-17T20:01:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-221829>

Shiran,

WHO statistics make interesting reading. Alzheimers and diabetes have been racing up the mortality charts since 2000. Neither featured in the world top 10 causes of death in 2005 but were 7th and 6th by 2015. Alzheimers jumped from 9th to 3rd place between 2000 and 2015 in Europe and America.

[www.who.int/gho/mortality\_burden\_disease/en/](http://www.who.int/healthinfo/global_burden_disease/causes_death/top_10/en/)

**2017 September webinar for me = deep ocean in lights = Enlightenment**

Here I'm learning to journal not in my natural language and in public so...

A year ago I started a journey ... the journey to meet nature..

Now in these days... I feel her . she is changing and so am I .

The cold air in the early morning light.

The different birds that are singing their special morning tune

The star in the sky that I can still see with the first light

The sunrise that is sometimes hidden among the clouds

The session is changing. I year ago I chose to change

**What did I learn from the September webinar** ?

The process =the journey =the way ...

It's important

After 7 years without sleep my body said stop.

**What did I do in the last year to be the change I am today?**

I Chose to take care of my self .

I I brought a caregiver to take care of my son at night.( huge step)

Put a focus on my sleep

Uvex Blue blockers after sunset , if exposed during the day I use Blue blockers and cover my skin .

Eye covers in sleep

Ear buds in sleep

No nnEmf in the house

Cold bath before bed

Sleeping for 8-10 hours

Wake up with the sun rise

Get lots of sun exposure during the day+ grounding

My body responded gradually to the change.

my body embraced the change.

I felt I was Reborn

The brain fog started to diminish

Focus came around ..

Gut issues started to get well.

My skin started to shine

the color of my skin attracted so may responses the solar callus blog was bingo

My relationship with my self got to another level there for my relationship with my kids and husband got to another level it's definitely linked.

Now days my sleep is calm deep and straight

With the season changing so is me sleep changing ,

I wake up 30 min before the sun rises.

I get as much AM light as possible

My next goal is to get the 23 & me test
Going to understand the the why in me ...

The September webinar was about tony....

Every one has a little tony inside them

If you let the light go in you will definitely see it .

Thanks @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) for lighting up the way for **me** to reconnect with nature

### WalterNL — 2017-12-13T18:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-230242>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=230230)
>
> Your definitely right and jack imprinted it into me food is not on the top of the list.
> I would like to ask you maybe you know that's the amount of deuterium in san benedetto water, I drink RO and some San Pelegríno but looking for another good mineral water , what is the most recommended?

In terms of deuterium levels they are almost the same near 145ppm. ([http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0375674212002038](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0375674212002038))
If you want mineral water with less deuterium find one from a source that from high mountains and/or from high latitude. Glacial water implies both, usually.

### WalterNL — 2017-12-15T21:22:57+0200

Wines can oxidize pretty fast, best way to slow it down is keep air exposure to a minimum, and cool it down. Those gadgets to close the bottle can help a bit.

Progesterone on 4th day should be low,
but not that low.

It is good idea to map whole cycle

LH
FSH
Progesterone
Estradiol

### shiran — 2018-02-14T21:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-234760>

**

My Mexican experience Feb 2018**

The journey to Mexico was definitely long.... the jet lag was a perfect circadian mess party it was tough...
But let's move to the good stuff
The era next to playa is a paradise to nature lovers!!
We hooked up with senior Cruze , such a kind man we we're very lucky to meet him ( thanks Jack for the connection)

We went to several senotes , I have to say It was a unique and fascinating experience I loved it!!!!!
**Sac actun - my favorite senote**
There is magic ther I swear..
I felt for moments in time as like I was a part of the heart beat of the earth
Seeing a new Stalactite inside the cave formaition Made me think who I was in front of the vast universe
As the flash light uncovered the minirals in the water it made my realize my body is in water that is billions of year old
With huge heath benefits
I felt as if my body is soaking it up like a spunge
I was freezing my ass off and I was with a wetsuit. I was shivering cold not use to Ct ing in the dark
But It is small compared to the sights and the wonderful feeling I had there.

A weard experience the night we came back from sac actun ,
I had a Nightmare , woke up terrified , took me a few minutes to get over it , definitely connected to that cave ...

The sun rise from the ocean is amazing!! I was amazed Everyday again and again
And the strength of the sun so early in the morning is perfect!

I had to biohack my balcony in the hotel room so that every part of my body could get in the Mexican sun ...attached a pic ...
the food was good we ate a very good tomahawk steak in Harry's
Mexico is Definitely a place to go back to

### shiran — 2018-05-13T13:04:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-241106>

**May 2018 update to the win**
I can see an improvement in blood tests, especially in :
HB
Ferritin
Iron
RDW
For me it's just an excellent Example. And I want to share with you my excitement
For many years these values were low and as soon as I realized that they would come up only with the sun and not with a supplement I took it as a project and I can tell you that this is excellent proof that our blood works in a wireless Fashion with the sun
I have not taken any Iron supplement in the past two years
I have not changed my diet, I do not eat many internal organs..
I was diagnosed with colitis about 10 years ago, I lost blood In outburstsand suffered from chronic low iron and hemoglobin Today I have no sign of the disease, Completely gone!
And this is only after exposure of about a year and a half to the sun and using the sun as a healing tool.

I still do not understand how to calculate the ratio between UREA and CREATNINE
I would be happy if someone could calculate and tell me the desired ranges
UREA- 23mg/dl
CREATININE- 0.89mg/dl

**note**: I did the blood tests in the morning an hour and a half from the waking. I did not take the Levothyroxine before the test.
I did the tests in the 3 day of the period
the Cholesterol rose From the last time I checked ( Dec) Thanks again to the sun And the coming summer.

I'm still changing my environment as much as possible for my benefit In order for it to be With as little radiation as possible, and it's a big challenge here
And of course my light environment Was changed in my favor

@Janz What do you think about the tests?
Do you think I should take a folic acid supplement before pregnancy with a value of 29.5nmol/l?
(I eat green leaves regularly)
thanks

### Joyfun — 2018-05-21T17:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-241668>

May 21, 2018 Hi Shiran, You caught my attention because you live in Israel. I live in Kiryat Arba. I have been following Dr. Kruse since March 2016. I could not have been able to come to Israel with the Sciatica I had for about 5 years. Then about 8 years ago, I bought a red LED light healing device which cleared up the pain. I did a lot of research and discovered that inexpensive red/blue LED grow lights have the same healing results. Here is the site I use: [https://www.aliexpress.com/item/LED...922.html?spm=a2g0s.9042311.0.0.27424c4dg6rUPB](https://www.aliexpress.com/item/LED-Grow-Light-AC220V-2W-5W-7W-E27-Red-Blue-LED-Plant-Growth-Light-for-Indoor/32373193922.html?spm=a2g0s.9042311.0.0.27424c4dg6rUPB) I purchase the 7W size to be effective. The grow light fits into a little night light with a switch and then into a 6 foot extension cord. The light can be put directly on the skin where you have pain. If it gets warm, you can back off the light up to about 3 inches and it covers a larger area. The light is about 660nm, perfect for healing. Google "660nm red LED light Healing" to find more information. I was hoping this was the light you intuitively knew you needed. Love and joy, Jean

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-241951>

May 24, 2018 Hi shiran,
I am in the sun at 8am for 10 minutes setting my eye clock, 30 minutes at 9am eating breakfast, 10 minutes at noon and 30 minutes at 2pm eating lunch. When I combined this schedule with shoes off, my weight went down from 58.9 kg (129.8 lb) on April 18, 2017 to 54.2 kg (119.49 lb) on July 29, 2017. I was not trying to lose weight! But this weight is OK as I am 5 feet 4 inches.
Both meals are eaten in the sun. This is a simple way to be sure to get in sun time for busy people.
Dr. Kruse really knows what he says that sun plus grounding and good water are a must.
I only wear grounding shoes now. My first pair was thong sandals: [http://www.groundology.co.uk/earthing/grounding-footwear](http://www.groundology.co.uk/earthing/grounding-footwear). The price for the pair I got was apx. 219 NIS (apx $61). Other grounded shoes are more expensive, but perhaps worth the money for the health results that any grounding shoes give.

### shiran — 2018-06-14T07:03:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-243374>

So happy to share with you something exciting that happened to me this week.
This week I first spoke to a group of people and not about food As expected from a Naturopathic consultant..
I spoke about how to create a healthy relationship with the environment through water light and magnetism.
And that is the name of my lecture.
I see it as a mission, And a challenge personally
To simplify the material for people and to create practical tools for their implementation.
I was surprised to find out that people are curious, want to know more about the subject and want to act. I built them a first step guide that I hand out at the end of the lecture
Here, five years ago, I began to study the principles of water and magnetism and I am grateful for it!
you guys are welcome to visit and LIKE my facebook page ...Even though it is in Hebrew
[https://www.facebook.com/shiranlight/?ref=bookmarks](https://www.facebook.com/shiranlight/?ref=bookmarks)

have An amazing day full of light!

### WalterNL — 2018-06-17T18:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-243609>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=243372)
>
> I wake up without a watch independently at Around 4 am
> Go to sleep before 10 PM
> I sleep well
> First light now is around 4:50
> If I wake up before I hear the birds and before first light does it mean that the clock in my body is not well tuned?

Check out this paper: [https://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(15)01157-4](https://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822\(15\)01157-4)

> **Highlights**
>
> -   •Preindustrial societies in Tanzania, Namibia, and Bolivia show similar sleep parameters
> -   •They do not sleep more than “modern” humans, with average durations of 5.7–7.1 hr
> -   •They go to sleep several hours after sunset and typically awaken before sunrise
> -   •Temperature appears to be a major regulator of human sleep duration and timing

So I wouldnt worry about waking up before sunrise.

> So I wouldnt worry about waking up before sunrise.

Sleep is a very sensitive part of me. Now I have the best sleep I've ever had in years. I get up half an hour before the birds begin to squeak, it's amazing what happens to the body when That the clock in the body is ticking properly

### shiran — 2019-01-05T17:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-259495>

As for 2018 summery I think this summer was pretty good regarding “Skin in the game “
I knew I wanted to get pregnant so I got as much sun as possible starting in the IR sun in the sunrise didn’t miss even one!
The got the Transition to UVA as much as I could, tried to stay out with a kiniki almost every day until 15:00 .
Got my blue light under control
Me and my husband are so fertile we smell each other and I’m pregnant no problems there...
And at September when I knew I got pregnant I tried to eat as much seafood as possible
At 7-10 weeks it was difficult to get it down
Had a little bit of nausea and a bit heartburn but not strong as the two others pregnancy’s this one was different... everything much easier.
I had a sense of smell like a sharks
So sharp! It’s amazing how nature makes a pregnant woman so sensitive to the environment
The one thing that was difficult for me mentally was waiting till week 12 to know if the baby was carrying the gene disorder that my son yair has , we didn’t want to bring another child with GSD to the world, so waiting so log without telling anyone was difficult

After week 12 I managed to eat seafood with no difficulty, oysters and shrimps for the win!!
I have no vomiting problems and overall I got to week 17 with no significant issues

I do have these few last weeks muscles cramps in my feet my toes especially and only at night
I had it also in the two other pregnancies
I eat seafood for magnesium and took only once a supplement but felt it was not good for me
Only hot water helps is get released I put now also some infrared light on my feet it helps too
I felt the baby moving the first time last week it’s an amazing feeling that can’t be expressed in words every pregnancy it feels like the first time
I wonder if the IR light in the sun penetrates the belly and gets to the baby directly? Maybe Jack can answer that for me...
I’ll keep you guys updated
I’m 5 mouths in and 4 more to go ..
I have a small little belly like my other pregnancy’s were , can’t see I’m pregnant until 7-8 mouths.. kind of nice
Usually my baby’s come out 2.5 kg..
wish you all a sunny 2019 happy new year

### JanSz — 2019-01-05T18:46:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-259504>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=259495)
>
> Had a little bit of nausea and a bit heartburn
>
> I do have these few last weeks muscles cramps in my feet my toes especially and only at night

heartburn---> lower esophageal sphincter not fully closed.
that sphincter senses stomach acid acidity level
closes only when it is low enough.

You may benefit by eating lime juice
or stomach acid pills.
Betaine Hydrochloride with Pepsin
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000OYMGU4/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000OYMGU4/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

\----------------------------------------------------------------
muscles cramps in my feet my toes

may be due to low potassium
may want to check that by eating

NoSalt Sodium-Free Salt Alternative
[https://www.amazon.com/NoSalt-Sodium-Free-Salt-Alternative-11/dp/B000H185N6/ref=sr\_1\_4\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1546706691&sr=8-4&keywords=no+salt+salt+substitute&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/NoSalt-Sodium-Free-Salt-Alternative-11/dp/B000H185N6/ref=sr_1_4_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1546706691&sr=8-4&keywords=no%2Bsalt%2Bsalt%2Bsubstitute&th=1)

### shiran — 2019-06-04T16:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-271989>

Hello Mito friends
Haven’t posted a long time .. I was working on optimizing my pregnancy
I gave birth on the 20 of may to my amazing wonder of life .
I had the most beautiful birth experience
Naturally no drugs, in the hospital but in a natural labor room were I could move be in water and just give birth naturally without any medical intervention except when
In my pregnancy I was diagnosed with GBS so I had to get antibiotics for that before my labor

It’s so amazing we women can experience this kind of wonder in our life I’m so grateful
His weight at birth was 2.700 kg
I’m breastfeeding
I started this summer with vitamin D of 155 all winter and during my pregnancy I was in the sun every day
Here in Israel they recommend giving vitamin D drops to all baby’s

Do you think I should give him ? [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

I do put him naked in the am sun then a few minutes in solar noon but not every day

His stomach is hurting now at the age of 2 weeks , I wonder if it’s somehow a part of all baby’s growing with a new digestive system that need to cope to the new world or is it something else...
Thanks have a good week everyone

### JanSz — 2019-06-04T17:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-271995>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=271989)
>
> In my pregnancy I was diagnosed with GBS so I had to get antibiotics for that before my labor

Guillain-Barré syndrome (**GBS**) is a rare neurological disorder in which the body's immune system mistakenly attacks part of its peripheral nervous system—the network of nerves located outside of the brain and spinal cord.

..

### shiran — 2019-06-04T19:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-this-is-me.19734/post-272006>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=271995)
>
> Guillain-Barré syndrome (**GBS**) is a rare neurological disorder in which the body's immune system mistakenly attacks part of its peripheral nervous system—the network of nerves located outside of the brain and spinal cord.

Regarding your back pain. I think I have the same thing you describe. Over the winter to a Dr, I described it as rhomboid pain widely across the whole middle back under the shoulder blades; but the rhomboids might be between the shoulder blades. For awhile this summer (my first in the sunshine), I thought it was because of a sleeping position; but then I had an epiphany hit me. What if it were kidney pain or adrenal pain? Mine seems kinda high compared to where I think the kidneys are, but who really knows exactly where the adrenals might be and where the pain would manifest. As the sunshine affected me this summer, the pain went down, but it lingers mildly after cloudy days still.

edit: i forgot to add that at the worst times of the pain this past winter and past couple years, it was located right at or on the muscle group that contracted highly during coughs and sneezes. At certain times, maybe 3 or 4 times total spaced over a few months, there was a spontaneous muscle spasm right at that pain site that woke me violently from sleep; wrenching my back in extreme pain, the worst it ever was. I was almost surprised that my back didn't break from those muscle spasms.

I think those nervous system spasms are/were from mold toxin issues and maybe even the pain itself. Of shoemaker's list of symptoms, I have shown all but 5 or so at the height of my issues. This list: [https://www.survivingmold.com/mold-symptoms](https://www.survivingmold.com/mold-symptoms)

### Dennis Clark — 2017-07-05T19:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-for-fuel-new-book-by-mercola.19737/post-218020>

The good news in this book is its emphasis on mitochondrial health. It touts "mitochondrial metabolic therapy" throughout. Bringing issues of mitochondrial health to the public is a great start, since the populace at large is still mostly unaware of what mitochondria are.

This book will probably be the topic of a post on my own blog, and those of others, soon. However, as far as the book generally goes, I'll just start with this quote:

"Hands down, changing your diet is the single most powerful thing you can do to improve the health of your mitochondria. Fasting is a close second..." (p. 237).

This is how the first paragraph begins in Chapter 11, Other Ways to Improve Your Mitochondrial Health. The best ideas for mitochondrial health are here, stashed in the final chapter of the book. Hmm.

Readers of this forum no doubt have a LOT to say about that!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-07T02:32:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-for-fuel-new-book-by-mercola.19737/post-218127>

Here is what you won't find in a diet book: REAL SCIENCE OF LIGHT:
This will shock the eye docs (and food guru's writing diet books) because they all were taught to believe in medical school as I was that the anterior chamber of the eye blocks all UV light. The picture below shows that this is false. It turns out recent data over the last ten years now shows that is utter bullocks. Now their disbelief will rise further. The small amount of UV light that does get through is the key stumulus to the melanocortin pathways I wrote about in my Cold Thermogenesis blog from long ago. What does it stimulate? POMC. Recent scientists 92007) have found another surprising link for clinicians beliefs but it has not been well publicized by those in know. The link is between tanning and cancer: p53--a gene long implicated as a cancer suppressor--appears to be key to helping us get a tan to build out more melanin to abosrb even more UV light.

The simple act of catching a few rays that get through the lens and cornea sets a complex biological process into motion. In response to ultraviolet (UV) light from the sun the eye is capable of using a second messenger system to activate the skin cells. This is done via POMC. Skin cells called kerotinocytes begin pumping out melanocyte-stimulating hormone (MSH), which in turn binds to MSH receptors on pigment-producing skin cells called melanocytes which sit right above human fat mass in the subcutaneous space. That mass is where leptin sits awaiting to be activated by solar photons to tell the hypothalmus just how much energy is in the body photoelectrically to make a stochastic calculus guess of how much food a human would need based upon the incident EMF's from the sun a human actually gets. This starts a cascade of events that leads to melanin production and a nice brown tan that increases our ability to absorb more sunlight because melanin is a UV fluorophore protein. Researchers at Dana Farber went even further. Even artificially raising levels of p53 with small amounts of UV light in cells boosted POMC expression, buttressing the idea that p53 activates POMC. Are you still afraid of the sun? Maybe you should be more worried about your glasses, sunglasses or your IOL implants now huh?In turning on POMC with UV light, p53 coincidentally prompts production of the peptide beta-endorphin, the brain's natural opiate and another POMC product. This opiate is built into the structure of POMC and it appears nature has built humans to become addicted to solar exposure. How is that for a reversal of forture for our pals in dermatology and opthomology? [https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn1227-exposing-eyes-alone-to-uv-light-can-trigger-a-tan/](https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn1227-exposing-eyes-alone-to-uv-light-can-trigger-a-tan/)

Change is difficult for low dopamine people (even doctors) because their brain consistently overestimates the value of what they presently have while simulataneously underestimating the value of what they may gain by giving that up. Unless you are prepared to give up something valuable to you right now will never be able to truly change at all, because you'll be forever in the control of things you can't give up. Low dopamine blinds us to this reality and keeps us unskilled in communicating with ourselves or nature.

This is the critical step in front of the leptin receptor in the central retinal pathways.. Well with your cancer, using ketosis for this specific diagnosis first, is acceptable. But as you know I don’t like settling for acceptable. I want people to understand why different tissues have different rules of quantum engagement. Mitochondrial density and hard core cell differentiation are one. In the brain neurons are at the their terminal pit stop from an stem cell transport. If you employ ketosis alone and do nothing else what might this do to a brain tumor? It might cause de-differentiation. Guess what we neurosurgeons see in grade 1-3 gliomas all the time? DE-DIFFERENTIATION TO A GBM. That is my concern.........a food guru does not go this deep hence my concern for the unripe mind. Dr. Clark: Thanks for the heads up. Possibly you can shed some light (no pun intended) on another issue. I paid my derm doc a visit today and told him about my daily routine of getting sun exposure in my eyes at first light. He went ballistic on me explaining the sun's rays are (UVA) dangerous at any time of day. He encouraged me to refrain from further exposure. He gave me a paper with the following quotes from doctors

"While UVB rays peak in intensity in the middle of the day, UVA rays remain fairly consistent in intensity from morning until evening. Likewise, the ability of UVA rays to damage your skin remains high throughout the day from morning until evening (not just during the middle of the day when the sun is high in the sky, but all day). This means that even when you are not tanning or getting burned by UVB rays, you may still be sustaining serious damage from UVA radiation—without ever realizing it!"

"The reason the sun is harmful is that UVA rays are out in full intensity; they’re out all day, all year, and at the same intensity as mid-day in the summer! This means that from sunup to sundown, January or July you’re getting the same hit of UVA. Plus, UVA penetrates your skin more deeply than UVB causing significant and irreversible damage to the deeper parts, and this causes wrinkles. As if that’s not enough, your sunscreen SPF value tells you nothing about how well your product blocks UVA. In fact many products don’t do a good job blocking the UVA rays, which is one of the big criticisms of sunscreens."

I am concerned about this as I have a history of both basal and squamous cell carcinoma. If I were to follow his guidelines would the Quantlet be a viable alternative?

# Thread: Neuropathy Symptoms down the legs and hormone problems?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuropathy-symptoms-down-the-legs-and-hormone-problems.19739/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joyzoggia — 2017-07-06T17:08:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/neuropathy-symptoms-down-the-legs-and-hormone-problems.19739/post-218088>

Hello Everyone,

I'm new to the forum, I have ended up here multiple times during my long journey trying to figure out what is wrong with me so I figure I should maybe post in here and see if I get a good opinion...

It has been over a year that I have been dealing with strange sensations down my legs ( mainly at night at the beginning). Felt a lot like Peripheral Neuropathy at the beginning. At the time I went ahead and got an MRI to find out I didn't really have anything in my spine, got checked for diabetes also nothing (did a 2hr glucose test). Most of the time these sensations down my legs would get worse when I would eat dairy, gluten, sugar in general, certain nuts and sometimes even chicken. It was almost impossible to predict what would give me these things. I also always felt very angry, a lot of mood swings, depression, anxiety, the list keeps going...

I went to an Endocrinologist and got my thyroid and other hormones checked:

**LH** 15.4 mIU/mL / **FSH** 6.4 mIU/mL

**Calcium** 5.6 mg/dL / **DHEA-Sulfate** 206.2 ug/dL

**TSH** 2.080 uIU/ml / **Prolactin** 24.8 ng/ml (came out high)

**Estradiol** 34 pg/ml / **17-OH-Progesterone LCMS** 91 ng/dL

**Vitamin D 25-Hydroxy** 37 ng/mL / **Total T4** 7.2 ug/dL

**Free T3** 2.2 pg/mL / **PTH** 28 pg/mL / **Cortisol-AM** 28.8 ug/dL (high)

**Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO)Ab** 23 IU/mL

( I have many more labs that I have done but thought these would be the most important)

While my Endocrinologist said "Everything looks fine" my primary care physician really didn't think so... she put me on a low dose Nature-Throid because she thinks my t3 was too low. At the beginning I got some relief ( but it literally lasted only 3 days..) She also told me to go back to my vitamin d3, something my endo had told me to stop because my d3 was "fine". I always feel a little bit of relief when I take it but doesn't last very long during the day.

I also haven't had my period in over 3 years naturally... since I was a kid I was always given birth control pills but no one really told me why, I'm from brazil and there I was given one called " Ciclo Elani" which is drospirenona 3mg and etinilestradiol 0.03mg. 4 years ago I decided to stop because I was tired of the bad rep birth control pills had and it also always messed up my stomach.

My primary care has different opinion as far as what is going on with me, she is positive I have leaky gut, which with the fact that I respond terribly to multiple foods makes me agree with it. I suspect candida but the symptoms of it match a lot estrogen dominance as well, which I'm not 100% sure if I do have, I read online about the progesterone/Estradiol ratio and when I calculated it, it seemed like I was really estrogen dominant.

I know Dr Jack talks about neuropathy and the effect estrogen, and multiple other factors that a lot of neurologist wouldn't think about, have on the nerves. He also talks about how any cause of leaky gut can also be an etiology for neuropathy even in a non diabetics.

I'm so lost at this point, I went to a gastro and he didn't want to test me, he put me right away on Xifaxan (antibiotics) and gave me VSL#3 (powerful probiotic) to do right after the treatment. I haven't been feeling really good since then... Not sure if it would be die off symptoms at this point or if it is just all of the other problems that could be going on with me. I know the antibiotics wouldn't take care of candida if I did have it so I'm on a very low car, low sugar diet during the treatment, and yet gaining weight. I constipate a lot, and it's always a surprise how my stool is gonna look like when I do get to use the bathroom.

I'm only 24, and all of this came to me as a surprise, it's been over a year that I have been dealing with this, and yet I was the healthiest person you could think of, I used to compete as a bodybuilder so to me diet and health was top priority. Not being able to barely walk my dogs now has been extremely depressing... exercising has been the biggest pain of my day too, sometimes I can barely get out of bed. I have a feeling of inflammation all over my body constantly during the day at this point, my legs almost feel like they have extreme water retention and causes to give me these symptoms. and I'm getting pretty frustrated with doctors, jumping from one place to another, a many trying to push me to go to a reumatologist so they can tell me is all in my head and I have fibromyalgia... which I do not believe on, is a symptoms, not a disease.

Any help or thoughts would be extremely appreciated, thank you so much for your time.

Joy

So, I could use some puzzle piecing and dot connecting on this issueive been dealing with for a while..like over a year. I'm in the process of hacking my environment right now. Got my grounding sheet. West my blue blockers...got earthing sandals coming, and bout to make a bunch of emf protective under garments made out of argen mesh...of course I go out first thing and ground blah blsh blsh...now I have structural issues...pinched nerves and all that, that I'm self treating with yoga self massage..stretching...etc etc...if anyone can accelerate my process I would be eternally grateful. Thanks mil?ion

### seanb4 — 2017-07-10T11:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-gastro-paresis.19740/post-218345>

I have gastroparesis. It would be helpful to know what you think caused it. If it's diabetes, I think ketosis would help, but I am sure you are already doing that. If it's pinched nerve, I am not sure. For me, it is problems with the autonomic system. I have noticed an increase in transit time from Helminthic therapy. There is the usual advice of not drinking water around meals, but I find it helpful to not do anything that requires high blood flow after eating, or vasodialation. I now avoid heat and make sure I am cold, I think this helps blood flow as heart doesn't have to pump extra to the gut and skin at the same time.

# Thread: Cold Adaptation Effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2017-07-07T22:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218192>

I wanted to start a thread for those who consider themselves cold adapted, and living in the ancient pathway. What sort of effects do you notice? I've been observing closely and its funny how different I am/feel than the average warm-adapted human.

Here is what I've noticed:
\-Extremely warm upon waking/radiating heat/dilated blood vessels
\-Feeling cold at night right before bed (especially if I cold shower right before bed)
\-During the day if stuck in the office, drinking cold water all day, always have goosebumps (until I go outside)
\-Radiating heat in the sun, but feeling comfortable/very high heat tolerance
\-Radiating heat after eating (especially high fat meals)
\-Never shivering, but goosebumps instantly as soon as there is any perception of cold on skin
\-Craving the cold (especially after eating when I'm radiating heat)
\-Flush/reddening of the face after exercising or eating
\-Enjoying freezing my ass off (sense of wellbeing), always finding ways to cool: cold pool, ice packs, cold showers, AC cranked in the car, drinking lots of ice water, etc.
\-Crave high fat foods, with no desire for sugar.

Anyone else?

### Gary — 2017-07-11T18:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218503>

Hello,

I'm in the process of cold adaptation with my daughter...can I ask you how long it took you to achieve your current effects and what type of CT you do?

Thanks

Gary

### kris90 — 2017-07-11T20:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218507>

I think I became cold adapted before I even did any CT, just by fasting for years and drinking a lot of cold water during the fast. So even when I added CT last year, I believe I was already cold adapted, so it was quite easy for me to get into it. But, I did actually go back to warm adaptation during a biohack I did this spring. And to be honest, I re-cold adapted within 1 weekend (Vermont) just by being outside in minimal clothing on a cold weekend, and taking a dip in the cold lake, and sleeping in a tent with temperatures as low as 45 F.

I normally do a cold shower upon waking (50 F water temp) and before bed. During the day, I'm always finding ways to keep cool (i.e. fasting, lots of ice water, AC cranked, cold dips in pool, etc). I'll sometimes do a cold bath with ice, but not often. Actually, that's something I want to start adding a few times a week since I'm so addicted to the cold now.

I think if you follow Jack's CT protocol, it shouldn't take more than a couple weeks if consistent. Face dunks is probably the best way to start if you know about the mammalian dive reflex.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-11T20:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218512>

I've been face dunking about four weeks now. I'm only recently ending my shower on cold (I shower every three days) and finding it not entirely unpleasant but I'm not all YAY! about it.

I'm taking it super slow. Because I've got so many protocols on the go right now I can't say for sure what the CT is doing specifically but I have noticed weirdly that I'm enjoying the rain more!!

### kris90 — 2017-07-11T21:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adaptation-effects.19742/post-218515>

I find adaptation is the hardest part of CT, because for instance, a 70 F pool to me sucks lol... It's cold that it's not an enjoyable swim, but not cold enough to induce the numbness that makes CT easy.

It's similar to how I feel about the fall or early winter here where I am. When it hits just below freezing it's cold and uncomfortable. But when it goes quite south of freezing, to me, it doesn't make a difference, if anything, it's better. It's that in-between phase that is the toughest I find. This probably helps mammals fatten in fall: they start getting uncomfortable with the cold, and carbs are still available, and make them feel comfortable and cozy. So for me now, the colder the water, the better, and I just jump right in now, and it feels amazing. The B-endorphin is through the roof and my skin is just numb, and there's no pain perception.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-12T08:40:27+0300
