My better half is fair skinned and always used to burn badly UNTIL...... she started doing CT with me . . . . . now she tans instead of burning. On sunny days we would CT in the swimming pool mostly with water up to the bottom of our ribcages and our heads, shoulders and arms in the sun for Founier effect.

Interestingly it seemed as though CT on its own was enough to tan without even getting sun - supposedly it increases melanin among other things. . . . when combined with a seafood diet . . .

> But Inuits vitamin D intake wasn’t dependent upon the sun. They get all that they need from their diet, heavy on types of fatty fish that are naturally rich in vitamin D. **The plentiful amounts of the vitamin kept them from developing less melanin.** In fact, before milk was fortified with D, people living outside of Northern Canada and Alaska loaded their diets with fishy products, such as cod liver oil, to get their daily supplement. **So despite their chilly climate and lack of sun exposure, it’s the Inuit diet that has kept them in their natural glow**.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-25T21:07:45+0300

When the sun has powerful UV light more oxygen is found in our blood changing its color to a deep red due to the increased amount of oxygen in it. From a biological/anthropological perspective, this signals health and vitality and a high redox potential to members of our species who see us in the nature. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/13077291](https://www.patreon.com/posts/13077291)

I recently had a pow-wow with my son's oncologist. He is in remission now, and we are questioning whether his protocol is necessary for the duration, and without dosage change. He had a 12x12x14 cm tumor in his chest that was absorbing the chemo that is now so very thankfully gone. Side effects are sky-rocketing.

I'm sharing because the first thing the dr said in our meeting was to compare his cancer to melanoma, because of the well established confusion about how some people can be in the sun all day and never get cancer, some people will burn instantly and develop melanoma. He was being supportive, actually, of our right to choose the course of our son's treatment because he can't know for sure at this point if the chemo potential benefit is worth the risk, and this was his way of describing that.

These onco docs and nurses mentioned frequently that the chemo makes him extra sensitive to sun, and therefore more susceptible to sun burn so "make sure he's covered!" "wear a hat!" "never too much sunscreen!" They told me there was absolutely no way to increase his RBC during treatment, and once it started falling it wouldn't stop, and they had a magic hemoglobin count # for when they would do a transfusion.

They also told me there is no evidence that the smart meter or tablet would have caused or contributed to my son's rare cancer. Genetic testing was negative, even though 70% of people with this rare cancer (pleuropulmonary blastoma) have a mutation.

Now that he is in remission and we are taking a break from treatment, I'll be putting together some educational information for his docs, and I'll be all over this site for the links and logic!! Thank you so much. Of all the docs out there, pediatric docs NEED to have EMF danger info on their radar. (I cannot avoid that pun.)

### Lahelada — 2017-08-15T14:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-callus-rx-naturally.19859/post-221694>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=221685)
>
> Do you think Epsom salts helped you tan? That's interesting!

A tan is good and bad but not for the conventional reasons. It is "bad" to get it too quickly as it reduces your ability to make Vitamin D. A tan is good because it reduces your ability to make Vitamin D . It is in effect a regulating mechanism of your vit d level.
The epsom salt helps with not burning too quickly, i. e prevents erythema appearing too quickly on newbie skin.It is magnesium _Sulfate_.
To recap a tan means you are getting to optimum level for your n=1. If you are optimal everything is in balance but in constant flux. It can also mean that you have had all the vitamin D your body can make with the cholesterol and sulfur it can lay its hands on. And then there are parts that will not tan at all. That story is more complicated.

# Thread: Andrew's Optimal Journey Begins
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T16:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219628>

Hello and thank you to all for welcoming me into the community. It has come at just the right time in my life where I think I need some help, and intuition has led me here... so I know I'm on the right path now, thank you.

I was advised to start a journal; this is good advice, as I think it will be very beneficial.

A bit of background on myself as requested, you might even be able to offer me some help right off the bat, as there is so much dense information available it's a little overwhelming to know where and how to start.

I'm not currently diagnosed with any specific medical illness or condition, however, I feel uneasy and dissatisfied with my physical and mental health. I'm also at odds emotionally and spiritually with my lifestyle.

I have 3 kids, recently separated from a long term marriage and am in a new relationship which is very supportive, and have inherited a step son, so 4 children - which is great as I love kids and a stable family home life.

I live and work from Manchester in the UK, which is typically quite a built up, densely populated city well known for being quite cold, grey and wet. I have my own business which is a digital agency, so I find the majority of my time is spent online with the computer or using the phone. Although I tend to avoid phone calls off the mobile device, I am inundated with SMS, emails and social media demands. My phone is often in my trouser pocket... Although I enjoy the creative aspects of what I do, overall, I have a lot of anxiety and stress that seems to overwhelm me mentally, and often it feels like I'm trapped on the typical hamster wheel, which I do try to juggle with an active family life with defined boundaries.

My parents are relatively healthy physically, with no known medical issues, though I think I've inherited their self-employed anxiety and stress related tendencies, being brought up in a home where everything was lived for future attainment and goal achievement and not being present or enjoying the NOW, or gratitude for what we already have. My dad's parents died of heart attacks, and on my mum's side, my grandad died of a heart attack but suffered much ill health from a genetic disorder which included muscle atrophy and diabetes. My mum's mum, my grandma, seems to be in good health at 87 and is only slightly over weight. My aunt passed away young from the same genetic disorder as my grandad, was very poorly and had diabetes. My uncle, my mum's brother, has the same genetic disorder and has diabetes and cancer and is obese. I've been tested for the genetic disorder and seem to be clear.

Overall, I'm slim although have battled and struggled with my diet and weight in the past historically. Being teased as a kid for being overweight led me to the gym, and weight training, which had positive and negative mental/body/confidence related associations. However, this path did lead me to become more educated about food and has been very insightful on my journey. I stumbled across Dr Mercola, Tim Ferriss, Dave Asprey and Mark Sissons over the years, and my intuition has strongly pulled me towards a low carbohydrate diet (ever since effortlessly losing weight on the Atkins diet many, many years ago). Something didn't sit well with me on the Atkins diet eating bacon and sausages, however, and I slowly gravitated more towards Tim Ferriss's slow carb or Dave Asprey's Bullet Proof suggestions. Something still didn't sit well with me at a gut feel level.

After my marriage broke down, I found I used food as an environmental, emotional and mental control factor, and became a big fan of intermittent fasting and fasting. So I was eating only one or two meals a day, and wouldn't eat anything that wasn't strictly controllable. This caused conflicts with my family life and being surrounded by 4 kids, who have different parents on the other side, and being brought up with what you would probably term the SAD in general.

Over the past 6 months I've gravitated more to a no grains, or limited grains diet, and I eat a lot of smoked salmon, grass fed meats, grass fed butter, coconut oil, MCT oil and seafood, all of which sits well with my on an intuitive stand point. I do still occasionally consume dairy (pasteurised cheese & cream, not milk) which I know I should either eliminate or switch to raw versions. For convenience, I eat a lot of nut butters, usually trying to stick to things like almond or walnut, but I'm a sucker for the blends and the legumes like peanut and cashew nut. I know I need to eliminate these. I do take around 40gml of Carlsons fish oil a day, supplement with things like Vitamin D, NAC, Magnesium, Iodine and others.

HOWEVER, I started to note things about myself and my life that I would class as _red flag_ signals:

**1)** Whenever I exercised with resistance training I feel completely exhausted and drained for about 72 hours, and ache so much, I started to question the benefits and have eventually ceased all forms of physical exercise. I've been resistance training and bodybuilding since I was 17, and had a stint of steroid use in my early 20s, so I'm no stranger to motivation or the mental drive to exercise. I'm not lazy. But I started to lose faith in the benefits from the negative physical impact. To me this was counter intuitive as I figured we should be able to do physically demanding exercise as humans without feeling like we've been steam rollered for 72 hours and unable to lift our limbs without wincing.

**2)** On top of heavy caffeine abuse (10 - 12 strong cups a day), I found myself daily using Modafinil, or Ephedrine (or equivalent CNS stimulants), even down to seeking out and buying numerous legal research chemicals such as Isopropylphenidate, 4F-MPH & 3-FPM. For years I have been convinced I have some form of mild ADD or ADHD or bi-polar after reading so many other peoples descriptions. I felt these drugs helped, but over the past 6 months, I realised, I was addicted to how they made me feel, and was seeking a 'hit' from them constantly.

**3)** About a year ago, after seeking out more chemical 'hits' I came across Kratom. For fear of the downsides of chemical side effects, I found the idea of a natural 'herbal' solution very appealing. I realised I was just seeking something to change my chemistry and distract me from day to day feelings in life. I quickly have gone from using 3 - 5g of Kratom a day, easily to 20 - 25g a day throughout every day without fail.

**4)** 12 months ago, after never being a drug user for 35 years of my life until these recent times I even picked up some cannabis and LSD after reading about micro dosing benefits. Very quickly I settled into a routine of happily vaping weed and swapping between regular LSD micro doses to full, high doses.

**5)** My new relationship after separating from my ex wife is with a very understanding, supportive woman who I love. She was a none smoker when we met, and she's battled smoking cigarettes for her entire life. Due to some personal family related stresses, we both smoked some cannabis, and then it was like a trigger for her to start smoking again. The bizarre thing that threw up a red flag for me, is that being a lifelong none smoker, and only ever vaping weed for a year, I decided to try smoking. I liked the way it made me feel, and quickly progressed in about a month to a 4 - 6 cigarettes a day routine. I don't know if I'm addicted, as I haven't stopped but I have noted I craze the buzz and resulting relaxation.

**6)** When I'm not working late at night, I try to avoid checking my phone or computer, however, I still go to bed exhausted, sometimes unable to keep my eyes open, and in the morning I can't wake up. It's a mission and chore to get out of bed with any energy, and it takes me an hour to an hour and a half to mentally lift the fog from my mind - usually helped by kratom, cigarette and caffeine. I know with every fibre of my being, that this is not life and certainly not what life can be for someone like me of my age.

Overall I'm aware of what I am doing and am intelligent enough to know that factors in my mind and life circumstances are throwing me off balance and I'm seeking/craving externally what is not being produced internally as it should be. As I'm sat at the computer staring at the dark grey clouds and rain outside, I feel a deep imbalance and dissatisfaction with where my life is circumstantially and feel uncomfortable that I cannot be at ease with how I am living. So I know I need a plan to optimise my health, more for my mental and emotional well-being.

Now, my parents live at the coast, beach side, in Southern England and get a lot of sun exposure, bare foot walking, fresh air and so on. My incongruence in my life which hits me hard is that I yearn for a life based on principles which I'm educated to know are healthy for me and my children, however I feel trapped in my high stress 'digital' work life, and with my separation from my children's mum, feel I cannot relocate for fear of losing the healthy contact I have with my children.

Every day I face this dissatisfaction and dilemma. Every day I do a little more research and try not to be overwhelmed, to make positive changes somehow, some way to a better life. An optimised life.

When I stumbled upon an article citing Jack Kruse about magnetism, sunlight and other elements, it was like somebody had finally shown me the box with the picture on for the jigsaw puzzle. Like I always had all the scattered pieces scattered chaotically around, but no idea on the bigger picture. I now feel I'm scratching the surface to see the bigger picture and actually see the map & plan on how, maybe, to finally link all of the pieces together in my own life. There is just so much to learn, and there is a fear of doing the wrong thing, when you think you're doing the right thing, that I'm hoping the interaction on this forum will be insightful and helpful.

Thank you for listening and God bless you all for being here supporting each other.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T17:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219631>

**Current Diet**
Most days consist of smoked salmon, boiled free range eggs, avocados, grass fed steak, green vegetables and green salad, nut butters (almond, peanut, cashew, walnut, macadamia), grass fed butter, coconut oil, organic ghee, 100% dark chocolate.

Occasionally white potatoes, lentils, rice, pasteurised cream, pasteurised cheese, and once a week I'll probably eat something more typical of a SAD with the kids or social gatherings and/or meal with other people at a restaurant.

I avoid alcohol, refined sugars as much as possible, and moderate with a view to attempting to avoid grains & processed foods.

**Current Supplementation**
40ml of Carlson's Fish Oil
Melatonin 3mg
DHEA 75mg
Greens & reds powder with added probiotics
Vit D3 1000iu
Vitamin K2 45mcg
Vitamin B3 40mg
Vitamin B6 20mg
Calcium 50mg
Magnesium Biglycinate Chelate 50mg
Zinc chelate 10mg
Olive leaf extract 500mg
Curcumin C3 400mg
Hawthorn Berry 300mg
Guggul Lipid 300mg
Hibiscus flower 100mg
Ubiquinol 100mg
Milk Thistle 750mg
NAC 750mg
Tumeric Root 150mg
Bupleurum Root 75mg
Dandelion Root 75mg
Artichoke Leaf Extract 30mg
Schizandra Berry powder 35mg
White Peony Root powder 35mg
Biotin 500mcg
Copper 2mg
Chromium Picolinate 300mcg
NA-R-Alpha Lipoic Acid, Gymnema Sylvestre, Berberine (undisclosed blend)

**Current Vices**
Nut butters (almond, peanut, cashew, walnut, macadamia) - daily
20g of Kratom - daily
High caffeine consumption - daily
1 - 6 cigarettes - daily
Lack of physical exercise

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T17:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219632>

Today I shall be completing this recommended survey: [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/) and will update the results on my journal thread.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219636>

**Oh dear! I scored 10 on the Scompy Quantum Quiz.... the lowest score: "_10 points and below : You may look great on the outside, but your mitochondria may disagree completely. Start with some simple changes first._"**

Although, one or two questions I felt were a little ambiguous such as "_Do you expose at least half of your skin surfaces to sunlight for more than 30 minutes a day at least 3+ times per week?_" ... I would suggest in the better sunlight days, the answer would be yes, but when it's grey, wet and miserable I would say no. It depends on the time of year.

"_Do you get out into midday UVB sunlight several times per week to build skin callus and produce Vitamin D_" ... again I would say, yes in the warmer, brighter days, but definitely not in the colder wet days, which we get plenty of here in Manchester.

"_Do you eat oysters or seafood on a regular basis?_" I eat a lot of salmon, so answered yes, but wasn't sure if this related to just crustaceous type seafood like prawns, in which case it would be a no.

"_Do you walk barefoot outside or touch earth, grass, trees, sand, stones etc at least 3 x a week for more than 15 minutes a day?_" - again it depends on the week and time of year, it really fluctuates. When the weather is good, yes absolutely, and otherwise no.

I'm partly disheartened by the outcome, but also encouraged that there is a lot I can do to make small, rapid and easy changes to my lifestyle.

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219638>

After watching my first Youtube video of Jack Kruse, this week I removed my monthly contact lenses (the type you keep in for a month without removing them at all) and threw them in the bin. I switched to wearing my prescription glasses, only when I need to read or look at the screen and taking them off as often as possible. I also started to stand barefoot in the garden on the grass on any day when it's not been raining with a view to build up my natural sun exposure in the outdoors. I'm yet to achieve this on dull and wet days, of which there are plenty.

I also read this post this morning: [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...LFz70joGzwfYpeYdbDXq0JNwlJp81G\_NfyGuk&fref=nf](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/?hc_ref=ARQuIr3k8CQMcgA_d12d7eK2KZqqUVLFz70joGzwfYpeYdbDXq0JNwlJp81G_NfyGuk&fref=nf)

"_Sunglasses, tattoo's, nnEMF from your job, and chronic work at night under man made light. A history of abuse looking to replace that dopamine and beta-endorphin and your best friend was another rocker I wrote about with this very same story..._" and I thought about my own substance abuse, and terrible lack of morning energy, although mild in comparison, perhaps it is my own inner demons chasing dopamine desperately....

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:19:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219640>

Today, this speaks volumes to me: "_I could have looked at the move as a stressful-one. Instead, I rolled with the punches, took each step at a time and ran the marathon. Is your cup half-empty or half-full? It’s true that technology’s intersection with our biology is shaping up to be our greatest hurdle to complicate our health as we move into the future. There are many elements we can control in our local and micro-environments. But if you lack a great lack of control where you live, it may require you to change your location to find a more optimal fishbowl._" ~ Scompy, [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/)

Where for a long time I would have had a negative spin on my existing situation, I can currently accept where I am as it being what it is, and look forward to making small but effective changes over the days, weeks and months to come.

### caroline — 2017-07-22T02:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrews-optimal-journey-begins.19860/post-219698>

keep going ......

If you can manage it - become a gold member and you will have access to all jack's monthly webinars and the follow up Q&A when you can ask Jack anything. This is worth it's weight in Gold!

Go for it, whatever the weather. I don't do storms but everything else is up for grabs. Challenge though, not misery or suffering.  I have an outside CT tub, trust me getting in during the winter when you've had to break the ice with a hammer can be a challenge ... but a few minutes of that and the dopamine lift is amazing. Grounding whilst sunbathing under cloudy skies or in the rain, there's still sunlight to be harvested, and circadian rhythms reset or reinforced .... I was sat in the CT tub once looking at a glorious blue sky to the east, whilst the black cloud right above me dropped buckets of water on my head. I just laughed with Mother Nature .... Dopamine isn't just about blazing sunshine, which is just as well considering our weather ...

# Thread: Hacking Make Up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 49
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-21T18:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219639>

There was some discussion a few weeks ago about stopping using make up.

I know it's the right thing to do and because I execute fairly quickly I began about two or three weeks ago.

This has been such a huge thing for me as my skin has always been terrible (only just realised that blue light was probably the cause of my acne from 14 - 37 (with the exception of two pregnancies/breastfeeding where my skin was flawless).

Because I have a bit of colour on my face it's working out not too bad. My skin on my face has always been quite red but it's not looking so red now against a browner complexion than a white one - if that makes sense?

I'm still using some powdered eyeliner and Dr. Haushka mascara (wearing mascara all these years is probably the reason my eyelashes need mascara to stand out) but that's all I'm putting on my face.

Psychologically this has been a real turning point: being willing to face the world without paint.

I wonder if other women here could share what they do with make up or what they've found since giving it up?

Any other hacks?

### Rela — 2017-07-21T22:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219675>

I am also going makeup free..... Since last 3-4 months I just did it twice for night parties.My melasma is worse but I am way more confident.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-22T00:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219689>

The current Patreon blog blows make up up.........

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-22T01:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219694>

Epic blog Jack and am looking forward to that new book!

My skin has done nothing but improve over the past two years and especially since giving up the foundation and powders. I only wear a little organic mascara and eyeliner once a week when hubby and I go out for our date day. Morning sun has been so amazing for my skin, and not just my face. I keep it simple ladies. I have nothing on my facial skin in my profile picture. That is my sun callus and just a little organic mascara and eyeliner. Your natural beauty is going to shine through when you begin to make your melatonin and dopamine and you sleep well and regenerate. You are going to go out in the world and sparkle like a beautiful diamond! I'm not being vain here either, just sharing the truth with you. You just have to begin and you will know it then. Take off your mask and set yourself free and begin! You won't look back. My favorite facial moisturizer has become organic coconut oil with some added jasmine oil and a little MB now. I also hack with a derma roller twice per week, to help build the collagen when I go out into the morning sun and I drink nothing but my good spring water and my seafood and fish are crucial too.

### caroline — 2017-07-22T02:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219700>

I never liked makeup anyway - it always felt like a mask to me so it was no big deal to go with out - and I have never had the patience for mascara etc.
A few times I would stop at a Channel makeup counter at a department store and they would make me up. I would just go -"Are you kidding me??? Is this how you think I look better??????"

I just use some oil in coconut oil with MB now . Ghee made my face breakout a little.

Just to throw a spanner in the works Amazing Emma ....A lot of us gave up all jewellery quite awhile back ......

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-22T08:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219717>

Thanks, ladies! Really appreciate your feedback and I'm inspired to keep at this! I have two friends who never wear make up and they're both beautiful!

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) yeah, I don't wear any now either. I took out my belly button ring when I discovered Jack a few months ago and now the wedding ring is off! I'm actually getting a new wedding ring made of wood!

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-22T12:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219727>

I think I wore a bit of mascara last year for DD's graduation. My vanity (I prefer to call it my dopamine lift ...) is getting a bit of colour through my hair every 12 weeks. Not enough grey for it to be a graceful grey IYKWIM, but its enough to look obvious in my otherwise naturally brown hair. But I do have a bet on with my hairdresser that I can stop it getting worse, or even possibly reversing it. She's interested because a lot of women are going grey early, and she herself is in a high nnemf blue light toxic environment. (She's made changes. She has not only got the pics from my handbag, I took in the file ) But I've seen women in there who obviously dye their hair, they are plastered in makeup, sat there having stuff painted on their nails. And staring at their phones if they are waiting. My hairdresser usually asks what crazy thing I'm up to .... not sure next time what she'll make of my fish oil hair conditioning treatment and fish oil face and body cream. Don't think she'll be offering to sell it through the salon.

### Billybats — 2017-07-22T14:23:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219733>

Hi Emma and others,

I am 53 years old and used make up on and off only for special occasions because I was allergic to it all. I have tried DIY, it was ok but still bothered me. I had awesome skin in high school but when I was 20 I tried birth control pills for less than a year and broke out ever since really bad. It has gotten better since menopause and when I was using progesterone cream for the same amount of time I have been in menopause but since I stopped using progesterone it started back a little. So I shall see what happens down the line. As for sunscreen, maybe used that a total combined of 2 years my whole life, allergic to that too.

### JanSz — 2017-07-22T18:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219750>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=219639)
>
> my eyelashes need mascara to stand out) but that's all I'm putting on my face.

One option is to have tattoo on eyelashes,
but the ink is likely worst than mascara.

### Inger — 2017-07-23T03:04:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219791>

using make up working indoors in fake light could be a good thing, no?
of course organic, good quality one

I am using less and less make up too.... my goal is NONE!
I go outside in the morning light with no make up every day.... and I tell women, never start using make up... it is so hard to quit...but the skin gets better the less you use
In fact - I have coworkers tell me I look younger now than my age...

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-23T08:54:24+0300

But I do have a bet on with my hairdresser that I can stop it getting worse, or even possibly reversing it. She's interested because a lot of women are going grey early, and she herself is in a high nnemf blue light toxic environment. (She's made changes. She has not only got the pics from my handbag, I took in the file ) But I've seen women in there who obviously dye their hair, they are plastered in makeup, sat there having stuff painted on their nails. And staring at their phones if they are waiting. My hairdresser usually asks what crazy thing I'm up to .... not sure next time what she'll make of my fish oil hair conditioning treatment and fish oil face and body cream. Funny you mention your hairdresser. Mine is having fertility problems. The light in the hairdressers is atrocious not to mention that she's using hairdryers near her groin (client seated of course) all day 5 days a week... Shame!

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-23T11:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-219809>

@[ScottishEmma](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20059/) This is one of the pics I've shown to my hairdresser, about the implications of blue light generally, but also on hair greying prematurely. (Pic From Reality 3). My hair hack is based around this, because I'm aware that having the hair colour is technically makeup, and I'm just disguising the heteroplasmy rate on my scalp.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-7-23_9-0-4-png.5386/)
Its also why I used a sun hat in very high UV (when we get it ). It has holes I can see through, I call it my kiniki hat . Still lets a lot of light through, but my hypothesis  is that my grey springs mainly from my hair parting, and the hat helps protect my scalp RBCs and hair pigment from over bleaching from high UV, and keeps the CSF cooler. My scalp massage with my mix was really good, (hindsight - maybe should have done a patch test first, but luckily my scalp and hair loved it).

### kvond — 2017-07-25T15:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220035>

I did here from a practitioner of a kind of brain pulsing - sorry it's a thing out here a doc invented for newborns and trauma from birth. In any event, metal glasses change the pulsations of the limbic system fluid in the brain.

Here's a blurb from them: Craniosacral Fascial Therapy (CFT) is based on Dr. Gillespie's theory that the Craniosacral and Fascial systems are one. This system originates in the body's core around the brain and spinal cord. The body's fascial web extends from that core outward in all directions. It surrounds and permeates every structure in the body down to the cellular level. Strain anywhere in that system may effect brain function and craniosacral mobility. CFT techniques are designed to facilitate and follow the release of strain allowing optimal mobility,
and therefore better function.

### Just Breathe — 2017-07-25T23:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220078>

I need less and less makeup the healthier I get! I'm down to eye makeup only and lip color for the office. No acne or old lady spots (melasma?) on my face anymore.
I also don't need coconut oil on my face (was helpful for acne and moisturization back then.) I use less body and hand lotion now too.

When I'm feeling rough, I get more sunlight, a red light treatment, or use a small bit of vitamin c in water and smear it on my face.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-26T11:26:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-220113>

>
> If I could just hack long beautiful eye lashes (my 2 year old daughter's are to die for!!) I'll be happy  I feel that without mascara I kinda look ill. My eyes are small.

My youngest brother is the one in the family with the best eyelashes . I got mine dyed once, didn't last long enough and I decided life's too short to fake it. When I talk to people if what they are noticing is that I'm not wearing mascara or other eye makeup, rather than the light in my eyes, they are not in my tribe. My DD's tutors at uni were horrified that she had never been vaccinated, and I think they were expecting some hippy type parents to turn up for her graduation last year, (I was soooooo tempted to oblige ....), but that was the last time I wore mascara. I now see my eyelashes as antenna - to be covered or clogged up as little as possible.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-231355>

I haven't worn makeup in years, and stopped colouring my grey years ago, too. I stopped just to avoid all the chemicals in them. That's the last thing to go, I suppose, as soon as I find a good recipe to make my own. I also stopped wearing earings in my pierced ears many years ago because I reacted to most metals. So at least I've been doing some things right, even if accidentally!

### julietanannen — 2023-01-18T12:15:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-319057>

It's all about finding what works for you and what makes you feel good
about yourself. And don't be too hard on yourself, taking care of your
skin and yourself is a journey, not a destination. Keep sharing your
experience and let's learn from each other!

### Rohitstad — 2023-01-18T12:30:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hacking-make-up.19861/post-319058>

Giving up makeup can be a big step, but it sounds like you're already seeing some positive changes in your skin. I agree that paying attention to skin care can help reduce the need for makeup. I also recommend checking out [bringingbeauty.co](https://bringingbeauty.co) for some tips and tricks on natural skincare. It's all about finding a balance that works for you and makes you feel confident. Keep up the good work!
And don't forget, even if you choose to continue using makeup, it's important to be mindful of the ingredients and choose products that are non-toxic and gentle on your skin.

# Thread: Question for the UK community
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T18:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219643>

Hey,

I'm new, and just about 80% of the way through Epi-Paleo RX, while attempting to digest as much info on the forum and on Jack's posts on the main site as I can in my spare time.

Im thinking, without running labs and tests on yourself, then it would be almost pointless (not saying completely pointless) but too chaotic to make too many changes, without knowing your baseline levels.

I have no idea what RX protocol/s to follow based on my symptoms, so am just muddling along for now. I did note a bunch of links and recommendations in Jack's book, which point to a lot of USA based resources.

Does anyone have a UK specific list of resources for labs and testing to make a start? That would be superb if anyone can help me out in that direction initially.

Thank you

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-21T19:18:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219651>

I know that Genova Diagnostics have a billion tests you can do through the post. Only thing is I'm not sure how you find a practitioner - they ask for that. If you search Genova in the forum you might get some idea there.

The other thing I'd say is that I've used plain old NHS for some tests in the past. As long as you go along with some approximate symptoms and ask specifically for hormone test (you could say you were having issues down below) or thyroid test (you could say you're feeling tired and cold or something) then usually your doc will come up trumps and run some blood work. Once the results are in you can ask for a print out and start there by posting on this forum.

Just an idea,,,

### AndrewLanghorn — 2017-07-21T19:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219654>

Heh great tips. I'll check out the Genova lead now.... I have a phobia of going to the doctor though, haven't been in years and years, so I'd have to try to get over that fear to make an appointment and goto the GP. However, if this is true and you're able to blag your way into free testing for some panels thanks to the NHS, I think I probably would go for it. Especially thyroid ad maybe testosterone testing. Thanks again Emma.

### drezy — 2017-07-22T01:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219692>

Sorry, us Americans have to crash every party... Also I follow Emma. I'd no sooner get in the way of Emma and her children's optimal as I'd run in between a momma polar bear (since Emma is dipping into the cold now) and her cubs. It's a fool's errand.

\*Warning slight political content ahead\* Your NHS and even my "private" insurance seem to do a great job at disease management. No mention of actual health there. It's similar to what I explained to my mom. In her childhood, when you got ice cream it was something like 4 ingredients. Now go get ice cream in a store and you'll fall asleep before you finish reading all the ingredients. Feels like we've overcomplicated things a bit in all domains.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-22T08:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-uk-community.19862/post-219719>

Haha, thanks @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

I definitely think your doc is worth pursuing if you can push through the awkwardness. My husband just did it for full hormone and thyroid panel. You'll literally be in there five mins then back again for the nurse to bleed you dry

I've done it three times now through the doc. First time my iron came back super low and I got a prescription - which I ditched and went for flurodix (this was before I knew much but I still knew I didn't want pills). Second and third time I wasn't given any feedback other than I was 'within range' but I still asked for the print out anyway then went home and googled the shit out of every single result!!

It'll save you a boat tonne of cash - but, that said, genova would probably go deeper than standard NHS test. It's a good starting point though...

# Thread: Patreon.....??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2017-07-22T07:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219714>

I am having a little trouble understanding this ...... anyone else?

I understand what it is but I am wondering .... do we pay for every blog that JK writes? do we have access again and again to the same blog we paid for?

Dr. K. mentioned that it is the price of a cup of coffee a month - but there seems to be a charge per blog and we are automatically charged when Jack puts up a new blog?

Given how prolific Jack is ......

and - how do I fit in more reading??????

All this is vitally important to me .......but I need to spend less time on my computer - not more, and I have a life to live with a guy I adore.

Unless I can speed read like Jack does - I think I am sunk.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-22T08:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219716>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=219714)
>
> I am having a little trouble understanding this ...... anyone else?
>
> I understand what it is but I am wondering .... do we pay for every blog that JK writes? do we have access again and again to the same blog we paid for?
>
> Dr. K. mentioned that it is the price of a cup of coffee a month - but there seems to be a charge per blog and we are automatically charged when Jack puts up a new blog?
>
> Given how prolific Jack is ......
>
> and - how do I fit in more reading??????
>
> All this is vitally important to me .......but I need to spend less time on my computer - not more, and I have a life to live with a guy I adore.
>
> Unless I can speed read like Jack does - I think I am sunk.

I've joined Caroline. I just copy and paste the article into a word document and print it out to read. You continue to have access, and there's a comments section, a bit like the blogs comments section. You pledge an amount for each article Jack does in the month, (min $1) but you can set a limit on how many articles per month you will pay for. So say you pledge $1 for each article, but set a limit of 4 articles, your max bill should be $4 in a month. (Plus VAT if you are UK). Because you can choose what you pledge, and can change it if you want, I think its great for people who want to support Jack for the blogs, or acknowledge how much help he gives on the forum, but can't swing a silver or gold membership.

### caroline — 2017-07-22T09:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219725>

thanks Sue - I will have to go back and check what I actually signed up for.....

I agree - it is great to acknowledge how much help Jack gives us.

### WalterNL — 2017-07-22T20:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219759>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=219753)

Just the ones on Patreon. The rest on this blog and Forum is free.

### drezy — 2017-07-22T21:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patreon.19863/post-219764>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=219753)
>
> So we have to pay now to read Jacks blog?

Honestly, too much is for free to begin with. Expecting more freebies really isn't productive.

If you can follow the subtle hints, read the comments in the Vermont 2017 youtube. One person was particularly feisty/douchebaggy. In the end I think that person had a valid question. Jack replied very directly and honestly. He's an honest guy and that's why so many end up running away from him or even try burning him. Jack has mentioned that his wife sees degradation and urges him to pull back from much of the effort of helping others.

**I am 1000% on team Sandra Kruse on this one.**

If I had to bet who will outlive the other between @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) and @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) currently.... I have to put my money on JanSz simply because I don't like losing money. Doc is burning the candle at both ends regardless of his knowledge and hacks.

Expecting Doc to crank out this type of cross-disciplinary analysis and connections endlessly for free is untenable.

I need Jack alive for the majority of my son's life and my grandchildren's early life. The man already turned back my own clock 10-20 years. Now I need him in the game longer for my lineage.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-07-22T22:09:20+0300

I love Jack like a little brother (Yes he is younger than me , lol) and have so much gratitude and appreciation for all that he does. I don't want to leave Sandra out of this either because not enough credit goes to her for what she does. And behind every good man stands a great lady who loves and support him and Jack needs to listen to his wife.

Thanks for sharing your thoughts here too Drezy!

Rock on all of you beautiful Starfish!

Personally, my health would be in the toilet hadn't I discovered Jack!

# Thread: Why is my Raynauds appearing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-22T09:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/post-219720>

I've had Raynauds for quite a number of years but normally it only really appears in Winter when I'm obviously cold or touching cold things.

I've had four flare ups in the last week and I'm wondering if Iodine or salt could somehow be causing it?

It obviously isn't the sun and yeah, I've had a handful (pun!) of cold baths but yeah, it's a bit weird, I never usually get it in Summer and I literally can't remember the last time I had it - not even last Winter...

Might the Lugols I've been applying topically be a culprit?

Any thoughts welcome!

### Foxglove — 2017-07-24T03:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/post-219883>

I don't know if you are taking anything, but I used to get Raynaud's from certain drugs, even when I wasn't cold. The vasoconstrictors like phenylephrine and, for some reason, a couple hours after using my steroid inhaler (although my doctor couldn't figure that one out). Have you written down every time it happens and try to find a pattern?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-24T08:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-my-raynauds-appearing.19864/post-219896>

> [Foxglove said:](/goto/post?id=219883)
>
> I don't know if you are taking anything, but I used to get Raynaud's from certain drugs, even when I wasn't cold. The vasoconstrictors like phenylephrine and, for some reason, a couple hours after using my steroid inhaler (although my doctor couldn't figure that one out). Have you written down every time it happens and try to find a pattern?

I've been high dosing salt straight under my tongue twice a day. I believe this may be the cause!

Thanks for your input

# Thread: Please can you tell me if there's anything I've missed?  Thanks a lot.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-22T14:54:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219735>

Looking at ways to improve health, and currently doing:

\* Sunlight exposure - AM (including circadian rhythm 'reset'), midday (vitamin D), sunset (feels good!).
\* No UV avoidance - no sunblock, no sunglasses. (Please ignore my out of date mugshot!)
\* CT - I swim in cold seawater.
\* Grounding - occurs when I go for a swim, plus I use earthing mats/sheets at home.
\* I.F. - 5 hour eating window.
\* DHA - I eat lots of seafood.
\* Wireless avoidance - I don't use any wireless devices.
\* Exercise - walking, and resistance, eg chest expander. Would like to start weights training.
\* RF blocking - RF blocking fabric on/around bed.
\* Blue light avoidance -
\+ My PC uses f.lux software.
\+ I completely turn off my PC monitor's blue color component.
\+ I use blue light blocking glasses in the evening.
\+ I use 'old fashioned' red colored light bulbs.

Not sure if this helps:

\* Freeze fruit and veg, to structure their internal water. (Would like to do this with water, but TBH, I can't be bothered ATM.)
\* Eat prebiotic fiber. (I find digestive enzymes helpful.)
\* Switch off most electrical circuits at night.

Wish list:

\* Infrared sauna/exposure. How can I do this on the cheap?

What about:

\* Blocking RF around me during the day?
\* Magnetise drinking water?
\* 'Color therapy' - red and purple?

Thanks very much for any comments about any of this!

**EDIT:** An "edit list" of things that I forgot to mention when I originally posted this thread -

\* Stress reduction techniques. (There are many.)

>
> Looking at ways to improve health, and currently doing:

\* Sunlight exposure - AM (including circadian rhythm 'reset'), midday (vitamin D), sunset (feels good!).
\[\\quote\]
Nice. I just tested my solar callous with 3 hours of UVindex 12(and thereabouts) light followed 2 days later by 4 hours of UV 13. Zero burn. I just look all bronze.

> \* Grounding - occurs when I go for a swim, plus I use earthing mats/sheets at home.

Careful, it could be double edged if what you're grounding to is noisy or just a bad ground.

> Wish list:
> \* Infrared sauna/exposure. How can I do this on the cheap?

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) is all over this angle look at the big light near the bottom of this post
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

### JanSz — 2017-07-22T16:46:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219738>

> [JKLearner123 said:](/goto/post?id=219735)
>
> plus I use earthing mats/sheets at home.
> \* DHA - I eat lots of seafood.

If you are grounding earthing mats with very short copper cable, to a separate 1" 10feet long copper pole that is sunk in the ground far away from any underground electrical stuff,.................
ask @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) for more details

\=====================================
Note;
DHA is a super very important.
Luckily small amounts last long time, very long half life.
Seafood usually comes with highly excessive EPA (that wrecks havoc with Omega6).

To decide if I should eat seafood I do:

**Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum**

Currently I eat seafood rarely.
But plan on getting small amount of linseed oil (ALA)

My results 10 days ago.
On a chart below, in column %Status=0 (zero)=ideal
acceptable is (plus minus 25%)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-22T17:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219741>

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) & @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), thanks a lot for your help/information!

Re: solar callous. I will investigate this topic. I saw JK discussing this topic recently. (Might have been on his faceboook feed?)

Re: grounding. I will investigate about the possibility of poor grounding. You've jogged my memory about something. I use dirty electricity filters in the house. I've tested them, using a DE meter, and they work well. For instance, with the DE filters switched off, the DE meter reading is high. With the DE filters switched on, the DE meter reading is about 10x less.

Re: 850nM LED light. Thanks! I had no idea about these devices. Very interesting. I am currently browsing 850nM LED lights on my local (UK) amazon site. I guess I'll need one that:

\+ Can be set to "on" during daylight hours, and not just automatically when it's dark.
\+ Works indoors
\+ Uses a standard (for UK) indoor electrical power point socket.

My first infrared sauna came from Craigslist for about $250. They were downsizing and I was the first to call. Not sure where you live, but check ads for nearby cities. I had an Craigslist search app set up for Houston and Austin. It notified immediately whenever a sauna was posted.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-219902>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), one of Dr Mercola's online articles says -

"It’s important to realize that photobiomodulation is highly biphasic in dose, meaning the benefit can disappear by using too much light...Ten minutes or half an hour does no harm at all … Mostly, I tell people they can use these things for 10 or 20 minutes a day and it’ll have major benefits."

You may want to reduce your timing from hours to minutes. I'm really sorry, but atm, I can't find the comment online that says that there were some problems with that particular LED unit. It may be due to its wattage? (For instance, aim for a wattage less than 10W. That unit seems powerful. It may be greater than 10W?) If I find that comment about the unit, I'll post back. I was just browsing the net two days ago, and spotted that comment, but now I can't find it. It would be great if Dr Mercola would post a follow-up about that specific unit, and if he still uses it, or has switched to using a different model. In the meantime, I have ordered a much smaller and less powerful unit, and will aim to use it for about 15 minutes a day..

You obviously buy the whole light frequency effect on health.

One 850nm light at whatever power compared to the [8,683,317,618,811,886,495,518,194,401,280,000,000 different frequencies](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-10-can-you-supplement-sunlight/) the sun generates.

It's like comparing a single one string cello versus an 8 [Undecillion](http://www.dictionary.com/browse/undecillion) member orchestra.

One frequency versus the complexity/information contained here:

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=220233)

So that one 850nm is considered little more special than the rest of that undecyllion.
Likely much lower power comparing to that large lamp dr Mercola help to find out.

### drezy — 2017-07-27T20:42:51+0300

### JKLearner123 — 2017-07-29T20:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-can-you-tell-me-if-theres-anything-ive-missed-thanks-a-lot.19865/post-220385>

^ Thanks for everyone's comments so far. I received the small 850nM LED light from Amazon. I'm just messing about with it atm, not sure what I'm doing. Just experimenting to see if anything works -

\- Put the light on the back of my neck. (Was thinking vagus nerve.)
\- Put it on my wrist etc. (Was thinking Quantlet when I tried this. Don't own one of those yet.)
\- I hold the light, so that its LEDs are facing my palm.
\- Put my bare feet on it. (Not sure what I was thinking, lol.)

The lights are warm, but don't burn. Too early to say if any of this helps. I'd also like to get a ~6hundred nM LED. I guess these will be brighter, and hotter?

# Thread: Matthew H's Health Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mbhemming — 2017-07-22T23:30:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/post-219777>

Optimum health journal

Hello. I welcome suggestions to help me move toward being optimal

\-main goal: I want to have the energy and dopamine status to allow me to work a full time job as a computer scientist.

\-about me

sex: M

age: 32

height: 6’3

weight between 170 -180(hard to gain weight even when eating a lot)

location: Halifax, Nova Scotia(I’m taking Jack’s advice and next month I’m moving outside the city to the ocean in a remote area)

ancestry: 95% north west Europe(especially english and irish)

education: undergrad in Math/Physics. Currently going back to school to do a masters degree in Computer Science

\-Main issue is periodic fatigue

\-description of fatigue:

\-I lay in bed not feeling like I want to move.

\-low cognition

\-low/no drive/motivation

\-I do nothing but I don’t feel very bored because there is so little actively in my mind

\-approximately each month I have a week where I can’t get out of bed(but its not allways episodic, like this. Sometime I just feel more fatigued for a day)

\-causes that trigger periods of fatigue: alcohol, stress, and exercise can all bring on one of these episodes

\-exercise: If I over-exercise, then I can be wiped out(I went and played basketball for almost 2 hours and I was in bed for 1.5 weeks afterward)

\-alcohol:I can pretty much guarantee that having 4 drinks in a night will cause me to be in bed for the better part of a week afterwards

\-stress:If I have a project due, sometimes I can ‘kick it into high gear’(**not** using stimulants). But then the next day, I crash even harder

\-extremely large meal: Overeating can stimulate a fatigue episode. If I eat really big, then I will feel full for days. This really throws me off. It does take a huge meal to do this. I am fine with even eating quite large meals.

\-sometimes I can feel great for about a week too. And sometimes I feel more or less normal

\-A psychiatrist says its not Bipolar

\-when I come back to feeling good, I will easily get work done. But when I’m feeling poor, its a total struggle

\-additional symptoms during fatigue periods

\-unusual urinary PH rhythm. Instead of the normal PH rhythm of most people who swing acidic in the day and alkaline at night. I stay at at PH of 6. In the past, it has gone up to 7 or 7.5 for a few consecutive days

\-lower cognition

\-low drive

\-social anxiety(present even when not fatigued). Sometimes the anxiety symptoms seem less, because of decreased activity in my mind. But other times, it can cause more anxiety

\-no verbal thoughts present in my mind. When I start feeling better, I notice verbal thoughts coming back in the morning

\-sliding into an OCD state sometimes(defiantly not all the time) during crashes where I will obsessively research something

\-cold hands and feet(during winter). But I notice I get warm palms after I eat

sleep:

\-when I’m feeling good, I sleep about 7 hours and feel rested. When I’m feeling fatigued, I sleep 9 or 9.5 hours and sometimes have a nap in the day too.

\-I go through periods where I naturally get tired at night but also have periods where I am vigilant at night. The sleep disturbances are fairly rare and don’t seem to correlate with the other symptoms listed. Since I started taking Jack’s recommendations about circadian biology(over past 1.5 months), I have found improved stability in sleep patterns. But still not perfect. I will have a number of nights where I fall asleep and wake up at the same time and then I will randomly get a sequence of nights where I have vigilance at that time. This happens despite being certain that I get no blue light after 8pm and finish eating at least 4 hours before bed(usually longer).

\-other observations

\-drinking 3 or 4 glasses of water(I get it out of a natural spring) can temporarily take away fatigue/sleepiness

\-vitamin D is currently at 53 ng/ml(see 2017 blood work attachment)

\-ferritin is at 231ng/ml

\-thyroid pryoxidase antibodies is ‘less than 3 IU/ml’ in 2015. They have the normal reference range at 0 to 5. But I understand you want this to be as close to 0 as possible. Soon,I will have new numbers soon and will have Thyroid Globulin Antibody too.

\-I was told by a naturopath that my morning cortisol was low but still in the mortal range(am coritisol=312 mol/L in 2016). See labs attachment

\-maternal haplotype = H

\-genetic weaknesses from 23 and Me: The only one is increased risk of alzheimer's- GSTP1 I105V(2 mutations), GSTP1 A114V(1 mutations). See detox attachment. I’m not sure what to conclude about this but I heard Jack talking about mutations on the genes associated with Glutathione. So I thought I should put it in.

\-besides the ‘fatigue weeks’ I can often be fatigued for a day or two at a time

\-I shiver easily after a few minutes in the ocean. I also shiver early and chatter my teeth really early in the winter. I’m very sensitive to the cold in the winter

\-symptoms where present when I was living over 2Km from cell towers, relatively low cell phone use, 3 WiFi routers in proximity.

\-what has helped periods of fatigue?

\-creatine _occasionally(not usually)_ pulls me out of the crashes

\-eating beef _occasionally(not usually)_ pulls me out too

\-Pepsi/Coke makes both me and my dad feel much better. My Dad has a lot of fatigue problems too and Cola helps him feel good too.

\-timeline of lifestyle

\-until I was 29 years old

\-had mononucleosis at age 19

\-symptoms slowly worsened during this time. But I was living really unhealthily during that time too.

\-more or less a ‘standard american lifestyle”

\-was a chronic over-eater but I still wouldn’t get fat. Addictive relationship to food

\-partied a lot on weekends. Binge drinking until 3am

\-In my early to mid 20’s I had extreme anxiety and panic attacks. These symptoms actually got better as my fatigue got worse

\-29 to 31 years old

\-drastic reduction in alcohol: some months I wouldn’t drink at all, other months I would have a couple night with around 4 drinks

\-began using methylphenidate. Worked at first but eventually didn’t work. Caused lots of anxiety

\-31 onward

\-lived on the 27th floor of a building with towers at 300m away.

\-fully stopped all alcohol

\-fully stopped all stimulants

\*noticed a definite improvement(but the problem is still present and bad)

\-past 3 months

\-went keto-paleo

\-past month

\-diet:I switched to eating at least half of my meals as seafood the other half paleo. Taking 3g of krill oil with non-seafood meals. Keto during the week. Moderate carbs on weekend. When I move to the ocean next within the next few weeks I will move to eating at least 3/4th of it as seafood. There is a dock just down the road where its easy to get mackrel, herring, sole, and calamari. And I will network with local fisherman for wholesale prices. I met a guy last year who’s father farms oysters and he said he could get me them for 25 or 50 cents. Often the docks have small buildings next to them where people are processing the seafood from their boat. It should be easy to network with them.

I am cooking the food to a lesser degree

I was doing IF before but I stopped it a couple weeks ago. I eat one meal 30 minutes after waking up (6:30am) and then another one at 3:45 pm(I did this because I heard jack say that 4-5pm is an optimal time to generate dopamine.

\-CT: Did the face dunking and now I go in the ocean(57 degrees). Some days I do the block of ice on chest. Adapting slowly but surely. I’m doing it 3 times per week and working up. My goal is to do it overnight before bed for an hour.

\-sunlight

\-getting AM sunlight

\-spending 3 or 4 days/week at the beach

\-I’m getting to a tonne of sunlight, generally

\-blue blocker Uvex at 8pm, sharp

\-when I move to the ocean I will have 4 beaches within a 10 minute drive. You get a cool breeze to so I find I can be in the sun all day there and not feel ‘drained’.

\-non native EMF

\-took Jack’s advice and threw out my microwave oven

\-cell tower close by. Currently moving outside the city. Although the problem began back when I was in a pretty low EMF environment. The place I’m moving has its closest tower at over 1km away.

\-reduced my personal wifi but I’m in a building so I’m surrounded by a dozen routers. This will change in the next month. I may have 1 or 2 routers in 60ft radius.

\-reduced my magnetic field expose as much as possible. Unplugging fridge when I’m in the kitchen, etc.

\-blue light reduction: Blue blocking glasses worn in almost all article light situations. Computers are at colour temp of 1900K and as dim as possible

\-cell phone. I now keep my phone 20ft away and route my texts and calls through my computer (ethernet). After I’m moved in

\-water. I get water out of a fresh spring and drink over a gallon per day.

Hey, Jack. Any changes I should make to the protocol above?

\-family history

Dad: Mental health issues(OCD, Depression, Anxiety, unusually obscure interpretations of events). COPD. Chronic fatigue.

Mom: breast cancer before she was 40. She is 60 and has been healthy since besides high blood pressure

grand dad: perfectly healthy until he died of hear attack at age 89. Still had perfect cognition.

nanny: she is 92 and perfect cognition. Celiac

grampy: 88, has alzheimer's(onset at mid 80’s), heart attack(age 80ish), skin cancer(not a serious type), bowel cancer, diabetes(onset in middle age)

grammy: rheumatoid arthritis, high blood pressure, was on blood thinner, died of stroke at age 72. She had a lot of relatives that had Dementia

Dad’s side: lots of food sensitive(celiac, dairy, soy), most women on my Dad’s side have Celiac.

allow for skin conditioning.

2016 labs
cortisol low
work on your Circadian Rhythm

low sodium, see 2017

2015labs
FreeT3=3.9(3.5-5.9)

eat selenium
Lugols
Look it up on that thread too.

After few months on selenium and lugols if FreeT3 is still low use
Natural thyroid Hormone from pigs (NTH)
Figure out dose by testing (usually no more than 3 grains).
But you have to have good circadian rhythm
good cortisol AM
for all to work.

DO NOT EAT KRIL OIL

To asses if I need to eat seafood

Welcome!! I'm still a newbie to the forum and learning from the experienced ones here, but two things stand out to me...

First, the brain fog. The "not doing anything but not bored." I had that for years because of inflammation. Your description of fatigue I can totally relate to. You can make it go away.

Second, you have relatives with celiac disease. You didn't say anything about gluten in your diet so I'm assuming you had exposure like everyone else. Was the alcohol that made you feel bad beer? I read a paper where close relatives to celiac often have a mucosal reaction to gluten even when they test negative for celiac and don't have the so-called "genes" for it. You could have been reacting to it in the past without knowing it. Paleo should eliminate gluten anyway, but that could have been a problem. Celiac and thyroid disease go together as a pair. Both will absolutely give you brain fog and anxiety.

Also, sub clinical thyroid malfunction can masquerade as bipolar symptoms. I often had days where I was amped up, couldn't sleep, sociable and talkative, did all my chores in a couple hours, and they were always followed by a crash where I couldn't get out of bed. Hashi runs in my family - I had a low antibody count and so-called "normal" hormone labs, vitamin D at 53, but all the symptoms of Hashimoto's. It was horrible. No one would give me thyroid replacement, but I'm glad now, because I found better ways to address it!

I'll let others tell you where the best place to start is with labs and protocols, because I'm not there yet. But I want you to know that I can relate to what you are going through, and you're in the right place. You deserve to feel great, instead of what you feel now.

### seanb4 — 2017-08-11T13:55:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221424>

Does anyone know what angle UVA shows up at?

If so we could probably make a crude guess using a site like this [http://solarelectricityhandbook.com/solar-angle-calculator.html](http://solarelectricityhandbook.com/solar-angle-calculator.html)

At sunrise (5:40) here its 0 Deg, at midday (13:10) 50 Deg. So you could calculate roughly the angle of the sun at any time of day...

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-16T09:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221755>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=221699)
>
> According to this graph, UVA appears around about 5 degrees. This makes me think I am missing The VIS + IR bit of the sun as due to terrain, I don't see sun until 35 minutes after rising. I estimate that the sun raises at around 7 degrees an hour....

If its light there's photons, and you don't need a lot for signalling (think how few blue ones can shut off melatonin at night ....). As the sky colour changes the ratio and dominance of different colours changes, and the brain also registers the increasing crescendo. Many species live on terrain that blocks the direct rays or the camera image of the rising sun. I see it that the photons are not the sun, but they are of the sun, information carriers, and that I am still bathing in the light, and harvesting, even if I can't see the source.

PS thanks for the pic ... It seems to suggest (from the pic I posted earlier) that when yellow becomes the dominant visible frequency in the morning, that UVA is kicking in.

### shiran — 2017-08-16T14:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221763>

Jack mentions the importance of the transition in the morning .
If we are anyway out there every morning, I'm curious why is it so important for you guys to know exactly when the transition is happening ?
People who have limited time in the morning for example..
I would like to be more specific in my recommendation to people regarding the morning exposure..

Another thing I was thinking about was..
If we sleep in a dark room no light gets in ( not like in nature)
how does the body senses it's morning and wakes up naturally ?

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-16T16:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-221771>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=221763)
>
> Jack mentions the importance of the transition in the morning .
> If we are anyway out there every morning, I'm curious why is it so important for you guys to know exactly when the transition is happening ?
> People who have limited time in the morning for example..
> I would like to be more specific in my recommendation to people regarding the morning exposure..
>
> Another thing I was thinking about was..
> If we sleep in a dark room no light gets in ( not like in nature)
> how does the body senses it's morning and wakes up naturally ?

If I understand it right, the blue and red in the early visible light switches the pituitary on, stimulating releasing pituitary hormones. The UV coming in switches the hormones off. So if you go straight out to UV it can affect hormone release. If you see the VIS but miss the UVA transition, you could leave the pituitary hormones switched on when they should be off (or at least the seasonal volume turned down IYKWIM). Also the early morning light and a period of UVA preconditions the skin for UVB. IIRC the first bit of UVA may cause DNA damage, but then it stimulates DNA repair, so the longer I stay out in the morning, the better overall response.

Because of animals underground, hibernation etc, I believe we are also heavily influenced by the earth's rotation, as well as the light cues from the environment that fine tunes us to day length and season. If I look at a picture of the magnetosphere, and think of my location entering the magnetotail at night, (change....), in the middle of the magnetotail (change) and then emerging in the morning, (change) I think of it as the darker the room is, the more sensitive I am to that part of the biological magnetic field rhythm of the earth at night, that it improves my sleep biological rhythms and I wake up at the right time without needing a light signal. Then my aim is to "fine tune" it with the natural frequencies of light during the day, turning things on and off, or turning the volume up and down at the right time, with hormones fluctuating, in line with the season and latitude.

I always sleep with wide open window, my bed is right at the window... and no curtains, so I wake up from the light shining on my face....
Funnily I can sleep past sunrise tho, when sun rises very early I can still sleep until 7 AM, that is when i mostly wake up.. My eyes face east-south when I wake up, but the light still come into my room and I love it . I do not like curtains and closed windows at all!
But I get no fake light at all into my bedroom window.. outside is only green and the heaven...

### drezy — 2017-08-31T03:39:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-222621>

Brent, I loved your questions to doc in the recent Q&A concerning Cuba. Your curiosity may have actually opened up a future residence for my wife and I and I'm grateful that you got doc to dump the info.

Short story:
We are making changes to the unit to make it more sensible. Even @[kvond](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17418/) did not receive her Beta yet. Sorry @[kvond](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17418/) I hope you are getting your morning light regardless

Longer story:

The bright OLED display really kinda sucked from a mitochondriac perspective. We've switched to a LCD screen. It does not emit any light since there is no point for a light sensor to emit it's own light. If you're using it in the dark then you need a little psychological counseling anyway...

Also we found a good LiPo rechargeable battery to slap in there to recharge over micro usb.

Reviewing the sensor manufacturer's data the UV-B is really stretching it:

-   UV-A sensor peak 365nm. The bandwidth (λ0.5) of the UVA channel is within a range of about 350 nm to 375 nm (ok I'm cool enough with that)
-   UV-B sensor peak 330nm. The bandwidth (λ0.5) of this UVB peak is in a range of about 315 nm to 340 nm. (That's kinda stretching it)

We don't want to BS people. The 330nm detection(spurious as it is) does actually help with UVindex erythemal calculations. So I think we're settling on UV-A+ UV-A accumulation(J/cm2) + UVi on the display at this point. I'll hit you with a PM. The good news for you is that a 12 yo performs the labor here. Child labor is cheap, but it's not just one way. I'm building my son up for kick-ass science projects through his educational period.

I literally shoveled horse crap for a few bucks when I was his age. If he puts together UV-A + UVi monitoring units for a few bucks then it seems like a fair generational improvement to me...

### brent-next — 2017-08-31T19:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/post-222663>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=222621)
>
> Brent, I loved your questions to doc in the recent Q&A concerning Cuba. Your curiosity may have actually opened up a future residence for my wife and I and I'm grateful that you got doc to dump the info.

Yes, Dr. Kruse gave some very useful information on Cuba in the August 2017 webinar. Members who haven't listened to it yet should do so, and non-members should consider signing up.

I asked a professional guide to Cuba (from Norway) what she thought about pollution in Cuba. She confirmed that downtown Havana is very polluted, with the outskirts of Havana being slightly less polluted.

She liked the resort towns of Cienfuegos and Trinidad and had this to say about levels of pollution away from Havana.

"Apart from Havana there is very little traffic in Cuba as very few people own their own car or can afford to pay for fuel."

It should be easy to find Canadians who have spent a lot of time in Cuba and can tell you more about it. Canadian travel agencies might also be a good source of up to date travel information.

### Lahelada — 2017-12-26T14:10:23+0200

How is the spectrometer going @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ?

I was just checking that my internal clock has adjusted to summertime (it hasn't ) and found out my UVA window. I looked at your figures for when UVA showed up for you in your post ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-and-uv-a-transition.19869/page-2#post-221513](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/spectroscope-to-map-out-the-ir-a-and-uv-a-transition.19869/page-2#post-221513) )when you measured the jump from ) to o.1 % UV. Assuming you measured in Texas that would mean that there is no UV in summer for around the first hour after sunrise. However...

Sean's graph however say 5 degree elevation. That would mean though that your actual window would end at 6.25 not 7.30 ,unless my calculator does not account for Daylight savings time. Or maybe your reading was not adjusted to daylight savings time ?

This is the calculator I use.

> [https://www.esrl.noaa.gov/gmd/grad/solcalc/azel.html](https://www.esrl.noaa.gov/gmd/grad/solcalc/azel.html)

> [philip malone said:](/goto/post?id=231121)
>
> Sounds quite cool : )

Uh oh, I'm getting called out on this one. This side project has been a dream project to work on with my son. It's also become a bit of an engineer-gone-wild (not as exciting as [girls gone wild](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Girls_Gone_Wild_\(franchise\)) BTW ) venture (imagine a pig discovering a cool patch of mud on a summer day).

The hardware designer on this project is pretty caught up. It's his software engineer that is behind a bit(though rumor has it he's tall and handsome FYI).

I'll break it down several month's design changes in pictures:

On the left is the spectral Irradiance responsivity of Rev 0.1. On the right is Rev 0.4 (worked on over the last 6 months). Note a bit of a spectra difference (2 to 14).

Our design goal when viewing simple color temperature meters ([lumu](https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1365060-REG/lumu_lumu_power_light_meter.html) - $269) was to come in lower than $200 as well as add fine resolution UV & IR detection. Would aspiring film students appreciate a cheaper light meter? I've got feelers out for this currently as well as a dialog with a film equipment rental house. Could many small film producers bankroll some mitochondriac economies of scale savings... Maybe, we'll see.

We bought [this UV meter](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01GT4EF24/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1) ($250) 4 months ago. Did we buy it just to use it personally? Nope. Can we match the results for less on a consumer level with the rev 1.0 sensor? Yep.

Note that from the image on the left(0.1) to the image on the right(0.4) it goes from 2 spectra to 14 spectra. The design goal is that if you have consumer level dollars but are interested in the spectrum from 300-900, like only people here would be, then there should be a solution for you.

We feel strongly in this house about people seeing their environment. We want to become a solution to part of that.

### Lahelada — 2017-12-28T04:00:48+0200
