Yeah I'm sort of picky too. I don't like any light and I keep a fan on. But I'm not getting very good sleep overall. That's why I'm asking.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-24T18:56:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219953>

I think its how you define "good sleep." I was an insomniac who blamed DH's snoring .... I reckoned he could sleep on a clothes line, whereas I needed things to be perfect - and they rarely were. The only decent nights sleep I got was when the sleep pressure of extreme exhaustion took over, but that's not the same as good sleep, to me that's survival mode sleep. The reason DH isn't nearly so bad is that he agreed not to eat after sunset. (Snoring improved considerably and he lost weight in weeks that took me MONTHS to achieve. ) I was moving to another room if he at least didn't try it. Thought my sleep was better until I've kept hacking it. Circadian rhythm, sunlight, grounding, CT, hacking different evening blue blocking options, different black out curtains and bedding. Its an ongoing hack process. Wake feeling refreshed, don't need ear plugs or a mask, asleep within minutes. No idea if he is snoring or not, (but he still doesn't snack in the evening) no full moon differences, I sleep through a storm ... I start on my side facing west, recovery position, 2 pillows, but no idea after that. He's happy because I am, we can sleep together, and I'm not blaming him any more.

### Penny — 2017-07-24T20:05:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-219957>

Its an ongoing hack process. Wake feeling refreshed, don't need ear plugs or a mask, asleep within minutes. No idea if he is snoring or not, (but he still doesn't snack in the evening) no full moon differences, I sleep through a storm ... I start on my side facing west, recovery position, 2 pillows, but no idea after that. He's happy because I am, we can sleep together, and I'm not blaming him any more.

I've had raging sleep success by putting a lamp with a red incandescent light on the bed and wearing a black out mask - I also can fall asleep quite fast if I shine an IR light on the back of my neck/lower head - be careful - you can burn with them if you fall asleep without turning it off... and being cold of course is everything...

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-25T11:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220012>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=219957)
>
> I've had raging sleep success by putting a lamp with a red incandescent light on the bed and wearing a black out mask - I also can fall asleep quite fast if I shine an IR light on the back of my neck/lower head - be careful - you can burn with them if you fall asleep without turning it off... and being cold of course is everything...

Masking my eyes makes them uncomfortable and hot, I've tried several, but maybe my response is because I have a touch of anxiety with them, possibly because with my eyes shut I'm intuitively aware that my eyelids and skin around my eyes can't "breathe" normally. I used to take them on holiday because usually resorts have lights outside to light up paths, and the curtains are never enough. I went from a sleep mask to putting up towels with clothes pegs over the curtains. Even better is thick black wheelie bin liners put on the glass with blue tack. Those are now on the holiday packing essentials list. I'll be ready for night 1.

### caroline — 2017-07-25T12:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220018>

Apparently lowering the foot of the bed - so head is higher - helps

I have also read on QFB - about a weighted blanket. It said \[I think\] it really helps autistic kids.

I would give this a go I think. There was a time when my DH was seriously ill and in the hospital. I stayed close by. It was freezing cold in my room and I didn't want to turn the heater on so I just put all the blankets in the room on top of me. I slept like a log and didn't move the whole night. Given the tragic circumstances - I couldn't believe that I even slept at all.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-25T13:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220024>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=220018)
>
> I have also read on QFB - about a weighted blanket. It said \[I think\] it really helps autistic kids.

I think that could relate to the senses [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-senses.11361/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-senses.11361/). If an autistic child doesn't sense the right fields, I think they could feel that they aren't anchored to their environment, as if they might float away, so "letting go" to sleep could be extremely stressful, if the environment they sense doesn't make them feel "safe." If its not the sensory issue, I think the weighted blanket may not be as successful. The senses list is one reason why I'm glad I don't need to sleep separately from DH, I could and would if I had to, but being with him I still get the proximal closeness, pheremonal sensing , magnetic field interaction etc...Being able to sleep now anyway, it adds to my sense of well being. Now I am able to sleep because of hacking light water and magnetism etc, his presence makes it a 1+1 = 3.

I just tried this last night and it worked! I had the lamp on for a few minutes and dang, I was so relaxed! I'm going to do this from now on.

Weighted blankets are for sensory issues - there was a blog on this - and I think it would be hotter than hell

It is SO bloody hot right now, I can't use the black out curtains I bought and the sleep mask was too hot - and it's not even August/September yet - our hottest months - I'm going for the ice pack tonight or just sleeping in the motorhome - zippo air flow - if you use the red light, I wouldn't fall asleep with your face pointed to it - every time I do, my eyes are blurry for awhile - this can't be good news for my biology Not sure reading at night with a red light is that great either - I just reread Quantum sleep and it seems to me it really has to be dark right after the sunset transition - or have lots of sex and you will immediately feel sleepy

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-26T08:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220103>

I might try it on a colder night here and see if I notice a difference.

The other thing I'd add is that my period is due and before then I feel even hotter - as in warm, not sexy - and that makes sleep worse too.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-26T10:35:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-220111>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=220051)
>
> Does that create a sauna in the room by bin lining the windows??

I've only done it once in Cornwall in April. The ground floor room was in a converted coach house, couldn't get a phone signal, but they had a floodlight lighting up the whole courtyard outside and we had large windows and a glazed door. Didn't have enough towels to peg up to get it blocked and even if we did there was too much light bleeding through the top bottom and sides of the supposedly blackout curtains.  We went to a supermarket to buy a dark king sized sheet and I suddenly had a picture in my head of bin liners stuck up ... Rigged it so that the windows were covered, but one for ventilation we covered the glass and opened the window, and used a towel over that part of the curtaining. So April in the UK no problem. In Fuerteventura we had an apartment and used towels in the bedroom, which on their own I thought would reduce the circulation of the air coming through the partly opened window, but we left the bedroom door open and opened a window in the bathroom and a sliding door in the lounge so that there was a pull through of air. I'll have to assess the layout in every apartment/hotel room we go to, but I think the liners would still work.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-26T16:55:17+0300

My friend has hacked them and wearing them for 20 minutes before bed has improved her sleep, and she thinks she is dreaming more. 20-30 minutes is about right for me too, I'm still hacking ones for earlier. If you wanted to try it, PM me, I could post you a piece of the gel, you would need some cheap as chips clear safety glasses, cut a paper template to get an exact fit for the inside of them and then cut the gel to fit.

....I used to sleep with a **_Bed Buddy_** Hot/Cold Pack Thermatherapy from _Bed Bath & Beyond_ on my chest at night in the winter and it was warm and relaxing but used microwave to heat and after I got rid of my microwave I changed to use a **_Fomentek_** _Hot/Cold water bag_ [https://fomentek.com/](https://fomentek.com/) The Fomentek bag are also great for using for castor oil packs.

### caroline — 2019-12-29T03:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284032>

Please keep us posted Martha if you get a weighted blanket.

I have probably posted this before....Once when my DH was in the hospital for an extended stay - I stayed in the Lodge that the hospital provides for family of very ill patients.
It was extremely cold one night and I didn't want to turn the heater on and so I piled all the blankets from the other beds on my single bed. I even had them doubled over. I had the best nights sleep, even tho my DH was gravely ill, and I never moved the entire night.

I think that was my version of a weighted blanket and I have been keen to try one ever since. I think all the weight made me feel safe and comforted.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-12-29T16:31:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284036>

Have you seen or read about Temple Grandin? She is an autistic university professor that pioneered low stress animal handling. An amazing, inspiring story. I highly recommend her 2010 movie for all , and any of her books for those interested in animals.
Anyway, while in college, struggling mightily with social stress, she built a squeeze chute to calm herself like the ones used in cattle handling. This squeeze effect may be similar to the weighted blanket or that provided by submersion in water. OG biohacker extraordinaire! Changed her environment and the environment of other stakeholders (animals).
I see many parallels for those with developed natural field sensitivities or nnemf . 5GCanary ! We can improve our environment and advocate for other less represented members of our ecosytem.

### Martha Ray — 2020-01-05T04:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-position.19872/post-284245>

> [Foxglove said:](/goto/post?id=219885)
>
> ...
> Just wondering if anyone has thoughts on this? I want my sleep to be more restful.

**I am very interested in better sleep.**
I deisred for many years (long b4 Jack Kruse).. to go to bed at sunset and wake with sunrise--- always made sense to me. But I never did it until this year and not acomplished at it...yet. Did read a Quirky but purposeful book on sleep titled
[Lights Out: Sleep, Sugar and Survival](http://www.amazon.com/Lights-Out-Sleep-Sugar-Survival/dp/0671038680), T. S. Wiley [https://www.amazon.com/Lights-Out-Sleep-Sugar-Survival/dp/0671038680](https://www.amazon.com/Lights-Out-Sleep-Sugar-Survival/dp/0671038680)
Very ancestral better re-read it now. I also read a great article that I think was by Victoria Boutenko author of _Green for Life_... will go try and find that one ..whoever it was she and her family were sleeping out doors in a 'yurt' ? type round building with high windows that opened .. again I will go try and find that... I hope I kept it--- It amazed me. If I find will post.

I sleep on my back but wish I could more or change positions a little more.... I wonder about sleep wear, beds, bedding etc. I have to have things just so to get to sleep -- find afer that. Have been testing an Avana Comfort Mattress Elevator for _**Incline Bed Therapy**_ and intersted in Weighted Blankets looking at **_Xalm_** [https://xalm.co.uk/products/xalm-weighted-blanket](https://xalm.co.uk/products/xalm-weighted-blanket) also on Amazon... I think this is the brand _Temple Grandin_ recommends but too expenisve for me might try a Yoga Pad like the[RAVI Yoga | For Yoga Practice & Calming](<http://RAVI Yoga | For Yoga Practice & Calming>) [https://myravi.com/shop-ravi-yoga/](https://myravi.com/shop-ravi-yoga/)

I just shared the info below with _Cozy Pure_ and _Flo-Bed_ bedding product companies I've done business with in the past so I cut and pasted it here also mentions _**Mouth Taping**_ that I believe has been a great find for our family. DH uses any wound tape but I started out with blue 3M wound tape for sensitive skin but I have such ultra sensitve skin I use _**Somnifix**_ [https://somnifix.com/products/mouth...MIxtrH4Kzr5gIVOYNaBR02fgfrEAAYASABEgJd1vD\_BwE](https://somnifix.com/products/mouth-strips-snoring-sleep-aid?gclid=EAIaIQobChMIxtrH4Kzr5gIVOYNaBR02fgfrEAAYASABEgJd1vD_BwE)
I have to remove some of the adheisive by stripping it putting in on clean organic fabric and pulling it off numerous times. **Pricey but so critical to our sleep** DH snors and I puff as my dentist explained. The Somnifix are supposed to be single use but I handle with care and get a week to 10 nights.
\-------------------------------

Avana Comfort 7" Elevator Mattress link - I am testing this product out now - so far so good.**
and Tip about Mouth Taping You Tube Video is below.

# Thread: Time with your kids.............
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-with-your-kids.19873/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-24T04:38:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-with-your-kids.19873/post-219888>

Today in a canoe in a cenote in the raging tropical sun........: In family relationships, love is really spelled T.I.M.E. We're all on borrowed time so we should make sure we use it wisely. Family for me, when I grew up, was a lot like nature. I realized early on I wanted to be different and take a unique path; like branches on a tree, we all grow in different directions yet our roots remain as one. I think if you want something to last forever, you must treat it differently than anything else. You shield it and protect it. You never abuse it. You must expose it to the elements to toughen it to make an enduring monument. You don’t make it common or ordinary, you add a bit 'extra' to make it extraordinary. If it ever becomes tarnished, you lovingly polish it until it shines. It becomes special because you have made it so, and it grows more beautiful and precious as time goes by but it never takes the path you expect and must be OK with that. Listen carefully to anything your children want to tell you, no matter what it is. If you don’t listen eagerly to the little stuff when they are little, they won’t tell you the big stuff when they are big, because to them all of it has always been big stuff.

### mbhemming — 2017-07-24T15:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-nova-scotia-canada.19882/post-219926>

Hello,

I'm here to get optimal and connect with similar minds.

I came across Dr. Kruse around 1.5-2 years ago. I was definitely intrigued because he was offering mechanism that lead to similar conclusion that others were making. Only the other people either had no mechanism or thought it was a different mechanism. As time went on, I was noticing that all the other medical thinkers that I respect were coming more and more in alignment with Dr. Kruse's ideas.

I've decided to do an experiment. For the next year, I am going to make the lifestyle changes that he recommends and see if I get better. I wont be able to move to the ocean(and away from EMF) until next month so I will officially start the experiment then.

I welcome you to check out my health journal to make suggestions about what might be wrong with me.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/matthew-hs-health-journal.19868/)

Matthew

# Thread: Can I turn off wifi but use ethernet on a wifi router....and pc ethernet light not on/won't work
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ValerieBee — 2017-07-24T23:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/post-219971>

Embarrassed to ask, but I'd rather be embarrassed than deal with more EMF than my family needs. I want to get rid of WIFI in the house ASAP. However, my partner, the homeowner, does not. I'm SAHM with a 4 and 1 year old. He works about 60 hours per week, so that's all time it could be off, but I still need to connect to the internet sporadically throughout the day. (Bills, emails, help like this on the forum... ) I am trying to configure it so it would only be on during evening hours when he's home and awake and wants to use it. We have a wifi router. Its looking like I cannot disable wifi while still keeping ethernet on? Do I need to buy a different type of router? I'm currently having an issue that Windows 10 has disabled my ethernet. I know it is not a problem with the router or the cord, the green light is not on at the desktop pc. Should I just put the WiFi on a kill switch and only plug it in when it is needed, rather than use the ethernet? Please forgive my ignorance here. I'm learning as much and as fast as I can here and I appreciate any help. TIA.

### drezy — 2017-07-25T01:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/post-219974>

No need to be embarrassed. That's kind of specific to spread out on a forum maybe. You can PM me and Caroline the site moderator (and or anyone else you are comfortable including in a PM) to keep yourself at ease that I'm not some internet hyper-predator. Hyper-predator even sounds like too much work for me... I'll describe what we did and how we did it.

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-25T04:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/post-219996>

Thank you. Will email tonight later or tomorrow. Getting everyone to sleep now......... but feel free to PM me.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-07-25T08:33:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-turn-off-wifi-but-use-ethernet-on-a-wifi-router-and-pc-ethernet-light-not-on-wont-work.19884/post-220008>

Sounds complicated! We switched off the wifi router and plugged the computer into the phone switch in the wall - granted we're UK, not sure if that's different. The WIFI could get switched on if need be but now I just use my husband's computer sporadically (to go on this forum mostly) during the day and I've no idea how to turn it on even if I wanted to.
